It does, however, block the passage of sound from one side to the other, assuming that the spell is not cast in such a way (such as in a field of grass) which would otherwise allow sound to move around the edges of the wall.

spell.name: Word of Distraction
entries: You attempt to distract a creature momentarily using a short phrase or another distracting sound.

The creature must attempt a Wisdom Saving Throw. If it fails, it has disadvantage on Perception checks against all objects and creatures other than you for 1 round. In addition, at the start of the creature's next turn, it must use it's action to make a Perception check to stare at you if you are within line of sight, or seek you if you are not.

spell.name: Animate Claw

components.m: a silver ring worth at least 25 gp

entries: Choose a severed hand within range. The hand must be from a murderer, and must have been severed no more than 24 hours ago. The target is animated into a crawling claw (the DM has the creature's game statistics).

On each of your turns, you can use a bonus action to mentally command the claw if it is within 60 feet of you. You decide what action the claw will take and where it will move during its next turn, or you can issue a general command, such as to guard a particular chamber or corridor. If you issue no commands, the claw only defends itself against hostile creatures. Once given an order, the claw continues to follow it until its task is complete.
The claw is under your control until you cast this spell again, after which it stops obeying any command you've given it.

spell.name: Speed Read

components.m: reading spectacles worth at least 1 gp

entries: For the duration of the spell, you gain the ability to read text at a rate of 100 pages per hour and have perfect comprehension of all knowledge learned. While holding the text, you can move through the text at a rate to maintain the speed at which you can read. Once the spell ends, you can remember details from the texts that you had read as if you would've read the text normally. This spell does not decipher text or coded messages nor grants you the ability to read languages that you do not know.

spell.name: Darkbeam
entries: This spell unleashes a 4-inch beam of darkness in a straight line from the caster's hand.
A creature must roll a Dexterity Saving Throw against your spell save DC to avoid being struck by the darkbeam.
On failure, the target either suffers {@damage 2d8} cold damage (if it is alive) or is under the effect of a {@spell Hold Monster|XPHB} spell untill the end of your next turn (if undead).
In addition, a creature affected by this spell are {@condition blinded|XPHB} for {@dice 1d4} rounds.

spell.name: Gradient Flux
entries: You infuse a creature with unstable arcane motion energy, causing its ability to move to behave erratically. Choose one creature within range. The target must succeed on a Constitution Saving Throw or be affected for the duration.
At the start of each of its turns, the affected creature flips a coin:

Heads: Exploding Gradient. When the creature first moves on its turn, it must immediately expend all of its movement in that direction in a straight line. It stops if it hits an unmoveable obstacle. This movement provokes opportunity attacks as normal.
Tails: Vanishing Gradient. The creature’s speed is halved until the start of its next turn. It may move as normal but only with the reduced speed, which can be split between actions.
The spell ends early if the target is {@condition incapacitated|TGTT} or {@condition restrained|TGTT}.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 2nd.

spell.name: Smog Cloud

components.m: a handful of ash

entries: You create a 20-foot-radius sphere of black smog centered on a point within range. The cloud spreads around corners, and its area is heavily obscured. The cloud lingers in the air for the duration.

Each creature that is completely within the cloud at the start of its turn must make a Constitution Saving Throw. On a failed save, the creature is choked as per the {@condition Choked|TGTT} Condition.
Creatures that don't need to breathe automatically succeed on this Constitution Saving Throw.
A moderate wind (at least 10 miles per hour) disperses the cloud after 4 rounds. A strong wind (at least 20 miles per hour) disperses it after 1 round.

spell.name: Spider Gout

components.m: a sack of poisonous saliva

entries: This spell creates a magical glob of spider venom in the caster’s mouth, which the caster can spit at any single target within range, as part of the casting of this spell.
Alternatively, the caster can hold the venom, enhancing its strength.
The venom can only be held for a number of rounds equal to half the caster's level. If held longer, the caster suffers the damage from the spell as it triggers inside its mouth.
To spit the venom glob after it has been held for any number of rounds, you must use your action.
When you spit, make a spell attack roll against the target. A creature struck suffers {@damage 2d8} poison damage, + {@damage 1d8} for each round the caster held the spell.

spell.name: Avoidance / Attraction
entries: By means of this spell, the caster sets up a natural repulsion or attraction between the affected object and all other living things except himself.

entries.name: Repulsion
entries: Any living creature attempting to touch the affected object is repulsed (unable to come closer than 1 foot), or repulses the affected object, depending on the relative mass of the two (a child attempting to touch an iron chest with an avoidance spell upon it will be thrown back, while the chest will skitter away from a giant-sized creature as the creature approaches).

entries.name: Attraction
entries: A creature is drawn to the object if the creature is smaller, or the object slides toward the creature if the creature is larger. It takes a successful Athletics check to remove the enchanted object once it has adhered to a creature.

The spell cannot be cast upon living things; any attempt to cast an avoidance spell upon the apparel or items carried by a living creature entitles the subject creature to a Dexterity Saving Throw to avoid the effect.

spell.name: Counterspell

time.condition: which you take when you see a creature within 60 feet of you casting a spell

entries: You attempt to interrupt a creature in the process of casting a spell. If the creature is casting a spell of 1st level or lower, its spell fails and has no effect. If it is casting a spell of 5th level or higher, the counterspell has no effect.
In any other case, make a spell attack roll against the creature's spellcasting DC. On a success, the creature's spell fails and has no effect.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, for each slot level above 3rd, increase the level of spells that counterspell automatically succeeds and fails against.

spell.name: Ward Dream

m.text: a dreamcatcher woven from a silver thread worth 10 gp

entries: You create a protective barrier around yourself and up to five willing creatures of your choice within a 10-foot radius. This barrier guards against any attempts to intrude or manipulate your and their dreams.

A creature under the effect of this spell has protection from dream-based intrusions and effects (such as the Dream spell, the Dreamwatch ability, etc.) while the spell is active. Attempting to penetrate the barrier’s defenses requires a successful Intelligence saving throw against the caster's spell save DC.
In addition, protected creatures gain advantage on saving throws against charm and fear effects originating from dream or mind-based sources.
The spell is dispelled if you move outside of its radius.

spell.name: Venom Bite
entries: The spell brings into being a small set of pointed teeth that are fired outward from the caster's body.
Make a ranged spell attack against the target; any creature struck by a set of teeth takes {@damage 2d8} poison damage, and must make a Constitution Saving Throw against your spell save DC.
If it fails, it is {@condition slowed|TGTT} as per the 3rd level spell, and can repeat its save at the end of each of its turns. On a successful save, the effect ends for it.
A creature targeted by multiple bites does not make additional saves.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, you create an extra set of teeth for each slot level above 2nd.

spell.name: Misdirection
entries: By means of this spell the caster misdirects the information a 2nd level (or lower) divination spell gathers.

When such a divination spell is cast, the caster can roll an Intelligence Saving Throw. On failure, the information their spell reveals will indicate the wrong answer - for example, {@spell detect magic|TGTT} will show no magic, {@spell see invisibility|XPHB} will show nothing, {@spell detect evil and good|XPHB} will show opposite alignment and so on.
The caster directs the spell effect upon the creature or item which is the target of the spell, meaning they decide what kind of wrong information the spell will detect.

spell.name: Nondetection

components.m: a pinch of diamond dust worth 25 gp sprinkled over the target, which the spell consumes

entries: For the duration, you hide a target that you touch from divination magic. The target can be a willing creature or a place or an object no larger than 10 feet in any dimension.
If the target is unwilling, it needs to make a Wisdom Saving Throw. If it succeeded, it is immune to the effect of the spell for the next 8 hours, provided it is being cast against its will.

The target can't be targeted by any divination magic or perceived through magical scrying sensors created by spells of 4th level or lower. Spells of higher level keep working, but this spell does add +5 to the check DC of the {@spell scrying|TGTT} spell.

spell.name: Animate Armor

components.m: a nonmagical suit of plate armor worth at least 1,500 gp

entries: You imbue a nonmagical suit of plate armor with magic, turning it into an animated armor for the spell's duration. Once the spell ends, or if the armor is reduced to 0 hit points, it reverts back to a nonmagical suit of plate armor.

The armor is under your control, and understands and obeys your spoken commands. Roll initiative for the armor, which has its own turns. It obeys any verbal commands that you issue to it (no action required by you). If you don't issue any commands to the armor, it defends itself from hostile creatures but otherwise takes no actions.
The DM has the armor's statistics.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: If you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the duration is concentration, up to 10 minutes. If you use a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the duration is 8 hours. If you use a spell slot of 7th level or higher, the duration is 24 hours. If you use a 9th level spell slot, the spell lasts until the armor is reduced to 0 hit points. Using a spell slot of 5th level or higher grants a duration that doesn't require concentration.

spell.name: Privacy Field
entries: You create a discrete field of energy that keeps your surrounding private. The field is a sphere centered around you and is up to 15 feet in radius.

Everything in the field becomes blurred, shifting and wavering to all who stand outside of it. Voices become muffled and muted, smells are weakened, and small vibrations dissipate when they pass through the field. This effect is hard to notice if not directly trying to gain sensory information from across the spheres boundaries, and requires a passive {@skill Perception} check against the caster's Spell Save DC to notice when not examined.

For the duration, any creature outside the field cannot glean information via senses (sight, smell, hearing) from inside the field. In addition, a creature trying to attack from outside has disadvantage on attack rolls against beings inside the field.
This spell also gives a +2 to saves against divination spells that aim to discover information about creatures inside the barrier.
A creature is immune to this effect if it can see through illusions, as with {@sense truesight}.

spell.name: Arachnophobia
entries: Upon casting this spells, the caster creates the illusion of thousands upon thousands of crawling spiders, running up and covering the body of the target.
The target must roll two Intelligence Saving Throw, and the effects of the spell are as follows:
- 2 Failures - the target is driven to complete panic by the arachnids, and uses it's turn trying to remove them from its body, rendering itself incapacitated.
- 1 Failure - the target believes it dodged the horde of spiders, but only momentarily, and will attempt to put as much distance from it as possible, as if under the effect of the {@spell fear|XPHB}.
- No Failures - the target is unaffected by the spell.

spell.name: Compel
entries: This spell has the same effect and restrictions as the {@spell command|XPHB}, with two notable differences:
- This spell targets either one or two creatures, your choice.
- This spell lasts for 2 rounds, and the command you give can be changed after the end of the first round without allowing for a repeated save.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, you can affect two additional creatures for each slot level above 3rd. The creatures must be within 30 feet of each other when you target them.

spell.name: Essonoz's Minor Runes
entries: You touch a nearby surface, creating a circular rune around the point you touched that lasts for the duration of the spell. Choose one of the following runes. The area and effects of the rune are determined by the type of rune you choose:

- Rune of Recall. You create a 5 ft radius rune that resembles a teleportation circle and glows faintly with blue light. For the duration, you can use a bonus action to transport any willing creature you can see within 30 ft of you, to an unoccupied space of your choice within the rune.

- Rune of Shielding. You create a 15 ft radius rune that is surrounded by a mostly transparent but clearly visible dome made out of pure arcane energy. The dome has AC 10 and 30 HP. The dome lasts for the duration of the spell.

- Rune of Flight. You create 15 ft radius rune that glows with bright yellow light. Any creature in the 100 ft tall cylinder based on the rune has a flying speed of 20 ft as long as it remains inside the area.

- Rune of Time Hopping. You create a 5 ft radius rune that resembles a clock and glows with green light. A creature that ends its turn inside the area of the rune vanishes from existence, and reappears at the start of its following turn in the same space, or in the nearest unoccupied space if that space is occupied. Once used, the creature can’t use the rune again. If the spell ends before a creature has reappeared, it reappears immediately and takes {@damage 1d10} force damage.

spell.name: Transfix
entries: You choose a point within range as an anchor. Each humanoid within a 20-foot radius of that point must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the humanoid becomes transfixed.
Any humanoid that enters the spell's area after it is cast must also make the saving throw (with advantage) or become transfixed.

Transfixed humanoids cannot move, cast spells, or take any actions. When the spell is cast and a humanoid becomes transfixed, you must state a condition that will release them, such as "Wait here until I return" or "Stay there until the sun goes down."

The condition must be specific and have a defined end. Impractical conditions are allowed, but indefinite conditions like "Stay here for all of eternity" will not work. If a humanoid does not understand the language in which the condition is given, it is unaffected by the spell even if it failed its saving throw.

spell.name: Polymorph
entries: You attempt to transform a creature that you can see within range into a Beast. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or shape-shift into Beast form for the duration. That form can be any {@filter Beast|bestiary|type=beast|miscellaneous=!swarm} you choose that has a {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB} equal to or less than the target's (or the target's level - 2 if it doesn't have a {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB}). The target's game statistics are replaced by the stat block of the chosen Beast, but the target retains its alignment, personality, creature type, {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, and {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}.

The target gains a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} of the Beast form. These {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} vanish if any remain when the spell ends. The spell ends early on the target if it has no {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} left.
The target is limited in the actions it can perform by the anatomy of its new form, and it can't speak or cast spells.
The target's gear melds into the new form. The creature can't use or otherwise benefit from any of that equipment.

spell.name: Scrying

components.m: a focus worth at least 1,000 gp, such as a crystal ball, a silver mirror, or a font filled with holy water

entries: You can see and hear a particular creature you choose that is on the same plane of existence as you. You roll a {@dice d20} and add your spell attack modifier to it, which is then modified by how well you know the target and the sort of physical connection you have to it. If a target knows you're casting this spell, it can fail the Wisdom Saving Throw voluntarily if it wants to be observed. The DC for the check is {@dc 22}, without additional increases the target may be granted due to magic or other circumstances.

On a successful check, the spell creates an invisible sensor within 10 feet of the target. You can see and hear through the sensor as if you were there. The sensor moves with the target, remaining within 10 feet of it for the duration. A creature that can see invisible objects sees the sensor as a luminous orb about the size of your fist.

entries.caption: Knowledge Modifiers
rows: Secondhand (you have heard of the target)
Firsthand (you have met the target, you are familiar with the target's aura)

entries.caption: Connection Modifiers
rows: Body part, lock of hair, bit of nail, or the like

entries: On a failed check, the target isn't affected, and you can't use this spell against it again for 24 hours. If you fail by more than 10, the target is aware of the attempt to scry on it.

Instead of targeting a creature, you can choose a location you have seen before as the target of this spell. When you do, the sensor appears at that location and doesn't move, and the DC for success is {@dc 15}.

spell.name: Andrui's Baneful Backfire

components.m: a bit of cat fur and a piece of carpeting roughly three inches square

entries: This spell enables the caster to protect his magical property and enchantments from tampering by other spellcasters. The backfire can be cast on any object or person under the effect of a spell, even temporarily endowed with magical properties (such as a character who has been slowed).
The backfire remains dormant until a creature attempts to cast {@spell dispel magic} on the object.

The backfire not only causes the dispel to fail automatically, but the dispelling caster loses one spell slot of a level equal to the spell slot level it used to cast the {@spell dispel magic|TGTT}. If it have no such spell slot, the backfire causes it to lose a spell slot of the next lower level, until the caster has lost one spell slot or has no spell slots to lose.

For example, suppose that a wizard is attempting to cast {@spell dispel magic} regularly on a door that has been wizard locked and is protected by the baneful backfire. Upon casting the dispel, the wizard instantly loses one 3rd level spell slot (in addition to the one used to cast {@spell dispel magic|TGTT} in the first place) and the door is still magically sealed.
The baneful backfire fails if the {@spell dispel magic} was cast with a higher level spell slot than the one used to cast the baneful backfire.

spell.name: Conduit
entries: Using this spell, the caster can temporarily enchant up to five missiles (either arrows, bolts, darts or any other weapon which has a ranged property but isn't used to move other weapons, meaning this spell cannot be applied to bows or crossbows) with spells of 4th level or less which have a casting time of 1 action.

This spell allows the caster to extend the range of a spell by the range of the missile weapon, and let others use their spell in their place. Unless otherwise specified when the spell is cast, the effects of the spell radiate forward from the point the missile strikes.

The casting time for the conduit spell does not include the casting times of the spells loaded into the missiles, which are considered to be cast directly after the end of the casting of this spell - meaning spell slots are spent, and material components are consumed, according to the requirements of these spells.
Each missile receives its own spell, and a missile cannot receive multiple enchantments, even from different castings of this spell.
To use the missiles to attack requires an action.

An enchanted missile releases its spell when the head of the missile strikes something, whether that be the target, the ground, a tree, a rock, or whatever. This means a spell with a radius, such as a fireball, is often more effective than a spell such as burning hands or shocking grasp.
If the cast spell requires concentration, the user of the missile is the one who will be concentrating.

spell.name: Lightningold's Golden Lightning

components.m: a small gold-infused gem worth at least 100 gold pieces

entries: A dazzling spell invented by the famous Gale Lightningold, this spell summons potent golden lightning to strike down foes.
When you cast this spell, a golden orb of crackling electricity is conjured above you.
Make a ranged spell attack against a target within range. A golden lightning will shoot from the orb at the target. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 8d8} lightning damage.

For the spell's duration, you can use your bonus action on each of your turns to direct a lightning to a new target within range or cause it to strike the same target again. Each subsequent hit against the same target increases the damage by {@damage 2d8}, to a maximum of {@damage 12d8} on the third and following hits.
Regardless of whether the attack hits or not, creatures in a 20-foot radius of the target must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking half of the damage caused by the lightning on a failed save.

spell.name: Greater Contact Other Plane

components.m: a salt crystal and a drop of the casters blood

entries: You mentally contact a god, the spirit of a long-dead sage, or some other mysterious entity from another plane. Contacting this extraplanar intelligence strains, and can even break your mind. When you cast this spell, make a {@dc 20} Intelligence Saving Throw. On a failure, you take {@damage 12d6} psychic damage and are insane indefinitely. While insane, you can't understand what other creatures say, can't read, speak only in gibberish, and have a greate urge to damage people and property in your vicinity. A {@spell greater restoration|XPHB} spell cast on you ends this effect.

On a successful save, you can ask the entity up to three questions dealing with either the present (i.e "what traps protect the crystal sword" or "where is the dark sceptre"), or the past (i.e "who killed the duke"). You must ask your questions before the spell ends. The DM answers each question truthfully, but does not provide information beyond what the question specifically asks for.

spell.name: Olo's Mirror of Memories

components.m: an item belonging to the target, and a mithril mirror worth 1750 GP which the spell consumes

entries: Invented by Olo The Bard, this spell is used to both incapacitate and gain information, but comes with a similar cost for the caster.
Choose a creature you can see within range; the target must make a Wisdom Saving Throw (which it can choose to fail) against your saving spell DC.

On failure, both you and the target gain the {@condition blinded|XPHB}, {@condition deafened|XPHB}, and {@condition paralyzed|TGTT} conditions, and begin experiencing each other's memories at an accelerated pace. For each round passing, you both experience one year from the life of the other, in chronological order (the first year you experience is the first year of the targets life).

The spell ignores effects which modified, removed, or repressed any of your or your target's memories, and allows you (and the target) to observe memories that the other may not be aware of, though not memories gained by casting this spell.
The spell lasts until either of you have viewed all of the life of the other, or until either of you is takes damage.

When the spell ends, you both recall the memories you viewed in general details, and must succeed on a {@dc 25} Intelligence Saving Throw to recall any specifics. You may recall specific details without the save only if you were aware of their existence before the spell was cast and intended to remember them.

spell.name: Fearheart’s Grasp of Death
entries: You send negative energy to engulf a creature that you can see within range, causing it inconceivable pain. The target must make a Constitution Saving Throw. It takes {@damage 10d8 + 40} necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
The soul of a humanoid killed by this spell rises at the start of your next turn as a shade that is permanently under your command, following your verbal orders to the best of its ability.

spell.name: Fearheart’s Orb of Darkness

components.m: a black diamond worth at least 1,000 gold pieces, which is consumed by the spell

entries: Created by Magna Um Fearheart, the first lich and the killer of Mauran Magefather, this spell summons a sphere of malevolent dark energy. The Orb of Darkness is a powerful force of necromantic energy that corrupts, drains, and obscures, and is the dark counterpart to Mauran's ball of light.

When you cast this spell, a dark, swirling sphere of void energy manifests, 3 feet in diameter, centered 5 feet above you. The Orb of Darkness has three functions, which you can activate as a bonus action on your turn:

- Necrotic Drain: The orb pulses with draining energy. Each creature of your choice within range must make a Constitution Saving Throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 10d12} necrotic damage and you regain hit points equal to half the damage dealt. On a successful save, the creature takes half damage and you gain no hit points. Undead creatures are instead healed by this effect.

- Terror Surge: The orb releases a wave of fear. Each creature of your choice within 60 feet must make a Wisdom Saving Throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 8d8} psychic damage and becomes {@condition frightened|XPHB} of you for 1 minute. A {@condition frightened|XPHB} creature can repeat the Wisdom Saving Throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. On a successful save, the creature takes half damage and isn't {@condition frightened|XPHB}.

- Void Cloak: The sphere shrouds the area in magical darkness. The darkness heavily obscures the area and cannot be dispelled by light sources or spells lower than 9th level. While in the area, you and creatures of your choice are invisible and gain resistance to all damage except radiant damage. Additionally, you and your allies can see through this darkness.

spell.name: Mauran's Ball of Light

components.m: a crystal orb embedded with a sapphire worth at least 500 gold pieces

entries: Mauran Magefather, known to be the first to rediscover the arcane arts after the collapse, created this signature spell as the epitome of versatility, a true wizard's tool. This radiant orb embodies the wisdom and might of Mauran, able to heal, harm, and illuminate.

When you cast this spell, a glowing sphere of pure light manifests, 3 feet in diameter, centered 5 feet above you. The Ball of Light has three functions, which you can activate as a bonus action on your turn:

- Healing Aura: The Ball of Light emanates soothing energy. Each creature of your choice within the radius regains {@dice 10d6} hit points when you activate this effect, and all negative status conditions affecting it end. Undead and constructs cannot be healed by this effect.

- Radiant Pulse: The sphere releases a burst of radiant energy. Each creature of your choice within range must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 12d10} radiant damage and is {@condition blinded} until the start of your next turn. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage and isn’t blinded.

- Illuminating Presence: The Ball of Light dispels magical darkness in its radius and provides bright light, which counts as daylight and functions as such. It also grants advantage on Perception checks and Wisdom Saving Throw to every creature you choose within range. In addition, the orb's light reveals invisible creatures and objects within its area.

spell.name: Veil Dream

m.text: a piece of obsidian inscribed with runes, worth 250 gp

entries: You cloak your dreams in shadowy illusions, making them hard to find for outside forces. This spell hides the caster's dreams from detection, scrying, or manipulation.

Attempts to observe or interact with the caster's dreams require two separate saving throws—the first is an Intelligence saving throw attempting to locate the dream, and the second is a Wisdom saving throw attempting to breach the veil.
Creatures failing the second save take {@damage 5d8} psychic damage and are {@condition stunned} until the end of their next turn.

spell.name: Dream Stride
entries: Dream Stride allows the caster to enter the dreamtime, the world of dreams, using their own body.
The caster can move through the dreamtime to explore the shifting landscapes of the dream world. For each minute spent in the dream realm, they can cover a mile in the material world.

The caster can interact with dream elements and creatures within the dreamtime. They can attempt to influence the dreams they visit, using their Constitution (Concentration) to make changes like any creature with the Dreamwalk ability might.

spell.name: Shared Dream

m.text: a shard of polished obsidian, worth 1000 gp

entries: You create a shared dreamscape, allowing willing participants to enter a collective dream. Choose up to six willing creatures within range. For the duration of the spell, all chosen creatures fall into a deep slumber and share a dream designed by you.
While in the shared dream:

items: All participants retain their consciousness and control, allowing clear communication and interaction.
You can shape the dream's environment as a bonus action.

entries: Participants are aware they are in a dream and can leave by waking up. Creatures not chosen to participate cannot perceive or interfere with it unless they have Dreamwalker abilities or similar magic.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the number of participants increases by two for each slot level above 5th.

spell.name: Nightmare Conduit

m.text: a piece of jagged obsidian, worth 500 gp, which the spell consumes

entries: You forge a terrifying link between a creature and its darkest nightmares, forcing them to confront their fears in a harrowing mental assault.
Choose one creature within range that you can see. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, they suffer the following effects:

items: {@b Psychic Backlash:} The target takes {@damage 5d10} psychic damage at the start of each of its turns.
{@b Frightened Condition:} The target becomes {@condition frightened} of you, unable to willingly move closer to you.
{@b Unraveling Sanity:} The target has disadvantage on attack rolls, saving throws, and ability checks for the duration.

entries: If this spell is cast in The Dreamtime, its effects are amplified:

items: The frightened condition extends to all creatures within 30 feet of the target, as their nightmares seep into the shared dreamspace.
As a bonus action on your turn, you can summon a Nightmare Construct (CR equal to half the target's level or lower) within 10 feet of the target.

entries: A creature frightened by this spell can make another Wisdom saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 7th level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 6th.

spell.name: Trace Sensor

time.condition: which you take upon spotting a sensor used for divination

m.text: an adorned mirror worth at least 100 gp

entries: This spell can be cast when you can see a sensor created by magic, such as through the {@spell arcane eye} spell.

The creature that cast the original spell must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, you gain a brief look at the caster and learn their approximate location (for example, "the city of Teremisis" rather than a precise address).
The caster is not aware that you cast this spell.
This spell automatically fails only if the caster is protected by the {@spell mind blank} or {@spell nondetection} spells.

spell.name: Pursuit

time.condition: which you take in response to a creature you can see using an ability to teleport

entries: When a creature you can see teleports, you can use this spell to follow in pursuit.
When you cast this spell, you teleport to the same general location as the creature, keeping the same distance and orientation you had relative to that creature before they teleported.
If the creature is using the {@spell teleport} spell and is subject to a mishap, you are not affected by that occurrence.

spell.name: Flex
entries: Flex is the pinnacle of spell manipulation achieved by mortals. By rethreading the weaves of magic themselves, you are able to alter spells to produce unparalleled effects.

When you cast this spell, choose a spell of 8th level or lower from any spell list. You cast the chosen spell as part of this spell without expending a spell slot for it and without needing to meet any requirements to cast that spell, including costly components.
The spell created by Flex cannot be countered or dispelled except by a 9th-level spell.
When you cast the chosen spell, select one of the following modifications:

items: {@b Mass:} If the chosen spell targets only one creature, it instead targets up to ten creatures of your choice that are within the spell's range.
{@b Assured:} If the chosen spell requires you to make an attack roll, the attack is automatically a critical hit.
{@b Elevated:} If the chosen spell requires a creature to make a saving throw, creatures affected by the spell automatically fail the first saving throw against it.
{@b Perpetuated:} If the chosen spell has a duration of 1 minute or longer, its duration becomes 48 hours or double its normal duration, whichever is longer.
{@b Expanded:} If the chosen spell creates an area of effect that is at least 10 feet in size, each dimension of that area is tripled.
{@b Unhindered:} The chosen spell ignores all forms of cover.
{@b Adaptive:} If the chosen spell deals damage, you choose the damage type when you cast it. That damage ignores resistance and immunity.
{@b Far:} The spell's range becomes the distance you can see.
{@b Shielding:} Immediately after casting the chosen spell, you gain the benefits of the {@spell shield} spell for as long as the spell is in effect.

{@b Reshaped:} You may alter the spell in a way not described above. Describe the desired alteration to the DM as precisely as possible. The DM determines whether the alteration is possible and its final effect. This alteration should modify the spell rather than fundamentally change its nature.

spell.name: Tasukeru's Shield of Dawn

m.text: a radiant sunlight crystal worth 800 gp

entries: You invoke the ancient oath of Tasukeru, Dawn Dragon and Warden of the First Light, manifesting a radiant aegis that stands against annihilation itself.
A dome of golden dawnlight forms in a 60-foot-radius sphere centered on you. The sphere moves with you, remaining centered on you for the duration.
Creatures you choose within the sphere gain the following benefits:

items: {@b Dawn's Protection.} The creature has resistance to all damage.

{@b Unbroken Vigil.} The creature is immune to the {@condition charmed|XPHB}, {@condition frightened|XPHB}, and {@condition stunned|TGTT} conditions, and it cannot gain levels of {@condition exhaustion|TGTT}.

{@b Steadfast Life.} When the creature would be reduced to 0 hit points, it instead drops to 1 hit point. Once a creature benefits from this feature, it cannot do so again for the duration of the spell.
{@b Sanctified Ward.} A necromancy spell cast from outside the sphere fails if it targets only creatures or areas inside the sphere.

entries: In addition, while a creature you chose is inside the sphere, the following effects on it are suppressed: effects that reduce its hit point maximum, effects that prevent it from regaining hit points, and effects that impose exhaustion.

Creatures of your choice that you consider hostile that enter the sphere for the first time on a turn or start their turn there must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 4d10} radiant damage and is {@condition blinded|XPHB} until the start of its next turn. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage and is not blinded.

spell.name: Kurai Shi's Blackening Death

m.text: a scale from a dead dragon worth 400 gp, blackened to ash

entries: You invoke the final doctrine of Kurai Shi, End Dragon of the Last Silence, calling forth a spreading annihilation that devours vitality, memory, and form.

Choose a point you can see within range. A 30-foot-radius sphere of crawling black entropy appears at that point for the duration. The sphere spreads around corners. Nonmagical light within the area is extinguished.
The sphere affects creatures, objects, and terrain within it:

items: {@b Creatures.} When a creature enters the sphere for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, it must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 8d10} necrotic damage and gains one level of {@condition exhaustion|TGTT}. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage and gains no exhaustion. If a creature fails the saving throw by 5 or more, it is withered until the spell ends. While withered, the creature's hit point maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the necrotic damage it took from this spell. This reduction lasts until the spell ends. If a creature dies while inside the sphere, its body is reduced to ash, as if affected by the {@spell disintegrate} spell.

{@b Objects.} Nonmagical objects in the sphere that are not being worn or carried rapidly decay and crumble to ash at the end of your next turn. Magical objects within the sphere become dulled while they remain inside it. Any bonuses they grant to attack rolls, damage rolls, or Armor Class are suppressed while the object remains in the sphere (to a minimum of +0).
{@b Terrain.} The ground within the sphere becomes lifeless and brittle. The area is {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} for creatures other than undead.

spell.name: Hakai's Devastation

components.m: a small chip of mica

entries: You unleash a focused burst of catastrophic resonance, choosing one creature or object you can see within range.

If the target is a creature, it must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 4d8} thunder damage, or half as much damage on a successful one. Either way, the creature is {@condition deafened|XPHB} until the start of your next turn.
If the target is a nonmagical object, it takes {@damage 5d8} thunder damage. If the object is made of crystal, glass, ceramic, or porcelain, it instead takes maximum damage from this spell.

You can target an object that is being worn or carried by a creature. If you do, the creature holding the object makes a Dexterity saving throw on behalf of the object. On a success, the spell has no effect on that object.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the damage increases by {@damage 1d8} for each slot level above 2nd.

spell.name: Fearheart's Burning Blood

components.m: a pinch of saltpeter and a drop of exposed blood

entries: You ignite the exposed blood of a wounded creature, transmuting it into searing, necrotic flames.

Choose one creature you can see within range that is bloodied. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature's blood combusts, sheds dim red light in a 10-foot radius, and it takes {@damage 4d6} fire damage at the start of each of its turns.
At the end of each of its turns, the target can repeat the saving throw, ending the spell on a success.

A creature without blood or similar vital fluids, such as most constructs, elementals, and undead, is unaffected by this spell. If the target has resistance or immunity to fire damage, the spell automatically fails.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the damage increases by {@damage 1d6} for each slot level above 4th.

spell.name: Annabel's Supervised Spacetime Fracture

components.m: A slipping rope

entries: You tear a minute rift in the fabric of spacetime at a point you can see within range. The rift is a transparent, shifting orb 5 feet in diameter. The area within 10 feet of the orb is {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}, as space warps and stretches around the fracture.
Place the orb within range. On subsequent turns, you can use your bonus action to teleport it to another space within range.

When the orb enters a creature's space, or a creature enters the orb's space, the creature immediately disappears, as it is sent into the near future. It must make a Charisma saving throw (which it can choose to fail).

On a successful save, the creature reappears immediately. On a failed save, the creature reappears 1 round later. During that time, the creature is considered {@condition incapacitated|TGTT}, as it momentarily ceases to exist. Ongoing effects affecting the creature that do not require concentration extend their duration by 1 round. If the creature failed its saving throw during its own turn, it continues its turn once it reappears.

When the creature reappears, it appears in a random unoccupied space within 10 feet of its original location. If no unoccupied space is available within that range, it appears in one of the nearest unoccupied spaces, chosen at random if there is more than one.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: Casting the spell with a spell slot of 5th or 6th level sends creatures 1 minute into the future on a failed save, instead of 1 round. With a 7th or 8th level slot, they are sent 1 hour into the future. With a 9th level slot, they are sent 1 day into the future.
{@i Choose A or B:} (A) {@item Greataxe|XPHB}, 4 {@item Handaxe|XPHB|Handaxes}, {@item Explorer's Pack|XPHB}, and 15 GP; or (B) 75 GP

(a) a {@item lute|phb} or (b) any other musical instrument

weapons: Martial weapons that have the {@filter Light|items|type=martial weapon|property=light} property
tools: Choose one type of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} or {@item Musical Instrument|XPHB}

entries: {@i Choose A or B:} (A) {@item Spear|XPHB}, 5 {@item Dagger|XPHB|Daggers}, {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} or {@item Musical Instrument|XPHB} chosen for the tool proficiency above, {@item Explorer's Pack|XPHB}, and 11 GP; or (B) 50 GP

{@i Choose A or B:} (A) {@item Chain Mail|XPHB}, {@item Shield|XPHB}, {@item Longsword|XPHB}, 6 {@item Javelin|XPHB|Javelins}, {@item Holy Symbol|XPHB}, {@item Priest's Pack|XPHB}, and 9 GP; or (B) 150 GP

{@i Choose A or B:} (A) {@item Studded Leather Armor|XPHB}, {@item Scimitar|XPHB}, {@item Shortsword|XPHB}, {@item Longbow|XPHB}, {@item Arrows (20)|XPHB|20 Arrows}, {@item Quiver|XPHB}, {@item Druidic Focus|XPHB} ({@item sprig of mistletoe|XPHB}), {@item Explorer's Pack|XPHB}, and 7 GP; or (B) 150 GP

weapons: Martial weapons that have the {@filter Finesse or Light|items|type=martial weapon|property=finesse;light} property

entries: {@i Choose A or B:} (A) {@item Leather Armor|XPHB}, 2 {@item Dagger|XPHB|Daggers}, {@item Shortsword|XPHB}, {@item Shortbow|XPHB}, {@item Arrows (20)|XPHB|20 Arrows}, {@item Quiver|XPHB}, {@item Thieves' Tools|XPHB}, {@item Burglar's Pack|XPHB}, and 8 GP; or (B) 100 GP

default: (a) a {@item light crossbow|phb} and {@item crossbow bolts (20)|phb|20 bolts} or (b) any {@filter simple weapon|items|source=phb|category=basic|type=simple weapon}
(a) a {@item component pouch|phb} or (b) an {@item arcane focus|phb}
(a) a {@item dungeoneer's pack|phb} or (b) an {@item explorer's pack|phb}

entries: {@i Choose A or B:} (A) {@item Leather Armor|XPHB}, {@item Sickle|XPHB}, 2 {@item Dagger|XPHB|Daggers}, {@item Arcane Focus|XPHB} ({@item orb|XPHB}), {@item Book|XPHB} (occult lore), {@item Scholar's Pack|XPHB}, and 15 GP; or (B) 100 GP

{@i Choose A or B:} (A) 2 {@item Dagger|XPHB|Daggers}, an {@item Arcane Focus|XPHB} ({@item Quarterstaff|XPHB}), a {@item Robe|XPHB}, a {@item Spellbook|XPHB}, and a {@item Scholar's Pack|XPHB}; or (B) 55 GP
Character level 4 or higher
Must be able to sleep and dream
Possess at least two abilities from the {@classFeature Dreamwalker Abilities|Dreamwalker|TGTT|1} list, one of which must be {@optfeature Dreamwalk|TGTT}

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain a specialty of your choice at 1st level. You gain another specialty at 5th, 9th, 13th, and 17th level.

classFeature.name: Amphibious Combatant
entries: You gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed. You can hold your breath for a number of minutes equal to 2 + your Constitution modifier (minimum 1 minute). You have advantage on checks and saving throws to avoid drowning.

classFeature.name: Battle Hardened
entries: When you are reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, you can make a {@dc 10} Constitution Saving Throw. On a success, you are reduced to 1 hit point instead. You can't use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Combat Medic
entries: You gain proficiency with healer's kits. When you use a healer's kit to stabilize a dying creature, that creature also regains 1 hit point. When you use your Second Wind feature, you can choose one creature within 5 feet of you that isn't you. That creature regains hit points equal to half the amount you regain.

classFeature.name: Stamina Enthusiast
entries: You gain an additional 2 stamina points. When you use your Second Wind feature, you can choose to regain a number of stamina points equal to your proficiency bonus instead of hit points.

classFeature.name: Fight Club
entries: You gain proficiency with improvised weapons. You can use a bonus action to identify a creature's primary attack form. If the creature is a martial weapon user, you have advantage on your next attack roll against the creature.

classFeature.name: Shield Specialist
entries: When you take the {@action Dodge} action while wielding a shield, you can use a bonus action to make one attack with your shield as an improvised weapon that deals {@damage 1d4} bludgeoning damage. You are proficient with this attack and add your Strength modifier to its damage.

classFeature.name: Stronghold Builder
entries: You have advantage on checks made to secure a structure or fortify a position. When you help allies build defenses, the time required is reduced by half. Once per long rest, you can create a simple but effective alarm system or trap using available materials.

classFeature.name: Sweeping Attacker
entries: When you deal damage to a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can use a bonus action to attempt to damage another creature with the same attack. The second creature must be within your reach and adjacent to the original target. If the original attack roll would hit the second creature, it takes damage equal to half the damage done to the original target. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Strength modifier (minimum 1), regaining all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Burst of Strength
entries: When you make a Strength check, you can spend 1 stamina point to add your proficiency bonus to the roll. You can use this feature even if you are already proficient in the check.

classFeature.name: Campaigner
entries: Your carrying capacity is doubled, as is the weight you can push, drag, or lift. Additionally, the number of hours you can travel before making a forced march saving throw is increased by your proficiency bonus.

classFeature.name: Clearsight Sentinel
entries: You gain darkvision with a range of 60 feet. You don't have disadvantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks made in lightly obscured areas.

classFeature.name: Extreme Leap
entries: When you take the Dash action and make a long jump after moving at least 20 feet, your jump distance is doubled for the turn. You can spend 1 stamina point to triple the distance instead.

classFeature.name: Mountaineer
entries: You gain a climbing speed equal to your walking speed. When you fail a Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check to climb, you can spend 1 stamina point to reroll the die and must use the new roll. Moving through nonmagical difficult terrain costs you no extra movement.

classFeature.name: Nightwatch
entries: During a long rest, the number of hours you can spend in light activity is increased by your proficiency bonus. One hour of that time can be spent on strenuous activity, like fighting or keeping watch, without interrupting the rest.

classFeature.name: Stable Footing
entries: You have advantage on saving throws and ability checks to avoid being knocked prone. When you take fall damage, you always land on your feet, and you reduce the fall damage you take by an amount equal to your proficiency bonus × 10.

classFeature.name: Weather Beaten
entries: You have advantage on saving throws made to resist the effects of extreme heat or cold, as well as adverse effects from wind or precipitation.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Fighter|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Fighter|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Fighter|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Fighter|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Battle Tactics
entries: You learn how to better use tactics during combat.

You learn two {@filter Battle Tactics|optionalfeatures|feature type=BT} of your choice. Many tactical moves enable you to gain benefits from certain actions or positions, or perform actions that carry additional effects.
You learn one additional tactical move of your choice at 7th, 10th, and 15th level. Each time you learn a new tactical move, you can also replace one move you know with a different one.

classFeature.name: Battle Tactics Options
entries: The following is a list of tactical moves you can choose from. You can learn two of these moves at 3rd level, and one additional move at 7th, 10th, and 15th level.

classFeature.name: Bardic Inspiration
entries: You can inspire others through stirring words or music. To do so, you use a bonus action on your turn to choose one creature other than yourself within 60 feet of you who can hear you. That creature gains one Bardic Inspiration die, a {@dice d6}.

Once within the next 10 minutes, the creature can roll the die and add the number rolled to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw it makes. The creature can wait until after it rolls the {@dice d20} before deciding to use the Bardic Inspiration die, but must decide before the DM says whether the roll succeeds or fails. Once the Bardic Inspiration die is rolled, it is lost. A creature can have only one Bardic Inspiration die at a time.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (a minimum of once). You regain any expended uses when you finish a long rest.
Your Bardic Inspiration die changes when you reach certain levels in this class. The die becomes a {@dice d8} at 5th level, a {@dice d10} at 10th level, and a {@dice d12} at 15th level.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain a specialty of your choice at 2nd level. You gain another specialty at 4th, 6th, 8th, 10th, 12th, 14th, 16th, 18th, and 20th level.

classFeature.name: Adept Speed
entries: Your speed increases by 10 feet while you aren't wearing armor or wielding a shield. This feature can be chosen multiple times, and its effects stack.

classFeature.name: Marathon Runner
entries: When you travel at a fast pace, you don't take the -5 penalty to your passive {@skill Perception} score. When you make a Constitution Saving Throw for a forced march, you can add a roll of your Martial Arts die to the result.

classFeature.name: Nimble Athlete
entries: You can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier for Athletics checks.

classFeature.name: Power Tumble
entries: You can use your Strength modifier instead of your Dexterity modifier for Acrobatics checks.

classFeature.name: Religious Training
entries: You are proficient in the Religion skill. When touching a holy object, you can spend 2 stamina points to ask the DM one question about it that can be answered with a "yes" or "no".

classFeature.name: Wilderness Training
entries: You are proficient in the Survival skill. For the purposes of your monk class features, you can consider beasts to be martial artists.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Monk|TGTT|2}.

classFeature.name: Gale Walk
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 4th level}
You have advantage on Athletics checks made to jump. As a bonus action, you can spend 1 stamina point to triple your jump distance for the turn.

classFeature.name: Wall Walk
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 4th level}

You can move up, down, and across vertical surfaces on your turn without falling during the move. As a bonus action, you can spend 1 stamina point to cast the spider climb spell on yourself, with a duration that lasts until the start of your next turn.

classFeature.name: Warrior's Awareness
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 4th level}
When you make a Survival check to avoid getting lost, you ignore penalties for traveling at a fast pace and can add a roll of your Martial Arts die to the check.

classFeature.name: Water Walk
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 4th level}

You can move across liquid surfaces on your turn without falling during the move. As a bonus action, you can spend 1 stamina point to cast the water walk spell on yourself, with a duration that lasts until the start of your next turn.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Monk|TGTT|2}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Monk|TGTT|2}.

classFeature.name: Agile Acrobat
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 8th level}
You gain proficiency in the Acrobatics skill if you don't already have it. In addition, your Dexterity score increases by 2, up to a maximum of 20.

classFeature.name: Perfect Flow
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 8th level}
When you roll initiative, you gain 1 Focus Point, up to your normal maximum.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Monk|TGTT|2}.

classFeature.name: Unhindered Flurry
entries: When you reach 8th level, your ability to hit without stop becomes a second nature to you. Your {@classFeature Flurry of Blows|Monk|XPHB||2} without expending any Focus Points.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Monk|TGTT|2}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Monk|TGTT|2}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Monk|TGTT|2}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Monk|TGTT|2}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Monk|TGTT|2}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain a specialty of your choice at 3rd level. You gain another specialty at 5th, 7th, 9th, 11th, 14th, and 19th level.

classFeature.name: Bestowed Understanding
entries: You can attempt to understand a language you don't know by making a {@dc 20} Linguistics check. You have disadvantage if the language is ancient or forgotten. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier, regaining all uses after a long rest.

classFeature.name: Divine Health
entries: You are immune to disease and have advantage on saving throws against being poisoned.

classFeature.name: Divine Vision
entries: You gain darkvision with a range of 60 feet. You always have the see invisibility spell prepared, and you can cast it once per long rest without expending a spell slot.

classFeature.name: Do Without
entries: You can go a number of days equal to your Charisma modifier without food and water before suffering any adverse effects.

classFeature.name: Exemplary
entries: You gain a bonus to Acrobatics and Athletics checks made to climb, jump, run, and swim. The bonus equals your proficiency bonus. When your group makes a group {@skill Acrobatics} or {@skill Athletics} check, you can choose one ally to use your roll's result instead of their own.

classFeature.name: Glorious Purpose
entries: When you undertake a quest that fulfills your oath, the DM chooses a skill crucial to the quest. You gain proficiency in that skill until the quest is complete. If you are already proficient, you gain expertise instead.

classFeature.name: Heraldic Order
entries: When you pay for minor supplies, lodging, or a meal, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 4 or higher, the merchant is impressed by your order and provides the service for free.

classFeature.name: Miraculous Discovery
entries: You gain a bonus to Investigation checks equal to your proficiency bonus, and your passive {@skill Investigation} score increases by 3. You can use your Charisma modifier instead of Intelligence for these checks.

classFeature.name: Naturalist
entries: Choose one terrain type from arctic, coast, desert, forest, grassland, mountain, subterranean, or swamp. You gain a bonus equal to your proficiency bonus on Nature and Survival checks made in that terrain.

classFeature.name: Prophetic Protection
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend a spell slot to gain temporary hit points equal to 5 × the spell slot's level. These temporary hit points last for 1 hour.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Paladin|TGTT|3}.

classFeature.name: Holy Avenger
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 5th level}
Once per turn when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can add {@damage 1d8} radiant damage to the damage roll. This damage increases to {@damage 2d8} at 11th level.

classFeature.name: Sacred Protection
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 5th level}

You can use your Divine Smite feature without expending a spell slot. When you do so, it deals radiant damage equal to your proficiency bonus. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Paladin|TGTT|3}.

classFeature.name: Pious Soul
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 7th level}
You have advantage on saving throws against being frightened.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Paladin|TGTT|3}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Paladin|TGTT|3}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Paladin|TGTT|3}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Paladin|TGTT|3}.

classFeature.name: Supreme Healing
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 19th level}

When you would normally roll one or more dice to restore hit points with a spell, you instead use the highest number possible for each die. For example, instead of restoring 2d6 hit points to a creature, you restore 12.

classFeature.name: Font of Magic
entries: You can tap into the wellspring of magic within yourself. This wellspring is represented by Sorcery Points, which allow you to create a variety of magical effects.

You have 2 Sorcery Points, and you gain more as you reach higher levels, as shown in the Sorcery Points column of the Sorcerer Features table. You can't have more Sorcery Points than the number shown in the table for your level. You regain all expended Sorcery Points when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
You can use your Sorcery Points to fuel the options below, along with other features, such as Metamagic, that use those points.

entries.name: Converting Spell Slots to Sorcery Points
entries: You can expend a spell slot to gain a number of Sorcery Points equal to the slot's level (no action required).

entries.name: Creating Spell Slots
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can transform unexpended Sorcery Points into one spell slot. The Creating Spell Slots table shows the cost of creating a spell slot of a given level, and it lists the minimum Sorcerer level you must be to create a slot. You can create a spell slot no higher than level 5.
Any spell slot you create with this feature vanishes when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: You have learned to untangle and reshape the fabric of reality in harmony with your wishes and music.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: You know two cantrips of your choice from the bard spell list. You learn additional bard cantrips of your choice at higher levels, as shown in the Bard class table.
Whenever you gain a Bard level, you can replace one of your cantrips with another cantrip of your choice from the bard spell list.

entries.name: Spell Slots
entries: The Bard class table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your spells of 1st level and higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Prepared Spells of 1st Level and Higher
entries: You prepare the list of spells that are available for you to cast. To start, choose four 1st-level spells from the bard spell list.

The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Bard levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Bard class table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots.
Whenever you gain a Bard level, you can replace one spell on your list with another Bard spell for which you have spell slots.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your bard spells. You use your Charisma whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability, for setting the saving throw DC, and for making a spell attack roll.

classFeature.name: Jack of All Trades
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you can add half your proficiency bonus, rounded down, to any ability check you make that doesn't already include your proficiency bonus.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain a specialty of your choice. You gain another specialty at 6th, 9th, 13th, 16th, and 20th level.

classFeature.name: Bewitching Companion
entries: Choose one skill you are proficient in. You gain a bonus to checks with that skill equal to your proficiency bonus. You gain the same bonus on checks made to influence your traveling companions.

classFeature.name: Brutish Confrontation
entries: Choose one skill you are proficient in. You gain a bonus to checks with that skill equal to your proficiency bonus. You also have advantage on ability checks made to prepare an ambush.

classFeature.name: Improvised Engineering
entries: You gain proficiency in the Engineering skill. If you are already proficient, you gain a bonus to checks with that skill equal to your proficiency bonus instead. You have advantage on Engineering checks to build temporary structures or reinforce existing ones.

classFeature.name: Marching Song
entries: Allies within 30 feet of you travel 1 mile per hour faster than normal. Your group cannot use a stealthy travel pace while you do this.

classFeature.name: Profitable
entries: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Performance} skill. If you are already proficient, you gain expertise instead. When you make a Performance check to earn money, you earn twice the normal amount.

classFeature.name: Resonance
entries: Choose one skill you are proficient in. You gain a bonus to checks with that skill equal to your proficiency bonus. When you or a companion who can hear you casts a ritual spell, its duration and number of targets are doubled.

classFeature.name: Sly Confidant
entries: Choose one skill you are proficient in. You gain a bonus to checks with that skill equal to your proficiency bonus. When you succeed on a contested Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma check against a creature, you have advantage on the next such check you make against it.

classFeature.name: Song of Rest
entries: When a creature that can hear you perform spends Hit Dice at the end of a short rest, it regains an additional {@dice 1d8} hit points.

classFeature.name: Showoff
entries: Choose one skill you are proficient in. You gain a bonus to checks with that skill equal to your proficiency bonus. When you succeed on a physical Dexterity (Acrobatics) or Strength (Athletics) check, a number of allies equal to your proficiency bonus gain advantage on the same check to overcome the same obstacle.

classFeature.name: Townie
entries: Choose one skill you are proficient in. You gain a bonus to checks with that skill equal to your proficiency bonus. While you have a positive reputation in a settlement, basic goods and spell components cost one-third less for you.

classFeature.name: Widely Known
entries: You don't have disadvantage on Prestige checks made outside your normal area of influence.

classFeature.name: Bard College
entries: At 3rd level, you delve into the advanced techniques of a bard college of your choice.

classFeature.name: Expertise
entries: Choose two of your skill proficiencies. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of the chosen proficiencies.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Bardic Inspiration (d8)
entries: At 5th level, your Bardic Inspiration die becomes a {@dice d8}.

classFeature.name: Bard College feature
entries: At 6th level, you gain a feature from your Bard College.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Bard|TGTT|2}.

classFeature.name: Font of Inspiration
entries: Beginning at 7th level, you regain one expended use of Bardic Inspiration when you finish a short rest, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Expertise
entries: Choose two more of your skill proficiencies. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of the chosen proficiencies.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Bard|TGTT|2}.

classFeature.name: Bardic Inspiration (d10)
entries: At 10th level, your Bardic Inspiration die becomes a {@dice d10}.

classFeature.name: Magical Secrets
entries: You've learned secrets from various magical traditions. Whenever you learn a new spell as you level up, you can choose the new spell from the Bard, Cleric, Druid, or Wizard spell lists. The chosen spell counts as a bard spell for you.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Bard|TGTT|2}.

classFeature.name: Bard College feature
entries: At 14th level, you gain a feature from your Bard College.

classFeature.name: Bardic Inspiration (d12)
entries: At 15th level, your Bardic Inspiration die becomes a {@dice d12}.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Bard|TGTT|2}.

classFeature.name: Superior Inspiration
entries: When you roll initiative, you regain expended uses of Bardic Inspiration until you have two if you have fewer than that.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Words of Creation
entries: You have mastered two of the Words of Creation: the words of life and death. You therefore always have the {@spell Power Word Heal} and {@spell Power Word Kill} spells prepared. When you cast either spell, you can target a second creature with it if that creature is within 10 feet of the first target.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Bard|TGTT|2}.

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: You have learned to cast spells through prayer and meditation. See chapter 10 of the Player's Handbook for the general rules of spellcasting.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: You know three cantrips of your choice from the cleric spell list. You learn additional cleric cantrips of your choice at higher levels, as shown in the Cantrips Known column of the Cleric table.
Whenever you gain a cleric level, you can replace one of your cantrips with another cantrip of your choice from the cleric spell list.

entries.name: Preparing and Casting Spells
entries: The Cleric table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your cleric spells of 1st level and higher. To cast one of these spells, you must expend a slot of the spell's level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a long rest.

You prepare the list of cleric spells that are available for you to cast, choosing from the cleric spell list. The number of spells you can prepare is shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Cleric table. The spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

entries.name: Additional Prepared Spells
entries: The spells {@spell Ceremony} and {@spell Thaumaturgy} are always prepared for you, and they don't count against the number of spells you can prepare each day.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for your cleric spells. The power of your spells comes from your devotion to your deity. You use your Wisdom whenever a cleric spell refers to your spellcasting ability. In addition, you use your Wisdom modifier when setting the saving throw DC for a cleric spell you cast and when making an attack roll with one.

classFeature.name: Divine Order
entries: You have dedicated yourself to one of the following sacred roles of your choice.

classFeature.name: Protector
entries: Trained for battle, you gain proficiency with martial weapons and heavy armor.

classFeature.name: Thaumaturge
entries: You know one extra cantrip from the Cleric spell list. In addition, your mystical connection to the divine gives you a bonus to your Intelligence ({@skill Arcana}) or Intelligence ({@skill Religion}) checks (your choice). The bonus equals your Wisdom modifier (minimum of +1).

classFeature.name: Channel Divinity
entries: You can channel divine energy directly from the Outer Planes to fuel magical effects. You start with two such effects: Divine Spark and Turn Undead, each of which is described below. Each time you use this class's Channel Divinity, choose which Channel Divinity effect from this class to create. You gain additional effect options from your subclass.

You can use this class's Channel Divinity twice. You regain one of its expended uses when you finish a short rest, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest. You gain additional uses when you reach certain Cleric levels, as shown in the Channel Divinity Uses column of the Cleric table.
If a Channel Divinity effect requires a saving throw, the DC equals your cleric spell save DC.

classFeature.name: Divine Spark
entries: As an action, you point your holy symbol at another creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself and focus divine energy at it. Roll {@scaledice 1d8|2-17|1d8} and add your Wisdom modifier. You either restore hit points to the creature equal to that total or force the creature to make a Constitution Saving Throw. On a failed save, the creature takes necrotic or radiant damage (your choice) equal to that total. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage.
The damage or healing roll increases by {@scaledice 1d8|7,13,18|1d8} when you reach 7th level ({@scaledice 1d8|7|1d8}), 13th level ({@scaledice 1d8|13|2d8}), and 18th level ({@scaledice 1d8|18|3d8}).

classFeature.name: Turn Undead
entries: As an action, you present your holy symbol and censure Undead creatures. Each Undead of your choice within 30 feet of you must make a Wisdom saving throw. If the creature fails its save, it is {@condition Frightened} and {@condition Incapacitated|tgtt} for 1 minute. For that duration, it tries to move as far from you as it can on its turns. This effect ends early on the creature if it takes any damage, if you have the {@condition Incapacitated|tgtt} condition, or if you die.

classFeature.name: Principles of Devotion
entries: At 2nd level, after much contemplation and communion with your greater entity, you pledge to uphold certain standards of behavior beyond what is necessary for adventure. In return, your greater entity grants you one of the following related boons. When you fail to uphold this pledge, you lose any benefits from it until 24 hours after you finish a long rest and rededicate yourself.

classFeature.name: Chaste
entries: In exchange for forgoing pleasures of the heart and flesh, you are hardened to the persuasions of others. Add your Wisdom modifier to any saving throws made to resist being charmed. You also gain one skill specialty chosen from Insight, Persuasion, or Religion.

classFeature.name: Destitute
entries: In exchange for forgoing material luxury, vendors who are not explicitly opposed to your greater entity are compelled to give you heavy discounts of up to 20%, and innkeeps offer free lodging.

classFeature.name: Devotional Integrity
entries: You adhere to a strict code of morality and ethics. When an action you take is honest and non-violent, until the end of your turn you are immune to the {@condition dazed|tgtt} condition and you do not suffer disadvantage or penalties on Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma checks made as part of that action.

classFeature.name: Image of the Divine
entries: You exhibit a uniform, makeup, tattoo, or body modification indicating your higher entity or religious order. Creatures have advantage when discerning your religious identity. You gain advantage on saving throws against fear and ability checks made against Intimidation. You also gain one skill specialty chosen from Insight, Intimidation, or Persuasion.

classFeature.name: Merciful
entries: In exchange for forgoing bloodshed—torture, attacking unconscious creatures, and dealing lethal wounds to creatures you reduce to 0 hit points—you are yourself granted mercy. When you have already succeeded on two death saving throws, you have advantage on death saving throws.

classFeature.name: Rule of Ritual
entries: You make formal religious observances and perform ritual timekeeping. You can spend 1 hour during a long rest to make these observances, and if you do you only need 4 hours of sleep instead of the normal 6. You learn the guidance cantrip if you do not already know it, and when you grant expertise dice with a spell of 1st-level or higher, the expertise die you grant increases by one step.

classFeature.name: Secrecy
entries: Your mission from your deity requires you to work undercover, concealing your true self with a secret identity. In exchange for forgoing the pleasure of true connection and friendship, you gain an expertise die on Deception checks.

classFeature.name: Severity
entries: In exchange for forgoing weaknesses such as helping or protecting others (except when it serves you), you add your Wisdom modifier to Perception checks made to find valuable objects, and on opposed checks made against the Persuasion check of another creature.

classFeature.name: Silence
entries: In exchange for speaking only to cast spells, you are able to compel other creatures to action with only a glance. When a creature within 10 feet of you makes a Persuasion check, you may add your Wisdom modifier to the result.

classFeature.name: Cleric Subclass
entries: You gain a Cleric subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialty that grants you features at certain Cleric levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Cleric level or lower.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain a specialty of your choice. You gain another specialty at 7th, 11th, 15th, and 20th level.

classFeature.name: Ancestral Guidance
entries: You always have {@spell speak with dead} prepared. You can cast it once per long rest without a spell slot and can target a grave marker, memorial, statue, painting, or other depiction of the deceased creature you wish to talk to instead of their corpse. If the chosen creature is not dead, or if such a creature never existed in the first place, the spell fails.

classFeature.name: Compassionate Nurse
entries: You gain a bonus to Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) checks equal to your proficiency bonus. You can always use your Wisdom modifier for Medicine checks.

classFeature.name: Faithful Historian
