entries: You gain a bonus to Intelligence ({@skill History}) checks equal to your proficiency bonus. You can always use your Wisdom modifier for History checks.

classFeature.name: Gentle Healer
entries: During a short rest, you can use your healer's kit to allow an ally to roll any Hit Dice they spend twice and take the higher result.

classFeature.name: Graceful Fall
entries: When you fall from a height greater than 30 feet, you are automatically affected by the {@spell feather fall} spell. This does not use your reaction.

classFeature.name: Monastic Austerity
entries: You ignore the first level of exhaustion you would gain each day.

classFeature.name: Numinous Awareness
entries: You automatically know if a creature that appears to be a beast is actually a celestial, fiend, or fey. You have advantage on checks made to deal with or gather information about magical wounds and maladies caused by these creature types.

classFeature.name: Premonition
entries: After each long rest, you receive a premonition. The DM will tell you if you received a helpful piece of information or if you get to pre-roll a {@dice d20}. If you pre-roll a {@dice d20}, you can replace any one roll made by a creature you can see within 30 feet of you with that result before your next long rest.

classFeature.name: Preservation
entries: You can sense the presence of poison or disease in any food, drink, or other consumable. You must have had direct contact with them or their container to do so.

classFeature.name: Righteous Path
entries: Once per day, you can be divinely guided for 1 hour. While guided, you are aware of enemies within 60 feet and know how to avoid them. You can also use an action to sense the presence, but not the location, of traps within 30 feet.

classFeature.name: Soothing Words
entries: You can reduce one ally's exhaustion level by one. You can't use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Supernal Intuition
entries: Choose a creature type: fiend, fey, celestial, elemental, or undead. You are aware when a creature of that type is within 60 feet of you, though not its exact location. You can select this feature multiple times for different creature types. The {@spell Nondetection|TGTT} spell protects against this feature.

classFeature.name: Theologian
entries: You gain a bonus to Intelligence ({@skill Religion}) checks equal to your proficiency bonus. You can always use your Wisdom modifier for Religion checks.

classFeature.name: Voice of Doom
entries: You gain a bonus to Charisma ({@skill Intimidation}) checks equal to your proficiency bonus. You can always use your Wisdom modifier for Intimidation checks.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Sear Undead
entries: Whenever you use Turn Undead, you can roll a number of d8s equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of {@dice 1d8}) and add the rolls together. Each Undead that fails its saving throw against that use of Turn Undead takes Radiant damage equal to the roll's total. This damage doesn't end the turn effect.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Divine Domain.

classFeature.name: Blessed Strikes
entries: Divine power infuses you in battle. You gain one of the following options of your choice.

classFeature.name: Divine Strike (Blessed Strikes)
entries: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the target to take an extra {@damage 1d8} necrotic or radiant damage (your choice).

classFeature.name: Potent Spellcasting (Blessed Strikes)
entries: You add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Cleric|TGTT|3}.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Divine Intervention
entries: Beginning at 10th level, you can call on your deity to intervene on your behalf when your need is great.

Imploring your deity's aid requires you to use your action. Describe the assistance you seek, and roll percentile dice. If you roll a number equal to or lower than your cleric level, your deity intervenes. The DM chooses the nature of the intervention; the effect of any cleric spell or cleric domain spell would be appropriate. If your deity intervenes, you can't use this feature again for 7 days. Otherwise, you can use it again after you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Cleric|TGTT|3}.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Improved Blessed Strikes
entries: The option you chose for Blessed Strikes grows more powerful.

entries.name: Divine Strike
entries: The extra damage of your Divine Strike increases to {@damage 2d8}.

entries.name: Potent Spellcasting
entries: When you cast a cleric cantrip and deal damage to a creature with it, you can give vitality to yourself or another creature within 60 feet of yourself, granting a number of temporary hit points equal to twice your Wisdom modifier.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Cleric|TGTT|3}.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Divine Domain.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Divine Intervention Improvement
entries: At 20th level, your call for intervention succeeds automatically, no roll required.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Cleric|TGTT|3}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain a specialty of your choice from the following list.

classFeature.name: Agile Sprinter
entries: You gain a bonus to Strength ({@skill Athletics}) and Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) checks made to climb, run, and swing. The bonus equals your proficiency bonus.

classFeature.name: Flock Step
entries: You gain a bonus to Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to hide within a group of five or more creatures. The bonus equals your proficiency bonus.

classFeature.name: Mark of the Wilderness
entries: You gain a bonus to Charisma ({@skill Intimidation}) checks equal to your proficiency bonus. You can use your Strength modifier instead of Charisma for Charisma ({@skill Intimidation}) and Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) checks.

classFeature.name: Natural Tracker
entries: After observing a creature for at least 1 minute, you gain a bonus to any ability checks made to track that specific creature. The bonus equals your proficiency bonus.

classFeature.name: Path of Blustery Autumns
entries: Your travel pace is increased by 10 feet per hour. Additionally, you can ignore nonmagical difficult terrain while not in combat.

classFeature.name: Path of Lean Winters
entries: You have advantage on saving throws made to resist the effects of cold weather. You can go a number of days equal to 3 + your Constitution modifier without food before suffering exhaustion.

classFeature.name: Path of Scorching Summers
entries: You have advantage on saving throws made to resist the effects of hot weather and thirst.

classFeature.name: Sharpened Senses
entries: You gain a bonus to Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}), Wisdom ({@skill Perception}), or Wisdom ({@skill Survival}) checks made to follow a creature's trail. The bonus equals your proficiency bonus.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Barbarian|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Barbarian|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Lead the Pack
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 6th level}

You gain a bonus to Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) checks made to climb, jump, run, and swim. The bonus equals your proficiency bonus. When your group makes a group Athletics or Acrobatics check, you can choose one ally to use your roll's result instead of their own.

classFeature.name: Path of Drowning Springs
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 6th level}

You gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed and can hold your breath for a number of minutes equal to 1 + twice your Constitution modifier. While swimming, you can use a bonus action and expend one use of Rage to move up to your swim speed in a straight line.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Barbarian|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Barbarian|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Barbarian|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Barbarian|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Barbarian|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Barbarian|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Druidic
entries: You know Druidic, the secret language of druids. While learning this ancient tongue, you also unlocked the magic of communicating with animals; you always have the {@spell Speak with Animals} spell prepared.

You can use Druidic to leave hidden messages. You and others who know Druidic automatically spot such a message. Others spot the message's presence with a successful {@dc 15} Investigation check but can't decipher it without magic.

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: Drawing on the divine essence of nature itself, you can cast spells to shape that essence to your will.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: At 1st level, you know two cantrips of your choice from the druid spell list. You learn additional druid cantrips of your choice at higher levels, as shown in the Cantrips Known column of the Druid table.
Whenever you gain a Druid level, you can replace one of your cantrips with another cantrip of your choice from the druid spell list.

entries.name: Preparing and Casting Spells
entries: The Druid table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your druid spells of 1st level and higher. To cast one of these druid spells, you must expend a slot of the spell's level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a long rest.

You prepare the list of druid spells that are available for you to cast, choosing from the druid spell list. When you do so, choose a number of druid spells equal to your Wisdom modifier + your druid level (minimum of one spell). The spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots.
You can also change your list of prepared spells when you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Primal Order
entries: You have dedicated yourself to one of the following sacred roles of your choice, which grants you benefits at 1st, 7th, and 15th level.

classFeature.name: Magician
entries: You know one extra cantrip from the Druid spell list. In addition, your mystical connection to nature gives you a bonus to your Intelligence (Arcana) or Intelligence (Nature) checks (your choice). The bonus equals your Wisdom modifier (minimum of +1).

classFeature.name: Warden
entries: Trained for battle, you gain proficiency with martial weapons and medium armor.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain two specialties of your choice. You gain another specialty at 5th, 9th, 13th, and 17th level.

classFeature.name: Aerial Surveyor
entries: While at a great height, you can spend 1 minute surveying your surroundings to familiarize yourself with a 5-mile radius. For the next 24 hours, you gain a bonus equal to your proficiency bonus on Wisdom (Perception) and Wisdom (Survival) checks you make while outdoors in that area. You can't use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Aquatic Delver
entries: You gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed. Additionally, you can hold your breath for a number of minutes equal to your Wisdom modifier before suffocating. While fully submerged, you also gain blindsight with a range of 10 feet.

classFeature.name: Cavern Skulker
entries: You can unerringly determine the true source of any sound you hear, ignoring echoes. While in complete darkness and not deafened, you gain blindsight with a range of 15 feet.

classFeature.name: Desert Dweller
entries: You are naturally acclimatized to hot climates. While in a desert or similar arid environment, you can spend 1 hour to find 1 day's worth of Supply (food and water). Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Eldritch Survivor
entries: You can make a Wisdom (Survival) check instead of an Intelligence (Arcana) check when dealing with magical environments, plants, or creatures. When you do so, you gain a bonus to the check equal to your proficiency bonus.

classFeature.name: Herbal Apothecary
entries: You gain a bonus to ability checks you make using an herbalism kit or to locate herbal ingredients. The bonus equals your proficiency bonus. Additionally, whenever you use an herbalism kit to find herbs, you gather twice the normal amount.

classFeature.name: Marshland Guide
entries: Moving through difficult terrain in swamps, bogs, or mud costs you no extra movement. You cannot become lost by nonmagical means while in marshlands. You also ignore any penalties for resting in wet or swampy environments.

classFeature.name: Master Forager
entries: You have advantage on ability checks made to find or harvest edible plants. When you do so, you gather twice the normal amount of Supply.

classFeature.name: Mountain Climber
entries: You gain a climbing speed equal to your walking speed. You gain a bonus to Strength (Athletics) checks made to climb equal to your proficiency bonus. You are also acclimated to high altitudes, including elevations above 20,000 feet. You ignore any penalties for resting on rocky or uneven terrain.

classFeature.name: Tundra Explorer
entries: You are naturally acclimatized to cold climates. You don't have disadvantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks caused by blizzards, and you automatically succeed on Constitution saving throws to maintain concentration during them. You also ignore any penalties for resting without warmth in extreme cold.

classFeature.name: Wild Shape
entries: The power of nature allows you to assume the form of an animal. As a bonus action, you shape-shift into a Beast form that you have learned for this feature. You can use this feature twice. You regain expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

You can stay in a beast shape for a number of hours equal to half your druid level (rounded down). You then revert to your normal form unless you expend another use of this feature. You can revert to your normal form earlier by using a bonus action on your turn. You automatically revert if you fall unconscious, drop to 0 hit points, or die.

entries.name: Known Forms
entries: You know four Beast forms for this feature, chosen from among Beast stat blocks that have a maximum Challenge Rating of 1/4 and that lack a fly speed. Whenever you finish a long rest, you can replace one of your known forms with another eligible form.

When you reach certain Druid levels, your number of known forms and the maximum Challenge Rating for those forms increases, as shown in the Beast Shapes table. Starting at level 8, you can adopt a form that has a fly speed.

entries.name: Rules While Transformed
entries: While in a form, you retain your personality, memories, and ability to speak, and the following rules apply:

items: When you assume a Wild Shape form, you gain a number of temporary hit points equal to your Druid level.

Your game statistics are replaced by the Beast's stat block, but you retain your alignment, personality, Hit Points, Hit Dice, Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores, class features, feats, languages, and proficiencies. You also gain the proficiencies of the creature. If a skill or saving throw modifier in the Beast's stat block is higher than yours, use the one in the stat block.
You can't cast spells, but shape-shifting doesn't break your concentration or otherwise interfere with a spell you've already cast.

Your ability to handle objects is determined by the form's limbs. You choose whether your equipment falls in your space, merges into your new form, or is worn by it. Worn equipment functions as normal, but its practicality is subject to the DM's discretion. Equipment that merges with the form has no effect.

classFeature.name: Wild Companion
entries: You can summon a nature spirit that assumes an animal form to aid you. As a Magic action, you can expend a spell slot or a use of Wild Shape to cast the {@spell Find Familiar} spell without material components.
When you cast the spell in this way, the familiar is a Fey and disappears when you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Druid Circle
entries: At 3rd level, you choose to identify with a circle of druids. Your choice grants you features at 3rd level and again at 6th, 10th, and 14th level.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, and 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Wild Resurgence
entries: Once on each of your turns, if you have no uses of Wild Shape left, you can give yourself one use by expending a spell slot (no action required).
In addition, you can expend one use of Wild Shape (no action required) to give yourself a 1st-level spell slot, but you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Druid|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Druid Circle feature
entries: At 6th level, you gain a feature granted by your Druid Circle.

classFeature.name: Elemental Fury
entries: The might of the elements flows through you, granting you a benefit based on your Primal Order.

entries.name: Magician
entries: You add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any Druid cantrip.

entries.name: Warden
entries: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon, you can cause the target to take an extra {@damage 1d8} cold, fire, lightning, or thunder damage (choose when you hit).

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Druid|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Druid Circle feature
entries: At 10th level, you gain a feature granted by your Druid Circle.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Druid|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Druid Circle feature
entries: At 14th level, you gain a feature granted by your Druid Circle.

classFeature.name: Improved Elemental Fury
entries: The option you chose for Elemental Fury grows more powerful.

entries.name: Magician
entries: When you cast a Druid cantrip with a range of 10 feet or greater, the spell's range increases by 300 feet.

entries.name: Warden
entries: The extra damage of your Primal Strike increases to {@damage 2d8}.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Druid|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Beast Spells
entries: Beginning at 18th level, you can cast many of your druid spells in any shape you assume using Wild Shape. You can perform the somatic and verbal components of a druid spell while in a beast shape, but you aren't able to provide material components.

classFeature.name: Epic Boon
entries: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Archdruid
entries: At 20th level, you can use your Wild Shape an unlimited number of times.

Additionally, you can ignore the verbal and somatic components of your druid spells, as well as any material components that lack a cost and aren't consumed by a spell. You gain this benefit in both your normal shape and your beast shape from Wild Shape.

classFeature.name: Timeless Body
entries: Starting at 20th level, the primal magic that you wield causes you to age more slowly. For every 10 years that pass, your body ages only 1 year.

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: An event in your past, or in the life of a parent or ancestor, left an indelible mark on you, infusing you with arcane magic. This font of magic, whatever its origin, fuels your spells.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: At 1st level, you know four cantrips of your choice from the sorcerer spell list. You learn an additional sorcerer cantrip of your choice at 4th level and another at 10th level.

entries.name: Spells Known of 1st Level and Higher
entries: You know two 1st-level spells of your choice from the sorcerer spell list.
You learn an additional sorcerer spell of your choice at each level except 12th, 14th, 16th, 18th, 19th, and 20th. Each of these spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

Additionally, when you gain a level in this class, you can choose one of the sorcerer spells you know and replace it with another spell from the sorcerer spell list, which also must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

classFeature.name: Sorcerous Origin
entries: Choose a sorcerous origin, which describes the source of your innate magical power. Your choice grants you features when you choose it at 1st level and again at 6th, 14th, and 18th level.

classFeature.name: Metamagic
entries: At 2nd level, you gain the ability to twist your spells to suit your needs. You gain two of Metamagic options of your choice from "{@filter Metamagic Options|optionalfeatures|feature type=MM|source=TGTT}". You gain another one at 3rd, 6th, 10th, 13th, and 17th level.
Each metamagic option is either Active or Passive:

items: Active metamagic options require you spend a defined amount of sorcery points to activate the metamagic upon casting a spell.

Passive metamagic requires you spend an action tuning yourself with your magic. Afterwards, your maximum amount of sorcery points is reduced by the metamagic's defined cost, and the metamagic affects any spell you cast from this point forward. You can spend another action to cancel a passive metamagic effect, regaining your invested sorcery points.

entries: You can use only one Active Metamagic option on a spell when you cast it, unless otherwise noted.

classFeature.name: Metamagic Options
entries: The following metamagic options are available to you when you learn a Metamagic option. The options are presented by type.

classFeature.name: Metamagic
entries: At 3rd level, you learn an additional Metamagic option from "{@filter Metamagic Options|optionalfeatures|feature type=MM|source=TGTT}".

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain a specialty of your choice. You gain another specialty at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 20th level.

classFeature.name: Draw Nourishment
entries: You can spend 3 sorcery points to treat one pound of organic matter (like bark or grass) as 1 day's worth of rations for yourself. This does not neutralize any poison the substance may contain.

classFeature.name: Hot Air
entries: While you have at least 1 sorcery point, your weight is halved, and you have resistance to fall damage.

classFeature.name: Lingering Touch
entries: When you touch a piece of a creature (like hair or a scale), you can instantly attune to it. If you later see the creature the piece belongs to, you immediately know it's a match, and the attunement ends.

classFeature.name: Retrace
entries: By meditating for 1 minute, you can perfectly retrace your steps from the last 4 hours.

classFeature.name: Mage Hunter
entries: When tracking a creature that can cast spells, you can use your Charisma modifier for any ability checks made to track it.

classFeature.name: Magnetic Step
entries: You can move up, down, and across vertical surfaces at half your speed without an ability check. You fall if you end your turn on such a surface unless you have another means of support.

classFeature.name: Strange Traces
entries: You can spend 1 sorcery point to obscure your group's trail for the next hour. Any creature trying to track you does so with disadvantage on its Wisdom (Survival) checks and becomes lost if it fails.

classFeature.name: Ominous Insight
entries: When you fail an Intelligence (Arcana, History, Nature, or Religion) check, you can reroll the check. If you fail the reroll, you have disadvantage on all Intelligence checks until you finish a short rest, and you can't use this feature again during that time.

classFeature.name: Wode Sense
entries: You automatically become aware of illusory terrain or magical effects meant to make you lost within 1 minute of being affected.

classFeature.name: Sorcerous Origin feature
entries: At 6th level, you gain a feature granted by your Sorcerous Origin.

classFeature.name: Metamagic
entries: At 6th level, you learn an additional Metamagic option from "{@filter Metamagic Options|optionalfeatures|feature type=MM|source=TGTT}".

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Sorcerer|TGTT|4}.

classFeature.name: Metamagic
entries: At 10th level, you learn an additional Metamagic option from "{@filter Metamagic Options|optionalfeatures|feature type=MM|source=TGTT}".

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Sorcerer|TGTT|4}.

classFeature.name: Metamagic
entries: At 13th level, you learn an additional Metamagic option from "{@filter Metamagic Options|optionalfeatures|feature type=MM|source=TGTT}".

classFeature.name: Sorcerous Origin feature
entries: At 14th level, you gain a feature granted by your Sorcerous Origin.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Sorcerer|TGTT|4}.

classFeature.name: Metamagic
entries: At 17th level, you learn an additional Metamagic option from "{@filter Metamagic Options|optionalfeatures|feature type=MM|source=TGTT}".

classFeature.name: Sorcerous Origin feature
entries: At 18th level, you gain a feature granted by your Sorcerous Origin.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Sorcerous Restoration
entries: At 20th level, you regain 4 expended sorcery points whenever you finish a short rest.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Sorcerer|TGTT|4}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain a specialty of your choice from the following list.

classFeature.name: Agile Athlete
entries: You gain a bonus to Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks equal to your proficiency bonus. You also gain a climbing speed equal to your walking speed.

classFeature.name: Analysis
entries: You gain a bonus to checks made with a poisoner's kit equal to your proficiency bonus. After 10 minutes of study, you can identify a poison and learn its recipe. You can then spend a day and half the poison's cost to create it.

classFeature.name: Boobytrapper
entries: You gain a bonus to Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand}) checks equal to your proficiency bonus. You can use a Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand}) check to conceal tiny traps like caltrops or a hunting trap; the check's result becomes the DC for a creature to spot the trap.

classFeature.name: Cat's Eyes
entries: You gain a bonus to Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks equal to your proficiency bonus. You also gain darkvision with a range of 30 feet. If you already have darkvision, its range increases by 30 feet.

classFeature.name: Delay Trap
entries: You gain a bonus to checks made with thieves' tools equal to your proficiency bonus. If you would trigger a mechanical trap on your turn, you can use your reaction to immediately end your turn. If you do, the trap's effect is delayed until the start of your next turn.

classFeature.name: Expertise Training
entries: You gain a bonus equal to your proficiency bonus on checks made with one of the following skills or tools: Acrobatics, Athletics, Investigation, Perception, Stealth, or any tool. You can choose this feature multiple times.

classFeature.name: Extra Skill Training
entries: You gain proficiency in one of the following: Acrobatics, Athletics, Investigation, Perception, Stealth, or any tool. You can choose this feature multiple times.

classFeature.name: Hide in the Shadows
entries: You gain a bonus to Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks equal to your proficiency bonus. You can try to hide when you are in dim light.

classFeature.name: Kip Up
entries: You gain a bonus to Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) checks equal to your proficiency bonus. Standing up from prone costs you no movement. When you are knocked prone, you can use your reaction to stand up immediately.

classFeature.name: Locksmith
entries: You gain a bonus to checks made with thieves' tools equal to your proficiency bonus. You can spend 1 minute using thieves' tools to either increase a lock's DC by 10 or create a key for it.

classFeature.name: Loot Runner
entries: You gain a bonus to Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks equal to your proficiency bonus. Your speed increases by 5 feet.

classFeature.name: Observer
entries: You gain a bonus to Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks equal to your proficiency bonus. In addition, your passive Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) score increases by 3.

classFeature.name: Quick Scan
entries: You gain a bonus to Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) checks equal to your proficiency bonus. You can use a bonus action to take the Search action.

classFeature.name: Scout Leader
entries: You gain a bonus to Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks equal to your proficiency bonus. When your group makes a Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) check, you can choose one ally to use your roll's result instead of their own. You can move stealthily at a normal pace while traveling.

classFeature.name: Sense for Secrets
entries: You gain a bonus to Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) checks equal to your proficiency bonus. By spending 1 minute observing an area, you can sense the presence of any nonmagical traps, secret doors, or other unusual features within 30 feet, though not their exact nature or location.

classFeature.name: Tuck and Roll
entries: You gain a bonus to Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) checks equal to your proficiency bonus. When you take falling damage, you can use your reaction to make a Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check and reduce the damage by the result of your roll.

classFeature.name: Unstable Poison
entries: You gain a bonus to checks made with a poisoner's kit equal to your proficiency bonus. During a short or long rest, you can brew one dose of basic poison without cost. This poison loses its potency at the start of your next rest.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Rogue|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Rogue|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Rogue|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Rogue|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Rogue|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Rogue|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Graceful Leap
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 13th level}

You can add a {@dice d10} to your Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) checks. As a bonus action, you can jump up to half your speed horizontally or up to 10 feet vertically. Any opportunity attacks provoked by this jump are made with disadvantage.

classFeature.name: Keen Eye
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 13th level}

You can add a {@dice d10} to your Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks. You don't have disadvantage on Perception checks in lightly obscured areas. If you have darkvision, you see in darkness as if it were bright light.

classFeature.name: Poison Expert
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 13th level}
You can add a {@dice d10} to checks made with a poisoner's kit and on saving throws against poison. You are immune to the effects of one poison of your choice.

classFeature.name: Practiced Dash
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 13th level}
You can add a {@dice d10} to your Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks. When you take the Dash action, you ignore difficult terrain and don't fall on slippery surfaces.

classFeature.name: Sense Aura
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 13th level}

You can add a {@dice d10} to your Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) checks. You can use an Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check to find magical traps. You can also spend 10 minutes to sense if an object or creature within sight is magical.

classFeature.name: Shadow Skulk
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 13th level}
You can add a {@dice d10} to your Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks. You also gain a passive Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) score of 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your proficiency bonus.

classFeature.name: Skeleton Key
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 13th level}
You can add a {@dice d10} to checks made with thieves' tools. When you successfully pick a lock, you can alter it to open with a key you possess (in addition or instead of the original keys).

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Rogue|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Rogue|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Rogue|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: As a student of arcane magic, you have learned to cast spells.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: You know three Wizard cantrips of your choice. When you reach 4th and 10th level, you learn another Wizard cantrip of your choice.
Whenever you finish a long rest, you can replace one of your cantrips from this feature with another Wizard cantrip of your choice.

entries.name: Spellbook
entries: Your spellbook is a Tiny object that weighs 3 pounds, contains 100 pages, and can be read only by you or someone casting {@spell Identify|TGTT}. It contains the level 1+ spells you know. It starts with six level 1 Wizard spells of your choice.
Whenever you gain a Wizard level after 1, add two Wizard spells of your choice to your spellbook. Each of these spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

entries.name: Prepared Spells of Level 1+
entries: You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast. To do so, choose four spells from your spellbook. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots.
The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Wizard levels, as shown in the Wizard class table.
If another Wizard feature gives you spells that you always have prepared, those spells don't count against the number of spells you can prepare with this feature.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your Wizard spells.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use an Arcane Focus or your spellbook as a Spellcasting Focus for your Wizard spells.

classFeature.name: Arcane Recovery
entries: You can regain some of your magical energy by studying your spellbook. When you finish a short rest, you can choose expended spell slots to recover. The spell slots can have a combined level equal to no more than half your Wizard level (round up), and none of the slots can be level 6 or higher.
Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Ritual Adept
entries: You can cast any spell as a ritual if that spell has the ritual tag and the spell is in your spellbook. You needn't have the spell prepared, but you must read from the book to cast a spell in this way.

classFeature.name: Scholar
entries: While studying magic, you also specialized in another field of study. Choose one of the following skills in which you have proficiency: {@skill Arcana}, {@skill History}, {@skill Investigation}, {@skill Medicine}, {@skill Nature}, or {@skill Religion}. You gain expertise in the chosen skill.

classFeature.name: Wizard Subclass
entries: You gain a Wizard subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialty that grants you features at certain Wizard levels.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, and 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain a specialty of your choice. You gain another specialty at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 20th level.

classFeature.name: Air Lift
entries: As an action, you can increase your vertical jump distance to 15 feet until the end of your turn. When you jump this way, you can move 10 feet horizontally for every 5 feet you descend.

classFeature.name: Detect Magic Savant
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Ability to cast detect magic}

When you cast {@spell detect magic|TGTT}, you can choose one of the following benefits: increase its duration by 10 minutes, increase its radius by 30 feet, or allow it to penetrate up to 2 feet of stone or 2 inches of common metal.

classFeature.name: Eidetic Memory
entries: You can accurately recall anything you have seen or read in the last month. When traveling a route you've taken in the past month, you have advantage on ability checks made against environmental hazards there.

classFeature.name: Illusion Detective
entries: {@i Prerequisite: At least one illusion spell in your spellbook}

You have advantage on Intelligence saving throws and Intelligence (Investigation) checks made to discern illusions. When you see through an illusion, allies within 30 feet have advantage on their own checks against it for the next minute.

classFeature.name: Loremaster of Creatures
entries: Choose one creature type: beasts, constructs, dragons, elementals, fey, fiends, giants, oozes, plants, or undead. When you encounter a creature of that type, you automatically know any relevant history or myths about it.

classFeature.name: Loremaster of Travel
entries: You have advantage on Intelligence (History) checks related to location-based myths and legends. When you arrive in a new settlement, the DM provides you with a useful fact about its culture, government, or populace.

classFeature.name: Persistent Mending
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Ability to cast mending}

After a long rest, you can enchant one piece of equipment for up to six creatures (including yourself). For the next 12 hours, the item instantly repairs itself if damaged. Enchanting a coat, goggles, or shoes grants additional minor benefits against weather or forced marches.

classFeature.name: Presto, Prestidigitation
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Ability to cast prestidigitation}

You and your gear are always kept clean by a constant, minor magical effect. You also add several new options to the {@spell prestidigitation} spell, including creating a temporary rope, gaining temporary darkvision through a coin, or creating a temporary sensor from a pebble.

classFeature.name: Memorize Spell
entries: Whenever you finish a short rest, you can study your spellbook and replace one of the level 1+ Wizard spells you have prepared for your Spellcasting feature with another level 1+ spell from the book.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Wizard Subclass.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Wizard|TGTT|4}.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Wizard Subclass.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Wizard|TGTT|4}.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Wizard Subclass.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Wizard|TGTT|4}.

classFeature.name: Spell Mastery
entries: You have achieved such mastery over certain spells that you can cast them at will. Choose a 1st-level and a 2nd-level spell in your spellbook that have a casting time of an action. You always have those spells prepared, and you can cast them at their lowest level without expending a spell slot. To cast either spell at a higher level, you must expend a spell slot.
Whenever you finish a long rest, you can study your spellbook and replace one of those spells with an eligible spell of the same level from the book.

classFeature.name: Epic Boon
entries: You gain an Epic Boon feat of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Signature Spells
entries: Choose two 3rd-level spells in your spellbook as your signature spells. You always have these spells prepared, they don't count against the number of spells you have prepared, and you can cast each of them once at 3rd level without expending a spell slot. When you do so, you can't cast them in this way again until you finish a short or long rest. To cast either spell at a higher level, you must expend a spell slot.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Wizard|TGTT|4}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain a specialty of your choice from the following list.

classFeature.name: Beast Speech
entries: You can cast {@spell speak with animals} at will, without expending a spell slot.

classFeature.name: Book of Ancient Secrets
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Pact of the Tome}

You can add two 1st-level ritual spells to your Book of Shadows. You can cast these spells, and any other warlock spells you know with the ritual tag, as rituals. You can also scribe other ritual spells you find into the book.

classFeature.name: Devil's Sight
entries: You can see normally in darkness, both magical and nonmagical, to a distance of 120 feet.

classFeature.name: Eldritch Sight
entries: You can cast {@spell detect magic|TGTT} at will, without expending a spell slot.

classFeature.name: Mirror, Mirror
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 3rd level}

You can use a reflective surface to commune with your patron during a rest. You can also use this invocation to cast {@spell augury} once per long rest without expending a spell slot or material components.

classFeature.name: Otherworldly Leap
entries: Your jump distance increases by 15 feet vertically, and 30 feet horizontally.

classFeature.name: Portents and Portals
entries: You automatically sense the presence of any planar portal within 1 mile. You have advantage on checks to locate it and gain a bonus equal to your proficiency bonus on checks made to interact with it.

classFeature.name: Whiff of the Beyond
entries: You automatically sense when an aberration, celestial, elemental, fey, fiend, or undead has been within 30 feet of you in the past 24 hours. You also gain a bonus to Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks equal to your proficiency bonus and have advantage on checks to track these creature types.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Warlock|TGTT|2}.

classFeature.name: Ascendant Step
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 9th level}
You can cast {@spell levitate} on yourself at will, without expending a spell slot or material components.

classFeature.name: One with Shadows
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 5th level}
As an action, you can become invisible while in dim light or darkness. The invisibility ends if you move, take an action, or use a reaction.

classFeature.name: Visions of Distant Realms
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 7th level}
You can cast {@spell arcane eye} once without expending a spell slot. You regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. At 15th level, you can cast it at will.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Warlock|TGTT|2}.

classFeature.name: Inscrutability
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 13th level}
You are constantly under the effects of the {@spell nondetection|TGTT} spell.

classFeature.name: Master of Myriad Forms
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 15th level}
You can cast {@spell alter self|TGTT} at will, without expending a spell slot.

classFeature.name: Shadowveil
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 15th level}
You can cast {@spell invisibility} at will, without expending a spell slot.

classFeature.name: Combat Methods
entries: Your 1st-level training unlocks tactical methods—disciplined techniques fueled by stamina.

items: {@b Choose Traditions.} Gain proficiency in two {@filter combat traditions|combatmethods} of your choice and learn three methods drawn from traditions you’re proficient with.
{@b Set the DC.} If a method calls for a saving throw, use your Method DC (see below) when determining the target’s difficulty.
{@b Spend Stamina.} When you activate a method, you spend points from your stamina pool (see below). Unless noted otherwise, you can use each method once per turn.
{@b Learn More.} The Methods Known column of the Combat Methods table shows when you gain additional methods, and Method Degree lists the highest degree you can select.
{@b Refresh and Replace.} Whenever you gain a level that grants a new method, you may also replace one method you know with another from a tradition you’re proficient with.

entries.name: Stamina Pool
entries: Your stamina pool equals twice your proficiency bonus. You regain all spent stamina when you finish a short or long rest.
See the {@variantrule Stamina|TGTT} rule for more information.

entries.name: Method DC
entries: Your Method DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice).
For complete details and edge cases, consult the {@variantrule Method DC|TGTT} rule.

entries.name: Combat Tradition Rules
entries: Combat traditions define which methods you can learn. Review {@variantrule Combat Traditions|TGTT} for  tradition summaries and more information

entries.name: Getting Started
entries: {@b Default Traditions.} For straightforward play, start with {@filter Tempered Iron|combatmethods|tradition=Tempered Iron} (reliable weapon techniques and defense) and {@filter Adamant Mountain|combatmethods|tradition=Adamant Mountain} (heavy strikes and resilience).

{@b Suggested Methods.} From Tempered Iron, consider {@combatmethod Zealous Stance|TGTT} and {@combatmethod Purge Magic|TGTT}. From Adamant Mountain, {@combatmethod Heavy Stance|TGTT} and {@combatmethod Catch Your Breath|TGTT} provide immediate battlefield versatility.

classFeature.name: Combat Methods
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you channel battlefield adaptability through combat methods.

items: {@b Choose Traditions.} Gain proficiency in two combat traditions from {@filter Biting Zephyr|combatmethods|tradition=Biting Zephyr}, {@filter Mirror's Glint|combatmethods|tradition=Mirror's Glint}, {@filter Rapid Current|combatmethods|tradition=Rapid Current}, {@filter Razor's Edge|combatmethods|tradition=Razor's Edge}, {@filter Spirited Steed|combatmethods|tradition=Spirited Steed}, or {@filter Unending Wheel|combatmethods|tradition=Unending Wheel}. Learn two methods from traditions you’re proficient with.
{@b Set the DC.} When a method calls for a saving throw, use your Method DC (see below) to set the difficulty.
{@b Spend Stamina.} Activating a method spends points from your stamina pool (see below). Unless stated otherwise, you can use each method once per turn.
{@b Learn More.} Use the Methods Known column to track how many methods you learn and Method Degree to see the highest degree you may select.
{@b Refresh and Replace.} Whenever you gain a level that grants a new method, you can replace one method you know with another from a tradition you’re proficient with.

entries.name: Stamina Pool
entries: Your stamina pool equals twice your proficiency bonus. You regain all spent stamina when you finish a short or long rest.
See the {@variantrule Stamina|TGTT} rule for more information.

entries.name: Method DC
entries: Your Method DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice).
For edge cases and advanced guidance, consult {@variantrule Method DC|TGTT}.

entries.name: Combat Tradition Rules
entries: Combat traditions define which methods you can learn. Review {@variantrule Combat Traditions|TGTT} for tradition summaries and detailed rules.

entries.name: Getting Started
entries: {@b Default Traditions.} For balanced play, start with {@filter Biting Zephyr|combatmethods|tradition=Biting Zephyr} (ranged control) and {@filter Rapid Current|combatmethods|tradition=Rapid Current} (speed and agility).

{@b Suggested Methods.} From Biting Zephyr, consider {@combatmethod Point Blank Shot|TGTT} and {@combatmethod Zephyr Stance|TGTT}. From Rapid Current, {@combatmethod Swift Stance|TGTT} and {@combatmethod Charge|TGTT} keep you mobile and lethal.

classFeature.name: Primal Focus
entries: You hone your mind and body into two survival modes: Predator and Prey. At the end of a long rest, choose a focus. You remain in that focus until you finish a long rest or use an Action to switch. You can switch a number of times equal to your Focus Switches; you regain all expended uses on a long rest.

items: {@b Focused Quarry.} As a Bonus Action, designate a creature you can sense within 60 feet as your Quarry. Once per turn when you hit your Quarry with an attack, deal extra damage: {@dice 1d4} (5th: {@dice 1d6}; 10th: {@dice 1d8}; 14th: {@dice 1d10}). The effect ends if you designate a new Quarry, fall unconscious, or finish a rest.
{@b Hunter’s Insight.} You have advantage on Wisdom (Survival) and Wisdom (Perception) checks to track or spot your Quarry, and you learn the Quarry’s creature type when you designate it.

{@b Hunter’s Dodge.} When a creature you can see attacks an ally or you within 30 feet, you can use your Reaction to grant a bonus to the target’s AC equal to your proficiency bonus for that attack. Uses equal your proficiency bonus; regain all expended uses on a long rest.
{@b Expertise.} Choose one skill proficiency; you gain Expertise in that skill.
{@b Languages.} You learn two languages of your choice.
{@b Tracker.} You gain a bonus equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum +1) on Wisdom (Perception) checks to find hidden or obscured tracks, creatures, or objects.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain two specialties at 1st level. You gain another specialty at 2nd, 4th, 6th, 8th, 10th, 12th, 14th, 16th, 18th, and 20th level. If a specialty has a prerequisite, you must meet it to learn that specialty.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Ranger|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Ranger|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Ranger|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Ranger|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Ranger|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Ranger|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Ranger|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Ranger|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Ranger|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Ranger|TGTT|1}.

classFeature.name: Beast Friend
entries: By spending 10 minutes feeding and befriending a willing beast that is Medium or smaller with CR 1/8 or less, it becomes your loyal companion. It acts independently, rolls its own initiative, and won’t attack unless attacked first. You can have only one such companion at a time.

classFeature.name: Build Shelter
entries: Spend 2 hours to build a shelter for up to 8 creatures. It protects from the elements and reduces the Region Modifier or encounter die by 2. The shelter collapses after a day unattended. You can’t use this again for one week.

classFeature.name: Calls of the Wild
entries: You can communicate simple ideas with beasts via sounds and gestures to ask for basic information or make simple requests.

classFeature.name: Ear to the Ground
entries: If you remain still for 1 minute, you gain tremorsense with a range of 30 feet until you move or take an action.

classFeature.name: Expert Foraging
entries: Once per long rest, take 1 minute to collect edible plants and grubs equal to your proficiency bonus. A creature can use an action to eat one to restore 1 hit point. They spoil after 1 hour.

classFeature.name: Forced Marcher
entries: Increase hours you can travel before forced-march saves by your proficiency bonus.

classFeature.name: Guide
entries: When performing the {@i Scout} journey activity, on a failure you instead succeed. Performing Scout doesn’t count against your activity limit for that travel segment.

classFeature.name: Healing Salves
entries: Once per long rest, spend 1 hour to create a healing salve with uses equal to 1 + your Wisdom modifier. As an action, a use restores {@dice 1d4} hit points. Expires after 24 hours.

classFeature.name: Herbal Bitters
entries: Spend 1 hour to brew two servings. A creature can use an action to drink a serving, reducing its exhaustion level by one for 4 hours. Expires after 24 hours.

classFeature.name: Huntsman
entries: You have advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks for the {@i Hunt and Gather} journey activity.

classFeature.name: Longwalker
entries: You do not gain exhaustion from lacking Supply during a journey. Alternatively, you may take that exhaustion to prevent up to two companions from gaining it. Once used, you can’t use this again for one week.

classFeature.name: Monster Mimic
entries: You have advantage on Charisma (Deception) and Charisma (Intimidation) checks to imitate the sounds and movements of a beast or monstrosity you know.

classFeature.name: Poisons and Antidotes
entries: Once per long rest, after 1 hour of searching with a DC 10 Wisdom (Survival) check, spend 10 minutes to create 3 doses of basic poison; or spend 1 hour to create one dose of antitoxin from the same plants.

classFeature.name: Read the Room
entries: By observing a group for 10 minutes, focus on one creature within 30 feet to learn its current motivations and goals (unless Int 3 or lower). As an action, shift focus to another creature.

classFeature.name: Relentless Pursuit
entries: Once you find a creature’s tracks, you gain a +10 bonus to subsequent checks to track that creature for the next 7 days.

classFeature.name: Swift Tracker
entries: You can track while traveling at a Normal pace. You can track at a Fast pace but with disadvantage.

classFeature.name: See the Unseen
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 5th level}
Once per long rest, focus your senses for 1 hour; during this time you can see invisible creatures and objects and perceive creatures/objects on the Ethereal Plane.

classFeature.name: Uncanny Tracker
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 5th level}

When you succeed on a Wisdom (Survival) check to track, gain one additional degree of success; if not using degrees, learn an extra vital detail (numbers, health, or time passed) not normally revealed.

classFeature.name: Master Tracker
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 9th level}
Spend 5 minutes to study a 30-foot-diameter outdoor area to learn all visual events in the last 24 hours as if witnessed. No auditory info. Once used, you can’t use again until a long rest.

classFeature.name: Fighting Style
entries: You adopt a Fighting Style feat. Alternatively, choose Druidic Warrior.

entries.name: Druidic Warrior
entries: You learn two Druid cantrips (e.g., {@spell guidance}, {@spell starry wisp|TGTT}). They count as Ranger spells for you, and Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for them. Whenever you gain a Ranger level, you can replace one with another Druid cantrip.

classFeature.name: Ranger Archetype
entries: You choose a Ranger Archetype at 3rd level. It grants features at 3rd, 7th, 11th, and 15th level.

classFeature.name: Ranger Archetype Feature
entries: Your Ranger Archetype grants an additional feature.

classFeature.name: Ranger Archetype Feature
entries: Your Ranger Archetype grants an additional feature.

classFeature.name: Ranger Archetype Feature
entries: Your Ranger Archetype grants an additional feature.

items: {@b Environmental Resilience.} You are immune to effects of extreme cold, extreme heat, and high altitude, and you automatically succeed on saving throws against exhaustion caused by natural travel or environmental effects.
{@b Efficient Camper.} When you undertake a camping or journey activity, you can perform a second activity in the same time segment.

{@b Temporary Hit Points.} As a Magic action or Bonus Action, gain temporary hit points equal to {@dice 1d8} + your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1). Uses equal your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1); regain uses on a long rest.
{@b Decrease Exhaustion.} Whenever you finish a short rest, your exhaustion level, if any, decreases by 1.
{@b Singular Focus (Bonus Action, 2 Stamina Points).} Choose a creature you can see; until the start of your next turn, your weapon attacks against it ignore disadvantage.
{@b Pursuit.} Your walking speed increases by 10 feet while in this focus.

{@b Intimidating Foe.} Once per turn when you hit with a weapon attack, force a Wisdom save vs. your spell save DC; on a failure, the creature is {@condition frightened} of you until the end of your next turn and its speed is 0 while frightened of you.

{@b Predator Eye (Bonus Action).} Make an Intelligence (Nature) check contested by the target’s Charisma (Deception) or passive Deception. On a success, learn one of the creature’s resistances or vulnerabilities.

{@b Groundshatter (Action, 3 Stamina Points).} Strike a surface within 5 feet; a 50-foot-long, 5-foot-wide line from that point becomes difficult terrain. Creatures and objects in the line take your weapon damage. Surfaces 1 foot thick or less are destroyed.
{@b Terrain Defense.} Gain a bonus to AC and Dexterity saves equal to half your proficiency bonus (minimum +1) when benefiting from cover or standing in difficult terrain.

{@b Improvised Sanctuary (Action).} Reinforce a 5-foot section of natural terrain into a 10-foot cube Fortified Position: Half Cover counts as Three-Quarters Cover, and creatures inside have advantage on attacks against targets obscured from the Ranger’s position. Lasts until you move more than 30 feet away or create a new one.
{@b Relentless Momentum.} Ignore speed reduction from damage, spells, or magical effects.
{@b Charging Strike.} When you move at least 10 feet straight toward a creature, your first weapon attack against it that turn has advantage.
{@b Deflection.} Creatures other than your Quarry have disadvantage on opportunity attacks against you.
{@b Unimpeded.} You ignore difficult terrain up to a distance equal to your speed when moving.

entries.name: Predator Focus
entries: {@b Blood Scent.} After you deal damage to a target, you know its exact direction and distance for the next hour, ignoring invisibility and magical concealment.

entries.name: Prey Focus
entries: {@b Inescapable Sight (Bonus Action).} Sense the exact location (direction and distance) of all hostile or obscured creatures within 60 feet until the end of your next turn.

classFeature.name: Ephemeral Insight
entries: After observing, reading, or questioning a creature, environment, or process for 1 hour, gain proficiency in one relevant skill or tool until you finish a long rest or use this feature again.

items: {@b Expertise.} Choose two skill proficiencies; you gain Expertise in them.
{@b Mental Fortitude.} You gain proficiency in Intelligence and Wisdom saving throws.
{@b Reliable Survivalist.} When you make an Intelligence (Nature) or Wisdom (Survival) check or checks related to navigation or tracking, treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.
{@b Internal Compass.} You always know north, the time of day, and the direction/distance to the nearest fresh water within 1 mile.
{@b Recall Path.} If you spent at least 1 hour in a location within the last year, you can meditate for 1 minute to recall it as if casting {@spell find the path}.
{@b Vigilance.} In terrain you traversed in the last year, you have advantage on Perception checks to notice traps or ambushes.

classFeature.name: Penetrating Senses
entries: You can see invisible creatures and objects, hidden doors, and portals within 60 feet; you automatically detect visual illusions and perceive the true form of shapechangers within this range.

items: {@b Primal Sight.} You gain blindsight with a range of 60 feet.
{@b Aura of the Trailblazer.} Allies within 30 feet gain a bonus to Stealth, Nature, and Survival checks equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum +1).
{@b Echoes of the Wild.} Spend 1 minute and make a DC 15 Survival or Nature check to reconstruct events up to 7 days ago (creatures, actions, magic) at a location.
{@b Iron Mind.} Your concentration can’t be broken as a result of taking damage.
{@b Retaliation.} Once per round when a creature misses you with an attack, you can use your Reaction to make one weapon attack against that creature.

classFeature.name: Apex Focus
entries: You can switch Focus freely (no Action) at the start of your turn or as desired.

entries.name: Predator Focus
entries: {@b Perfect Hunter.} Your speed is doubled and you can’t suffer disadvantage on weapon attack rolls.

entries.name: Prey Focus
entries: {@b Perfect Survivor.} At the start of each turn, gain temporary hit points equal to your Ranger level. While not wearing heavy armor, you gain a +2 bonus to AC.

classFeature.name: Combat Methods
entries: At 2nd level, you refine subtle strikes and escape artistry through combat methods.

items: {@b Choose Traditions.} Gain proficiency in two combat traditions from {@filter Biting Zephyr|combatmethods|tradition=Biting Zephyr}, {@filter Mist and Shade|combatmethods|tradition=Mist and Shade}, or {@filter Rapid Current|combatmethods|tradition=Rapid Current}. Learn two methods from traditions you’re proficient with.
{@b Set the DC.} When a method calls for a saving throw, apply your Method DC (see below).
{@b Spend Stamina.} Activating a method expends stamina (see below). Unless noted otherwise, each method may be used once per turn.
{@b Learn More.} Track your options using the Methods Known and Method Degree columns of the class table.
{@b Refresh and Replace.} Whenever you gain a new method from leveling, you may replace one method you know with another from a tradition you’re proficient with.

entries.name: Stamina Pool
entries: Your stamina pool equals twice your proficiency bonus. You regain all spent stamina after a short or long rest.
See {@variantrule Stamina|TGTT} for full rules.

entries.name: Method DC
entries: Your Method DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice).
Consult {@variantrule Method DC|TGTT} for additional nuances.

entries.name: Combat Tradition Rules
entries: Combat traditions govern which methods you can learn. Review {@variantrule Combat Traditions|TGTT} for tradition summaries and detailed rules.

entries.name: Getting Started
entries: {@b Default Traditions.} For classic rogue play, start with {@filter Mist and Shade|combatmethods|tradition=Mist and Shade} (stealth and deception) and {@filter Rapid Current|combatmethods|tradition=Rapid Current} (swift repositioning).

{@b Suggested Methods.} From Mist and Shade, consider {@combatmethod Deceptive Stance|TGTT} and {@combatmethod Perplexing Flurry|TGTT}. From Rapid Current, {@combatmethod Swift Stance|TGTT} and {@combatmethod Speed Over Strength|TGTT} bolster your agility and subterfuge.

classFeature.name: Combat Methods
entries: Beginning at 2nd level, your disciplined focus manifests as combat methods.

items: {@b Choose Traditions.} Gain proficiency in two combat traditions from {@filter Mirror's Glint|combatmethods|tradition=Mirror's Glint}, {@filter Rapid Current|combatmethods|tradition=Rapid Current}, {@filter Razor's Edge|combatmethods|tradition=Razor's Edge}, or {@filter Unending Wheel|combatmethods|tradition=Unending Wheel}. Learn two methods from traditions you’re proficient with.
{@b Set the DC.} When a method calls for a saving throw, apply your Method DC (see below).
{@b Spend Focus or Stamina.} Activate methods by spending Focus points or stamina (see the insets below). Unless specified otherwise, each method can be used once per turn.
{@b Learn More.} Reference the Methods Known column to track how many methods you learn and the Method Degree column for the highest degree available to you.
{@b Refresh and Replace.} Whenever you gain a level that grants a method, you may replace one method you know with another from a tradition you’re proficient with.

entries.name: Stamina & Focus
entries: Your stamina pool equals twice your proficiency bonus and refreshes after a short or long rest.
As a monk, you may spend Focus points in place of stamina to power methods. For more information about stamina, read {@variantrule Stamina|TGTT}.

entries.name: Method DC
entries: Your Method DC equals 9 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength, Dexterity or Wisdom modifier (your choice).
For complete guidance, see {@variantrule Method DC|TGTT}.

entries.name: Combat Tradition Rules
entries: Combat traditions define accessible methods. Review {@variantrule Combat Traditions|TGTT} for tradition overviews and usage rules.

entries.name: Getting Started
entries: {@b Default Traditions.} For monks seeking harmony between martial prowess and spiritual growth, consider {@filter Unending Wheel|combatmethods|tradition=Unending Wheel} (circular motions and redirected force) and {@filter Rapid Current|combatmethods|tradition=Rapid Current} (fluid movement and speed). These traditions complement the monk's philosophy of flowing, adaptable combat.

{@b Suggested Methods.} From Unending Wheel, try {@combatmethod Throwing Stance|TGTT} and {@combatmethod Victory Pose|TGTT}. From Rapid Current, {@combatmethod Swift Stance|TGTT} and {@combatmethod Charge|TGTT} keep you mobile and ready to redirect foes.

classFeature.name: Combat Methods
entries: At 2nd level, your oath combines martial precision with combat methods.

items: {@b Choose Traditions.} Gain proficiency in two combat traditions from {@filter Sanguine Knot|combatmethods|tradition=Sanguine Knot}, {@filter Spirited Steed|combatmethods|tradition=Spirited Steed}, or {@filter Tempered Iron|combatmethods|tradition=Tempered Iron}. Learn two methods from traditions you’re proficient with.
{@b Set the DC.} Apply your Method DC (see below) whenever a method requires a saving throw.
{@b Spend Stamina.} Activating a method consumes stamina (see below). Unless otherwise stated, you can use each method once per turn.
{@b Learn More.} Use the Methods Known column to track how many methods you learn and the Method Degree column for the highest degree you can select.
{@b Refresh and Replace.} Whenever you level and gain a new method, you may replace one method you know with another from a tradition you’re proficient with.

entries.name: Stamina & Spell Slots
entries: Your stamina pool equals twice your proficiency bonus and refreshes after a short or long rest.
As a bonus action, you can expend a spell slot to regain stamina equal to the slot’s level + 2. Review {@variantrule Stamina|TGTT} for details on channeling divine power into methods.

entries.name: Method DC
entries: Your Method DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice). Paladins may instead use their spell save DC if preferred.
Consult {@variantrule Method DC|TGTT} for comprehensive rulings.

entries.name: Combat Tradition Rules
entries: Combat traditions determine your method options. Review {@variantrule Combat Traditions|TGTT} for tradition summaries and guidelines.

entries.name: Getting Started
entries: {@b Default Traditions.} For paladins who wish to embody divine justice and protection, consider {@filter Tempered Iron|combatmethods|tradition=Tempered Iron} (reliable defense and tactical strikes) and {@filter Sanguine Knot|combatmethods|tradition=Sanguine Knot} (sacrificial techniques and protective abilities). This pairing reflects the paladin's role as both guardian and righteous warrior.

{@b Suggested Methods.} From Tempered Iron, consider {@combatmethod Zealous Stance|TGTT} (attack bonus reflecting divine fervor) and {@combatmethod Imposing Glare|TGTT} (frighten enemies with righteous presence). From Sanguine Knot, consider {@combatmethod Shield Wall|TGTT} (tactical defense with allies) and {@combatmethod Shoulder Check|TGTT} (protect allies by repositioning enemies). These methods emphasize the paladin's dual nature as protector and divine warrior.

classFeature.name: Combat Methods
entries: At 2nd level, your primal ferocity crystallizes into combat methods.

items: {@b Choose Traditions.} Gain proficiency in two combat traditions from {@filter Adamant Mountain|combatmethods|tradition=Adamant Mountain}, {@filter Mirror's Glint|combatmethods|tradition=Mirror's Glint}, {@filter Rapid Current|combatmethods|tradition=Rapid Current}, {@filter Tempered Iron|combatmethods|tradition=Tempered Iron}, or {@filter Tooth and Claw|combatmethods|tradition=Tooth and Claw}. Learn two methods from traditions you’re proficient with.
{@b Set the DC.} When a method requires a saving throw, apply your Method DC (see below).
{@b Spend Stamina.} Activating a method expends stamina (see below). Unless otherwise noted, each method can be used once per turn.
{@b Learn More.} Consult the Methods Known column to track how many methods you learn and Method Degree for the highest degree available to you.
{@b Refresh and Replace.} Whenever you gain a new method from leveling, you may replace one method you know with another from a tradition you’re proficient with.

entries.name: Stamina Pool
entries: Your stamina pool equals twice your proficiency bonus. You recover all stamina after a short or long rest.
See {@variantrule Stamina|TGTT} for full rules and interactions with Rage-fueled tactics.

entries.name: Method DC
entries: Your Method DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice).
For advanced interactions, refer to {@variantrule Method DC|TGTT}.

entries.name: Combat Tradition Rules
entries: Combat traditions set the framework for the methods you can learn. Review {@variantrule Combat Traditions|TGTT} for summaries and usage rules.

entries.name: Getting Started
entries: {@b Default Traditions.} Capture raw might with {@filter Adamant Mountain|combatmethods|tradition=Adamant Mountain} and primal savagery with {@filter Tooth and Claw|combatmethods|tradition=Tooth and Claw}.
{@b Suggested Methods.} Adamant Mountain’s Heavy Stance and Lean Into It reinforce durability; Tooth and Claw’s Springing Stance and Bounding Strike emphasize feral aggression.

classFeature.name: Focus Speech
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 15th level}
You understand all spoken languages. Moreover, any creature that can understand a language can understand what you say.

classFeature.name: Hurricane Walk
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Gale Walk feature}

As a bonus action, you can spend 1 stamina point to cast the fly spell on yourself, with a duration that lasts until the start of your next turn. You also have advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to move silently until the start of your next turn.

classFeature.name: Instant Step
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Adept Speed feature, 11th level}

As an action, you can spend 4 stamina points to teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 500 feet. You can bring objects you are wearing or carrying and one willing creature of your size or smaller. When you reappear, you are invisible until the start of your next turn.

classFeature.name: Shadow Walk
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 11th level, proficiency in Stealth}

As a bonus action, you can teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see, provided both your starting space and destination are in dim light or darkness. You then have advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks until the start of your next turn.

classFeature.name: Sixth Sense
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 11th level}
You have advantage on initiative rolls. You can use your Wisdom modifier instead of Intelligence for Intelligence (Arcana, History, Investigation, Nature, and Religion) checks.

classFeature.name: Seek Truths
entries: You gain a bonus to Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) checks made to discern lies equal to your proficiency bonus. After you successfully see through a creature's lie, you have advantage on Insight checks against that creature for the next 24 hours.

classFeature.name: Sense Import
entries: Your Divine Sense ability also alerts you when you enter a place of great holy or unholy importance, and you learn basic information about its significance.

classFeature.name: Silvered Tongue
entries: You gain a bonus to Charisma ({@skill Intimidation}) and Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) checks equal to your proficiency bonus. After successfully intimidating or persuading a creature, you have advantage on subsequent checks against it for the next 24 hours.

classFeature.name: Undaunted
entries: When in a place you've never been before, you can use your reaction to gain advantage on ability checks and attack rolls until the end of your next turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier, regaining all uses after a long rest.

classFeature.name: Focus
entries: At 1st level, you learn how to enhance your focus to better control yourself in the dream.
You gain proficiency in Constitution saving throws.

classFeature.name: Dreamer (Dreamwalker)
entries: Also at 1st level, you learn more ways to manipulate the Dreamtime and your actions within it. You gain the {@feat Dreamer|TGTT} feat.
That feat grants you two Dreamwalker abilities from the {@classFeature Dreamwalker Abilities|Dreamwalker|TGTT|1} list below.
You gain this feat again at 4th, 7th, and 9th level. At 4th level, one of the abilities you gain must be {@optfeature Dreamjump|TGTT}, assuming you don't have it already.
