classFeature.name: Dreamer (Dreamwalker)
entries: At 4th level, you gain the {@feat Dreamer|TGTT} feat again.
That feat grants you two Dreamwalker abilities from the {@classFeature Dreamwalker Abilities|Dreamwalker|TGTT|1} list below.
One of the abilities you gain must be {@optfeature Dreamjump|TGTT}, assuming you don't have it already.

classFeature.name: Dreamer (Dreamwalker)
entries: At 7th level, you gain the {@feat Dreamer|TGTT} feat again.
That feat grants you two Dreamwalker abilities from the {@classFeature Dreamwalker Abilities|Dreamwalker|TGTT|1} list below.

classFeature.name: Dreamer (Dreamwalker)
entries: At 9th level, you gain the {@feat Dreamer|TGTT} feat again.
That feat grants you two Dreamwalker abilities from the {@classFeature Dreamwalker Abilities|Dreamwalker|TGTT|1} list below.

classFeature.name: Dreamwalker Abilities
entries: The following Dreamwalker abilities are available to you whenever a feature allows you to learn Dreamwalker abilities. You must meet an ability's prerequisites to learn it.

classFeature.name: Lucid Focus
entries: Your connection to the Dreamtime and the discipline required there hones your mind and will.
Starting at 1st level, you can use a bonus action to grant yourself a Lucid Focus die, a {@dice d6}. For the next round, you can roll the die and add the number rolled to one of the following:

items: An attack roll
A Wisdom saving throw
A Constitution saving throw (but only outside the Dreamtime or other people's dreams)

entries: You can wait until after you roll the d20 before deciding to use the Lucid Focus die, but must decide before the DM says whether the roll succeeds or fails.
Once the Lucid Focus die is rolled, it is consumed. You can have only one Lucid Focus die at a time.
Your Lucid Focus die changes when you reach certain levels in this class. The die becomes a {@dice d8} at 3rd level, a {@dice d10} at 6th level, and a {@dice d12} at 10th level.

classFeature.name: Intuition
entries: While in the dream world, many have difficulty controlling their clothing, often giving away their emotional state.
At 2nd level, you gain advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) checks made inside the Dreamtime and regular dreams.

classFeature.name: Control
entries: Also at 2nd level, you become better at controlling your stray thoughts in the Dreamtime.
You make the roll described in "Constant Effort" only once every 8 hours, and when rolling 1 on the roll, you treat it as having rolled a 2.

classFeature.name: Lucid Awareness
entries: The Dreamwalker gains an almost supernatural awareness of their surroundings within the Dreamtime.

At 3rd level, you can make Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks to determine if anybody is using dreaming abilities within 30 feet per Dreamwalker level of you. A successful check allows you to know in what direction it is being used, and how far.
With a Concentration check ({@dc 15}), you may also determine what abilities are being used.

classFeature.name: Focus Improvement
entries: At 4th level, your focus improves. You gain advantage on Constitution checks and saving throws.

classFeature.name: Needful Search
entries: Starting at 5th level, you may find objects in the Dreamtime when you most need them.
In order to find them, you must make a Concentration check with a DC dependent on the need itself:

entries.caption: Needful Search DC by Item Rarity

entries: The DM may give bonuses to cases of Great Need using the following guide:
A successful check brings you one step closer to your goal (by {@optfeature Dreamjump|TGTT}), and for each 5 points the DC is beaten by, you get an additional step closer.
{@b Steps:} Continent → Country → Town → Street → Building → Floor → 5 feet → 1 foot.

Each additional jump adds +5 to the DC. Failed checks may not be retried, and any attempt with a need that has already been searched for will produce no effect for the first {@dice 12d12} years, whether the original search was successful or not.

classFeature.name: Dreamhaven
entries: Starting at 6th level, you may use your skill in navigating the Dreamtime to shelter other travelers.
Creatures within 30 feet of you of your choice gain a bonus to their Concentration checks equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum +1).

In addition, you gain an ability to shield the dreams of yourself and others outside of the Dreamtime. You are aware when someone tries to watch or enter your regular dreams, as well as the dreams of creatures within 30 feet of you.

You can attempt to stop them from doing so; roll a Concentration check, and the result becomes a DC for their attempt—if they fail, they lose their grasp on the dream and must roll again to find it before making another attempt.

classFeature.name: Waking Dream
entries: At 7th level, your ability to blur the bounds between dream and reality allows you to cross into the Dreamtime in the flesh.
As an action, you can attempt to walk into the Dreamtime in the flesh, requiring a Concentration check ({@dc 30}).
If you succeed, you can travel the Dreamtime and manipulate it using all the abilities you possess, and exit it with another Concentration check ({@dc 30}).
You may not use {@optfeature Dreamwatch|TGTT} to enter regular dreams in the flesh, though you can still observe them.
While in the Dreamtime in this manner, your body still requires food, water, and sleep in a regular manner.

classFeature.name: Dream Supremacy
entries: At 8th level, you learn to weaponize your dreamwalking while in the waking world, making yourself an opponent not to be trifled with.

When you hit a target with an attack, spell, or other ability that has a chance to fail affecting the target and does damage, you may attempt to either push them into the Dreamtime, or drag them along with you.

entries.name: Push
entries: Both you and the target roll opposing checks. The target may choose to roll either a Constitution or Wisdom saving throw, and you must roll a Concentration check. The target gains a bonus of 15 to their roll if they do not possess any dreamwalking abilities.
On the target's failure, they are transported into the Dreamtime, as if by {@spell plane shift}.

entries.name: Drag
entries: This can only be used if your attack happened in melee range. When you attempt to move your target into the Dreamtime, you may go with them on a success, provided that you have been within 5 feet of the target when you used this ability.
This allows you to enter the Dreamtime in the flesh without using your "Waking Dream" ability, though you will still need to use it to exit the Dreamtime.

classFeature.name: Focus Improvement
entries: At 9th level, your focus improves beyond normal capabilities. You gain expertise in Constitution checks and saving throws (double your proficiency bonus).

classFeature.name: Just a Weave
entries: At 10th level, you learn the essential truth—that the Dreamtime is only a dream, and all that happens within it can be treated as such.

While in the Dreamtime, you can use an action to replicate the effect of a spell you know the effect of (meaning you don't need to know how to, or be able to cast it; simply to know the effect you are trying to create). This action is the same as casting the spell, though no spell slot or material components are necessary, nor is the ability to cast a spell.

If you do, the effects of the spell are still confined to the Dreamtime—you cannot, for example, use this ability to cast {@spell plane shift} to move yourself to the Feywild, nor can you cast {@spell wish} and use it to turn your real body immortal.
This ability allows you to cast spells with casting time longer than 1 action in 1 action, but the spell is always cast at the lowest level possible.

classFeature.name: Combat Methods
entries: At 2nd level, your infernal training expands into combat methods—martial techniques fueled by hellish stamina.

items: {@b Choose Traditions.} Gain proficiency in two combat traditions from {@filter Eldritch Blackguard|optionalfeatures|feature type=CTM:EB}, {@filter Gallant Heart|optionalfeatures|feature type=CTM:GH}, or {@filter Sanguine Knot|optionalfeatures|feature type=CTM:SK}. Learn two methods from traditions you're proficient with.
{@b Set the DC.} Apply your Method DC (see below) whenever a method requires a saving throw.
{@b Spend Stamina.} Activating a method consumes stamina (see below). Unless otherwise stated, you can use each method once per turn.
{@b Learn More.} Use the Methods Known column to track how many methods you learn and the Method Degree column for the highest degree you can select.
{@b Refresh and Replace.} Whenever you level and gain a new method, you may replace one method you know with another from a tradition you're proficient with.

entries.name: Stamina
entries: Your stamina pool equals twice your proficiency bonus and refreshes after a short or long rest.
Review {@variantrule Stamina|TGTT} for details on powering methods with stamina.

entries.name: Method DC
entries: Your Method DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice). Illriggers may instead use their Interdict save DC if preferred.
Consult {@variantrule Method DC|TGTT} for comprehensive rulings.

entries.name: Combat Tradition Rules
entries: Combat traditions determine your method options. Review {@variantrule Combat Traditions|TGTT} for tradition summaries and guidelines.

entries.name: Getting Started
entries: {@b Default Traditions.} For illriggers who balance dark power with tactical martial prowess, consider {@filter Eldritch Blackguard|optionalfeatures|feature type=CTM:EB} (fiendish combat techniques enhanced by dark magic) and {@filter Sanguine Knot|optionalfeatures|feature type=CTM:SK} (sacrificial techniques and protective abilities). This pairing reflects the illrigger's mastery of both infernal and martial power.

{@b Suggested Methods.} From Eldritch Blackguard, consider methods that combine weapon strikes with infernal energy. From Sanguine Knot, consider methods like {@optfeature Shield Wall|TGTT} (tactical defense) and {@optfeature Shoulder Check|TGTT} (repositioning enemies).

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: Through your infernal training and diabolic contracts, you have developed specialized abilities that set you apart. You gain a specialty of your choice. You gain another specialty at 5th, 7th, 9th, 11th, 14th, and 19th level. Each specialty can only be selected once unless it says otherwise.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Illrigger|TGTT-ILL|3} feature.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Illrigger|TGTT-ILL|3} feature.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Illrigger|TGTT-ILL|3} feature.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Illrigger|TGTT-ILL|3} feature.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Illrigger|TGTT-ILL|3} feature.

classFeature.name: Specialties
entries: You gain another specialty of your choice from the {@classFeature Specialties|Illrigger|TGTT-ILL|3} feature.

classFeature.name: Baleful Presence
entries: Your infernal nature radiates menace. You gain a bonus to Charisma ({@skill Intimidation}) checks equal to your proficiency bonus. After successfully intimidating a creature, you have advantage on subsequent {@skill Intimidation} checks against it for the next 24 hours.

classFeature.name: Dark Resilience
entries: You gain resistance to fire damage. If you already have resistance to fire damage, you instead gain resistance to necrotic damage.

classFeature.name: Diplomatic Intervention
entries: You can spend 1 stamina and use an action to quell strong emotions within 10 feet. You choose which of the following effects to apply to each sentient creature within the area:

items: A target suffering the {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened} condition makes a new saving throw against the DC of the effect that caused the condition and gains a bonus equal to your Charisma modifier on the roll.

A target that has hostile feelings towards creatures of your choice makes a Charisma saving throw against your Interdict save DC or has its hostile feelings suppressed until the end of your next turn. This suppression ends early if the target is attacked or sees its allies being attacked.

classFeature.name: Do Without
entries: You can go a number of days equal to your Charisma modifier without food and water before suffering any adverse effects.

classFeature.name: Endure Elements
entries: For one hour you can ignore environmental effects caused by extreme heat or cold. This feature does not protect you from damage. Once you have used this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Faceless Mask
entries: You have honed your ability to conceal your presence from others. You have advantage on {@skill Stealth} and {@skill Deception} checks made to blend into a crowd or assume a false identity.

classFeature.name: Forked Tongue Mastery
entries: You gain a bonus to Charisma ({@skill Deception}) and Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) checks equal to your proficiency bonus. After successfully deceiving or persuading a creature, you have advantage on subsequent {@skill Deception} and {@skill Persuasion} checks against it for the next 24 hours.

classFeature.name: Infernal Constitution
entries: You double your Strength score when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can lift, push, or drag. During a short rest, you can participate in strenuous activity (such as scouting, sparring, or physical exercise) for up to 30 minutes and still gain the benefits of the rest. During a long rest, you can participate in strenuous activity for up to 1 hour, and your required sleep time is halved.

classFeature.name: Infernal Rejuvenation
entries: You can use an action to channel infernal energy to heal an injured ally within reach. Spend one or more of your ally's hit dice. Your ally recovers hit points equal to the total you roll on the expended hit dice.

classFeature.name: Infernal Tracker
entries: When tracking a creature you have previously interdicted, you can focus on the lingering seal energy it leaves in its wake, allowing you to use your Charisma for any ability checks made to track it.

classFeature.name: Negate Fall
entries: When you fall, or jump down, from a height and spend 1 or more stamina, you can treat the falling distance as 30 feet less for each stamina you spend.

classFeature.name: Soul Reader
entries: You can sense the general emotional state of any creature you can see within 30 feet. You have advantage on {@skill Insight} checks against creatures you have interdicted.

classFeature.name: Hellish Avenger
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 5th level}
Once per turn when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can deal an extra {@damage 1d8} fire damage. This damage increases to {@damage 2d8} at 11th level.

classFeature.name: Purge Toxins
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 5th level}

You gain resistance to poison damage and advantage on saving throws against the {@condition poisoned} condition. Additionally, as an action you can spend 2 stamina to end one poison affecting you. If more than one poison is affecting you, you end one poison you know is present (otherwise you end one at random). You can end additional poisons with the same action by spending 1 additional stamina per poison.

classFeature.name: Infernal Awareness
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 7th level}

You gain {@sense blindsight} with a range of 10 feet—you can sense the presence of creatures through infernal perception. You may take this specialty a second time at 11th level or higher to extend the range to 30 feet.

classFeature.name: Suggestive Words
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 7th level}

As an action, you make a short suggestion to a creature within 10 feet that can hear and understand you. The target makes a Wisdom saving throw against your Interdict save DC. On a failed save, it follows your suggestion to the best of its ability. If the suggestion would harm the target or its allies, the target automatically succeeds on its saving throw.
You can use this specialty a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all spent uses when you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Infernal Supremacy
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 19th level}
When you reduce a creature to 0 hit points, you regain one use of Invoke Hell. Additionally, your Interdict save DC increases by 1.

subclassFeature.name: Child of the Sun Bloodline
entries: There is simplicity in the summer sun, whose light is impossible to overcome. For in what realm could one be bold enough to attempt to tame the sun's great power? Shield yourself from it, and its intensity seems to magnify between your fingers. Hide yourself away, and you only let something else scorch in your place. Attempt to flee from it, and you run forever without once separating yourself from its reach. And in that ever-blazing light a Child of the Sun gains their dazzling power.

Perhaps your lineage can be traced to outsiders with an affinity for the summer sun, or perhaps you have an ancestor who infused a pure droplet of the season's power into their blood. Regardless of how it came to be, the summer's power has never once diluted, and now rises through you into this world with silent and commanding brilliance.

subclassFeature.name: Glimpse of the Sun
entries: Starting at 1st level, the first powers of the sun awaken in you. You learn the {@spell light} cantrip, but it doesn't count against your number of sorcerer cantrips you know. When you cast this cantrip, you can designate which creatures within 20 feet of you can perceive the light. All others cannot see the light created by the cantrip.

When you reach 3rd level, when you have an ongoing {@spell light} cantrip, you can spend a sorcery point as an action and choose a creature within 20 feet of the light source who can't already see it. The light from your cantrip suddenly flares with dazzling brightness, and the creature must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC or be {@condition blinded} until the end of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Summer's Defiant Blood
entries: Your blood hums with volatile power, and your racing heartbeat reverberates with arcane energy, making it the perfect conduit for your spells. Whenever a creature makes an attack roll against you or forces you to make a saving throw, you can add your Charisma modifier to the damage roll of the next spell you cast before the end of your next turn. You can only benefit from this feature one per round.

subclassFeature.name: Sun Spells
entries: When you reach a Sorcerer level specified in the Sun Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.caption: Sun Spells
rows: {@spell Daybreak|Ar1}, {@spell Fearie Fire|XPHB}, {@spell Continual Flame|XPHB}, {@spell Flaming Sphere|XPHB}

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Archer
entries: An Arcane Archer studies a unique method of archery that weaves magic into attacks to produce supernatural effects. Arcane Archers are some of the most elite archers in existence. They stand watch over the fringes of mortal domains, keeping a keen eye out for trespassers and using magic-infused arrows to defeat monsters and invaders before they can reach settlements.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Archer Lore
entries: At 3rd level, you learn magical theory or some of the secrets of nature—typical for practitioners of this elven martial tradition. You choose to gain proficiency in either the {@skill Arcana}, {@skill Religion} or the {@skill Nature} skill, and you choose to learn either the {@spell prestidigitation} or the {@spell druidcraft} cantrip.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Shot
entries: At 3rd level, you learn to unleash special magical effects with some of your shots. When you gain this feature, you learn two Arcane Shot options of your choice (see "Arcane Shot Options" below).

Once per turn when you fire a missile from a ranged weapon as part of the {@action Attack} action, you can apply one of your Arcane Shot options to that missile. You decide to use the option when the missile hits a creature, unless the option doesn't involve an attack roll. You can use this ability a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses of it when you finish a short or long rest.
You gain an additional Arcane Shot option of your choice when you reach certain levels in this class: 7th, 10th, 15th, and 18th level. Each option also improves when you become an 18th-level fighter.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Shot Options
entries: The Arcane Shot feature lets you choose options for it at certain levels. The options are presented here in alphabetical order. They are all magical effects, and each one is associated with one of the schools of magic.
If an option requires a saving throw, your Arcane Shot save DC is calculated as follows:

subclassFeature.name: Assassin
entries: {@i Practice the Grim Art of Death}

An Assassin's training focuses on using stealth, poison, and disguise to eliminate foes with deadly efficiency. While some Rogues who follow this path are hired killers, spies, or bounty hunters, the capabilities of this subclass are equally useful for adventurers facing a variety of monstrous enemies.

subclassFeature.name: Assassinate
entries: You're adept at ambushing a target, granting you the following benefits.

entries.name: Initiative
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} rolls.

entries.name: Surprising Strikes
entries: During the first round of each combat, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against any creature that hasn't taken a turn. If your Sneak Attack hits any target during that round, the hit is a critical hit.

subclassFeature.name: Infiltration Expertise
entries: You are expert at the following techniques that aid your infiltrations.

entries.name: Masterful Mimicry
entries: You can unerringly mimic another person's speech, mannerisms, handwriting, or all of it if you have spent at least 1 hour studying them.
If a wary creature suspects something is amiss, you have advantage on any Charisma ({@skill Deception}) check you make to avoid detection.

entries.name: Fake Identities
entries: You can unfailingly create false identities for yourself. You must spend seven days and 25 gp to establish the history, profession, and affiliations for an identity. You can't establish an identity that belongs to someone else. For example, you might acquire appropriate clothing, letters of introduction, and official-looking certification to establish yourself as a member of a trading house from a remote city so you can insinuate yourself into the company of other wealthy merchants.
Thereafter, if you adopt the new identity as a disguise, other creatures believe you to be that person until given an obvious reason not to.

entries.name: Roving Aim
entries: Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} isn't reduced to 0 by using Steady Aim.

subclassFeature.name: Thief
entries: {@i Hunt for Treasure as a Classic Adventurer}

A mix of burglar, treasure hunter, and explorer, you are the epitome of an adventurer. In addition to improving your agility and stealth, you gain abilities useful for delving into ruins and getting maximum benefit from the magic items you find there.

subclassFeature.name: Fast Hands
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can do one of the following.

entries.name: Sleight of Hand
entries: Make a Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand|XPHB}) check to pick a lock or disarm a trap with {@item Thieves' Tools|XPHB} or to pick a pocket.

entries.name: Use an Object
entries: Take the {@action Utilize|TGTT} action, or take the {@action Magic|XPHB} action to use a magic item that requires that action.

When you take the {@action Utilize|TGTT} action with an item that can be used either as an action or a bonus action, you may use it as a bonus action and still gain the benefit of using it as an action, as detailed in the {@variantrule Item Utilization|TGTT} rules.
In addition, if an item can be used by swapping one attack for its use, you can use it as a bonus action.

subclassFeature.name: Use Magic Device
entries: You've learned how to maximize use of magic items, granting you the following benefits.

entries.name: Thief of Arcane Secrets
entries: You ignore all class, race, and level requirements on the use of magic items.

entries.name: Attunement
entries: You can attune to up to four magic items at once.

entries.name: Charges
entries: Whenever you use a magic item property that expends charges, roll {@dice 1d6}. On a roll of 6, you use the property without expending the charges.

entries.name: Scrolls
entries: You can use any {@item Spell Scroll|XDMG}, using Intelligence as your spellcasting ability for the spell. If the spell is a cantrip or a level 1 spell, you can cast it reliably. If the scroll contains a higher-level spell, you must first succeed on an Intelligence ({@skill Arcana|XPHB}) check ({@dc 10} plus the spell's level). On a successful check, you cast the spell from the scroll. On a failed check, the scroll disintegrates.

subclassFeature.name: College of Glamour
entries: The College of Glamour is the home of bards who mastered their craft in the vibrant realm of the Feywild. Tutored by satyrs, eladrin, and other fey, these bards learn to use their magic to delight and captivate others.

subclassFeature.name: College of Lore
entries: Bards of the College of Lore know something about most things, collecting bits of knowledge from sources as diverse as scholarly tomes and peasant tales. Their loyalty lies in the pursuit of beauty and truth, not in fealty to a monarch or following the tenets of a deity.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: You gain proficiency with three skills of your choice.

subclassFeature.name: Cutting Words
entries: You learn how to use your wit to distract, confuse, and otherwise sap the confidence and competence of others. When a creature that you can see within 60 feet of you makes an attack roll, an ability check, or a damage roll, you can use your reaction to expend one of your uses of Bardic Inspiration, rolling a Bardic Inspiration die and subtracting the number rolled from the creature's roll. You can choose to use this feature after the creature makes its roll, but before the DM determines whether the attack roll or ability check succeeds or fails, or before the creature deals its damage. The creature is immune if it can't hear you or if it's immune to being {@condition charmed}.

subclassFeature.name: Magical Discoveries
entries: You learn two spells of your choice. These spells must come from the Cleric, Druid, or Wizard spell list. A spell you choose must be of a level for which you have spell slots.
You always have the chosen spells prepared, and they count as bard spells for you but don't count against the number of bard spells you can prepare.

subclassFeature.name: Peerless Skill
entries: When you make an ability check, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration. Roll a Bardic Inspiration die and add the number rolled to your ability check. You can choose to do so after you roll the die for the ability check, but before the DM tells you whether you succeed or fail.

subclassFeature.name: College of Valor
entries: Bards of the College of Valor are daring skalds whose tales keep alive the memory of the great heroes of the past, and thereby inspire a new generation of heroes.

subclassFeature.name: Martial Training
entries: You gain proficiency with martial weapons, medium armor, and shields.
You can use a simple or martial weapon as a spellcasting focus for your bard spells.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Inspiration
entries: A creature that has a Bardic Inspiration die from you can use it for one of the following effects.

entries.name: Defense
entries: When the creature is hit by an attack roll, that creature can use its reaction to roll the Bardic Inspiration die and add the number rolled to its AC against that attack, potentially causing the attack to miss.

entries.name: Offense
entries: Immediately after the creature hits a target with an attack roll, the creature can roll the Bardic Inspiration die and add the number rolled to the attack's damage against the target.

subclassFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.
In addition, you can cast one of your cantrips that has a casting time of an action in place of one of those attacks.

subclassFeature.name: Battle Magic
entries: After you cast a spell that has a casting time of an action, you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action.

subclassFeature.name: Life Domain
entries: The Life Domain focuses on the vibrant positive energy that sustains all life. Clerics who tap into this domain are masters of healing, using that life force to cure many hurts.

subclassFeature.name: Life Domain Spells
entries: Your connection to this divine domain ensures you always have certain spells ready. When you reach a Cleric level specified in the Life Domain Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

subclassFeature.name: Disciple of Life
entries: When a spell you cast with a spell slot restores hit points to a creature, that creature regains additional hit points on the turn you cast the spell. The additional hit points equal 2 plus the spell slot's level.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Preserve Life
entries: As an action, you present your holy symbol and expend a use of your Channel Divinity to evoke healing energy that can restore a number of hit points equal to five times your cleric level. Choose creatures within 30 feet of yourself, and divide those hit points among them. This feature can restore a creature to no more than half of its hit point maximum.

subclassFeature.name: Blessed Healer
entries: The healing spells you cast on others heal you as well. Immediately after you cast a spell with a spell slot that restores hit points to one creature other than you, you regain hit points equal to 2 plus the spell slot's level.

subclassFeature.name: Supreme Healing
entries: When you would normally roll one or more dice to restore hit points to a creature with a spell or Channel Divinity, don't roll those dice for the healing; instead use the highest number possible for each die.

subclassFeature.name: Light Domain
entries: The Light Domain emphasizes the divine power to bring about blazing fire and revelation. Clerics who wield this power are enlightened souls infused with radiance and the power of their deities' discerning vision, charged with chasing away lies and burning away darkness.

entries.subclassFeature: Channel Divinity: Radiance of the Dawn|Cleric|TGTT|Light|TGTT|3

subclassFeature.name: Warding Flare
entries: You can interpose divine light between yourself and an attacking enemy. When you are attacked by a creature within 30 feet of you that you can see, you can use your reaction to impose disadvantage on the attack roll, causing light to flare before the attacker before it hits or misses.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Radiance of the Dawn
entries: As an action, you present your holy symbol, and any magical darkness within 30 feet of you is dispelled. Additionally, each hostile creature within 30 feet of you must make a Constitution saving throw. A creature takes radiant damage equal to {@damage 2d10} + your cleric level on a failed saving throw, and half as much damage on a successful one. A creature that has total cover from you is not affected.

subclassFeature.name: Improved Warding Flare
entries: You can now use your Warding Flare feature when a creature that you can see within 30 feet of you attacks a creature other than you.
Additionally, when you use Warding Flare, you can give the target of the triggering attack a number of temporary hit points equal to {@dice 2d6} plus your Wisdom modifier.
You regain all expended uses of your Warding Flare when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Corona of Light
entries: As an action, you can activate an aura of sunlight that lasts for 1 minute or until you dismiss it using another action. You emit bright light in a 60-foot radius and dim light 30 feet beyond that. Your enemies in the bright light have disadvantage on saving throws against your Radiance of the Dawn feature and any spell that deals fire or radiant damage.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Trickery Domain
entries: The Trickery Domain offers magic of deception, illusion, and stealth. Clerics who wield this magic are a disruptive force in the world, puncturing pride, mocking tyrants, freeing captives, and flouting hollow traditions. They prefer subterfuge and pranks to direct confrontation.

subclassFeature.name: Blessing of the Trickster
entries: As an action, you can choose yourself or a willing creature you can see within 30 feet to have advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks. This blessing lasts until you finish a long rest or until you use this feature again.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Invoke Duplicity
entries: As a bonus action, you create a perfect illusion of yourself that lasts for 1 minute, or until you dismiss it (no action required). The illusion appears in an unoccupied space that you can see within 30 feet of you. As a bonus action on your turn, you can move the illusion up to 30 feet to a space you can see, but it must remain within 120 feet of you.

For the duration, you can cast spells as though you were in the illusion's space, but you must use your own senses. Additionally, when both you and your illusion are within 5 feet of a creature that can see the illusion, you have advantage on attack rolls against that creature, given how distracting the illusion is to the target.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Cloak of Shadows
entries: As an action, you become invisible until the end of your next turn. You become visible if you attack or cast a spell.

subclassFeature.name: Trickster's Transposition
entries: Whenever you use your bonus action to move the illusion of your Invoke Duplicity, you can teleport, swapping places with the illusion.

subclassFeature.name: Improved Duplicity
entries: You can create up to four duplicates of yourself, instead of one, when you use Invoke Duplicity. As a bonus action on your turn, you can move any number of them up to 30 feet, to a maximum range of 120 feet.
The illusion of your Invoke Duplicity has grown more powerful in the following ways:

items.name: Shared Distraction
entries: When you and your allies make attack rolls against a creature within 5 feet of the illusion, the attack rolls have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

items.name: Healing Illusion
entries: When the illusion ends, you or a creature of your choice within 5 feet of it regains a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Cleric level.

subclassFeature.name: War Domain
entries: War has many manifestations. Clerics who tap into the magic of the War Domain excel in battle, inspiring others to fight the good fight or offering acts of violence as prayers.

subclassFeature.name: War Priest
entries: As a bonus action, you can make one attack with a weapon or an unarmed strike. You can use this bonus action a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Guided Strike
entries: When you or a creature within 30 feet of you misses with an attack roll, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity and give that roll a +10 bonus, potentially causing it to hit. When you use this feature to benefit another creature's attack roll, you must use your reaction to do so.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: War God's Blessing
entries: You can use your Channel Divinity in the following additional ways:

items: When a creature within 30 feet of you makes an attack roll, you can use your reaction to grant that creature a +10 bonus to the roll. You make this choice after you see the roll, but before the DM says whether the attack hits or misses.

You can cast {@spell Shield of Faith} or {@spell Spiritual Weapon|PHB} without expending a spell slot or requiring concentration. When you cast either spell in this way, the spell lasts for 1 minute, but it ends early if you cast that spell again, have the {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} condition, or die.

subclassFeature.name: Avatar of Battle
entries: You gain resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks.

subclassFeature.name: Circle of the Zodiac
entries: {@i Harness Secrets Hidden in Constellations}

The Circle of the Zodiac has tracked heavenly patterns since time immemorial, discovering secrets hidden amid the constellations. By understanding these secrets, the Druids of this circle seek to harness the powers of the cosmos.

subclassFeature.name: Zodiac Form: Month
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape feature to take on a zodiac form rather than shape-shifting.

While in your zodiac form, you retain your game statistics, but your body becomes luminous, your joints glimmer like stars, and glowing lines connect them as on a star chart. This form sheds {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 10-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 10 feet. The form lasts for 10 minutes. It ends early if you dismiss it (no action required), have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, or use this feature again.
Whenever you assume your zodiac form, choose which of the following monthly constellations glimmers on your body; your choice gives you certain benefits while in the form.

subclassFeature.name: Beaver
entries: When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you is hit by an attack, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to reduce the damage it takes by an amount equal to your druid level + your proficiency bonus.

subclassFeature.name: Aurochs
entries: (Musimon, Heiðrún, Takam)
You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength checks and Strength saving throws, and you add your proficiency bonus to those rolls.
You also count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

subclassFeature.name: Horse
entries: (Arion, Sleipnir, Shabdiz)
Your walking speed is doubled, and you can take the {@action Dash} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Octopus
entries: While submerged in water, you can breathe normally, gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed, and suffer no penalties to attack rolls or vision.
Additionally, your melee reach increases by 5 feet while submerged.

subclassFeature.name: Peacock
entries: When a creature targets you directly with an attack or a harmful spell, it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC.
On a failed save, the creature must choose a new target or forfeit the attack or spell.
On a successful save, the creature is immune to this effect for 24 hours.

subclassFeature.name: Roc
entries: You can use your action to cast {@spell gust of wind} or {@spell warding wind|xge} without expending a spell slot.

subclassFeature.name: Bee
entries: When you activate this form, and as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on subsequent turns while it lasts, you can make a ranged spell attack against one creature within 60 feet. On a hit, the attack deals radiant damage equal to {@damage 1d8} + your Wisdom modifier.
The damage increases to {@damage 2d8} at 10th level and {@damage 3d8} at 14th level.

subclassFeature.name: Hound
entries: (Laelaps, Warg, Uridimmu)

As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can mark one creature you can see within 60 feet. The marked creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against you, and you always know its location while the form lasts.
You can have only one creature marked at a time.

subclassFeature.name: Cat
entries: You gain a +{@dice 1d4} bonus to your passive Perception.
Additionally, when you make a Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) check or a Dexterity ({@skill Stealth} or {@skill Acrobatics}) check, you can treat a roll of 7 or lower on the {@dice d20} as an 8.

subclassFeature.name: Griffon
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against being frightened.
Additionally, when you hit a creature with a melee attack using your action, you can make one additional melee attack as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Bulette
entries: Your Armor Class increases by half your proficiency bonus (rounded up).
You also gain a burrowing speed equal to half your walking speed.

subclassFeature.name: Phoenix
entries: If you are knocked unconscious while in this form and become stable through a successful {@skill Medicine} check or by succeeding on death saving throws, you regain hit points equal to {@dice 2d8} + your Wisdom modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Zodiac Form: Star Week
entries: Whenever you enter your Zodiac Form, you may choose one Month Constellation and one Star Week Constellation from the options below. You gain the benefits of both while the form lasts.

subclassFeature.name: Sequoia
entries: (Lotus Tree, Yggdrasil, Acacia).
You gain temporary hit points equal to your druid level, and resistance to the damage of the first successful attack made against you after entering the form.

subclassFeature.name: Unicorn
entries: Whenever you cast a spell using a spell slot that restores hit points to a creature, you or another creature within 30 feet regains hit points equal to {@dice 1d8} + your Wisdom modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Raven
entries: (Corvus, Huginn & Muninn, Nephthys Ravens).
You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on initiative rolls and can’t be surprised.
Additionally, once per turn when a creature you can see within 30 feet makes an attack roll, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on that roll.

subclassFeature.name: Kitsune
entries: (Temmusian Fox, Fenrir, Salawa).
When a creature misses you with an attack, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to teleport up to 15 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

subclassFeature.name: Hillstep Turtle
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Constitution saving throws, and you can ignore effects that would push, pull, or knock you prone.

subclassFeature.name: Owlbear
entries: Once on each of your turns, when you deal damage with a weapon attack or a spell, you deal additional force damage equal to your Wisdom modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Almiraj
entries: Once per turn when you miss with an attack roll or fail a saving throw, you can roll a {@dice d4} and add it to the roll.

subclassFeature.name: Bat
entries: You gain {@sense blindsight} out to 10 feet, and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} and darkness don’t impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on your {@skill Perception} checks.

subclassFeature.name: Pseudodragon
entries: When you make an Intelligence or Wisdom ability check, or a Constitution saving throw to maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB}, you can treat a roll of 9 or lower as a 10.

subclassFeature.name: Aurumvorax
entries: (Chrysomallos, Goldhorn, Sphinx).
You gain temporary hit points equal to your Wisdom modifier + your proficiency bonus.
You also have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) and Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) checks related to wealth, trade, or ancient lore.

subclassFeature.name: Salmon
entries: Difficult terrain doesn’t cost you extra movement.
Additionally, once while in this form, when you fail a saving throw, you can choose to succeed instead. When you do, the form immediately ends.

subclassFeature.name: Lizard
entries: Whenever you cast a spell using a spell slot that restores hit points to a creature, you or another creature within 30 feet regains hit points equal to {@dice 1d8} + your Wisdom modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Full Zodiac
entries: At the start of each of your turns while in your Zodiac Form, you can change which constellation glimmers on your body as a free action without consuming a wild shape use.

subclassFeature.name: Wild Magic
entries: Your innate magic comes from the wild forces of chaos that underlie the order of creation. This chaotic magic churns within you, waiting for any outlet.

subclassFeature.name: Spell Breaker
entries: You always have the {@spell Counterspell|TGTT} and {@spell Dispel Magic} spells prepared. In addition, you can cast {@spell Dispel Magic} as a bonus action, and you can add your proficiency bonus to its ability check.
When you cast either spell with a spell slot, that slot isn't expended if the spell fails to stop a spell.

subclassFeature.name: Maestro Principiante
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you gain the following features:

entries.name: Divisi
entries: You excel in harmonizing with the rhythm of combat, syncing your actions seamlessly. Upon rolling initiative, you can perform a Divisi, rolling twice and retaining both results. At the beginning of each combat round, you have the choice to utilize either roll to establish your initiative for that round.

entries.name: Baton Mastery
entries: You can cast spells through movement alone. When you cast a bard spell that requires a somatic component, you can cast it without providing verbal or material components (unless the component has a specified cost or is consumed by the spell).

entries.name: Non Sequitur
entries: You conduct your allies' actions to harmonize with the shifting battlefield. When you give a Bardic Inspiration die to a creature, they can use their reaction to move up to their speed and make an attack or cast a cantrip they know that only affects one creature.

subclassFeature.name: Adagio
entries: Starting at 6th level, you can use your musical prowess to disrupt your enemies' tempo in the midst of battle.

As a bonus action, expend one Bardic Inspiration die to select a number of creatures you can see, up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one), within 60 feet. The chosen creatures must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC.
On a failure, they are affected by the {@spell slow} spell until they succeed on a saving throw at the end of their turn or until your concentration ends.
The target can attempt the saving throw at the end of each of its turns to end the effect prematurely on a success.

subclassFeature.name: Prestissimo
entries: Starting at 14th level, as a bonus action, you can start conducting an accelerating symphony of combat that increases the tempo of your allies' actions. This symphony lasts for 1 minute or until you're {@condition restrained|tgtt} or {@condition incapacitated|tgtt}.

Choose a number of creatures you can see and that can see you within 60 feet of you, up to a number equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one). For the duration, the creatures are under the effect of the {@spell haste} spell, including all after-effects.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.
In addition, whenever a creature affected by the symphony takes a reaction as part of your {@italic Non Sequitur} feature, their movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: When you join the College of The Jester at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with the {@skill Performance} skill, and can choose an additional skill to be proficient in between {@skill Acrobatics} and {@skill Persuasion}.

subclassFeature.name: Jester's Acts
entries: At 3rd level, you gain access to a selection of {@filter Jester's Acts|optionalfeatures|feature type=JA} that allow you to perform specific tricks during your performance.

You can choose three different Jester's Acts, and you gain an additional one at 6th level and 14th level. You can change a chosen act with another when you reach these levels, as well as part of your Bardic Versatility feature.
When a saving throw for an act is required, the DC is equal to 8 + your Performance skill bonus.

subclassFeature.name: Jester's Acts Options
entries: The following options are available to you when you choose your Jester's Acts at 3rd level, and when you gain additional acts at 6th and 14th level.

subclassFeature.name: Gifted Acrobat
entries: At 6th level, your skill with acrobatic methods and tricks increases considerably. You gain the following features:

items: You gain climbing speed equal to your walking speed.
You can attempt to escape a {@condition grappled|TGTT|grapple} as a bonus action.
When you're {@condition prone}, you can stand up by spending 10 feet of movement, rather than half your speed.

subclassFeature.name: Unparalleled Skill
entries: Jesters have to be the best at what they do, and they do a lot.
Also at 6th level, choose one of your skill proficiencies. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses the chosen proficiency.

subclassFeature.name: Jester's Privilege
entries: At 14th level, you can invoke the ancient right of Jester's Privilege.
When you use your bardic inspiration, you can make a Performance check. Creatures within 60 feet of you that can hear you must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC equal to your Performance check result).

On a failed save, they are {@condition charmed} by you until the end of your next turn. While charmed, they are compelled to prevent any harm from coming to you by whatever means necessary, regardless of any actions taken by you or your allies.
Once you use this ability, you cannot use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Lucid Insight
entries: By the time you officially join the College of Surrealism at 3rd level, you have sharpened your mind further than the average person. When you roll for a Wisdom saving throw you may add your Charisma modifier to the result.

subclassFeature.name: Warped Reality
entries: At 3rd level, warping another's mind requires finesse. As a bonus action, spend 1 Bardic Inspiration die and choose a creature within 60 feet of you. Roll a {@dice d6} and refer to the Warped Reality Table to determine the condition affecting your target and roll your bardic inspiration die to determine the number of rounds the effect will last.
The target can attempt a Charisma saving throw at the end of each of its turns to end the effect prematurely on a successful saving throw.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short rest.

entries.caption: Warped Reality Table
rows: {@bold Warped Sight.} Creating a makeshift vision that completely hides the surroundings from the creature, it is {@condition blinded} as per the {@spell blindness/deafness} spell.
{@bold Warped Mind.} Seeping deep into the creature's mind, you create a lively nightmare, convincing the creature it is {@condition frightened} of you or a creature of your choice.

{@bold Warped Smell.} Your power to mess with others' minds and senses comes to your aid as you create the most alluring smell. A creature of your choice is now completely {@condition charmed}. On its turn, the creature is compelled only to use all its movement to advance towards you in the shortest path possible, invoking opportunity attacks if possible.

{@bold Warped Hearing.} Your ability to bend one's sense of reality has given you the ability to seemingly mute a creature's surroundings. The creature is now {@condition deafened} as per the {@spell blindness/deafness} spell.
{@bold Warped Sense of Space.} Forcing mental constraints on your chosen creature, you make it believe it is physically {@condition incapacitated|tgtt}.

{@bold Warped Physical State.} Convincing illusions are as powerful as a knife to the throat. A creature you choose fully believes it has no functioning legs, whatever the reason is, and is rendered {@condition prone}.

subclassFeature.name: Canvas of the Mind
entries: Starting at 6th level, you harness the ethereal energies of the Dreamtime to shape illusions and perceptions on the battlefield.

With a flick of your fingers, you designate an area within 30 feet of you, creating a zone in whichever orientation, henceforth referred to as the "Canvas". The Canvas can be sized as a rectangle sized 40×30, 30×30, or 20×30 feet big.

You may choose an ally to join you in creating within the Canvas. The ally must be within the Canvas borders, visible only to them and you, and must remain within it to assist you. If the ally leaves the Canvas, they can no longer help you and you may not invite a different ally until the next use of this ability.

Enemies may leave and enter the Canvas, affected by it only when encompassed by it. Enemies may see the shimmering of the Canvas on a successful {@skill Perception} check, with the DC equaling 10 + your Charisma modifier.

On each of your individual turns, both you and your chosen ally can influence the Canvas by expending Bardic Inspiration die from your dice pool as an action, to weave a subtle distortion of reality. This distortion can take one of the following forms:

entries.name: Illusion of Strength
entries: {@bold Boon}: By infusing the illusion with a sense of unmatched strength, your target gains a {@dice 1d6} bonus on their next attack roll. The dreamlike confidence empowers them to strike with unparalleled force.

{@bold Bane}: Alternatively, you can project an illusion onto a target, making them perceive themselves as physically weaker. This imposes a {@dice 1d6} penalty on their next attack roll as doubt and insecurity cloud their actions.

entries.name: Ephemeral Resilience
entries: {@bold Boon}: Your touch infuses your target with a fleeting sense of invulnerability. They gain a {@dice 1d6} bonus to their AC until your next turn as the Dreamtime shields them momentarily from harm.

{@bold Bane}: Instilling your target with the sudden realization they aren't as resilient as they once thought, imposing a {@dice 1d4} penalty to their AC until your next turn, making them easier to hit.

entries.name: Whispers of Fleetness
entries: {@bold Boon}: Your target gains the illusionary perception of enhanced speed. You increase their movement speed by 15 feet until the start of your next turn.
{@bold Bane}: Your target is made to feel a sudden sluggishness, reducing their movement speed by half until the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Kinetic Mastery
entries: {@bold Boon}: You shape the Dreamtime to instill a heightened sense of control in your target. They feel an enhanced connection with their own body, gaining advantage on Wisdom saving throws until the start of your next turn.

{@bold Bane}: You project an illusion that disrupts your target's sense of bodily control. This feeling of disconnection imposes disadvantage on Wisdom saving throws until the start of your next turn.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Guiding Whispers
entries: Starting at 14th level, you've become a master empath. As an action, you can choose a single creature and manipulate their feelings as you see fit.

Surging reassurance, confidence, and pride, you convince the creature their next attack will hit, and thus provide them with advantage. Forcing self doubt, incompetence, and fear, you convince the creature their next attack will fail, and so it has a disadvantage on its next attack roll.
The target must be within 90 feet range and visible to your eyes. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.
In addition, you gain complete control over your {@italic Canvas of the Mind} feature. Whenever you use your Bardic Inspiration whilst inside the Canvas, you do so as a bonus action.

subclassFeature.name: Beauty Domain
entries: Beauty is a concept that is interpreted differently by every culture, creed, and individual. For some it is an objective standard, unchanging and permanent. For others, true beauty must be fleeting and temporary to be appreciated.
The beautiful can be used to uplift and inspire, but it can also be used to twist, control, and manipulate the naive.

Clerics of Beauty recognize that most things are beautiful in their own way, and will go to great lengths to avoid bloodshed. When compelled to fight, Beauty clerics look to end conflicts quickly and decisively with powerful enchantment magic.

entries.subclassFeature: Channel Divinity: All Eyes on Me|Cleric|TGTT|Beauty|TGTT|3

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Cantrip
entries: When you adopt this Divine Domain at 3rd level, you learn the {@spell friends} cantrip. It counts as a cleric cantrip for you, but it doesn't count against your total number of Cantrips Known.
When you cast {@spell friends} targeting a creature that is already friendly towards you, they do not realize they have been charmed and don't become hostile when the spell ends.

subclassFeature.name: Beautiful Distraction
entries: Beginning at 3rd level, you can distract others with your beauty. As a reaction, when a creature within 60 feet that you can see is hit by an attack, you attempt to draw attention to yourself with your beauty, making the attacker reroll his attack.
Once you use this feature, you must complete a short or long rest before you can use it again.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: All Eyes on Me
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you can use your Channel Divinity to force creatures to focus on you regardless of anything else around them. As an action, present your holy symbol and force up to 5 creatures of your choice who can see you within 30 feet to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, their attention is focused on you, giving them disadvantage on attack rolls, ability checks and saving throws until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Blinding Beauty
entries: Beginning at 6th level, when you use your Channel Divinity to activate {@i All Eyes on Me}, creatures who fail their saves are {@condition blinded} in addition to the regular effect of the ability. Furthermore, the effect lasts until the end of your next turn, instead of the start of it.

subclassFeature.name: Potent Spellcasting
entries: Starting at 8th level, you add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

subclassFeature.name: Heavenly Beauty
entries: You have become an avatar of celestial beauty. Your enemies attack with uncertainty, fearful of damaging the wondrous being they behold. Upon reaching 17th level, when a creature targets you with an attack, it must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it must choose another target within range to attack instead. If there is not available target then their attack misses or fails, as they are overwhelmed by your beauty.

subclassFeature.name: Beauty Domain Spells
entries: You gain domain spells at the cleric levels listed in the Beauty Domain Spells table. Once you gain a domain spell, you always have it prepared, and it doesn't count against the number of spells you can prepare each day.

subclassFeature.name: Blood Domain
entries: The domain of Blood represents the vital essence of life itself, but also the power that can be drawn from its spilling. Clerics who follow deities of blood understand that life and death are intertwined, and that through bloodshed, great power can be achieved.

These clerics often serve dark gods of sacrifice, war, and vampirism, though some follow deities of life who understand that blood is the source of vitality. They wield weapons that draw blood and use necromantic magic to manipulate the life force of their enemies.

Clerics of the Blood Domain are comfortable on the battlefield, wielding martial weapons and dark magic in equal measure. They understand that to draw blood is to touch the essence of mortality itself.

subclassFeature.name: Darkness Domain
entries: The domain of Darkness represents the night, shadows, and the unknown depths where light cannot reach. Clerics who serve deities of darkness understand that the night is not merely the absence of light, but a primordial force with power of its own.

These clerics may serve gods of night, shadows, secrets, or the void itself. They embrace the darkness and learn to see through it, using shadow magic to confound and terrify their enemies. Some serve benevolent deities who protect travelers in the night, while others follow more sinister powers that dwell in eternal shadow.
Darkness clerics are masters of shadow magic and fear, able to extinguish light and shroud themselves in protective darkness. They see clearly where others stumble blind.

subclassFeature.name: Lust Domain
entries: The domain of Lust represents desire, passion, and the magnetic pull between souls. Clerics who serve deities of lust understand that desire is one of the most fundamental forces that drives mortal behavior, capable of inspiring great acts or leading to ruin.

These clerics may serve gods of love, beauty, passion, or temptation. They wield enchantment magic that can influence hearts and minds, drawing others to their will through charisma and supernatural allure. While some use this power to help others find connection and happiness, others manipulate desires for darker purposes.
Lust clerics are masters of enchantment and persuasion, able to read the deepest desires of those around them and exploit those yearnings to achieve their goals.

subclassFeature.name: Madness Domain
entries: The domain of Madness represents the fractured mind, chaos, and the breakdown of reason. Clerics who serve deities of madness have gazed into the abyss and been forever changed, their minds twisted by contact with incomprehensible truths and alien thoughts.

These clerics serve eldritch gods from beyond reality, deities of chaos and insanity, or dark powers that exist in the spaces between reason and nightmare. They can project their shattered thoughts outward, inflicting confusion and terror upon their enemies. Some were driven mad through their devotion, while others sought out madness as a path to hidden knowledge.
Madness clerics are unpredictable and dangerous, wielding psychic magic that fractures the minds of their foes and speaking words laced with chaos.

subclassFeature.name: Time Domain
entries: The domain of Time represents the flow of moments, the inevitability of change, and the power to manipulate the river of causality. Clerics who serve deities of time understand that the present is merely a fleeting instant between past and future, and that those with sufficient power can bend that flow.

These clerics may serve gods of fate, destiny, history, or the passage of ages. They can slow or hasten the flow of time, glimpse possible futures, and alter the sequence of events. Some use this power to ensure favorable outcomes, while others seek to preserve the proper flow of time against those who would disrupt it.
Time clerics are masters of temporal manipulation, able to reorder initiative, speed allies, slow enemies, and react with supernatural swiftness.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: When you adopt this Divine Domain at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with all martial weapons that deal slashing and piercing damage, as clerics of the Blood Domain are familiar with implements of all sorts that draw blood.

subclassFeature.name: Blood Domain Spells
entries: You gain domain spells at the cleric levels listed in the Blood Domain Spells table. Once you gain a domain spell, you always have it prepared, and it doesn't count against the number of spells you can prepare each day.

subclassFeature.name: Blood Affinity
entries: Starting at 3rd level, when you draw blood you can use your dark arts to enhance yourself. You can use your reaction to draw on the blood, giving yourself advantage on your next attack roll against a creature you already harmed and that is not at full health. In addition, when you roll damage for such an attack, you can roll the damage dice twice and take the larger outcome. You can use this reaction a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Blood Curse
entries: At 3rd level, you gain access to this Channel Divinity. As an action, you can curse a target within 60 feet, as long as it has blood in its veins and you have damaged it in the past. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it is {@condition restrained|tgtt} for 1 minute as its blood congeals in its body. It can repeat this saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

subclassFeature.name: Sanguine Boost
entries: Starting at 6th level, the magic of your Blood Affinity grows in power. When using your reaction to activate Blood Affinity, choose up to 5 creatures; they gain a single use of the same reaction Blood Affinity provides you, with the same benefits and restrictions.

subclassFeature.name: Divine Strike
entries: Beginning at 8th level, your god imbues your weapon attacks with their divine wrath. Once on each of your turns, when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 necrotic damage to the target. When you reach 14th level, the extra damage increases to 2d8.

subclassFeature.name: Vampiric Mastery
entries: Your god has shown you the dark techniques necessary to draw power from death. Starting at 17th level, when you kill a creature with a necromancy spell of 2nd-level or higher, you can use your reaction to regain one of your expended spell slots. The spell slot you recover must be at least one level lower than the spell you cast to kill the creature.

subclassFeature.name: Eyes of Night
entries: When you adopt this Divine Domain at 3rd level, your god grants you enhanced sight so you may better serve in the shadows. You gain darkvision out to a distance of 90 feet. If you already have darkvision, its range increases to 90 feet. Your darkvision allows you to see in magical, as well as mundane, darkness, and you see in darkness as in bright light.

subclassFeature.name: Darkness Domain Spells
entries: You gain domain spells at the cleric levels listed in the Darkness Domain Spells table. Once you gain a domain spell, you always have it prepared, and it doesn't count against the number of spells you can prepare each day.

entries.caption: Darkness Domain Spells
rows: {@spell arms of hadar}, {@spell wall of darkness|tgtt}

subclassFeature.name: Shroud of Darkness
entries: Starting at 3rd level, when a creature that you can see within 30 feet targets you with an attack, you can use your reaction to cause wisps of darkness to billow around you, imposing disadvantage on their attack roll. Creatures that can see through magical darkness are immune to this feature. You can use this reaction a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Cloying Darkness
entries: At 3rd level, you can use your Channel Divinity to force darkness on the world. As an action, you can grasp your holy symbol and conjure a sphere of darkness. Light sources within 30 feet are extinguished, and magical lights produced by a spell of a level equal to half your cleric level or lower are dispelled. In addition, creatures of your choice within 30 feet must make a Constitution saving throw, taking necrotic damage equal to 2d10 + your cleric level on a failure and half on a success.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Night Terrors
entries: Starting at 6th level, you can also use your Channel Divinity to bring the terror of night to your enemies. As an action on your turn, you may conjure a cloud of darkness with a 10-foot radius, that is centered on a creature within 30 feet that you can see. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, it takes 8d4 psychic damage and becomes frightened (as per the {@spell fear} spell) of the darkness around it. On a success, it only takes half as much damage and is not frightened. This effect lasts for 1 minute, and the target may make a Wisdom saving throw at the end of its turn to end this effect. You can use your bonus action at the start of your turn to move the cloud of darkness to the position of the creature you targeted. You cannot use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Potent Spellcasting
entries: Your dark god infuses your magic with the power of eldritch darkness. Starting at 8th level, you add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

subclassFeature.name: Night Supreme
entries: Upon reaching 17th level, you can momentarily bring true darkness onto the world. As an action, you summon magical darkness that emanates from you in a 60-foot radius. Creatures of your choice within the aura have disadvantage on saving throws against spells and effects that deal cold or necrotic damage, or cause the frightened condition. This aura of darkness lasts for 1 minute, until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or choose to end it as an action on your turn.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: When you choose this Divine Domain at 3rd level, your god has imbued you with magnetic charisma that tempts all who meet you. You gain proficiency in the Deception and Persuasion skills if you don't already have them.

subclassFeature.name: Lust Domain Spells
entries: You gain domain spells at the cleric levels listed in the Lust Domain Spells table. Once you gain a domain spell, you always have it prepared, and it doesn't count against the number of spells you can prepare each day.

subclassFeature.name: Deepest Desires
entries: Beginning at 3rd level, you know how to extract the deepest desires of those around you. By asking a creature who can hear and understand you within 10 feet of you what they desire most, you can compel them to make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, the target eagerly tells you what they most desire. Creatures who've failed this save have disadvantage on saving throws against enchantment spells you cast for the next 24 hours. On a success, or if the target creature lacks desires, the target ignores the question. Either way, the target does not perceive the question as magical. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1).

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Impulsive Infatuation
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you can use your Channel Divinity to overwhelm a creature with a flash of intense admiration for you, driving them to rash action in your defense. As an action, present your holy symbol and force one creature you can see within 30 feet to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is charmed by you until the start of your next turn, and it must immediately use its reaction to make a weapon attack against a target you designate. If there are no valid targets, it uses its reaction to admire you.

subclassFeature.name: Enchanting Presence
entries: Your god has blessed you with an otherworldly elegance that disarms those who behold you. Beginning at 6th level, when you cast an enchantment spell of 1st-level or higher and the target is within 5 feet and can see you, you can impose disadvantage on their saving throw against that spell. Once this feature causes a target to fail their saving throw, you must finish a short or long rest before you can use it again.

subclassFeature.name: Potent Spellcasting
entries: Starting at 8th level, you add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

subclassFeature.name: Supplicant of the Flesh
entries: At 17th level, you have become the embodiment of lust, and other creatures find it difficult to deny your whims, even when you cause them harm. Dealing damage to a creature charmed by you never ends the condition, nor allows the creature to make additional saving throws to end the condition.

subclassFeature.name: Shattered Mind
entries: When you adopt this Divine Domain at 3rd level, your mental state is irrevocably altered by the twisted power of your god. You gain resistance to psychic damage and become immune to having your thoughts read—a creature attempting to read your mind suffers 1d8 psychic damage. You are also unable to gain the benefit of any spell or ability that allows for mental communication.

subclassFeature.name: Words of Chaos
entries: You can also project the chaos within your mind into the world through your twisted words. At 3rd level, you learn {@spell vicious mockery}. It counts as a cleric spell for you, but it doesn't count against your total number of Cantrips Known.

subclassFeature.name: Madness Domain Spells
entries: You gain domain spells at the cleric levels listed in the Madness Domain Spells table. Once you gain a domain spell, you always have it prepared, and it doesn't count against the number of spells you can prepare each day.

entries.caption: Madness Domain Spells
rows: {@spell vicious mockery}, {@spell dissonant whispers}, {@spell hideous laughter}

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Touch of Madness
entries: At 3rd level, you gain this additional Channel Divinity option. As an action on your turn, you can grasp your holy symbol and force a creature within 30 feet to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the madness of your god infects their mind, and they are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} for up to 1 minute. While {@condition incapacitated|tgtt}, they spend their turn babbling incoherently and use their movement to move in a random direction. At the end of each turn, the creature can repeat the saving throw, ending this effect on a successful save.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Paranoia
entries: At 6th level, you gain this additional Channel Divinity option. As an action, you present your holy symbol towards a creature of your choosing within 30 feet of you and invoke the name of your deity. The creature must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, it becomes frightened (as per the {@spell fear} spell) of the nearest visible creature within 30 feet of it. For the next minute, at the start of its turn, the affected creature takes 2d4 psychic damage and the focus of its paranoia switches to the nearest visible creature within 30 feet. The effect ends if there are no creatures visible to the target within 30 feet of it at the start of its turn. At the end of each turn, the creature can repeat the saving throw, ending this effect on a successful save.

subclassFeature.name: Potent Spellcasting
entries: The madness festering within your own mind empowers your cantrips. Starting at 8th level, you can add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

subclassFeature.name: Mantle of Insanity
entries: You are a conduit for Madness to enter the world. Beginning at 17th level, when a creature that you can see within 60 feet of you attacks, you can use your reaction to force them to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, they attack a target of your choice within the range of their movement. You can use this reaction a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Chronological Interference
entries: Beginning at 3rd level, you can exert certain control over time, changing the order in which events transpire, albeit minimally. As a bonus action on your turn, you can choose two creatures who have not yet taken their turn this round; switch the order in which they act, each taking the other’s place in the initiative order. This change is now the new initiative order. You can use this bonus action a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Right on Time
entries: Also starting at 3rd level, you can tweak time to make sure you react faster to danger. You can add your Wisdom modifier to your initiative roll.

subclassFeature.name: Time Domain Spells
entries: You gain domain spells at the cleric levels listed in the Time Domain Spells table. Once you gain a domain spell, you always have it prepared, and it doesn't count against the number of spells you can prepare each day.

entries.caption: Time Domain Spells
rows: *gift of alacrity<sup>†</sup>, feather fall, fortune’s favor<sup>†</sup>, immovable object<sup>†</sup>*

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Temporal Manipulation
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you can use your Channel Divinity to manipulate the flow of time to aid or hamper others in combat. When a creature you can see within 60 feet uses their action in a way that requires them to roll a d20, you can use your reaction to grant them advantage (by speeding up time around them), or disadvantage (by slowing it) on their action. This reaction requires a use of your Channel Divinity.

subclassFeature.name: Eyes of the Future Past
entries: Beginning at 6th level, you gain the power to see through time itself. As a bonus action on your turn, you can activate this ability - once you do, it will stay active until you dismiss it (no action required), up to 1 minute. Activating this ability turns your eyes golden and your pupils into hourglasses, and allows you to look forward or backwards in time (as per your choice upon activation). For each round this ability is active, you can choose to see your surroundings 1 hour further into the past of future. starting with the second round, you can choose to keep looking at the same hour, a round into the past or future. While using this ability, you are under the {@condition blinded} condition with respect to everything happening in the present around you. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses of it when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Potent Spellcasting
entries: Starting at 8th level, you add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

subclassFeature.name: Temporal Mastery
entries: Upon reaching 17th level, your command of time and its effects becomes unrivaled. You gain the following benefits: * You add the spells *time stop* and *time ravage*<sup>†</sup> to your list of domain spells. Like your other domain spells, they are always prepared and count as cleric spells for you. * You can control how time affects you. At the end of a long rest, you may choose to make yourself 1 year younger or older. * If a magical ability affects your age, you can choose to be unaffected by it.

subclassFeature.name: Summoner’s Magic
entries: Your fiendish heritage grants you the ability to summon creatures with ease. When your Spellcasting feature lets you learn or replace a sorcerer cantrip or a sorcerer spell of 1st level or higher, you can choose the new spell from any spell list, provided the spell summons, conjures, or creates creatures.
