You must otherwise obey all the restrictions for selecting the spell, and it becomes a sorcerer spell for you.

subclassFeature.name: Summoned Ferocity
entries: The essence of the fiendish realms enhances your summoning abilities. Starting 1st level, when you cast a spell that summons a creature, you can add your Charisma Modifier to the creature's attack rolls, skill checks and saving throws. Additionally, you can communicate telepathically with any creature you summon while it is within 120 feet of you.

subclassFeature.name: Infernal Companion
entries: Also at 1st level, you learn the *find familiar* spell and can cast it as a ritual. When you summon your familiar, it takes the form of a small fiend (imp, quasit, or other appropriate fiendish creature), but its behavior reflects your alignment. Your familiar can use the {@action Help|TGTT} action as a bonus action.

subclassFeature.name: Hellish Summoner
entries: Starting 6th level, when you summon creatures using a spell, you can spend 2 sorcery points to strengthen their bond with you. When you do so, the following benefits apply: You have advantage on any checks to maintain control over the summoned creatures, and to maintain concentration on the summoning spell. The summoned creatures gain temporary hit points equal to your sorcerer level. As a bonus action, you can swap places with any of your summoned creatures.

subclassFeature.name: Dark Dominion
entries: At 14th level, you gain the ability to exert control over summoned creatures through dark means. As a bonus action, choose a summoned creature (any creature who was summoned by a spell) within 30 feet of you that you can see. The creature must succeed on a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC or be charmed by you for 1 minute. You can spend 2 sorcery points to impose disadvantage on the saving throw. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all uses after a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Summoned Aggression
entries: Also at 14th level, when you cast a spell that summons creatures, the summoned creatures can make one extra attack as part of their attack action.

subclassFeature.name: Infernal Legion
entries: When you reach 18th level, your infernal heritage pushes your summoning prowess to a peak. When you cast a summoning spell that creates multiple creatures, you can summon double the number of creatures. Additionally, when you cast a conjuration spell to summon creatures that requires concentration, you can treat it as if it doesn't require concentration. Once you use the second aspect of this ability, you must take a long rest before using it again.

subclassFeature.name: Heroic Spells
entries: Your heroic soul grants you access to spells that embody the essence of legends. The Heroic Spells table shows the spells you can learn. When your Spellcasting feature lets you learn or replace a sorcerer cantrip or a sorcerer spell of 1st level or higher, you can choose the new spell from the Heroic Spells table instead of the sorcerer spell list.

entries.caption: Heroic Spells
rows: *bane*, *bless*, *command*, *compelled duel*, *divine favor*, *heroism*, *protection from evil and good*, *sanctuary*, *shield of faith*
*aid*, *branding smite*, *find steed*, *magic weapon*, *spiritual weapon*, *warding bond*, *zone of truth*
*aura of vitality*, *beacon of hope*, *crusader's mantle*, *death ward*, *spirit guardians*, *spirit shroud*
*aura of life*, *aura of purity*, *death ward*, *find greater steed*, *guardian of faith*, *stoneskin*

subclassFeature.name: Over Soul
entries: Also at 1st level, your heroic soul allows you to channel the power of legends. As a bonus action, you can spend 1 sorcery point to gain advantage on your next attack roll, ability check, or saving throw. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all uses after a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Legendary Weapon
entries: Also at 1st level, when you manifest your weapon through the Over Soul feature, you can modify its form in unique ways as part of the bonus action you use to summon the weapon, or as a separate bonus action on your turn.
Choose one of the following properties:

items.name: Ancient's Reach
items.entry: The weapon's reach extends by 5 feet. When you choose this option you can spend sorcery points to extend the reach by 5 more feet for every sorcery point you spend.

items.name: Colossal Might
items.entry: Your manifested weapon increases in size and mass. The weapon deals additional damage equal to half your proficiency bonus (rounded down), and you have advantage on Strength (Athletics) checks to grapple, shove, or break objects while wielding it.

items.name: Hero's Return
items.entry: Your manifested weapon gains the {@itemProperty T|XPHB|thrown} property (20/60). If it already has the thrown property, its range is doubled. When you throw the weapon and hit a target, it instantly returns to your hand after the attack.

items.name: Rending Strike
items.entry: You imbue your manifested weapon with destructive force. When you hit a creature with your manifested weapon, you can force it to make a Strength saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier). On a failure, the creature is knocked prone or pushed 10 feet away (your choice). When you choose this option you can spend sorcery points to extend the push distance by 5 more feet for every sorcery point you spend.

items.name: Hero's Flame
items.entry: Your manifested weapon bursts into radiant or fiery light. The weapon sheds bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for another 30 feet. Additionally, once per turn when you hit with this weapon, you can deal an additional 1d6 radiant or fire damage (your choice).

items.name: Heroic Versatility
items.entry: You can add or remove one standard weapon property to your manifested weapon.

subclassFeature.name: Hero's Reflex
entries: At 6th level, when you cast a spell that requires an attack roll or forces a saving throw, you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

In addition, when you hit a creature with a weapon attack made on your bonus action using the weapon granted by your Over Soul feature, you gain temporary hit points equal to the spell slot's level + your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1).

subclassFeature.name: Manifest Legend
entries: Starting at 14th level, you can enter a heightened state of ancestral power. As an action, you can expend 3 sorcery points to gain the following benefits for 1 minute:

items: You gain temporary hit points equal to your sorcerer level.
You have advantage on weapon attack rolls made with your manifested weapon.
You can attack twice, instead of once, when you take the Attack action on your turn.
Your weapon deals an additional 1d6 radiant damage on a hit.

entries: Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Eternal Hero
entries: At 18th level, you are a perfect vessel for your heroic ancestor's power. Your Over Soul feature is always active, including both manifestations, without requiring an action or sorcery points to maintain.

If you are attuned to a magic weapon or armor, you can spend a short rest passing its magic onto the corresponding manifestation. The manifestation can only hold the magic of one item, and if you lose your attunement to the item, the manifestation loses the magical abilities gained in this process.
In addition, when you drop to 0 hit points, you can instead drop to 1 hit point and immediately regain hit points equal to your sorcerer level + your Charisma modifier.
Once you use this feature to drop to 1 hit point, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Improved Familiar
entries: At 3rd level, You learn the {@spell Find Familiar} spell. If you already know it, you instead learn another 1st level Wizard spell of your choice. You don't need to provide material components for the Find Familiar spell.
Any beast with a Challenge Rating of 0 you summoned through the Find Familiar spell has the following benefits:

items: The familiar takes its turn on your initiative alongside you.
The familiar's intelligence score equals 8 + Your proficiency bonus.
The familiar knows all of the languages you know, and is able to speak in any language it knows.
The familiar's AC, saves, and skills with which it is proficient are increased by your proficiency bonus.
The familiar's Maximum HP equals 3 times your wizard level.

You can dismiss your familiar as a bonus action. In addition, if your familiar is dismissed, you can use a bonus action to summon it for a single turn - you cause it to instantly reappear in any unoccupied space within 30 feet of you, and it is dismissed again at the end of your turn.

subclassFeature.name: Wizard’s Apprentice
entries: On your 6th level, Your familiar can cast any wizard cantrip you know. Once it casts a cantrip this way, it can't do so again for 1d4 rounds.

When your familiar casts a spell, it can perform the necessary verbal components with its mouth, the somatic components with its limbs, and it doesn't need material components as long as they don't have a gold-piece value. It uses your Spell Attack Bonus and Spell Save DC.
In addition, your familiar can store small items in its pocket dimension by holding the items while it is dismissed.
The familiar can store a total of 20 pounds of items in its pocket dimension, and each item must be at most 1 foot on a side and weigh no more than 3 pounds.
The familiar may carry an item of your choice out of the pocket dimension when you call it. It may only carry one item this way. You may allow the familiar to choose the item on its own.
If your familiar is dismissed while carrying items that are too large or heavy to fit inside the pocket dimension, the items stay behind while your familiar is dismissed into the pocket dimension.
The familiar can't maintain concentration or take actions while in its pocket dimension.

subclassFeature.name: Shared Senses
entries: After you reach 10th level, When your familiar is within 100 feet of you, you can switch between using your own senses and your familiar's without spending actions. Using your familiar's senses works as described in the Find Familiar spell.
In addition, when your familiar is within 100 feet of you, you can cast spells through your familiar as if you were located in its location.

subclassFeature.name: Tiny Wizard
entries: You taught your familiar to cast spells through its telepathic connection with you.

When your familiar is within 100 feet of you and you aren't {@condition incapacitated|tgtt}, it can cast a spell of 4th level or lower that you have prepared by expending one of your spell slots of the same level or higher (with your consent). The rules for familiar spellcasting are the same as described in this order's 6th level feature.

subclassFeature.name: Expanded Spell List
entries: The Power of the Horror lets you choose from an expanded list of spells when you learn a warlock spell. The following spells are added to the warlock spell list for you.

subclassFeature.name: Devastating Strike
entries: At 1st level, you can use your action to make a Devastating Strike against your opponent, as you are channeling your fiendish side to enhance your body.

Make an unarmed strike against your opponent. You can roll a d8 in place of the normal damage of your unarmed strike. If your attack hits, you can push the creature up to 15 feet away from you in a straight line.

Your fiendish side stays more pronounced for a while, granting you a thicker skin and more pronounced muscles. Until the start of your next turn, as long as you are not wearing any armor, your Armor Class equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Constitution modifier.
The damage and maximum pushing distance increase as you gain warlock levels, as shown in the Devastating Strike table.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (a minimum of once). You regain any expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Lone Survivor
entries: At 6th level, if you have no allies within 30 feet of you, you become immune to the frightened condition.

subclassFeature.name: Unearthly Manifestation
entries: At 6th level, your abnormal physique is getting more foothold in the world. You gain proficiency in Constitution saving throws. Furthermore, your Devastating Strike is now considered a magical attack.

subclassFeature.name: Degenerating Touch
entries: At 10th level, when you hit an opponent with an unarmed strike, you can corrupt them with fiendish energy.
If they fail a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC, you can degrade one of their Immunities / Resistances until the end of your next turn.
Degraded Immunity becomes a resistance, degraded resistance becomes nothing.

subclassFeature.name: Imploding Infestation
entries: At 14th level, you can utilize your fiendish nature to its utmost potential, spreading carnage and destruction on a whim.
When you hit a target with an unarmed strike you can transfer fiendish essence into it that begins to grow.
That creature becomes infected until the end of your next turn.
At the end of your next turn, the infected creature can no longer contain the essence and implodes violently, taking 6d12 necrotic damage and spreading the infection to nearby creatures.

Every creature within 30 ft of the imploding creature must make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC or become infected. Also, every creature within 30 ft has their speed halved until the start of your next turn, due to disorientation from the implosion.

Infected creatures thereafter implode violently in a similar manner at the end of your next turn. Subsequent implosions deal one less damage die. (Creatures infected by the first implosion suffer 5d12, infected by the second suffer 4d12 and etc).
An infected creature cannot get infected again for 24 hours. A creature that has made a saving throw against the effect does not make additional saving throws until the end of your next turn.
You are immune to the effects of the implosion and the infection. You can, before activating this feature, choose up to five creatures to extend this immunity to.
Once you use this feature, you have to complete a long rest before you can use it again.

subclassFeature.name: Oath of Bastion Spells
entries: You gain oath spells at the paladin levels listed.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Sentry's Lingering Aura
entries: As an action, you can touch an ally and use your Channel Divinity to surround that ally with your lingering presence. This aura remains for 1 minute or until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt}. For the duration, the ally cannot be knocked prone or moved against its will, and any creature of your choice that enters within 15 feet of the ally must make a Wisdom saving throw or become frightened for the aura's duration. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature's saving throw is successful or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to your Sentry's Lingering Aura for the next 24 hours.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Shield of the Helpless
entries: As a reaction, when an ally within 30 feet of you is targeted by an attack, you can use your Channel Divinity to project your presence between the attacker and the target. The attack targets you instead of the original target, regardless of range or distance.

items: If the attack is a melee attack, the attacker is mystically drawn toward you, teleporting within 5 feet of you before the attack is made.
If the attack is ranged, the projectile is redirected mid-flight toward you, as if you were in its original path.

entries: When you use this feature, you gain resistance to all damage from that attack.

subclassFeature.name: Armor Bond
entries: Your armor becomes an extension of yourself. Once you take this oath, you can magically bond an armor suit by spending 8 hours doing light activity in it. Once you bond an armor suit, it fits perfectly and never rusts or wears down. While wearing your bonded armor, you are immune to any effects that would force you to remove it, and can sleep in your armor without ill effects.

subclassFeature.name: Fortifying Aura
entries: Your presence fortifies those around you. While you are wearing armor, you and friendly creatures within 10 feet of you gain temporary hit points equal to your Proficiency Bonus whenever they start their turn and are not unconscious. At 18th level, the range of this aura increases to 30 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Bastion's Sustenance
entries: Your armor sustains you in ways beyond mere protection. You can go twice as long without food and water as normal, and after every short or long rest, your armor restores 1 point of exhaustion to you as it magically tends to your body.

subclassFeature.name: Indomitable Guardian
entries: At 15th level, your armor becomes an extension of your will, allowing you to stand against overwhelming forces. While wearing your armor, you gain the following benefits:

items: **Damage Resistance**. You gain resistance to non-magical bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.

**Unstoppable Advance**. If you moved at least 10 feet in a straight line towards a target and then immediately hit it with a melee weapon or an Unarmed Strike, you may use your bonus action as a part of that attack to knock it prone, and push the target away from you up to the same amount of distance you traveled forward to it.

entries: Additionally, you cannot be forcibly disarmed of your shield, and magical effects that would attempt to remove or bypass your armor automatically fail.

subclassFeature.name: Eternal Bastion
entries: At 20th level, you become an unshakable pillar of defense. When you activate this ability as an action, your armor grows larger, becoming like an immovable bulwark of metal. Plates of stone and metal rise from the ground, fusing with your armor to reinforce your stature. You stand as an indomitable tower, impervious to the mightiest of foes, and gain the following benefits:

items: **Living Fortress**. You gain immunity to non-magical bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage, and resistance to all other damage types.

**Unyielding Ward**. All allies within 30 feet of you gain resistance to all damage as long as they remain within the aura. In addition, any damage they would take is reduced by an amount equal to your Charisma modifier.

**Undying Duty**. If you are reduced to 0 hit points while in this form, you can choose to drop to 1 hit point instead. Once this happens, the form ends and cannot be activated again until you finish a long rest.

entries: Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Oath of Inquisition Spells
entries: You gain oath spells at the paladin levels listed.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Blind The Impure
entries: You can use your Channel Divinity to enhance your smites and steal the eyesight of those who oppose you, preventing them from doing harm.
As an action, you can activate this ability; for the next minute, whenever you smite a creature, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be blinded until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Defy The Heretics
entries: You can use your Channel Divinity to thwart spellcasters and their powers.

When a spell is cast within 30 ft of you, you can use your Channel Divinity as a reaction to attempt to interrupt the casting. If the spell's level is equal to or lower than half your paladin level rounded up, and no higher than 5th level, the spell fails and has no effect. If the spell is of a higher level, make a Charisma check. The DC equals 10 + the spell's level. On a success, the spell fails and has no effect.

If you succeed in interrupting the spell, you gain a spell slot of the same level as the spell you interrupted. This slot can only be used for your Divine Smite feature, and you lose all slots you gain using this feature when you take a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Sense
entries: When you take this oath at 3rd level, your familiarity with magic and its practitioners exceeds normal boundaries. You can use your Charisma modifier, instead of Intelligence, for Intelligence (Arcana) rolls.

Additionally, your Divine Sense feature now senses the presence of spellcasters - you detect creatures who've cast a spell in the last 7 days, and you know the level of the highest level spell they cast during that time.

subclassFeature.name: Suppressive Aura
entries: Starting at 7th level, you emanate a 10 feet aura which suppresses magic while you aren't {@condition incapacitated|tgtt}. Whenever an enemy starts its turn there or enters it for the first time in its turn, it takes psychic damage equal to half your paladin level, rounded up.
Whenever an enemy casts a spell while in this aura, it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or take psychic damage equal to 1d8 per spell level, up to 6d8.
At 18th level, the range of this aura increases to 30 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Unfazed Believer
entries: Starting at 15th level, you can call upon the power of your faith and resolve to embolden and empower yourself temporarily. You can use your bonus action to activate Unfazed Believer for 1 minute. For that duration, you can use d10s instead of d8s when you roll your divine smite and improved divine smite damage, and you gain the effects of your choice of one of the three divine blessings described below.
Once you use this feature, you must complete a short or long rest before using it again.

items: **Divine Clarity** - You have advantage on Dexterity and Intelligence saving throws, as well as on Opportunity Attacks and Intelligence (Investigation) checks.
**Divine Endurance** - You have advantage on Constitution and Strength saving throws. In addition, your hit point maximum and current hit points increase by your paladin level.
**Divine Soul** - You have advantage on Wisdom, Charisma and Death saving throws, and immunity to the charmed condition. In addition, you can use your Cleansing Touch feature as a bonus action.

subclassFeature.name: Blessed Inquisitor
entries: At 20th level, you can manifest a spark of divine power in defense of the mortal realms. As an action, you gain the following benefits for 1 hour:

items: You gain the benefits of the {@spell True Seeing} spell for the duration.
You gain the effects of the Unfazed Believer ability for the duration, but instead of choosing one of the three divine blessings, you gain the benefits of all three.

Whenever you make an attack with a melee weapon or an unarmed strike, you may replace the attack's damage type with radiant damage. If you do so, you may add your Charisma modifier to the attack's damage.
At the start of each of your turns, if you have no remaining uses of your channel divinity, you regain one use of your channel divinity.

entries: Once you use this action, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiency
entries: When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you gain Expertise in the Performance skill if you don’t already have it. You also gain the ability to treat weapons you are holding as if they have the {@itemProperty Concealed|TGTT} Trait.

subclassFeature.name: Dance of the Country
entries: You learn how to perform the famed Dance of the Country, and how to utilize its charms in both battle and entertainment. As a bonus action on your turn, you can start Dancing, and can stop doing so at will; the Dance ends early if you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt}, {@condition paralyzed|tgtt} or {@condition restrained|tgtt}, or if you don any type of heavy armor. Your Dance is active for 1 minute, and when it ends, you must attempt a {@dc 10} Constitution Saving Throw, or suffer one level of {@condition exhaustion|tgtt}. While the Dance of the Country is active, you gain the following benefits:

items: You gain a bonus to your AC equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of +1).
You gain advantage on Dexterity (Acrobatics) rolls.
You can make a Sneak Attack against creatures within your melee range without the need for advantage.

entries: All the other rules for Sneak Attack still apply to you.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses of it when you finish a short rest.

subclassFeature.name: Tantalizing Shivers
entries: Your Dance becomes remarkably enticing for those who see it. As a bonus action while Dancing, you can make a Charisma (Performance) check contested by a creature’s Wisdom (Insight) check. The creature must be able to see you. If you succeed on the check the creature becomes {@condition charmed} for one round. While charmed by your Dance, the creature is {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} and has a speed of 0. If you attack a creature charmed by your Dance, you have advantage on your attack roll. The effect ends for an affected creature if it takes any damage or if someone else uses an action to shake the creature out of its hypnotic fascination with the Dance.

subclassFeature.name: Fluid Step
entries: You gain the benefit of the Disengage action while Dancing, and other creatures cannot gain the benefit of Disengaging from you while you are Dancing.

subclassFeature.name: Percussive Strike
entries: Your Dance becomes so alluring and tempting that those who do not guard themselves become easy targets for your blade. When you begin your Dance, every hostile creature that can see you must roll a Wisdom Saving Throw. The DC for the save is 8 + your Proficiency bonus + your Charisma Modifier. A creature that fails is somewhat distracted by your Dance, and you have advantage on your attack roll against it for as long as you keep your Dance active.

subclassFeature.name: Gambler's Tools
entries: You gain proficiency with card and dice sets. Additionally, you can use dice, cards, and coins as weapons, according to the following stats:
Special - Coins can ricochet, hitting a wall or floor and change direction. When attacking in range using a coin, you can treat half cover as no cover.

subclassFeature.name: Gambler's Folly
entries: When you cast a spell using a tool described in your Gambler’s Tools feature, you are also treating the casting as a bet - if you win the bet, the spell is cast normally. If you lose, the spell is still cast, but you also roll a d100 and consult the Gambling Table for the outcome. If the result of the table dictates that the spell is not cast, or is changed somehow, follow the instructions in the table. Spells of 1st and 2nd level use 3/4 odds - meaning that you roll a d4 and if you roll a 4, you lose the bet. Spells of 3rd level use 2/3 odds - you roll a d6 and on 5 or 6, you lose the bet. Spells of 4th level use 1/2 odds - you toss a coin and on tails, you lose the bet.

entries.caption: Gambling Table
rows: Wall of force appears 10 ft in front of the gambler
Gambler smells like a skunk for spell duration, gaining a disadvantage on Charisma (Persuasion) checks
Gambler shoots forth eight nonpoisonous snakes from fingertips. Snakes do not attack and disappear after an hour.
Gambler's clothes itch for the next hour (-2 to initiative, move down the initiative order if in the middle of combat)
Gambler glows as per a {@spell light} cantrip
Spell effect has 60 ft radius centered on gambler
Next sentence spoken by gambler thunders, and can be heard up to 600 feet away
Gambler's hair grows one foot in length
Gambler's face is blackened by small explosion, making him {@condition blinded} for 1 round

Gambler develops allergy to his magical items. Character cannot control sneezing until all magical items are removed. Allergy lasts {@dice 1d6} minutes, and sneezing prevents speaking or casting spells, and gives disadvantage on attack rolls.

Gambler's head enlarges for {@dice 1d4} rounds, as per {@spell Enlarge/Reduce} spell. Only the head is affected, and while this is not harmful to the gambler, it is up to the DM to decide if there are any other effects.
Gambler reduces (as per {@spell Enlarge/Reduce} spell) for 10 minutes
Gambler rolls a Wisdom Saving Throw. On failure, the Gambler falls madly in love with target until a {@spell remove curse} is cast on them
Spell cannot be dismissed at will by gambler for the next hour.
Gambler is subjected to the {@spell Polymorph} spell, with a randomly chosen animal as target
Colorful bubbles come out of gambler's mouth instead of words. Words are released when bubbles pop.
{@spell Tongues} spell targets all within 60 feet of gambler, including the gambler, but with a reversed effect (making affected targets unable to understand any language for the duration)

Gambler's feet enlarge for {@dice 1d3} minutes, reducing movement speed by half and giving them -4 to initiative rolls. If this happens in the middle of combat, the gambler moves down in the initiative order
Gambler suffers same spell effect as target
{@spell Cause fear} with 60ft radius centered on gambler. All within radius except the gambler must make a saving throw.
Gambler speaks in a squeaky voice for {@dice 1d6} days
Gambler gains X-ray vision of 60ft for {@dice 1d6} rounds
Gambler rolls a Constitution Saving Throw. On failure, the gambler ages 10 years
10ft X 10ft pit appears immediately in front of gambler, 5ft deep per level of the gambler
{@spell Reverse Gravity} spell affecting only the gambler for 1 round
Colored streamers pour from gambler's fingertips
Gambler becomes {@condition invisible|tgtt} for 1 hour (as {@spell invisibility} spell)
Gambler casts {@spell Color Spray} without using a spell slot
Stream of butterflies pours from gambler's mouth
Gambler leaves monster-shaped footprints instead of his own until a {@spell dispel magic} is cast
{@dice 3d10} gems shoot from gambler's fingertips. Each gem is worth {@dice 1d6} × 10 gp.
Music fills the air for 10 minutes
{@spell Create food and water} is cast
All normal fires within 60ft of gambler are extinguished
One magical item within 30ft of gambler (randomly chosen) is permanently drained of magic
One normal item within 30ft of gambler (randomly chosen) becomes permanently magical (roll randomly on magic item tables, item can gain any magic item's properties between uncommon and very rare)
All magical weapons bonuses within 30ft of gambler are increased by +2 for 1 turn
Smoke trickles from the ears of all creatures within 60' of gambler for 1 turn
{@spell Dancing lights} is cast on the target
All creatures within 30ft of gambler begin to hiccup (cannot cast spells requiring verbal component, -1 to hit) for 5 rounds
All doors, secret doors, portcullises, etc. (including those locked or barred) within 60ft of gambler swing open
Gambler and target exchange places (only after spell is cast)
Spell affects random target within 60ft of the gambler
Spell fails but spell slot is not consumed
Sudden change in weather (temperature rise, snow, rain, etc.) lasting {@dice 1d6} hours
Deafening bang affects everyone within 60 ft. All those who can hear must roll a Constitution Saving Throw or be {@condition stunned|tgtt} for {@dice 1d3} rounds.
Gambler and target exchange voices until a {@spell remove curse} is cast
Gate opens to randomly chosen outer plane; 50% chance for extra-planar creature to appear.
Spell functions but shrieks like a shrieker for the duration (minimum 1 round)
Spell effectiveness (range, duration, area of effect, damage, etc.) decreases by half
Spell switches with another spell from the same level
Spell becomes a Living Spell, uncontrolled by the gambler.
All weapons within 60 ft of gambler glow for 10 minutes
Spell functions; any applicable saving throw is not allowed
Spell appears to fail when cast, but occurs {@dice 1d4} rounds later
All magical items within 60 ft of gambler glow for {@dice 2d8} days
Gambler and target both make a Wisdom Saving Throw. If they both fail, they switch bodies for {@dice 2d10} rounds

Target's feet enlarge for {@dice 1d3} minutes, reducing movement speed by half and giving them -4 to initiative rolls. If this happens in the middle of combat, the target moves down in the initiative order
Target becomes {@condition invisible|tgtt} as per the {@spell invisibility} spell
{@spell Lightning Bolt} spell shoots toward target
Target enlarged as per the {@spell Enlarge/Reduce} spell
1,000 lbs. of non-living matter within 10 feet of target vanishes
{@spell Flesh to Stone} is cast on the target
{@spell Heal} spell on everyone in 10ft radius centered on gambler
Target is {@condition charmed} as per {@spell Charm Monster}
{@spell Calm Emotions} is cast on the target
Disenchanter summoned for 1 minute in front of target
Small, black raincloud forms over target and starts raining for 10 rounds.
Heavy object (boulder, anvil, etc.) appears over target and falls for {@damage 2d20} points of damage
Target begins sneezing for {@dice 1d6} rounds. sneezing prevents speaking or casting spells, and gives disadvantage on attack rolls.
Spell effect has 60' radius centered on target (all within radius suffer the effect)
Target's clothes itch for {@dice 1d10} rounds (-2 to initiative, move down the initiative order if in the middle of combat)
Target rolls Wisdom Saving Throw, on failure falls madly in love with gambler until a {@spell dispel magic} is cast.
Target rolls Constitution Saving Throw, on failure race randomly changes until canceled by {@spell dispel magic}
All cloth on target crumble to dust
Target sprouts leaves (no damage caused, can be pruned without harm)
Target sprouts new useless appendage (wings, arm, ear, etc.) which remains until {@spell dispel magic} is cast
Target changes color (canceled by {@spell dispel magic})
Spell has a minimum duration of 1 minute (i.e., a {@spell fireball} creates a ball of flame that remains for 1 turn, a lightning bounces and continues, possibly rebounding, for 1 turn, etc.)
Spell effectiveness (range, duration, area of effect, damage, etc.) is doubled

subclassFeature.name: Gambler's Spellcasting
entries: You draw your magical power from Lady Luck herself, relying on gambling activities to unlock your magical potential.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: At 3rd level, you know three cantrips of your choice from the warlock spell list. You learn an additional warlock cantrip of your choice at 10th level.

entries.name: Preparing and Casting Spells
entries: The Gambler table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your gambler spells of 1st level and higher. To cast one of these spells, you must expend a slot of the spell’s level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a long rest.

You prepare the list of gambler spells that are available for you to cast, choosing from the warlock spell list at the end of a long rest. When you do so, choose a number of warlock spells equal to 2d4. The spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots.
You can change your list of prepared spells when you finish a long rest. Preparing a new list of spells requires time spent gambling: at least 1 minute per spell level for each spell.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: You don’t have a regular Spellcasting Ability. Instead, each time you cast a spell, you roll 1d6 and use the result as your Gambling Modifier for the spell. The power of your spells comes from your will to gamble your destiny.

You use your Gambling Modifier whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability. In addition, you use your Gambling modifier when setting the saving throw DC for the spell you cast and when making an attack roll with one.
Spell save DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Gambling
Spell attack modifier = your proficiency bonus + your Gambling

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use a card set, a dice set, or coins as a spellcasting focus for your gambler spells.

subclassFeature.name: Extra Luck
entries: You have honed the ability to manipulate chance to your advantage. As a bonus action, you can gain advantage on an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw. You can use this feature before or after the roll, but before any results are determined. You regain all uses of Extra Luck after finishing a long rest. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. When you do, roll a d100 and consult the Gambling Table for the outcome.

subclassFeature.name: Versatile Gambler
entries: Your versatility as a Gambler increases, allowing you to adapt to different situations. You can now roll 3d6 when determining the amount of spells you can prepare at the end of a long rest, and your Gambling Modifier increases to 2d4.

subclassFeature.name: Master of Fortune
entries: Your mastery of luck and chance reaches its peak. When you roll on the Gambler's Table of Effects, you can roll twice and choose which result to use. Additionally, when you roll a natural 1 on an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you can choose to treat it as a natural 20 instead. You can do so a number of times equal to your proficiency modifier. If you choose to treat a natural 1 as a natural 20, roll a d100 and consult the Gambling Table for the outcome.

subclassFeature.name: Quick Hands
entries: You can manipulate objects with incredible speed. You can use a bonus action, instead of an action, to activate or use non-magical objects such as caltrops, ball bearings, bear traps, vials, flasks, and other items that typically require an action. At 6th level, you can also use magical potions with a bonus action. At 10th level, you can use any magic item that requires an action (such as wands or other activated items) as a bonus action.

subclassFeature.name: Trickster’s Shenanigans
entries: You learn a set of devious tricks fueled by special dice called Trickster Dice, which allow you to use utility methods in combat.

{@b Trickster Dice.} You have four trickster dice, which are {@dice d8}s. A trickster die is expended when you use it. You regain all of your expended trickster dice when you finish a short or long rest. You gain another trickster die at 9th, 13th, and 17th level.

{@b Tricks.} You gain access to a variety of {@filter tricks|optionalfeatures|feature type=TT}. At the end of a long rest, you can prepare a number of tricks from the list of tricks. The number of tricks you can prepare begins at 3 and increases by 1 at 7th (4 tricks), 10th (5 tricks), 15th (6 tricks), and 19th (7 tricks) levels.
{@b Saving Throws.} Some of your tricks require a saving throw to resist the effects. The DC for your tricks is calculated as follows:
{@b Trick save DC} = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Dexterity or Intelligence modifier (your choice).

subclassFeature.name: Trickster's Tricks Options
entries: The following is a list of tricks you can choose from. You can prepare 3 of these tricks at 3rd level, and one additional trick at 7th (4 tricks), 10th (5 tricks), 15th (6 tricks), and 19th (7 tricks) levels.

subclassFeature.name: Sticky Hands
entries: Your deftness in combat allows you to steal from your enemies mid-battle. When you successfully hit a creature with a melee sneak attack, you can use your bonus action to attempt a Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check with advantage to steal one of their belongings. The item must weigh less than 20 pounds and must not be held by the target. Items you can typically steal include hats, amulets, rings, bracelets, or items in pockets and pouches. This does not allow you to steal articles of clothing or items held in the target's hand.

subclassFeature.name: The Switch
entries: You learn to use your agility to turn your enemies’ spells against them. As a reaction when you are targeted by a spell and there is a creature within 10 feet of you, you can attempt a contested Dexterity (Acrobatics) check against that creature's Dexterity (Acrobatics). If you succeed, the creature becomes the new target of the spell.

subclassFeature.name: Master of Mischief
entries: You have become a true master of using objects and tricks to turn the tide of battle. You can manipulate objects in combat in ways no one else can anticipate. When you use your Quick Hands feature, you can take two different item actions during the same bonus action, allowing you to, for example, throw caltrops and drink a potion in one fluid movement. Additionally, if you use an object or item as part of your turn (this includes throwing, activating, or interacting with magical and non-magical items), you regain all expended trickster dice at the end of that turn.

subclassFeature.name: Precise Strike
entries: Your training has taught you how and where to strike, to maximize the damage and hinder your foes.

At 3rd level, you learn three {@filter Precise Strike Methods|optionalfeatures|feature type=PS} from the list at the end of this subclass. When you hit another creature with an unarmed strike, you can spend 2 ki points to attempt a Precise Strike, the effects of which are detailed for each specific strike.
You learn one additional Precise Strike Method of your choice at 6th, 11th, and 17th level. Each time you take a long rest, you can replace one Precise Strike Method you know with a different one.

Precise Strikes can only be used on creatures who possess the relevant organs. Whether a creature is immune to a certain Precise Strike or a certain aspect of it is up to the judgment of the DM, and should be told to the player before they opt to use the ability.

subclassFeature.name: Precise Strike Methods
entries: The following Precise Strike Methods are available to you. You learn three of them when you choose this archetype at 3rd level, and one more at 6th, 11th, and 17th level. You can replace one Precise Strike Method you know with a different one whenever you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Deflect Strike
entries: You can use your "Deflect Missiles" ability when you are hit by a melee weapon attack as well as ranged weapons attacks. If you catch a melee weapon in this fashion, you can use it to attack the enemy holding it as part of the same reaction, though you do so at a disadvantage. As long as you keep holding the weapon, your enemy attacks with disadvantage. Taking the weapon away requires a successful contested athletics check, though your opponent can also gain control of the weapon by scoring a hit on you with it.

subclassFeature.name: Brace
entries: Your training and experience make you not just better at dispensing harm, but at avoiding it too. When you are hit with a critical hit, you can spend 1 ki point as a reaction to make it non critical instead, reducing it's damage and potentially making it a miss.

subclassFeature.name: Battlefield Terror
entries: The power sheer destructiveness of your attacks plants fear in your enemies. When you use a Precise Strike Method against a creature and it fails its save, every hostile creature within 30 ft of you must roll a wisdom saving throw. A creature that fails its save is Frightened of you, and has a movement speed of 0. This effect lasts until the end of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Animal Style
entries: You choose one of the five animals as your primary inspiration. This choice grants you additional abilities based on the animal you select.

entries.name: Snake
entries: Your {@classFeature Flurry of Blows|Monk||2} feature now produces 1 extra hit.

entries.name: Crane
entries: You can spend 1 ki point to parry as a reaction, gaining +2 to your AC until start of your next turn.

entries.name: Mantis
entries: When you use an unarmed strike, you may also grapple as part of the attack, if it hits. The DC for your grapple is your to hit roll.

entries.name: Monkey
entries: When you use your {@classFeature Step of the Wind|Monk||2} feature, you can both {@action Disengage} and {@action Dash} as part of the same bonus action.

entries.name: Tiger
entries: When you attack using an unarmed strike, you can also shove as part of the same attack if it hits.

subclassFeature.name: Animal Versatility
entries: You gain proficiency with one of the following skills of your choice: Acrobatics (Monkey), Perception (Snake), Athletics (Tiger), Insight (Crane), or Sleight of Hand (Mantis). In Addition, whenever you gain a subclass feature in this subclass, you may change one chosen animal with a different one of the same level.

subclassFeature.name: Primal Fury
entries: You learn to channel the primal power of your chosen animal. You can choose the same animal you selected at 3rd level or a different one.

entries.name: Snake
entries: You gain advantage on rolls made to avoid or escape grapple. Additionally, when you hit an enemy with an unarmed strike, you can choose to deal either bludgeoning, piercing, or poison damage.

entries.name: Crane
entries: You gain advantage on your Dexterity saving throws.

entries.name: Mantis
entries: When you attack a {@condition grappled} creature using your unarmed strike, you have advantage.

entries.name: Monkey
entries: You gain climbing speed equal to your movement speed, and can move through the space of hostile creatures.

entries.name: Tiger
entries: When you shove an enemy, you can also let out a primal roar. Creatures within 10 feet of you must make a Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition frightened} of you until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Beastial Connection
entries: You gain deeper connection to the style of your chosen animal, learning to better utilize its powers. You can choose the same animal you selected previously or a different one.

entries.name: Snake
entries: You gain advantage on your {@classFeature Flurry of Blows|Monk||2} attacks.

entries.name: Crane
entries: Your {@classFeature Deflect Missiles|Monk||3} feature uses {@dice 2d10} instead of {@dice 1d10}.

entries.name: Mantis
entries: Other creatures provoke opportunity attacks from you when they enter your reach.

entries.name: Monkey
entries: You can {@action Dodge} as a bonus action without spending ki to do so.

entries.name: Tiger
entries: You have advantage on saving throws against effects causing the {@condition Frightened} condition, and can spend 1 ki point to end it for you or for an ally of your choice within 30 feet of you.

subclassFeature.name: Feral Mastery
entries: You master the intricacies of the chosen animal style, and become able to use its benefits to the fullest extent. You can choose the same animal you selected previously or a different one.

entries.name: Snake
entries: Your unarmed strikes deal an additional {@damage 2d4} poison damage, and creatures hit by your {@classFeature Flurry of Blows|Monk||2} are {@condition poisoned} until the end of their next turn.

entries.name: Crane
entries: You can make an opportunity attack against a creature that misses you with a melee attack, without using your reaction.

entries.name: Mantis
entries: Your unarmed strikes now score a critical hit on a roll of 18-20.

entries.name: Monkey
entries: If you use your bonus action to {@action Dash}, {@action Dodge}, or {@action Disengage}, you can use it again.

entries.name: Tiger
entries: Your {@classFeature Martial Arts|Monk||1} damage die become {@dice 2d6}, and your unarmed strikes deal force damage.

subclassFeature.name: Hidden Arts
entries: You begin your training in the art of hiding your combat movements inside performance. You become proficient with the Acrobatics and Performance skills, if you are not already proficient in them. When using the Performance skill, you may use Dexterity instead of Charisma as the base stat for it.

subclassFeature.name: Rhythmic Step
entries: You learn how to approach combat with a rhythmic step that enhances both your attacking and evading capabilities. This method can be activated as a bonus action on your turn by spending 2 ki points, and can be dismissed at will; It ends early if you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt}, {@condition prone}, {@condition paralyzed} or {@condition restrained|tgtt}, or if you don any type of armor or shield. The Rhythmic Step is active for 1 minute, and when it ends, you must attempt a Constitution Saving Throw ({@dc 10}), or suffer one level of {@condition exhaustion|tgtt}. While the Rhythmic Step is active, you gain the following benefits: When a creature enters your reach while you already have another creature in it, you can move up to your remaining speed as a reaction, without provoking attacks of opportunity. Your movement dazzles your opponents, making it harder to attack you when you move. When a creature attacks you for the first time in combat, it has disadvantage on it’s attack rolls for the rest of its turn. You gain advantage on Dexterity saving throws and ability checks.

subclassFeature.name: Balanced Whirlwind
entries: You become accustomed to the unpredictable nature of your own movements, able to readjust at the blink of an eye to new situations that require you to move. You gain the following benefits while using your Rhythmic Step: You become immune to the Prone condition. When an enemy hits you with a melee attack and is within your reach, you can use your reaction to counter attack with an unarmed strike. Roll an unarmed strike against the attacking enemy.

subclassFeature.name: Pendulum Swing
entries: Your movement when executing the Rhythmic Step becomes more erratic and powerful, giving it the following benefits: When you attack an enemy and move at least 10ft around it (meaning you don't end in the same spot you started in), you can flank it by yourself, gaining advantage on your subsequent attacks until the end of your turn. When an enemy attacks you with an opportunity attack, it has disadvantage on the attack roll.

subclassFeature.name: Maestro Kick
entries: You become the epitome of movement and unpredictability on the battlefield. When you miss an attack, you can expend one ki point to make it a hit instead. You can supplement ki for your reactions. When you use your reaction, you can spend 2 ki points instead of spending your reaction.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiency
entries: You gain proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Athletics, Nature, Insight, Investigation, or Perception. Alternatively, you learn one language of your choice.

subclassFeature.name: Warder Bond
entries: You can form a magical bond with another willing creature of your choice, by performing an hour long ritual together. The bond confers the following benefits: An emphatic link is formed between the two of you, and you are both aware of each other’s feelings and general health at all times, as long as you are both alive and on the same plane of existence. When you both are within 30ft of each other, you are able to sense each other’s location without seeing each other. If you are more than 30ft apart, you only know the general direction to the other, as long as you are both on the same plane of existence. The warder gains advantage on constitution saving throws made when they are within 30ft of their bonded partner. The benefits the bond confers improve on levels 7, 10 and 15. The bond can be nullified by the warder at any time, but is forcefully severed when either the warder or the bonded creature die. The act of the severing carries a heavy toll for the warder. When a creature that was bonded to the warder dies, the warder must roll a Wisdom Saving Throw ({@dc 15}). On failure, roll on the long term madness table provided in the DMG and gain the resulting madness.

subclassFeature.name: Bodyguard
entries: You learn how to fight and protect another creature from harm. When you are within 15ft of your bondmate, and they are targeted for an attack, you can spend your reaction to move closer and attempt to prevent the attack from landing with a cunning swing of your weapon or a dexterous push of your bondmate. When you do so, you move the required distance to stand beside your bondmate, and make a Dexterity or Strength saving throw. Your result becomes the AC for the attack being made against your bondmate.

subclassFeature.name: Warding Senses
entries: You can use a bonus action on your turn to survey the area around you, both with your physical senses and with your powerful arcane intuition. You become aware of the location of all hostile creatures within 60ft, and remain aware until the end of your next turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus.

subclassFeature.name: Warder Bond Improvement
entries: The power of the warder bond increases, and it confers these additional benefits: The distance required for knowing the exact location and gaining advantage on constitution saving throws increases to 60ft. When rolling initiative, both the warder and the bondmate act in sync; they both roll initiative and use the higher result (bonuses included) between the two of them.

subclassFeature.name: Warding Blow
entries: You learn how to better use your talents in combat to protect your bondmate. When an enemy within your range targets your bondmate with an attack or ability, you can use an Opportunity Attack against that enemy as a reaction. If you hit, the enemy has disadvantage on his attack against your bondmate, or your bondmate has advantage in his save against the ability, whichever is applicable.

subclassFeature.name: Warder Bond Improvement
entries: The power of your warder bond grows even stronger, and you gain the following additional benefits from it: The bond starts conferring more physical power to the Warder. In addition to rolling constitution saves with advantage, they can now roll both strength and dexterity saves with advantage. The Warder can also roll skill checks for dexterity and strength based skills with advantage, if they are proficient in them.

subclassFeature.name: Warder’s Duty
entries: The magical energy gained by the bond sustains you, giving you more energy. You only need 2 hours of sleep to complete a long rest, and can eat and drink once every three days to sustain yourself without any ill effects.

subclassFeature.name: Warder Bond Improvement
entries: The power of your warder bond reaches its peak, and it confers the following benefits: You and your bondmate can communicate telepathically while within 60ft of each other.

subclassFeature.name: Perfect Sync
entries: The bond allows the warder and their bondmate to act in perfect sync, each taking the best attributes of the other. When either one of the bonded creatures rolls a saving throw, both roll, and the creature rolling uses the higher result (bonuses included).

subclassFeature.name: Manifest Chains
entries: At 3rd level, you learn the secrets necessary to manifest a pair of chains that stem from your inner self.

When you rage, you can choose to manifest a pair of spectral chains, connected to your arms. The chains function as an extension of your psyche, and you may use them as an elongated appendage to perform actions that do not require fine motor skills.
This includes both object interactions, such as grabbing a lever or pulling a cart towards you, and ability checks, such as an acrobatics roll to grab a nearby support beam to swing from.
You determine the appearance of the chains, and they vanish when your rage ends.
While the chains are present, you gain the following benefits:

items: You can use the chains to attack other creatures. They count as a martial weapon, have a range of 15 feet, have the {@itemProperty L|XPHB|light} and {@itemProperty F|XPHB|finesse} properties, and deal {@damage 1d8} force damage on a hit. The damage dealt by the chains increases to {@damage 1d10} at 6th level, {@damage 1d12} at 10th level, and {@damage 2d6} at 14th level.
When you hit a creature with a weapon attack using chains, you can choose to {@condition grappled|phb|grapple} or shove the creature in addition to dealing damage.

When grappling a creature, or attempting to move a creature grappled by your chains, you count as 1 size category larger than you regularly do. When moving a creature grappled by your chain, you can do so without moving yourself, using your complete control over the chains. While doing so you spend your movement speed as you would have if you had moved, and can also use your bonus action to double your movement speed, but only for the purpose of moving chained creatures.

subclassFeature.name: Chain Imprisonment
entries: At 6th level, the power of your chains increases. You gain the following benefits:

items: The range of your chains increases to 20 feet.
The chains count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

When you successfully grapple a creature using your chains, you can choose to attempt to restrain it. The creature must make a Strength saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier) or become {@condition restrained|tgtt} until the grapple ends. While a creature is {@condition restrained|tgtt} by your chains, it takes force damage equal to your barbarian level at the start of each of its turns. The creature can use its action to attempt to break free of the grapple.

subclassFeature.name: Chain Control
entries: At 10th level, you master more of the power of your chains, and gain the following benefits:

items: The range of your chains increases to 25 feet.
When grappling a creature, or attempting to move a creature grappled by your chains, you count as 2 size categories larger than you regularly do, instead of 1.
When you grapple a creature using your chains, you can immediately use them to shove it 10 feet in any direction, as long as it is still within range of the chains when it finishes moving.

subclassFeature.name: Unchained Fury
entries: At 14th level, your mastery of the chains increases to a peak. You gain the following benefits:

items: You manifest 4 sets of chains when you enter rage instead of 2. Whenever you use the Extra Attack feature to attack twice, you can instead attack three times if all the attacks are made with your chains.
The range of your chains increases to 30 feet.
You can grapple any creature with your chains, regardless of size, and can move them using your chains without expending extra movement to do so.

subclassFeature.name: Improved War Magic
entries: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can replace two of the attacks with a casting of one of your level 1 - 3 Wizard spells that has a casting time of an action.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Eldritch Knight)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the {@filter Arcane Knight|combatmethods|tradition=Arcane Knight}, and {@filter Eldritch Blackguard|combatmethods|tradition=Eldritch Blackguard} combat traditions. You learn two additional methods from these traditions.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Battle Master)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in 2 extra {@filter combat traditions|combatmethods} of your choice. You learn one additional method from these traditions.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Shadow)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the {@filter Mist and Shade|combatmethods|tradition=Mist and Shade} combat tradition. You learn one additional method from this tradition.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Swashbuckler)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the {@filter Comedic Jabs|combatmethods|tradition=Comedic Jabs}, and {@filter Gallant Heart|combatmethods|tradition=Gallant Heart} combat traditions. You learn one additional method from these traditions.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Kensei)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in 1 extra {@filter combat traditions|combatmethods} of your choice. You learn one additional method from this tradition.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Arcane Archer)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in two of the following combat traditions: {@filter Biting Zephyr|combatmethods|tradition=Biting Zephyr}, {@filter Razor’s Edge|combatmethods|tradition=Razor's Edge}, {@filter Unending Wheel|combatmethods|tradition=Unending Wheel}, {@filter Unerring Hawk|combatmethods|tradition=Unerring Hawk}. You learn one additional method from these traditions.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Champion)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in two of the following combat traditions: {@filter Adamant Mountain|combatmethods|tradition=Adamant Mountain}, {@filter Gallant Heart|combatmethods|tradition=Gallant Heart}, {@filter Tempered Iron|combatmethods|tradition=Tempered Iron}. You learn one additional method from these traditions.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Cavalier)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the {@filter Gallant Heart|combatmethods|tradition=Gallant Heart}, and {@filter Spirited Steed|combatmethods|tradition=Spirited Steed} combat traditions. You learn one additional method from these traditions.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Banneret)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the two of the following combat traditions: {@filter Adamant Mountain|combatmethods|tradition=Adamant Mountain}, {@filter Sanguine Knot|combatmethods|tradition=Sanguine Knot}, {@filter Spirited Steed|combatmethods|tradition=Spirited Steed}. You learn one additional method from these traditions.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Open Hand)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the {@filter Adamant Mountain|combatmethods|tradition=Adamant Mountain}, or {@filter Tempered Iron|combatmethods|tradition=Tempered Iron} combat traditions. You learn one additional method from this tradition.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Debilitation)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the {@filter Adamant Mountain|combatmethods|tradition=Adamant Mountain}, or {@filter Tempered Iron|combatmethods|tradition=Tempered Iron} combat traditions. You learn one additional method from this tradition.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Oathbreaker Paladin)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the {@filter Eldritch Blackguard|combatmethods|tradition=Eldritch Blackguard} combat tradition. You learn one additional method from this tradition.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Warder)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the {@filter Tempered Iron|combatmethods|tradition=Tempered Iron} and {@filter Gallant Heart|combatmethods|tradition=Gallant Heart} combat traditions. You learn one additional method from these traditions.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Samurai)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the {@filter Razor's Edge|combatmethods|tradition=Razor's Edge} and {@filter Gallant Heart|combatmethods|tradition=Gallant Heart} combat traditions. You learn one additional method from these traditions.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Echo Knight)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the {@filter Mirror's Glint|combatmethods|tradition=Mirror's Glint} and {@filter Mist and Shade|combatmethods|tradition=Mist and Shade} combat traditions. You learn one additional method from these traditions.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Psi Warrior)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the {@filter Rapid Current|combatmethods|tradition=Rapid Current} and {@filter Mirror's Glint|combatmethods|tradition=Mirror's Glint} combat traditions. You learn one additional method from these traditions.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Rune Knight)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the {@filter Adamant Mountain|combatmethods|tradition=Adamant Mountain} and {@filter Tempered Iron|combatmethods|tradition=Tempered Iron} combat traditions. You learn one additional method from these traditions.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Shackled)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the {@filter Unending Wheel|combatmethods|tradition=Unending Wheel} combat tradition. You learn one additional method from this tradition.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Five Animals)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the {@filter Tooth and Claw|combatmethods|tradition=Tooth and Claw} combat tradition. You learn one additional method from this tradition.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Elements)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the {@filter Biting Zephyr|combatmethods|tradition=Biting Zephyr} combat tradition. You learn one additional method from this tradition.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Mercy)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the {@filter Sanguine Knot|combatmethods|tradition=Sanguine Knot} combat tradition. You learn one additional method from this tradition.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Long Death)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the {@filter Mist and Shade|combatmethods|tradition=Mist and Shade} combat tradition. You learn one additional method from this tradition.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Drunken Master)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the {@filter Rapid Current|combatmethods|tradition=Rapid Current} combat tradition. You learn one additional method from this tradition.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Sun Soul)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the {@filter Biting Zephyr|combatmethods|tradition=Biting Zephyr} combat tradition. You learn one additional method from this tradition.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Astral Self)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the {@filter Mirror's Glint|combatmethods|tradition=Mirror's Glint} combat tradition. You learn one additional method from this tradition.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Ascendant Dragon)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the {@filter Biting Zephyr|combatmethods|tradition=Biting Zephyr} combat tradition. You learn one additional method from this tradition.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Methods (Cobalt Soul)
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the {@filter Razor's Edge|combatmethods|tradition=Razor's Edge} combat tradition. You learn one additional method from this tradition.

optionalfeature.name: Careful Spell (Passive)
entries: {@italic Cost: 1 sorcery point (Passive)}

When you cast a spell that forces other creatures to make a saving throw, you can protect some of those creatures from the spell's full force. To do so, choose a number of those creatures up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one creature). A chosen creature automatically succeeds on its saving throw against the spell.

optionalfeature.name: Distant Spell (Passive)
entries: {@italic Cost: 1 sorcery point (Passive)}
When you cast a spell that has a range of 5 feet or greater, you can double the range of the spell.
When you cast a spell that has a range of touch, you can make the range of the spell 30 feet.
This feature does not affect spells affected by the {@optfeature Bestowed Spell|TGTT} metamagic feature.

optionalfeature.name: Empowered Spell (Passive)
entries: {@italic Cost: 1 sorcery point (Passive)}
When you roll damage for a spell, you can reroll a number of the damage dice up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one).
You must use the new rolls.

optionalfeature.name: Extended Spell (Passive)
entries: {@italic Cost: 1 sorcery point (Passive)}
When you cast a spell that has a duration of 1 minute or longer, you can double its duration, to a maximum duration of 24 hours.

optionalfeature.name: Resonant Spell (Passive)
entries: {@italic Cost: 2 sorcery points (Passive)}
When you cast a spell, rolls made to dispel or counter it suffer disadvantage.

optionalfeature.name: Split Spell (Passive)
entries: {@italic Cost: 1 sorcery point (Passive)}

When you cast a spell that has an area of effect of 10 feet or more, you can split the area of effect between two different points within the spell range. The split can be of any ratio you desire—50:50, 60:40 and so on, but must be in 5 feet increments.

optionalfeature.name: Supple Spell (Passive)
entries: {@italic Cost: 2 sorcery points (Passive)}
When you cast a spell that has an area of effect of 10 feet or more, you increase or decrease the area of effect by up to half of its original size, rounded up to the nearest 5 feet increment.

optionalfeature.name: Transmuted Spell (Passive)
entries: {@italic Cost: 1 sorcery point (Passive)}
When you cast a spell that deals a type of damage from the following list, you can change that damage type to one of the other listed types: acid, cold, fire, lightning, poison, thunder.

optionalfeature.name: Warding Spell (Passive)
entries: {@italic Cost: 2 sorcery points (Passive)}
When you are maintaining {@status concentration} on a spell, your AC increases by 1.

optionalfeature.name: Aimed Spell (Active)
entries: {@italic Cost: 2 sorcery points (Active)}
When you cast a spell that requires an attack roll, you can spend 2 sorcery points to add {@dice 1d6} to the attack roll.
You can use {@optfeature Aimed Spell (Active)|TGTT} even if you have already used a different Active Metamagic option during the casting of the spell.

optionalfeature.name: Bestowed Spell (Active)
entries: {@italic Cost: X sorcery points (Active), where X is the spell's level}
When you cast a spell with a range of self, you can spend a number of sorcery points equal to the spell's level to change the range to touch.

optionalfeature.name: Bouncing Spell (Active)
entries: {@italic Cost: 3 sorcery points (Active)}

When you cast a spell that targets a single target, you can spend 3 sorcery points when the target succeeds on its initial saving throw against the spell to make it bounce to another target within range.

optionalfeature.name: Focused Spell (Active)
entries: {@italic Cost: X sorcery points (Active), where X is the spell's level}
When you roll for {@status concentration} on a spell, you can spend a number of sorcery points equal to the spell's level to reroll one of the dice you used, potentially turning failure to a success.
You can use {@optfeature Focused Spell (Active)|TGTT} even if you have already used a different Active Metamagic option during the casting of the spell.

optionalfeature.name: Heightened Spell (Active)
entries: {@italic Cost: 3 sorcery points (Active)}

When you cast a spell that forces a creature to make a saving throw to resist its effects, you can spend 3 sorcery points to give one target of the spell disadvantage on its first saving throw made against the spell.

optionalfeature.name: Lingering Spell (Active)
entries: {@italic Cost: X sorcery points (Active), where X is the spell's level}

When you lose {@status concentration} on a spell, you can spend a number of sorcery points equal to the spell's level to make the spell's effect linger until the end of your next turn, without you needing to concentrate on it.

optionalfeature.name: Overcharged Spell (Active)
entries: {@italic Cost: 4 sorcery points (Active)}
When you roll damage for a spell, you can spend 4 sorcery points to refrain from rolling and produce maximal damage for the spell.
You can use {@optfeature Overcharged Spell (Active)|TGTT} even if you have already used a different Active Metamagic option during the casting of the spell.

optionalfeature.name: Quickened Spell (Active)
entries: {@italic Cost: 2 sorcery points (Active)}
When you cast a spell that has a casting time of 1 action, you can spend 2 sorcery points to change the casting time to 1 bonus action for this casting.

optionalfeature.name: Seeking Spell (Active)
entries: {@italic Cost: 2 sorcery points (Active)}
If you make an attack roll for a spell and miss, you can spend 2 sorcery points to reroll the {@dice d20}, and you must use the new roll.
You can use {@optfeature Seeking Spell (Active)|TGTT} even if you have already used a different Active Metamagic option during the casting of the spell.

optionalfeature.name: Subtle Spell (Active)
entries: {@italic Cost: 1 sorcery point (Active)}
When you cast a spell, you can spend 1 sorcery point to cast it without any somatic or verbal components.

optionalfeature.name: Twinned Spell (Active)
entries: {@italic Cost: X sorcery points (Active), where X is the spell's level (1 if the spell is a cantrip)}

When you cast a spell that targets only one creature and doesn't have a range of self, you can spend a number of sorcery points equal to the spell's level to target a second creature in range with the same spell (1 sorcery point if the spell is a cantrip).

To be eligible, a spell must be incapable of targeting more than one creature at the spell's current level. For example, {@spell magic missile} and {@spell scorching ray} aren't eligible, but {@spell ray of frost} and {@spell chromatic orb} are.

optionalfeature.name: Vampiric Spell (Active)
entries: {@italic Cost: X sorcery points (Active), where X is half the spell's level rounded up}
When you cast a spell that deals damage and has no duration, you can spend sorcery points equal to half the spell's level (rounded up) to regain a number of hit points equal to the damage dealt.
You can use {@optfeature Vampiric Spell (Active)|TGTT} even if you have already used a different Active Metamagic option during the casting of the spell.

optionalfeature.name: Burrower
entries: As a bonus action on your turn, you can use your unique body to burrow underground and move there, leaving tunnels behind you.

This ability is usable when the ground is either sand, snow, dirt or mud. When underground, you can move freely as if moving through difficult terrain. You are considered {@condition hidden} to creatures standing on the ground when underground, and spells that cause damage through the ground (ex. {@spell earth tremor|XGE}) succeed automatically and deal maximum damage.
You can stay underground for as long as you can hold your breath.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (a minimum of once). You regain any expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Extra Appendages
entries: You grow two extra appendages on your body, in a place of your choosing.
The appendages can be tentacles, hands, prehensile tails, or anything else you desire, but will always function the same way.
The appendages can be used to manipulate objects just like your normal hands can, and can be used in combat to attack or grapple.
The appendages do not deal any special type of damage and do not allow you to attack more times than what you would normally be able to.

optionalfeature.name: Abomination's Physique
entries: Your improved physique grants you the Powerful Build trait. Additionally, your movement speed is improved by 10 feet, and your jumping distances by 5 feet.

optionalfeature.name: Gravity Defied
entries: You can move up, down, and across vertical surfaces freely up to your speed, while leaving your hands free. If you finish your turn on a wall, you fall from it.
Additionally, you can cast {@spell spider climb} once without expending a spell slot or materials. You regain the ability to do so once you finish a long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Leaper
entries: As a bonus action on your turn, you can leap up to half your speed, getting from point to point without touching the ground. Your movement is too fast to provoke opportunity attacks.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (a minimum of once). You regain any expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Spiked Carapace
entries: Spikes grow out of your skin, making getting close to you a foolish endeavor.

When you are hit with a melee attack by a creature standing up to 5 feet from you, the creature needs to roll a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC. On failure, it takes {@damage 1d4} piercing damage.
In addition, you gain advantage on checks against being {@condition grappled}.

optionalfeature.name: Pantomime
entries: As an action, you can perform a pantomime that distracts a creature within 30 feet of you. The creature must make a Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition charmed} by your performance until the end of its next turn. While charmed, the creature's speed is reduced to 0 and it has disadvantage on attack rolls.

optionalfeature.name: Prankster
entries: As an action, you can perform a mischievous prank on a creature within 30 feet of you. The creature must make a Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition dazed|TGTT} until the end of its next turn.

optionalfeature.name: Trickster's Disengagement
entries: You somersault and cartwheel away from enemies, deftly avoiding their attacks and gaining the upper hand. You can use this act as a bonus action to disengage from up to five creatures.

optionalfeature.name: Tumbler
entries: As a bonus action, you can perform a tumbling trick that allows you to move through the space of hostile creatures for the rest of the turn.

optionalfeature.name: Dazzling Disguise
entries: You use your wit and flair for theatrics to create a convincing disguise, granting advantage on Charisma ({@skill Deception}) checks for the next hour.
This act takes 1 minute to be activated.

optionalfeature.name: Jester's Juggle
entries: As a bonus action, you juggle your weapons and spell focus to confuse your foes. Each hostile creature within 30 feet must make a Wisdom saving throw or become enamoured by your act until the start of their next turn. While enamoured, attacks against a creature are done with advantage.

optionalfeature.name: Fool's Folly
entries: When you use your Bardic Inspiration, you can use this act to taunt your opponents with a series of biting insults, causing them to hesitate and second-guess their actions. When you use this act, you force a creature of your choice within 60 feet to make an Intelligence saving throw, becoming {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} until the end of their next turn on a failed save.

optionalfeature.name: Laughing Lunge
entries: You charge forward with a reckless abandon, letting out a piercing laugh as you strike your target. You can use this act to make your attack action with advantage, dealing an additional {@damage 1d6} psychic damage.
This act requires the expenditure of one use of Bardic Inspiration.

optionalfeature.name: Jester's Jaunt
entries: You create an illusory image of yourself that confuses and distracts your enemies. You can use this act to cast the spell {@spell mirror image} without expending a spell slot or using material components by expending one use of your Bardic Inspiration.

optionalfeature.name: Ridiculous Ruse
entries: You create a false narrative or distraction, leading your enemies astray and enabling you or an ally to escape. You can use this act to cast the spell {@spell silent image} without expending a spell slot or using material components, by expending one use of your Bardic Inspiration.

optionalfeature.name: Jester's Agility
entries: You use your acrobatic skill to dodge attacks and move with unparalleled grace. You can use this act as a reaction to grant yourself a bonus to AC equal to your proficiency bonus until the start of your next turn.
This act requires the expenditure of one use of Bardic Inspiration.

optionalfeature.name: Witty Wordplay
entries: You use your sharp wit to confuse and demoralize your opponents. When you use your bardic inspiration, you can use this act to impose disadvantage on the next attack roll made by a creature within 60 feet against you or an ally.

optionalfeature.name: Jester's Jest
