entries: As a bonus action, you unleash a hilarious quip or insult that causes your target to become distracted. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw or become unable to take reactions until the end of their next turn.

optionalfeature.name: Disarming Strike
entries: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one trick die to attempt to disarm the target. Add the trick die to the attack's damage roll, and the target must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, they drop one item they are holding.

optionalfeature.name: Trip Attack
entries: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one trick die to attempt to knock the target {@condition prone}. Add the trick die to the attack's damage roll, and the target must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, they are knocked {@condition prone}.

optionalfeature.name: Swing Away
entries: When a creature misses you with a melee attack, you can expend one trick die as a reaction to move up to half your speed without provoking opportunity attacks. Add the trick die to your next weapon damage roll if you make an attack before the end of your next turn.

optionalfeature.name: Deafening Strike
entries: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one trick die to attempt to deafen the target. Add the trick die to the attack's damage roll, and the target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, they are {@condition deafened} until the end of your next turn.

optionalfeature.name: Blinding Strike
entries: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one trick die to blind them. Add the trick die to the damage roll, and the target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, they are {@condition blinded} until the end of your next turn.

optionalfeature.name: Noise Maker
entries: You can expend one trick die to throw an object and create a distraction. You create a loud noise at a point within 60 feet that can be heard up to 300 feet away. Creatures within 30 feet of the point must make a Wisdom saving throw or be distracted, giving all attacks against them advantage until the end of your next turn.

optionalfeature.name: Rebounding Throw
entries: When you throw an object (like a dagger, flask, or stone), you can expend a Trickster die to cause the object to ricochet off a surface or enemy, making a second attack against a different target. You add the Trickster die to the second attack's damage roll.

optionalfeature.name: Weaponized Debris
entries: When you are holding an object that could plausibly be used as a weapon (a rock, chair, bottle, etc.), you can expend one Trickster die to make a ranged or melee attack with it. Add the Trickster die to the damage roll of the improvised weapon, and if the target fails a Dexterity saving throw, the target suffers an additional effect depending on the type of object:
{@i Fragile Object:} The object breaks and the target is {@condition choked|TGTT} until the start of your next turn.
{@i Sturdy Object:} The target is {@condition dazed|TGTT} until the start of your next turn.

{@i Flammable Object:} If the object contains flammable material (like oil), the target is set on fire, taking {@damage 1d4} fire damage at the start of each of its turns until it uses an action to extinguish the flames.

optionalfeature.name: Rapid Deployment
entries: As a reaction to being attacked, you can expend a Trickster die to instantly deploy a defensive item (like throwing ball bearings at your feet, a smoke bomb, or deploying a shield). Roll the Trickster die and subtract it from the triggering attack roll, potentially causing the attack to miss.

optionalfeature.name: Explosive Flask
entries: When using a flask of oil or alchemist's fire, you can expend a Trickster die to augment the effect. Add the Trickster die to the damage roll, and the affected area is considered difficult terrain for creatures other than you until the end of your next turn.

optionalfeature.name: Instant Barrier
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend a Trickster die to set up a defensive object or structure in your space, such as flipping over a table, setting up a shield, or placing another large item between you and your enemy. You gain half cover from attacks until the start of your next turn. The Trickster die is added to your AC for the duration.

optionalfeature.name: Arm Snap
entries: This Precise Strike is aimed at the manipulating limbs of a creature, such as arms, claws and tentacles.
{@b Save:} Constitution
{@b Save DC:} Ki save DC - 2
{@b Effect:} On a failed save, any ability checks or attacks made using the wounded limb suffers disadvantage for {@dice 1d4} rounds or until the target is healed.

{@b Critical Hit Effect:} On a failed save against a critical hit, the attack severs an important muscle or tendon and the limb becomes useless until healed with magic or a successful {@dc 15} Medicine check is made.

optionalfeature.name: Air Draining Strike
entries: This Precise Strike is aimed at the chest, the well protected center of mass of a creature, usually affecting its lungs and respiratory system.
{@b Save:} Constitution
{@b Save DC:} Ki save DC
{@b Effect:} This Method causes the target great physical distress. On a failed save, damage rolls by the target do not gain any benefit from Strength or Dexterity bonuses for {@dice 1d4} rounds.
{@b Critical Hit Effect:} On a failed save against a critical hit, in addition to the above effect, the target doesn't benefit from Strength and Dexterity bonuses for its attack rolls.

optionalfeature.name: Ear Clap
entries: This Precise Strike is aimed at the organs used to hear. Creatures without external ears are immune to this Precise Strike.
{@b Save:} Constitution
{@b Save DC:} Ki save DC - 4
{@b Effect:} On a failed save, the target is {@condition deafened} for {@dice 1d4} rounds.

{@b Critical Hit Effect:} On a failed save against a critical hit, in addition to being {@condition deafened}, the target is disoriented, suffering disadvantage to Strength and Dexterity saving throws for the duration.

optionalfeature.name: Eye Gouge
entries: This Precise Strike is aimed at whatever organs a creature uses to see. At the DM's discretion, this strike might also target sensory organs such as antennae, potentially negating abilities like blindsense. This attack affects two eyes at the same time, and so creatures with more than 2 will not be affected before all their eyes have been damaged in this way.
{@b Save:} Dexterity
{@b Save DC:} Ki save DC - 4
{@b Effect:} On a failed save, the target is {@condition blinded} for {@dice 1d4} rounds.

{@b Critical Hit Effect:} On a failed save against a critical hit, one eye is lost or destroyed and the target is permanently blind in that eye unless sight is returned with a {@spell lesser restoration} spell or stronger healing magic.

optionalfeature.name: Finger Smash
entries: This Precise Strike is aimed at delicate, often small limbs used for manipulation of objects.
{@b Save:} Dexterity
{@b Save DC:} Ki save DC - 2

{@b Effect:} On a failed save, any attack rolls and ability checks made using the wounded hand suffer disadvantage for {@dice 1d4} rounds, including attack rolls with two-handed weapons. If the target is wielding a weapon or other held object, it must make a successful Strength or Dexterity saving throw (target's choice) or drop what it was holding.

{@b Critical Hit Effect:} On a failed save against a critical hit, the hand is permanently damaged and the resulting penalties also become permanent until healed with magic or a successful {@dc 15} Medicine check is made.

optionalfeature.name: Temple Strike
entries: This Precise Strike is aimed at the head, the area where most creatures have their brain and main sensory organs. Creatures without heads are immune to the effects of this strike.
{@b Save:} Constitution or Dexterity (target's choice)
{@b Save DC:} Ki save DC - 2
{@b Effect:} On a failed save, the target loses {@status concentration} and cannot concentrate until the start of your next turn.
{@b Critical Hit Effect:} On a failed save against a critical hit, the regular effect of the strike lasts for {@dice 1d4} rounds.

optionalfeature.name: Heart Bursting Punch
entries: This Precise Strike is aimed at any small, likely fatal location on a creature, such as the heart.
{@b Save:} Constitution
{@b Save DC:} Ki save DC - 6
{@b Effect:} On a failed save, the target gains one point of {@condition exhaustion|tgtt}.
{@b Critical Hit Effect:} On a failed save against a critical hit, the target gains two points of {@condition exhaustion|tgtt} instead of 1.

optionalfeature.name: Leg Sweeping Kick
entries: This Precise Strike is aimed at the ambulatory limbs of a creature, such as feet and legs.
{@b Save:} Strength or Dexterity (target's choice)
{@b Save DC:} Ki save DC - 2

{@b Effect:} On a failed save, the target's speed is halved for {@dice 1d4} rounds if it has two or fewer legs, and by 10 feet if it has more than two legs. The speed penalty for hits on legs stack, but the creature's speed cannot be reduced below 5 feet per round. Additionally, any skill or ability checks involving movement (such as {@skill Acrobatics} or {@skill Athletics} checks) suffers disadvantage.
{@b Critical Hit Effect:} On a failed save against a critical hit, the target is knocked {@condition prone} in addition to the original effect.

optionalfeature.name: Neck Chop
entries: This Precise Strike is aimed at the connection between head and body. Injuries to the neck keep a creature from speaking easily.
{@b Save:} Constitution
{@b Save DC:} Ki save DC - 2
{@b Effect:} On a failed save, the target is {@condition Choked|TGTT} for {@dice 1d4} rounds.
{@b Critical Hit Effect:} On a failed save against a critical hit, the target becomes completely mute for {@dice 1d4} rounds.

optionalfeature.name: Low Blow
entries: This Precise Strike is aimed at the vitals: critical organs not protected by bone. Vitals for non-humanoid creatures can include nearly any location that is relatively hard to hit, poorly protected, and debilitating if struck.
{@b Save:} Constitution
{@b Save DC:} Ki save DC - 2
{@b Effect:} On a failed save, the target is {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} for 1 round.
{@b Critical Hit Effect:} On a failed save against a critical hit, the duration of the above effect is increased to {@dice 1d4} rounds.

optionalfeature.name: Pierce Defenses
entries: This Precise Strike focuses on targeting the armor, rather than the enemy. An armor is either an object worn or a thick layer of protective matter. If the target's AC comes from speed, you can flavor this strike as if it hampers the target's movement.
{@b Save:} Dexterity or Constitution (target's choice)
{@b Save DC:} Ki save DC
{@b Effect:} On a failed save, the target's AC is reduced by 1 for {@dice 1d4} rounds.

{@b Critical Hit Effect:} On a failed save against a critical hit, the damage to the armor becomes permanent, and can only be fixed by spending 8 hours fixing it with the appropriate tool check ({@item leatherworker's tools|XPHB} or {@item smith's tools|XPHB}) or by a {@spell lesser restoration} spell if the damage is to the target's body.

optionalfeature.name: High Ground
entries: Knowing your way around combat lets you better take advantage of high ground.
When standing 5 feet or more above an enemy, you gain a +2 bonus to hit with ranged attacks against said enemy.

optionalfeature.name: Sweeping Blows
entries: Enemies often underestimate those who are below them, and you know how to take advantage of that.
When attacking in melee an enemy that is 5 feet or more higher than you, you gain a +2 bonus to hit.

optionalfeature.name: Charging
entries: You can attempt to overwhelm your enemies with a ferocious attack.

When moving at least 10 feet before attacking an enemy with a melee attack, you can attempt to knock the enemy {@condition prone} (as per the {@action Shove} action) as part of the same attack, using the attack's hit score as the DC for the enemy's roll.

optionalfeature.name: Flanking
entries: Your expertise in combat lets you turn every small advantage you gain into a decisive blow.
When attacking a flanked enemy (not necessarily flanked by you), you can use an opportunity attack against the flanked enemy at the start of their turn.

{@note This ability relies on the table both using the Flanking optional rule, and having it give a +2 bonus instead of advantage. If either of these assumptions is false, this ability allows the user to benefit from a +2 to hit when flanking in melee.}

optionalfeature.name: Eye of the Storm
entries: Being surrounded might be a death sentence for others, but for you it is a place to shine.
When being flanked by enemies, you can use your reaction to make one of the flanking enemies attack you with disadvantage until the end of their turn.

optionalfeature.name: Hammer and Anvil
entries: When your enemy cannot retreat, you can press the advantage.
When attacking an enemy with a wall, tree, or other inanimate object that is larger than them and prevents them from moving through its space behind them, you gain a +2 bonus to hit against them.

optionalfeature.name: Back to the Wall
entries: Fighting with no place to retreat only gives you another reason to push forward.

When you are fighting with a wall, tree, or other inanimate object that is larger than you and prevents you from moving through its space behind you, roll a Dexterity saving throw for any melee attack against you, using the to hit value as the DC.
If you succeed, the attack hits the object behind you, and half the damage it would have done is reflected back to the attacker.

optionalfeature.name: Covering Attack
entries: You attack your enemy with flair and accuracy, demanding all of their attention, and allowing one of your allies to safely retreat from that same foe.

When you attack an enemy that has one of your allies within melee range, until the start of the enemy's turn, the first movement of one of your allies that would have given the enemy a chance at an opportunity attack does not do so.

optionalfeature.name: Goading Movement
entries: A fighter knows how to make an enemy follow into a trap.
When you move away from an enemy, if the same enemy enters the range of your melee attack on their next turn, you can use an opportunity attack against that enemy.

optionalfeature.name: Last Ditch Evasion
entries: Thanks to a combination of skill and luck, you narrowly avoid an attack but leave yourself perilously exposed.
When you are hit by an attack, you can use a reaction to jump to the side and save yourself from taking damage. If you do, you become {@condition slowed|TGTT} until the end of your next turn.

optionalfeature.name: Daring Feint
entries: You know how to make an enemy expose themselves with a smart feint.
You can choose to make a feint, spending your reaction on it. If you do, your next attack can be made with advantage, and your critical range for it will be 19-20.
{@note If you benefit from another feature which changes your critical range, this feature enlarges the range by 1. For example, if your critical range is 18-20, using this feature it will be 17-20.}

optionalfeature.name: Sheathing the Sword
entries: You allow an attack to come through in order to gain a chance of landing a decisive blow.
When an enemy attacks you, you may choose to let the attack hit automatically. If you do, you can use your reaction to attack with advantage, and your critical range for the attack will be 19-20.
{@note If you benefit from another feature which changes your critical range, this feature enlarges the range by 1. For example, if your critical range is 18-20, using this feature it will be 17-20.}

optionalfeature.name: Dying Surge
entries: No one has experienced losing consciousness in battle as much as the fighter.
When reaching 0 hit points, you can use your reaction to perform an opportunity attack against an enemy in range before losing consciousness.

optionalfeature.name: Pact of Transformation
entries: Your body is no longer only mortal, as you begin to exhibit traits of your fiendish aspect manifesting.
Your skin changes color and texture, your body grows and changes. You may grow horns and juts on your skin, your eyes may glow, you may grow fangs and any other change to your body may happen.
You creature type changes to {@b monstrosity}.
You gain one Eldritch Invocation of your choice with the Pact of Transformation prerequisite.

Changes are cosmetic and {@b do not have} any mechanical effect beyond changes to your weight and height. Your size may not become larger than {@b Medium}, though you may be taller or wider than what is normal for your original race.

optionalfeature.name: Dreamwalk
entries: The most basic ability of all dreamers. All of your regular dreams can be lucid dreams, per your choice. More importantly, this ability allows you to enter the Dreamtime in your sleep.

You arrive in The Dreamtime dressed as you normally dress and carrying the equipment that you normally carry. By default, you appear at the location at which you are physically located in the real world. However, you can attempt to appear in another location by making a Concentration check.

entries.caption: Dreamwalk Location DC
rows: A place you have visited only briefly or can visualize to some extent
A place you have never seen or been, but have knowledge of and have seen depictions of

entries: Locations can be accessed by knowing them ("I want to appear at my home town square"), by relational description ("I need to appear in the room next to the one I am in now"), or by description of a place known to you by name and description only.
While in The Dreamtime, you can move, act, and cast spells just as in the real world. Refer to the Real World Consequences rules for more information.

You may exit The Dreamtime at any time as an action. With a Concentration check ({@dc 15}), you may awaken immediately in the real world; otherwise, you revert to a normal sleep, to awaken as you normally would.
Finally, you become much better at maintaining your concentration on the dream and avoiding stray thoughts. You make the Constant Effort check only once every hour, and the DC for you is 10.

optionalfeature.name: Dreamwatch
entries: You learn not only how to dream, but how to access the dreams of others. While in the Dreamtime you can attempt to identify, observe, and/or enter the dream of a specific other person (who must be asleep and dreaming at the time).
Dreams appear to you as glowing globs of light with no clear distinguishing features, which you can see when you close your eyes in the Dreamtime.

entries.name: Finding a Dream
entries: To find a specific person's dream, make a Concentration check. The DC varies according to your relationship with the person, as well as their physical proximity to you in the real world. If you have entered this person's dreams before, you gain a +5 bonus on your check.

entries.caption: Dreamwatch DC by Relationship
rows: Intense love or hate between you
Someone you have seen or met once
Someone you have only heard of

entries.name: Observing a Dream
entries: If you only observe a dream, you see all that is unfolding within it, but cannot affect it in any way; furthermore, you run the risk of being sucked into it. Every hour of observation, you must roll a Concentration check to resist the dream's pull. The DC is determined by your relationship with the dreamer:

entries.caption: Observation Resistance DC
rows: Intense love or hate between you
Someone you have seen or met once
Someone you have only heard of

entries.name: Entering a Dream
entries: Entering another person's dream is dangerous, because the dreamer's psyche creates the very reality of the dream, and you risk falling under its control. Once you enter, you are a fixture of the dreamer's imagination. Leaving the dream (back to the Dreamtime) requires a check against {@dc 10}, which can be attempted once every 10 minutes.

Every 10 minutes in another's dream, you must make a Concentration check ({@dc 10}) if you don't leave. On failure, you become {@condition ensnared|TGTT}—each action you try to take requires a Concentration check contested by the dreamer's Wisdom saving throw. On failure, you cannot take another action for 10 minutes, including attempting to exit, and act in accordance with the narrative of the dream.

If, while ensnared or rolling against becoming ensnared, you fail by 10 or more, you lose all sense of your own identity, becoming a complete slave of the dreamer's psyche. You cannot exit the dream or attempt any voluntary action until the dreamer wakes up or their dreams move on.
If there is an intense emotional relationship between yourself and the dreamer, make all Concentration checks inside their dream with a -10 penalty.

The dreamer usually has no way of knowing that you entered their dream—they may recall dreaming about you when they awake, but cannot be sure you were more than a figment. However, if the dreamer possesses this ability, they know immediately as you enter and can distinguish you from their imagination without fail.

You may attempt to make the dreamer aware of your nature with a {@skill Persuasion} check with disadvantage against their Wisdom saving throw. You may also communicate a message; the dreamer must make an Intelligence check ({@dc 15}) upon awakening to recall the message. If expecting such a message or knowing you have this ability, they gain +5 to the roll.

optionalfeature.name: Dreambend
entries: You learn the essential talent of a dreamwalker—the alteration of the dream. While inside the Dreamtime or another person's dream, you can shape the reality of the dream realm by force of will.

You can alter your dress, your equipment, your appearance, or the dress, equipment, or appearance of someone else. In fact, you can alter any person or item that has been dreamed, including effects from the Dreamtime Consequences Table; what you can't change are things "native" to the dream world—your surroundings, items you pick up, and objects or buildings. You also can't alter objects or creatures that are physically brought into the Dreamtime from another world (via {@spell plane shift}, for example).

entries.name: Changing Yourself
entries: Make a Concentration check as an action:

items: A result of 10 or higher allows you to change your clothing or gear.

A result of 20 or higher allows you to change your own physical features, similar to {@spell alter self} (your statistics do not change), but without the limitation of same limb assembly (you can have more or less limbs).

entries: If you change your features or give yourself equipment you don't normally carry, you must make a Concentration check any time you are distracted, as if concentrating on a spell.

entries.name: Changing Others
entries: Use the same rules, but with changes:

items: If the target does not have Dreambend: DCs are 15 (clothing/gear) and 25 (physical features).
If the target has Dreambend: The check is opposed by their Concentration check.

entries: If you succeed in changing the target, they can change themselves back with a successful Concentration check of their own.

entries.name: Conjuring Items
entries: You can conjure up small items; if you try to create items not immediately on your person, the DC becomes 15.
Items created cannot be magical and must be familiar to you to some extent.

optionalfeature.name: Dreamjump
entries: You can travel to any point in The Dreamtime that you can envision—that you have previously seen or been to in either the Dreamtime or the real world.

Traveling in this manner requires an action and a Concentration check ({@dc 15}). You gain a +5 bonus to the roll if you are extremely familiar with the location or can see it from where you currently are in the Dreamtime.
To another person in the Dreamtime, someone dreamjumping seems to disappear in a blur.
With a successful {@skill Perception} check ({@dc 25}), you can tell where the person dreamjumped to, and attempt to follow even if you have never seen or been to the target location.

optionalfeature.name: Dreamorph
entries: You are able to use your mastery of the Dreamtime to force a change onto another person, causing that change to occur whenever they are in the dream world.

You can cause an effect that you have created using the {@optfeature Dreambend|TGTT} ability to have a sustained duration within the Dreamtime without having to hold it. Furthermore this effect will take place whenever that person dreams themselves into the Dreamtime while it remains in effect.
You may release a person morphed at any time with a free action, but it must take place in the Dreamtime and both you and the subject must be present.

entries.name: Using Dreamorph
entries: Make a Concentration check as an action:

items: Target without Dreambend: {@dc 20} to bind items, {@dc 30} to bind physical features.
Target with Dreambend: Opposed Concentration check. On failure, target gains a final Wisdom saving throw (DC = 10 + your Intelligence modifier + amount your check exceeded theirs).

entries: If the target fails, they are changed and can't change back without your help or until the duration ends.
Duration: Your level + the number you exceeded the Concentration check by, in days.

optionalfeature.name: Dreamforge
entries: You can bring into dreamlike existence objects imbued with magical properties, harnessing the boundless potential of your imagination within the dream realm.
To create an object, you must (as an action) roll a Concentration check with a DC determined by the item's rarity:

items: Simple magical items (common or uncommon): {@dc 15}
Moderate magical items (rare): {@dc 20}
Complex magical items (very rare or legendary): {@dc 25}

entries: You must be familiar with the item to some extent. If highly familiar, you gain +5 to your roll.
Upon success, the item manifests within the Dreamtime and functions as intended, but effects are confined to the dream realm.
The item's existence is tied directly to your concentration on it (as if concentrating on a spell). Once concentration fails or you dismiss the item, it dissipates.

optionalfeature.name: Dreamake
entries: Drawing upon the boundless creativity of the Dreamtime, this ability enables you to craft intricate dream constructs ranging from fantastical creatures to immersive landscapes, limited only by your imagination and willpower.
As an action, make a Concentration check to create a dream construct:

items: Minor constructs (creatures CR up to 1, terrain up to 60 ft radius): {@dc 20}
Moderate constructs (creatures CR up to 4, terrain up to 240 ft radius): {@dc 25}
Major constructs (creatures CR up to 8, terrain up to 500 ft radius): {@dc 30}

entries: You must have some familiarity with the construct. The construct manifests at a point you can see and behaves according to your will.
The construct's existence is tied to your concentration (as a spell). Once concentration fails or you dismiss it, it dissipates from the Dreamtime.

optionalfeature.name: Dreamsnatch
entries: You can enter another person's dreams and forcefully pull them into the Dreamtime, keeping them there until you choose to release them.

entries.name: Pulling Into Dreamtime
entries: Using {@optfeature Dreamwatch|TGTT}, enter another person's dream. If you are able to resist being pulled into their dream, you can attempt to pull them into the Dreamtime by making a Concentration check ({@dc 25}). The target receives a Wisdom saving throw (DC = your check result) or an opposed Concentration check, their choice.
A creature who is being dreamsnatched can choose not to resist and come voluntarily—in such a case, the DC is only 15, and no save is rolled.

entries.name: Preventing Exit
entries: A character with this ability can prevent someone in the Dreamtime from leaving by making an opposed Concentration check against their attempt to leave.

entries.name: Pulling Into Your Dream
entries: You can use this ability to take someone from the Dreamtime into your own dreams, using the same check and mechanics.

If you leave the Dreamtime after snatching a target without dreamwalking abilities, they will eventually drift back into their own dreams (immediately if you snatched them into your own dream and then left). This takes {@dice 1d4} hours in dream time.

optionalfeature.name: Dreamshare
entries: Using this ability, you create a shared dream, inviting multiple participants to interact within a safe and controlled environment. The shared dream is an extension of your own dream, rather than a separate plane like The Dreamtime.

Creating the shared dream is a free action you take as you fall asleep. When you activate this ability, you choose any number of creatures you know who will be invited. Chosen creatures automatically locate your dream using {@optfeature Dreamwatch|TGTT} and may enter willingly.
All participants retain full consciousness and control, allowing clear communication. Participants are aware they are in a shared dream and may choose to leave by waking.

entries.name: Control Over the Shared Dream
entries: {@b Expulsion:} You can attempt to expel any participant at will. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC = 10 + your Intelligence modifier + your proficiency bonus). On failure, they are immediately ejected and wake up. A creature that succeeds cannot be expelled again for 24 hours.

{@b Environmental Manipulation:} You can reshape the dream's terrain, objects, or ambient conditions as an action. Make a Concentration check: {@dc 10} for minor changes (lighting, simple objects), {@dc 15} for major changes (terrain features, large objects), {@dc 20} for extreme changes (significant alterations, surreal effects).
You must maintain concentration on the shared dream (as a spell). If broken, the shared dream ends and all participants immediately wake up.
If a hostile entity or Dreamwalker attempts to invade, they must first locate it using {@optfeature Dreamwatch|TGTT} and then make an opposed Concentration check against you to gain partial control.
{@b Safety:} The shared dream is inherently safe—no real-world harm can occur unless abilities like {@optfeature Dreamsnatch|TGTT} connect it to The Dreamtime.
{@b Important:} While actively interacting within the dream, participants cannot benefit from rest.

optionalfeature.name: Dreamveil
entries: You develop the ability to protect your dreams from outside influence—to hide your dreams from prying minds and shield them from unwanted attention.
As a bonus action on your turn or as part of falling asleep, you may activate your dreamveil and gain the following benefits:

items: {@b Hidden Dream:} You attempt to make your dreams invisible to others with dreamwalking abilities. When you fall asleep, roll a Concentration check; the result is added to the DC others have when attempting to locate your dreams with {@optfeature Dreamwatch|TGTT} or snatch you using {@optfeature Dreamsnatch|TGTT}.

{@b Mind Shelter:} You gain extra protections from attempts to invade your mind. Spells and abilities that affect your mind (deal psychic damage, cause mental conditions, or directly mention your mind and dreams) are rolled at disadvantage, or you roll against them with advantage.

entries: The effects last for 8 hours. Once you use this ability, you must take a long rest before using it again.

optionalfeature.name: Daydream
entries: You can enter the Dreamtime while still partially awake in the real world, interacting with both realms simultaneously.
With a Concentration check ({@dc 20}), you can fall into a sleeplike trance. While in this trance, you may enter the Dreamtime as if you were asleep.

However, you maintain a vague awareness of your surroundings in the real world, and can converse with people there—perhaps describing what you see in the Dreamtime, or passing questions and answers back and forth between the real world and someone you have met in the Dreamtime.
You may not take any actions in the real world other than conversing with those around you. Loud noise, pain, or movement will snap you out of your trance.

race.name: Nyuidj
entries: Nyuidj (pronounced "NEW-idge") are mortal creatures who have developed a profound connection to the Dreamtime. No one truly knows how they came to be—some think they are spirits of the dead that somehow wandered into The Dreamtime and fused with unsuspecting visitors, others think they are creations of travelers who went out of control.

entries.name: Age
entries: Nyuidj mature and age at the same rate as regular humans, though their connection to the Dreamtime often makes them appear timeless.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Nyuidj tend toward Neutral alignments. Their fluid understanding of morality in the dream world can make them difficult to categorize by typical ethical standards.

entries.name: Size
entries: Nyuidj are of medium size, with their height and weight comparable to humans.

entries.name: Dual Mind
entries: Your mind exists partially in the waking world and partially in dreams. You have advantage on all Wisdom saving throws.

entries.name: Mental Discipline
entries: Your connection to the Dreamtime has fortified your mind against intrusion. You have resistance to psychic damage.

entries.name: Mind Link
entries: You can speak telepathically to any creature you can see, provided the creature is within a number of feet of you equal to 10 times your level. You need to share a language with the creature for it to understand your telepathic utterances.

When you're using this trait to speak telepathically to a creature, you can use your action to give that creature the ability to speak telepathically with you for 1 hour or until you end this effect as an action. You can give this ability to only one creature at a time.

entries.name: Dreamer
entries: Nyuidj always dream lucid dreams and have an affinity to the Dreamtime.
You gain the {@optfeature Dreamwalk|TGTT} ability.
You then learn one other Dreamwalker ability of your choice from the list below. You must meet any prerequisites.

items: {@optfeature Dreamwatch|TGTT}
{@optfeature Dreambend|TGTT}
{@optfeature Dreamjump|TGTT} ({@i Prerequisite:} {@optfeature Dreamwalk|TGTT} ability)
{@optfeature Dreamorph|TGTT} ({@i Prerequisite:} {@optfeature Dreambend|TGTT} ability)
{@optfeature Dreamforge|TGTT} ({@i Prerequisite:} {@optfeature Dreambend|TGTT} ability)
{@optfeature Dreamake|TGTT} ({@i Prerequisite:} {@optfeature Dreambend|TGTT} ability, {@optfeature Dreamforge|TGTT} ability)
{@optfeature Dreamsnatch|TGTT} ({@i Prerequisite:} {@optfeature Dreamwatch|TGTT} ability)
{@optfeature Dreamshare|TGTT} ({@i Prerequisite:} {@optfeature Dreamwatch|TGTT} ability)
{@optfeature Dreamveil|TGTT} ({@i Prerequisite:} {@optfeature Dreamwatch|TGTT} ability, {@optfeature Dreamwalk|TGTT} ability)
{@optfeature Daydream|TGTT} ({@i Prerequisite:} {@optfeature Dreamwalk|TGTT} ability)

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and two other languages of your choice.

entries.name: The Affinity
entries: Each child develops a small magical talent by the time they hit puberty, which develops more as they practice it and grow.

Choose one cantrip, except for {@spell Eldritch Blast}, {@spell Shillelagh}, {@spell Vicious Mockery}, or {@spell Guidance}. Your spellcasting ability for it is Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma (your choice).

entries.name: Dauntless Heritage
entries: The children are a race who experienced many hardships through the ages, always to stand up again and continue fighting for their future.
You gain advantage on saving throws against being frightened.

entries.name: Age
entries: Children reach adulthood in their late teens and live around a century.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Children tend toward no particular alignment.

entries.name: Size
entries: Children vary widely in height and build, from barely 5 feet to well over 6 feet tall. Regardless of your position in that range, your size is Medium.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common. Common evolved from the old language of the empire and is used throughout the lands of Thelemar, with each country using a different dialect of the general common language.
You speak one dialect of common that is appropriate for your culture. The options are Lexalian, Olympian, Jaknian, Clairnian and Hubian.

entries.name: Feywild Connection
entries: A dendulra has a special magical connection to the feywild that allows them to channel some of its magic. At 1st level you gain the cantrip {@spell druidcraft}. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast the spell {@spell entangle} once per day without using material components or a spell slot.
Your spellcasting ability for these spells is Charisma.

entries.name: Fey Ancestry
entries: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can't put you to sleep.

entries.name: Bubbling Energy
entries: Due to their innate connection to the Feywild, the dendulras don't need as much sleep as normal creatures. You can complete a long rest in 4 hours of sleep, instead of 8.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: Born of magical creatures that see the world through different eyes, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Step of Feywild
entries: The magic of the Feywild coursing through their veins allows the dendulras to channel said energy in unique ways.
As a bonus action, you can magically teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. Once you use this trait, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

When you reach 3rd level, this ability grows: When you use your Step, up to two creatures of your choice that you can see within 10 feet of where you appear must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you for 1 minute, or until you or your companions deal any damage to it. The DC for the saving throw equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier.

entries.name: Age
entries: Dendulras will reach adulthood in their late teens, but live to be as old as 400 years.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Dendulras often tend toward Chaotic Alignments.

entries.name: Size
entries: Dendulras vary widely in height and build, from barely 4 feet to well over 6 feet tall. Regardless of your position in that range, your size is Medium.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common (General and one dialect) and Sylvan, language of fey creatures. Thanks to your fey blood you can also, through sounds and gestures, communicate simple ideas with Small or smaller beasts.

entries.name: Shadow Affinity
entries: Descathi are kins to the shadows, being able to hide with ease. You can attempt to hide even when you are only lightly obscured by foliage, heavy rain, falling snow, mist, and other natural phenomena.

entries.name: Fey Ancestry
entries: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can't put you to sleep.

entries.name: Shadowfell Blood
entries: You have resistance to necrotic damage.

entries.name: Hollow Rest
entries: The Descathi are fueled by magic from the shadowfell that gives them energy, despite their shriveled bodies. You can complete a long rest in 4 hours of sleep, instead of 8.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: Born of a world wrapped in shadows, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Step of Shadowfell
entries: Their innate connection to the shadowfell allows the descathi to draw upon it and use it in unique ways.
As a bonus action, you can magically teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. Once you use this trait, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

When you reach 3rd level, this ability grows: When you use your Step, up to two creatures of your choice that you can see within 10 feet of you before you teleport must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be frightened of you until the end of your next turn. The DC for the saving throw equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier.

entries.name: Age
entries: Descathi will reach adulthood in their late teens, but live to be as old as 400 years.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Descathi often tend toward Chaotic Alignments.

entries.name: Size
entries: Descathi vary widely in height and build, from barely 4 feet to well over 6 feet tall. Regardless of your position in that range, your size is Medium.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common (General and one dialect) and Stygian, language of shadow creatures.

entries.name: Elemental Empowerment
entries: Because of your elemental heritage, you have a duality when it comes to damage from the element of your ancestry. You have resistance to your elemental type, as only your mortal half is damaged by the element, but your primordial half is empowered by it. Whenever you are damaged by this element from a source other than yourself, you can use your reaction to deal {@damage 1d6} of damage of the same element type in your next attack, be it ranged or melee, weapon or magic.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus.
The damage increases to {@damage 2d6} when your proficiency bonus is +3, {@damage 3d6} at +4, {@damage 4d6} at +5, and {@damage 5d6} at +6.

entries.name: Age
entries: Genasi mature at about the same rate as Children and reach adulthood in their late teens. They live somewhat longer than humans do, easily reaching into their second century.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Varied and self-confident, many Genasi tend to follow the paths of their elemental heritage. Air genasi trend toward chaotic, Earth toward lawful, Water toward neutral, and Fire toward extremes.

entries.name: Size
entries: Genasi are as varied as their mortal parents, exhibiting physical builds and characteristics of them. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common (General and one dialect) and Primordial. Additionally, you speak the elemental dialect language of the element you are associated with.

entries.name: Eyes of the Abyss
entries: The blood of fiends give tieflings a special eyesight. You have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Age
entries: Tieflings mature at the same rate as Children but live a few years longer.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Tieflings might not have an innate tendency toward evil, but many of them end up there. Evil or not, an independent nature inclines many tieflings toward a chaotic alignment.

entries.name: Size
entries: Tieflings are about the same size and build as Children. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common (General and one dialect) and Mictlanian. Mictlanian is the language of hell, one spoken by fiends and creatures of the abyss.

entries.name: Dragon Scales
entries: While you are not wearing armor, your AC equals 13 + your Dexterity modifier.

entries.name: Ancestral Affinity
entries: You have resistance to psychic damage and advantage on saving throws against effects causing the frightened condition.

entries.name: Draconic Nature
entries: You are descended from either a Fear Dragon or a Safety Dragon. This choice is made at 1st level and determines your unique abilities, reflecting the deep imprint of your ancestor's domain.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Age
entries: Young dragonborn grow quickly. They walk hours after hatching, attain the size and development of a 10-year-old Children kid by the age of 3, and reach adulthood by 15. They live to be around 80.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Dragonborn tend to extremes, making a conscious choice for one side or the other in the cosmic war between good and evil, law and chaos. They inherit this extremity from their draconic ancestors.

entries.name: Size
entries: Dragonborn are taller and heavier than Children, standing well over 6 feet tall and averaging almost 250 pounds. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common (General and one dialect) and Draconic. Draconic is thought to be one of the oldest languages and is often used in the study of magic. The language sounds harsh to most other creatures and includes numerous hard consonants and sibilants.

items.name: Fey Ancestry
entries: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can't put you to sleep.

entries.name: Bite
entries: Your bite is a natural weapon, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. On a hit, it deals {@damage 1d4} + your Strength modifier piercing damage.

entries.name: Thrill of the Hunt
entries: If you can see or hear a creature that is below its maximum number of hit points, you can use your bonus action to mark that target for a number of hours equal to your proficiency bonus. For the duration, you have advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}, {@skill Survival}) checks you make to track or locate the target, and if you are within 30 feet of the target, you can grant an ally advantage on a melee attack roll they make against the target using your reaction, which consumes the mark.
You can use this trait once, and regain use of it when you complete a long rest.

entries.name: Rampage
entries: When you reduce a creature to 0 hit points with a melee attack on your turn, you can take a bonus action to move up to half your speed and make a bite attack.

entries.name: Carrion Feeder
entries: You have advantage on Constitution saving throws against disease.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Age
entries: Gnolls mature at 5 years, and live up to three decades.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: The Grasp of Yeenoghu and keen hunting instincts makes many Gnolls chaotic evil.

entries.name: Size
entries: Gnolls are between 6½ and 7½ feet tall, and weigh 200-280 pounds. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Speed
entries: You have a base walking speed of 30 feet.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common (General and one dialect) and Mictlanian. Mictlanian is the language of hell, one spoken by fiends and creatures of the abyss.

entries.name: Ogre Toughness
entries: Your hit point maximum increases by 1, and it increases by 1 again whenever you gain a level.

entries.name: Enraged
entries: When you are below half your hit point maximum, you can use a bonus action to expand your critical hit range to 19–20 for 1 minute. Once you use this trait, you must finish a long rest before you can do so again, and you suffer one level of exhaustion when it ends.

entries.name: Powerful Build
entries: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Age
entries: Half Ogres mature at 15 years, and live up to seven decades.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Ogre blood pushes many half ogres towards chaos.

entries.name: Size
entries: Half Ogres are between 7 and 9 feet tall, and weigh 300-500 pounds. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Speed
entries: You have a base walking speed of 30 feet.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common (General and one dialect) and Jötunn, language of ogres, firbolgs and goliath.

entries.name: Burrower
entries: You have a burrow speed of 15 feet through non-rocky soil. If you have a pickaxe or shovel, this burrow speed increases to 20 feet.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light, but only shades of gray.

entries.name: Center of Attention
entries: As an action on your turn, you can distract nearby foes. This can be done by cowering pathetically, shrieking furiously, boasting arrogantly, or through different means. Until the end of your next turn, your allies gain advantage on attack rolls against enemies within 10 feet of you that can see you.
Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Skilled Trapper
entries: You are proficient with {@item thieves' tools|PHB}, and whenever you make an ability check to create traps, you can add double your proficiency bonus to the check, rather than your normal proficiency bonus.

entries.name: Fleeting Courage
entries: When you succeed on a saving throw, your next attack roll or saving throw you make before the end of your next turn has advantage.

entries.name: Age
entries: Kobolds reach adulthood at age 6 and can live up to 120 years, but rarely do so. Those that live within or near draconic communities tend to live longer.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Their reliance on the strength of their group makes them tend toward law.

entries.name: Size
entries: Kobolds are between 2 and 3 feet tall and weigh between 25 and 35 pounds. Your size is Small.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common (General and one dialect) and Draconic.

items.name: Powerful Build
entries: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

entries.name: Creature Type
entries: You are an Undead. You are immune to the {@classFeature Turn Undead|Cleric||2|PHB} ability.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Deathless Nature
entries: You do not need to breathe or eat.

entries.name: Sentry's Rest
entries: You do not sleep. Instead, you can finish a long rest in 4 hours if you spend those hours in light activity, such as keeping watch or standing still. Additionally, magic cannot put you to sleep.

entries.name: Spectral Fortitude
entries: You have resistance to Poison and Necrotic damage. You also have advantage on saving throws against the {@condition poisoned} condition.

entries.name: Radiant Frailty
entries: You have vulnerability to Radiant damage.

entries.name: Language of the Dead
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common, Mictlanian, and one other language of your choice. When speaking with an undead creature, you can choose to pour supernatural intent into your words. If you do so, the undead acts as if affected by the {@spell suggestion} spell  and always understands you, regardless of language barriers.

entries.name: Trained
entries: You gain proficiency with light and medium armor, ranged martial weapons, and polearms.

entries.name: Pillars of Society
entries: You gain proficiency in one of the following skills: Religion, Athletics, or Might. When you make a Religion check related to the Olympian Mythos, you can add double your proficiency bonus to the roll.

entries.name: Speak to the Heavens
entries: You can speak Celestial (you cannot read or write it).

entries.name: Soft Skills
entries: You gain proficiency in one of the following skills: Deception, Insight, Sleight of Hand, History, or Culture. When you make a History check related to Clairnian politics, you can add double your proficiency bonus to the roll.

entries.name: Bag of Tricks
entries: You gain proficiency with either the Forgery Kit or the Disguise Kit.

entries.name: Trade Secrets
entries: You gain proficiency in one of the following skills: Persuasion or Investigation. When you make a Persuasion check while haggling, you can add double your proficiency bonus to the roll.

entries.name: Tools of the Trade
entries: You gain proficiency with two artisan's tools of your choice.

entries.name: Happy Generalists
entries: You gain proficiency in two of the following skills: Persuasion, Investigation, Deception, Insight, Sleight of Hand, History, Religion, Athletics.

entries.name: Trilinguals
entries: You gain proficiency in two languages of your choice from the following: Avian (Tengu), Jötunn, Gob, Loxodon, Tabaxi, Minotaur.

entries.name: Expert Swimmers
entries: You gain a swim speed equal to your walking speed, and you can hold your breath for twice the normal duration.

entries.name: Unending Breath
entries: You can hold your breath indefinitely while you're not incapacitated, and have endless breath while breathing out or in. You breathe only as a habit, and not as a natural need for air.

entries.name: Spirit of the Winds
entries: You know the {@spell gust|XGE} cantrip and can cast it without verbal or material components. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast {@spell feather fall} once as a 1st-level spell, and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. When you reach 5th level, you can cast {@spell gust of wind} once, and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest.
Constitution is your casting ability for these spells.

entries.name: Lightning Empowerment
entries: Your elemental empowerment is to Lightning damage.

entries.name: Languages
entries: In addition to Common and Primordial, you speak Auran, the dialect language of air and flight based creatures and the Djinni of the elemental plane of air.

entries.name: Earth Walk
entries: You can move across difficult terrain made of earth or stone without expending extra movement.

entries.name: Soul of the Earth
entries: You know the {@spell mold earth|XGE} cantrip and can cast it without verbal or material components. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast {@spell earth tremor|XGE} once as a 1st-level spell, and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. When you reach 5th level, you can cast {@spell earthbind|XGE} once, and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest.
Constitution is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

entries.name: Acid Empowerment
entries: Your elemental empowerment is to Acid damage.

entries.name: Languages
entries: In addition to Common and Primordial, you speak Terran, the dialect language of earth based creatures and the Dao of the elemental plane of earth.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. Your ties to the Elemental Plane of Fire make your darkvision unusual: everything you see in darkness is in a shade of red.

entries.name: Heart of Fire
entries: You know the {@spell produce flame} cantrip and can cast it without verbal or material components. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast {@spell burning hands} once as a 1st-level spell, and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. When you reach 5th level, you can cast {@spell pyrotechnics} once, and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest.
Constitution is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

entries.name: Fire Empowerment
entries: Your elemental empowerment is to fire damage.

entries.name: Languages
entries: In addition to Common and Primordial, you speak Ignan, the dialect language of fire based creatures and Efreeti of the elemental plane of fire.

entries.name: Amphibious
entries: You can breathe underwater and have a swim speed the same speed as your walking speed.

entries.name: Anima of Water
entries: You know the {@spell shape water} cantrip and can cast it without verbal or material components. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast {@spell create or destroy water} once as a 1st-level spell, and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. When you reach 5th level, you can cast {@spell Snilloc's snowball swarm} as a 3rd-level spell once, and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest.
Your spellcasting ability for these spells is Constitution.

entries.name: Cold Empowerment
entries: Your elemental empowerment is to cold damage.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You speak Aquan, the dialect language of water based sea creatures and the Marid of the elemental plane of water.

entries.name: Mental Fortitude
entries: You have resistance to psychic damage.

entries.name: Dark Queen's Blessing
entries: You have advantage on all Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws against magic.

entries.name: Rangda's Legacy
entries: You know the {@spell mind sliver|TCE} cantrip. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast the spell {@spell Tasha's caustic brew|TCE} as a 2nd-level spell once with this trait and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. When you reach 5th level, you can cast the {@spell Tasha's mind whip|TCE} spell once with this trait and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest.
Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

entries.name: Warm Embrace
entries: You have resistance to cold damage.

entries.name: Temptation's Guidance
entries: You are proficient in the Persuasion skill, and may add double your proficiency bonus whenever you are using the skill.

entries.name: Mara's Legacy
entries: You know the {@spell friends} cantrip. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast the spell {@spell charm person} as a 2nd-level spell once with this trait and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. When you reach 5th level, you can cast the {@spell suggestion} spell once with this trait and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest.
Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

entries.name: Hellish Resistance
entries: You have resistance to fire damage.

entries.name: Infernal Luck
entries: You may reroll one ability check or attack roll you make; you may do so only before you know whether the roll was successful.
Once you use this trait you must complete a long rest before using it again.

entries.name: Asmodeus' Legacy
entries: You know the {@spell thaumaturgy} cantrip. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast the spell {@spell hellish rebuke} as a 2nd-level spell once with this trait and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. When you reach 5th level, you can cast the {@spell blindness/deafness} spell once with this trait and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest.
Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

entries.name: Terrifying Exhalation
entries: When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can replace one of your attacks with an exhalation of terrifying energy in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 + your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus). On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 1d10} psychic damage and has disadvantage on the first attack roll or saving throw it makes before the start of your next turn. On a successful save, the creature takes half damage. The damage increases to {@damage 2d10} at 5th level, {@damage 3d10} at 11th level, and {@damage 4d10} at 17th level. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Aura of Dread
entries: Starting at 5th level, as an action, you can emanate an aura of subtle dread in a 15-foot radius for 1 minute. Hostile creatures that start their turn in the aura must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 + your Charisma modifier + your proficiency bonus) or be frightened of you until the end of your next turn. A creature that succeeds is immune to the aura for 24 hours. You can use this trait once per short or long rest.

entries.name: Resonant Ward
entries: When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can replace one of your attacks with an exhalation of resonant energy in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 + your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus). On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 1d10} psychic damage and has disadvantage on the first attack roll or saving throw it makes before the start of your next turn. On a successful save, the creature takes half damage. The damage increases to {@damage 2d10} at 5th level, {@damage 3d10} at 11th level, and {@damage 4d10} at 17th level. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Aura of Protection
entries: Starting at 5th level, as a bonus action, you can emanate an aura of bolstering energy in a 15-foot radius for 1 minute. You and friendly creatures that start their turn in the aura gain temporary hit points equal to your proficiency bonus. These temporary hit points last until the start of their next turn. You can use this trait once per short or long rest.

itemUpgrade.name: Alchemist
entries: While you are wearing this armor, you regain 2 additional hit points when you drink a potion of healing.

itemUpgrade.name: Mariner
entries: This weapon or ammunition fired with it magically repels water. Attack rolls you make using it aren't made with disadvantage for being underwater.

itemUpgrade.name: Thief
entries: A favorite of cutpurses and thieves. Once per day while wearing this armor you can reroll a failed Dexterity check. If you do so, you must use the new roll.

itemUpgrade.name: Warrior
entries: Popular among mercenaries and soldiers, this gemstone ensures you keep your sword in your hand. While you are wielding this weapon, you can't be disarmed of this weapon while you are conscious.

itemUpgrade.name: Arrow-Catcher
entries: This gemstone has three charges. While you are wielding this shield, when you or a friendly creature within 10 feet of you are the target of a ranged weapon attack you can expend a charge as a reaction to impose disadvantage on the attack. You must choose to do so before the roll. The gemstone regains {@dice 1d3} charges daily at dawn.

itemUpgrade.name: Bound Armor
entries: As a bonus action while you aren't wearing armor, you can cause this armor to appear in a flash of light around you—instantly donning it. Also as a bonus action while you are wearing this armor, you can cause the armor to vanish entirely, instantly doffing it.

itemUpgrade.name: Bound Weapon
entries: As a bonus action, you can cause this weapon to disappear or appear instantly in a free hand.

itemUpgrade.name: Cat
entries: While wielding this weapon, as a bonus action you can activate this gem by speaking its command phrase. For 1 hour, you can see normally in darkness to a distance of 120 feet. Once used, this property can't be used again until the following dawn.

itemUpgrade.name: Chaos
entries: A popular project among apprentice artificers, this gem embodies the chaos of unbound magic. When you score a critical hit with an attack using this weapon, roll on the Wild Magic Surge table in the Player's Handbook to create a magical effect. If the effect is a spell that requires concentration, it doesn't require concentration in this case and lasts for its full duration.

itemUpgrade.name: Daywalker

item: a suit of armor with a helmet or hood

entries: While wearing this armor with the hood drawn, you are unaffected by sunlight.

itemUpgrade.name: Elemental Shield

item: a suit of armor or a shield

entries: When you first affix this gem to a suit of armor or a shield, choose one of the following damage types: acid, cold, fire, lightning, or thunder. While wearing (armor) or wielding (shield) this item, whenever you take damage of the chosen type you can use your reaction to reduce the damage by an amount equal to twice your character level + your Constitution modifier and suffer one level of exhaustion. You can change the chosen damage type over the course of a short or long rest by spending at least 30 minutes in contact with the gemstone.

itemUpgrade.name: Featherfoot
entries: While you are wearing (armor) or wielding (weapon or shield) this item, you have a standing jump distance equal to your current walking speed. Each foot you clear while jumping costs a foot of movement, and you can't jump further than your remaining movement in a turn.

itemUpgrade.name: Knock
entries: While you are wearing this armor, you can rap your fist on an object to cast the {@spell knock} spell on it. Once used, this property can't be used again until the following dawn.

itemUpgrade.name: Nondetection
entries: While you are wearing (armor) or wielding (weapon or shield) this item, you are hidden from divination magic. You can't be targeted by such magic or perceived through magical scrying sensors.

itemUpgrade.name: Serpent
entries: When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can attempt to poison it. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or become {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. The creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns. On success, the effect ends. Once used, this property can't be used again until the following dawn.

itemUpgrade.name: Bastion

item: a gauntleted suit of armor or a shield

entries: While you are wearing (armor) or wielding (shield) this item, you can use a bonus action to press your fist to the ground and speak its command word, causing a 10-foot radius immobile dome of force to spring into existence around and above you that remains stationary and lasts for 1 minute. The dome automatically repels nonmagical ranged attacks attempting to pass through it, and the area inside the dome is considered difficult terrain for hostile creatures. Once used, this property can't be used again until the following dawn.

itemUpgrade.name: Berserker
entries: When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can expend a number of Hit Dice up to half your character level (rounded down). For each Hit Die spent this way, roll the die and add it to the damage of the attack. You take an amount of damage equal to the damage dealt in this way, including any extra damage from a critical hit. This damage to you can't be reduced or prevented in any way. Once used, this property can't be used again until the following dawn.

itemUpgrade.name: Chalice
entries: This gemstone stores spells cast into it, holding them until the attuned wearer uses them. The gem can store up to 2 levels worth of spells at a time. Any creature can cast a spell of 1st through 2nd level into the gem by touching the gem as the spell is cast. The spell has no effect, other than to be stored in the gem. If the gem can't hold the spell, the spell is expended without effect. The level of the spell used to cast the spell determines how much space it uses. While you are wearing (armor) or wielding (shield) this item, you can cast any spell stored in the gemstone. The spell uses the slot level, spell save DC, spell attack bonus, and the spellcasting ability of the original caster, but is otherwise treated as if you cast the spell. The spell cast from the gem is no longer stored in it, freeing up space.

itemUpgrade.name: Death
entries: Whenever you kill a Small or Medium humanoid with an attack using this weapon, it rises immediately as a zombie under your control with 1 hit point, as per the {@spell animate dead} spell. The creature uses the {@creature zombie} statistics. It remains animate for 1 minute, after which time it collapses and dies.

itemUpgrade.name: Hunt

item: a simple or martial weapon with the ammunition property

entries: While you are wielding this weapon, you can use an action on your turn to mark a creature you can see within 90 feet of you. For 1 hour, when you hit the marked creature with a ranged attack using this weapon, you can use a bonus action to teleport to an unoccupied space within 30 feet of it that you can see. Once used, this property can't be used again until the following dawn.

itemUpgrade.name: Journey
entries: While you are wearing this armor, your speed increases by 10 feet. In addition, you can travel at a fast pace without suffering the normal penalty to passive Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks, and your food and water requirements are halved.

itemUpgrade.name: Magebane
entries: This gemstone has three charges. When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can expend a charge to cause any spell effects of 3rd level or lower on the target to end. Additionally, for each spell of 4th level or higher on the target, make an ability check using your weapon ability. The DC equals 10 + the spell's level. On a successful check, the spell ends. The gemstone regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.

itemUpgrade.name: Phoenix
entries: When you're reduced to 0 hit points and start dying while wearing this armor, this gem immediately casts a {@spell fireball} centered on you. At the start of your next turn, instead of rolling a death saving throw, you regain {@dice 1d6} hit points. Once used, this property can't be used again until the following dawn.

itemUpgrade.name: Soultrap
entries: When you kill a creature with a challenge rating equal to or higher than your level with this weapon, you regain one expended spell slot. This spell can't be of a level higher than your proficiency bonus. Once used, this property can't be used again until the following dawn.

itemUpgrade.name: Superconductor
entries: This gemstone captures and stores magical potential for short-term use, and can hold a number of spell levels equal to the proficiency bonus of the creature attuned to it. When you are targeted by a spell while holding this weapon, the gemstone gains a number of charges equal to the level of the spell, up to the maximum the gemstone can hold. When you hit a creature with an attack using this weapon, you can expend any number of charges to deal an extra {@dice 1d6} force damage per charge expended. This item loses any accrued charges when you finish a short or long rest.

itemUpgrade.name: Warmage

item: a suit of armor or a shield

entries: This gemstone has three charges. While you are wearing (armor) or wielding (shield) this item and fail a concentration saving throw that you make to maintain concentration on a spell due to taking damage, you can expend a charge as a reaction to reroll the saving throw. If you do so, you must use the new roll. You can expend a spell slot of 1st level or higher to recover an expended charge.

itemUpgrade.name: Blood Weapon

item: a simple or martial melee weapon

entries: When you score a critical hit with an attack using this weapon, you regain a number of hit points equal to the damage dealt to a maximum of the hit points lost by the target. No effect against constructs or undead.

itemUpgrade.name: Displacement

item: a suit of light or medium armor

entries: When you take damage from a weapon attack, you can teleport 30 feet to an unoccupied space that you can see. Once you teleport in this way, you can't do so again until the start of your next turn.

itemUpgrade.name: Dragonbane
entries: When you hit a dragon with this weapon, it takes an extra {@dice 2d6} damage of the weapon's type and must succeed on a Strength saving throw or its flying speed (if any) is reduced to 0 feet until the end of its next turn. If the target is airborne, it descends at 60 feet per round until it reaches the ground or the effect ends. For the purpose of this weapon, "dragon" refers to any creature with the dragon type, including dragon turtles and wyverns.

itemUpgrade.name: Earthshaker

item: a simple or martial melee weapon with the heavy property

entries: While you are wielding this weapon, you can use an action to strike a point on the ground within reach, creating a seismic disturbance as per the {@spell earthquake} spell with a duration of 1 round. Once used, this property can't be used again until the following dawn.

itemUpgrade.name: Giant Slayer
entries: When you hit a giant with this weapon, it takes an extra {@dice 2d6} damage of the weapon's type and must succeed on a Strength saving throw or fall {@condition prone}. For the purpose of this weapon, "giant" refers to any creature with the giant type, including ettins and trolls.

itemUpgrade.name: Mark/Recall
entries: While you are wearing this armor, you can use an action to magically mark a surface on which you are standing. While a location is marked in this way, you can spend 1 minute concentrating on it (as if concentrating on a spell). At the end of the period, you and up to five willing creatures within 5 feet of you teleport to the marked location, and the mark vanishes. Once you mark a location, you can't do so again until the following dawn. If you mark another location while you already have a mark, the older mark vanishes.

itemUpgrade.name: Overshield
entries: While you are wearing this armor, you gain 8 temporary hit points at the start of each of your turns.

itemUpgrade.name: Retribution

item: a suit of armor or a shield

entries: While you are wearing (armor) or wielding (shield) this item, whenever you take damage from a creature you have advantage on your next attack roll against creatures of that type before the end of your next turn.

itemUpgrade.name: Wolfsbane

item: a simple or martial melee weapon

entries: In darkness, the unsheathed blade or head of this weapon sheds moonlight, creating bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 15 feet. In addition, when you hit a shapechanger with this weapon, it takes an extra {@dice 2d6} radiant damage and must succeed on a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, it immediately assumes its true form if it was shapechanged and it can't shapechange until the end of its next turn.

itemUpgrade.name: Force of Will
entries: While you are wearing this armor, you can't be affected by enchantment magic unless you choose to be.

itemUpgrade.name: Mime
entries: This gemstone is able to mimic the properties of magic items. A creature can spend a short rest focusing on a magic item and this gemstone. The magic item must be of the same type as the type of equipment to which this gem is affixed. Focus usually takes the form of quiet meditation or other appropriate activity. If the short rest is interrupted, the copying process fails. Otherwise, at the end of the short rest the gemstone gains the properties of the magic item, excluding any fixed bonuses such as those to attack and damage rolls, spell save DC, and Armor Class. If a property has charges, it is copied with no remaining charges. Only the properties of one magic item can be copied at a time. The mimicking process overwrites any existing copied properties. The process automatically fails when attempting to mimic the properties of artifacts.

itemUpgrade.name: Tempest
entries: Once on each of your turns, when you hit a creature with this weapon you can deal an extra {@dice 1d10} lightning damage. An arc of lightning then leaps from that target to as many as three additional creatures of your choice within 30 feet of it, which also take {@dice 1d10} lightning damage.

itemUpgrade.name: Volant
entries: While you are wearing this armor, you gain a flight (hover) speed equal to twice your walking speed.

itemUpgrade.name: Comfortable

item: light, medium, or heavy armor; conflicts with quick-release clasps tag

entries: Cushioned to maximise comfort, you can sleep in this armor and gain the benefits of a long rest.

itemUpgrade.name: Camouflaged

item: light or medium armor that lacks disadvantage on Stealth checks

entries: Originally made for hunters who stalked perceptive prey, this armor gives advantage on {@skill Stealth} checks if the camouflage reasonably fits the terrain.

itemUpgrade.name: Inflatable
entries: This armor has a cord that, when pulled, allows you to rise out of the water and float.

itemUpgrade.name: Oil Coating

item: medium or heavy armor; conflicts with glue coating tag

entries: You have advantage on checks to escape a grapple and disadvantage on checks to grapple others. This tag is automatically removed after 24 hours of wear or at the end of a combat where the wearer was grappled or grappled another creature.

itemUpgrade.name: Glue Coating

item: light, medium, or heavy armor; conflicts with oil coating tag

entries: You have disadvantage on checks to escape a grapple and advantage on checks to grapple others. This tag is automatically removed after 24 hours of wear or at the end of a combat where the wearer was grappled or grappled another creature.

itemUpgrade.name: Hardened
entries: A creature takes half damage from the critical hit dice if the damage is bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing from a nonmagical source.

itemUpgrade.name: Form Fitted
entries: You gain a +3 bonus to {@skill Acrobatics} checks.

itemUpgrade.name: Tailored

item: light armor; conflicts with grappling hook and mirrored tags

entries: This set of armor is disguised to look like a normal set of clothes. The player and DM can determine the style. A creature can discover the armor is a disguise on a successful {@skill Investigation} check ({@dc 15}).

itemUpgrade.name: Blessed
entries: You gain resistance to necrotic damage while wearing this armor.

itemUpgrade.name: Mirrored

item: any armor; conflicts with tailored tag

entries: You gain resistance to radiant damage while wearing this armor.

itemUpgrade.name: Specifically Tempered

item: any armor; conflicts with copper plated tag; can only be applied once

entries: You gain resistance to one of the following damage types of your choice: fire, cold, lightning, or thunder.

itemUpgrade.name: Super Spiked

item: medium or heavy armor with the spiked tag

entries: Your armor is completely covered in points of varying sizes. You gain the following benefits and drawbacks:

items: Your unarmed attacks deal {@damage 1d6} piercing damage.
When a creature hits you with a melee attack that isn't made with a reach weapon or a natural weapon, the attacker takes {@damage 1d6} piercing damage.
Any creature that attempts to grapple you does so with disadvantage.
When you roll a natural 1 on an {@skill Acrobatics} check, you take piercing damage equal to half your level.

itemUpgrade.name: Copper Plated

item: medium or heavy armor; conflicts with specifically tempered tag

entries: You gain resistance to acid damage while wearing this armor.

itemUpgrade.name: Diving Armor
entries: This armor contains a pocket of breathable air, allowing you to breathe underwater for 30 minutes at a time.

itemUpgrade.name: Heavy
entries: You have disadvantage on {@skill Acrobatics} checks and the armor's Strength minimum requirement increases by 1 (if there is no Strength minimum, it becomes 13). However, you cannot be pushed, pulled, or knocked {@condition prone} without a successful {@skill Athletics} check (DC 10 + your Athletics modifier) for melee, or an Arcana check for spells.

itemUpgrade.name: Magnetic
entries: On any melee attack against you with a metal weapon, an attack roll of 1 causes the metal weapon to stick to the armor.
