You can use a bonus action on your turn to attempt to disarm the attacker with a competing {@skill Athletics} check. Otherwise, the attacker can use a bonus action on their next turn to attempt a {@dc 12} {@skill Athletics} check to remove the weapon.

itemUpgrade.name: Hidden Crossbow
entries: Your armor has a hidden {@item light crossbow|PHB} with stats as described in the Player's Handbook. The crossbow cannot be disarmed since it is attached to you. A creature can make a {@dc 15} {@skill Investigation} check to notice the hidden crossbow.

itemUpgrade.name: Hidden Blades
entries: Your armor has two hidden {@item dagger|PHB|daggers} as described in the Player's Handbook. The daggers cannot be disarmed since they are attached to you. A creature can make a {@dc 15} {@skill Investigation} check to notice the sheathed daggers.

itemUpgrade.name: Grappling Hook

item: light armor; conflicts with tailored tag

entries: Your armor has a built-in grappling hook with a 30-foot range. Make a ranged attack against a target. If the attack hits, the type of target determines the outcome:

items: If the target is a structure or a creature of size Huge or larger, you can pull yourself through the air to the target.

If the target is a creature of size Large or smaller, you can attempt to pull the creature up to 10 feet closer to you. Both the target and you make competing {@skill Athletics} checks. The target moves 10 feet toward you if it loses the contested check.

entries: At the end of your turn, the grappling hook shoots back to your armor.

combatMethod.name: Bombing Formation
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. You form up and coordinate your salvos into a single devastating bombing run. When you fire a weapon with the {@itemProperty SLV|salvo} property while in this formation, any other ships in the formation can use their reaction to fire one weapon with the {@itemProperty SLV|salvo} property. Any effects that can shoot down or block weapons with the {@itemProperty SLV|salvo} property treat all munitions fired in this way as a single salvo.
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Lock On
entries: {@b Action (1 Stamina Point)}. Dogfighting is all about putting your enemy in your sights and staying out of theirs, taking the best shots while staying on target. Choose a target in your combat zone. The target makes a Dexterity or Strength saving throw (it’s choice) or you lock on to it. While locked on a target you have advantage on attacks against it. This lock on ends if you attack a different target or attempt to lock onto a different target.

combatMethod.name: Scouting Formation
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. As a bonus action, you and your starfighter squadron adopt a scouting formation. While in this formation, you and other pilots in the formation gain a bonus to Wisdom (Perception) checks and initiative rolls equal to your proficiency bonus.
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: A Game of Chicken
entries: {@b Action (1 Stamina Point)}. You fly head on against another starfighter, daring them to blink first and swerve out of the way. Choose another starfighter operating in the same combat zone and make an opposed Charisma check against it. On a success that starfighter’s pilot becomes {@condition dazed|tgtt} for the next {@dice 1d6} rounds. On a tied result you instead ram the chosen ship (see Star Captain's Manual).

combatMethod.name: Barrel Roll
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. Your starfighter spins and jukes in response to oncoming fire. As a reaction to being targeted by an attack, you force the attack to suffer disadvantage.

combatMethod.name: Escort Formation
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. The squadron forms up around a single member to protect them from fire. When you enter this formation, choose one of the starfighters in the formation. Until the start of your next turn, any attacks made against the chosen ship have disadvantage and attacks made against any other ships in the formation have advantage.

combatMethod.name: Lose Them
entries: {@b Action (1 Stamina Point)}. You break direct line of sight with your enemies by flying into an area that’s dangerous or obscured. You may only perform this starship method while operating in a combat zone with the obscuring property. Any ships with a lock on you must make a Wisdom saving throw or lose their lock.

combatMethod.name: Shake Them Off
entries: {@b Action (1 Stamina Point)}. The end of many a good pilot has been a turret or enemy starfighter that stayed locked on for just a bit too long. It’s best to shake them off with some evasive flying. One ship of your choice that is locked on to you makes a Dexterity saving throw or loses its lock on you.

combatMethod.name: Targeted Fire
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. Lining up the shots, you carefully pick out your target along a ship’s massive frame. Choose a deck or weapon system on a capital ship in your combat zone and make an attack targeting that ship. These attacks have disadvantage if the chosen deck is a bridge or engines deck. Every time you hit with an attack made this way, the target ship makes a Constitution saving throw with a –2 penalty for every 10 damage dealt by the attack. On a failed save a deck suffers the deck damaged critical malfunction, or a weapon system suffers the weapon system damaged critical malfunction. A deck that already has the deck damage critical malfunction suffers the deck disabled critical malfunction instead.

combatMethod.name: Dive Bomb
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. Throwing personal safety by the wayside, you dive directly at your target to ensure the hit regardless of what little time there is to pull away afterward. Make an attack with advantage against a target in the same combat zone. After the attack make a {@dc 15} Strength Saving Throw or Dexterity Saving Throw saving throw (your choice). On a failed save you ram the target of your attack (see Star Captain's Manual) unless your target can successfully save to avoid you.

combatMethod.name: Interceptor Formation
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You and your wingmen corral your targets to keep them from escaping. Whenever you successfully lock on to a starfighter, any other ships in the formation can use their reaction to lock on to that same target. While locked on ships in the formation have advantage on attacks against it. If you lose the lock on a target other ships in the formation lose that lock as well.
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Pull Up
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. Suddenly the empty space in front of you is occupied and you desperately try to pull your ship out of a collision course!When you fail a saving throw to avoid a collision or attempted ramming, use your reaction to reroll the failed saving throw, taking the new result.

combatMethod.name: Stealth Formation
entries: {@b Bonus Action (3 Stamina Points)}. As a bonus action, you and your starfighter squadron adopt a stealth formation. While in this formation, you and other pilots in the formation gain a bonus to Dexterity (Stealth) checks equal to your proficiency bonus.
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Ace Maneuvering
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. You pull a method either too brilliant or foolhardy for your tail to predict—putting them in your crosshairs. One starfighter of your choice currently locked on to you makes a Wisdom saving throw or its lock on you is broken and you lock on to it instead. While locked on to a target you have advantage on attacks made against it. This lock on ends if you attack a different target or attempt to lock on to a different target.

combatMethod.name: Hit the Brakes
entries: {@b Reaction (3 Stamina Points)}. You suddenly decelerate, causing your foe to fly past you and into your gunsights. Choose another starfighter operating in the same combat zone which has locked on to you. The starfighter makes a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, you make an attack against the target.

combatMethod.name: Trench Run
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You fly dangerously close to avoid the guns firing above you and line up a perfect shot, but doing so puts you right in the crosshairs of the close range guns. You may only perform this starship method while operating in a combat zone surrounding a capital ship. Until the end of your next turn, any attacks that target you have disadvantage, and you have disadvantage on saving throws against weapons with the {@itemProperty ASF|anti-starfighter} property. Any attacks you make during your next turn have advantage and their critical hit range is increased by 1 (for example a weapon that would score a critical hit on a 20 instead scores a critical hit on an attack roll of 19–20).

combatMethod.name: Quantum Slip
entries: {@b Bonus Action (3 Stamina Points)}. You use irregularities in the space-time continuum to continuously jink your ship, making it impossible to target you. Until the start of your next turn, when an attack would hit your starfighter, it misses instead, and when you would fail a saving throw, you succeed instead.

combatMethod.name: Trust Your Instincts
entries: {@b Bonus Action (3 Stamina Points)}. Dispensing with targeting systems and electronic aids, you focus your mind and make an attack out of pure instinct. Your next attack with a starfighter weapon before the start of your next turn automatically hits its target.

combatMethod.name: Catch Your Breath
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You have a limited well of stamina that you can draw on to protect yourself from harm. You regain hit points equal to {@dice 1d6} + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier (minimum 0).

combatMethod.name: Cleaving Swing
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. After you slam your weapon into an opponent you work the momentum of the blow into another strike at a second enemy nearby in a devastating display of might. When you hit with a melee weapon attack using a weapon with the {@itemProperty H|XPHB|heavy} property, you can use your reaction to make an additional melee weapon attack against a second creature that is also within your reach.

combatMethod.name: Heavy Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. As a bonus action, you enter a heavily-braced stance. While in this stance, you gain a bonus to Strength (Athletics) checks equal to your proficiency bonus, you gain a bonus equal to your proficiency bonus on saving throws made to resist being moved or knocked {@condition prone}, and you ignore the first 10 feet of difficult terrain you move through on your turn. This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Lean Into It
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. Not just commiting to an assault, you shoulder into it with all the strength you can bear and deliver hits that resonate in the very bones of your foes. When you activate this method, you take the Attack action and make a weapon attack, as well as any additional attacks granted by Extra Attack. Until the start of your next turn, when you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack it makes a Strength saving throw or it is knocked {@condition prone}.

combatMethod.name: Reactive Knockdown
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. Putting yourself in just the right position as you strike, your unbalancing hit sends a retreating opponent into a reeling stumble and then the ground. When a creature within your reach provokes an opportunity attack, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack. On a hit, the creature makes a Strength saving throw or it moves 10 feet in a straight line and is knocked {@condition prone}.

combatMethod.name: Shrug It Off
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. Girding yourself when your body is assaulted by more than clenched fists or sharp steel, you defy weakness and overcome. When an effect causes you to be {@condition frightened}, {@condition poisoned}, or {@condition stunned|tgtt}, you can use your reaction to prevent it.

combatMethod.name: Warding Wield
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. With seeming ease you whirl your weapon about your body, weaving it in a threatening pattern that makes your opponents hesitant to attack. Until the start of your next turn, your AC increases by 2.

combatMethod.name: Battering Strike
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. A powerful blow smashes a weapon from your enemy’s grasp. When you hit a creature using a melee weapon that has the {@itemProperty H|XPHB|heavy} property, you can use your reaction to force it to make a Strength saving throw. On a failure, a weapon it is wielding (your choice) flies from its grip to land in a randomly determined space 10 feet away. A creature that is two or more size categories larger than you has advantage on its saving throw.

combatMethod.name: Crushing Blow
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. With a bone crushing strike you briefly deprive an opponent of their very ability to move. Make a melee weapon attack roll using a weapon that has the {@itemProperty H|XPHB|heavy} property. A creature damaged by the attack makes a Strength saving throw, and on a failure it is {@condition paralyzed|tgtt} until it takes damage or the end of your next turn (whichever comes first).

combatMethod.name: Unbreakable
entries: {@b Reaction (3 Stamina Points)}. At the edge of death, you cling firmly to life. When you fail a death saving throw, you instead succeed. You can take this reaction even if you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt}.

combatMethod.name: Stand Tall Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. You set yourself firmly against methods. You count as being one size category larger. When you use an Adamant Mountain method that requires a saving throw against a creature smaller than you, it has disadvantage on its saving throw.
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Unstoppable
entries: {@b Reaction (3 Stamina Points)}. You power through your enemy’s special attacks. When an effect causes you to be {@condition paralyzed}, {@condition petrified|tgtt}, or {@condition stunned|tgtt}, you can use your reaction to prevent it. You can take this reaction even if you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt}.

combatMethod.name: Wild Swing
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. You wield your weapon with abandon, striking seemingly at random. When you miss with an attack using a weapon that has the {@itemProperty H|XPHB|heavy} property, you can use your reaction to make an attack with the same weapon against another target within your reach.

combatMethod.name: Unyielding
entries: {@b Reaction (3 Stamina Points)}. Badly wounded, you force yourself to keep fighting. When you are reduced to 0 hit points, you can use your reaction to regain hit points equal to {@dice 1d12} + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier (minimum 0). You can take this reaction even if you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt}.

combatMethod.name: World-Shaking Strike
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. You smash the ground, knocking down your enemies with a shockwave. You hit the ground using a weapon that has the {@itemProperty H|XPHB|heavy} property, sending out a shockwave in a 30-foot cube centered on you. Other creatures in the area each make a Strength saving throw or take damage as if hit by the weapon and fall {@condition prone}.

combatMethod.name: Frigid Strike
entries: {@b Action (1 Stamina Point)}. With a shivering whisper of clouding breath, you strike at your target and shift the chill onto them. Make a melee weapon attack. If you deal damage, your target’s Speed is reduced by 15 feet until the end of your next turn.

combatMethod.name: Grasp of the Storm
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. Make a single melee weapon attack against a creature within 15 feet. The range of this attack increases to 15 feet and deals lightning damage, The target must then make a Strength saving throw, whether you dealt damage or not, or be pulled up to 10 feet closer to you.

combatMethod.name: Malicious Mark
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. As a bonus action, you can mark a creature within 15 feet with a sigil. Alternatively, you may activate this stance by expending your reaction after dealing weapon damage, marking the creature you damaged. While a target is marked in this way, any attempt to use the {@action Help|TGTT} action against that target is a bonus action, rather than an action. This stance ends early if you wish to mark another creature or if the target is more than 30 feet away from you.
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Warding Flourish
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. Choose an ally within 15 feet that you can see. As long as that ally stays within 15 feet of you, they increase their AC by 2. You can only ward one creature at a time, and cannot use this ward on yourself. This effect ends if you can no longer take actions.

combatMethod.name: Blazing Pursuit
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. If a creature moves out of your reach, you may expend your reaction to move in the same direction up to 20 feet and make a single melee attack against the creature if they are within your reach. This movement does not provoke attacks of opportunity, and your weapon attacks deal fire damage, as well as its original types, until the end of your next turn.

combatMethod.name: Duelist's Sigil
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. As part of this stance, you trace a rune onto a melee weapon. Your mark is transferred to the next creature you successfully hit with the inscribed weapon. If the marked creature attempts to attack any other target but you, it takes force damage equal to your Intelligence modifier, regardless of whether the attack hits. This stance ends early if you wish to mark another creature or if the target is more than 30 feet away from you.
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Mystic Feint
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. When a creature makes a melee attack against you, you can use your reaction to gain a bonus to your AC against that attack equal to your proficiency bonus. After the attack resolves, you can choose to switch places with the attacker. This movement does not provoke opportunity attacks.

combatMethod.name: Quickening
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. You can expend your reaction to gain advantage on your initiative check.

combatMethod.name: Arcane Dismissal
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. Choose up to three creatures within your reach to make Strength saving throws. On a failed save, the target takes basic melee damage and is pushed back 15 feet in a straight line.

combatMethod.name: Groundshatter
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. When you use this ability, choose an adjacent square. When you strike this square a 50 feet long, 5 feet wide, line of difficult terrain appears in the direction of your choosing. You may also choose to make the square you’ve chosen the center of a 40-foot line. Creatures and objects within the line when it is created take basic melee damage. Striking a surface that is 1 foot thick or less, such as a rooftop or bridge, severely damages it, causing creatures and objects in the area of effect to fall.

combatMethod.name: Reactive Ward
entries: {@b Reaction (1-3 Stamina Points)}. When you or an ally within 15 feet are targeted by a spell with a duration of 1 round or less, you may briefly manifest a 5-foot-square shield centered the target. The creature gains three-quarters cover (+5 to AC, Dexterity saving throws, and ability checks made to hide) against the spell, potentially turning a hit into a miss. You may also spend up to a total of 3 stamina on this reaction, protecting one extra creature within range for each additional point spent.

combatMethod.name: Dimensional Strike
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You teleport a distance up to your Speed. If there is a creature within your reach, you can make a single melee weapon attack, which you add double your proficiency bonus to.

combatMethod.name: Flame Burst
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. After dealing damage with a melee weapon, select three squares within your reach. These squares erupt into flames 5 feet high and count as difficult terrain and until the end of your next turn. Creatures that start their turn within the flames take fire damage as though hit by your weapon, as does any creature that attempts to move through the flames.

combatMethod.name: Grasping Gauntlet
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. Choose a creature of no more than one size category larger than you that is within 30 feet. The target must then make a Strength or Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, you deal basic melee damage and the target becomes {@condition grappled|tgtt} by a gauntleted hand made of force. This effect requires concentration as though it were a spell. You may use your bonus action each turn to maintain the grapple for up to a number of rounds equal to half your level. When you maintain a grapple, the target takes basic melee damage on your turn. A creature that becomes {@condition grappled|tgtt} while in the air remains in its current square, even though its flight speed becomes 0, though at the Narrator’s discretion it may have to make a saving throw or fall once it is no longer {@condition grappled|tgtt}. At the beginning of each of its turns, a creature {@condition grappled|tgtt} by this method can make a Strength saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success. This effect also ends early if you target another creature with the gauntlet or if you move more than 45 feet from the target.

combatMethod.name: Argent Strike
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. Make a melee weapon attack. On a hit, the target is {@condition blinded} and {@condition deafened} and can’t benefit from either blindsight or tremorsense, in addition to taking the weapon damage. At the beginning of each of its turns, a creature affected by this method can make a Constitution saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success.

combatMethod.name: Stormrider
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. As an action, teleport up to your Speed to an unoccupied space you can see. All enemies within 10 feet of the space must make a Constitution saving throw or take lightning damage as if hit by your weapon and be pushed back 5 feet.

combatMethod.name: Bounding Charge
entries: {@b Action (1 Stamina Point)}. If you have Extra Attack, you may use this method in place of one attack. You command your animal companion to move up to its base movement speed in a straight line toward a creature. If your animal companion ends this movement within melee range, it makes one attack against that creature. Your animal companion cannot take the Dash action and this method on the same turn.

combatMethod.name: Fetch!
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. Choose a creature of the up to one size category larger than your animal companion within 20 feet. When your animal companion next makes a melee attack against that creature, it can also use its bonus action to make a Grapple against that creature. On a success, your animal companion may move 10 feet toward you, dragging the creature along with it.

combatMethod.name: Get Back!
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. When a creature successfully damages your animal companion, you order it to retreat. As long as it can hear you, your companion moves up to its Speed toward you in the most direct course it is able to, stopping at the end of its movement or when you are between it and the creature that injured it (whichever comes first). It can also choose to move up to twice its Speed, taking a level of fatigue in the process. This movement does not provoke attacks of opportunity from creatures your animal companion can see.

combatMethod.name: Vigilant Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. You command your animal companion to take a stance. While this stance is active, your animal companion only adds half your proficiency bonus to its attack and damage rolls. However, it also adds half your proficiency bonus to its AC.
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Coordinated Trip
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You command your animal companion to make a Knockdown method against a creature within its reach. If you are also within melee reach of the creature, choose one of the following benefits:• The method DC for the attack is increased by {@dice 1d4}• Your animal companion may use Knockdown on a creature one size category larger than it normally would.

combatMethod.name: Goading Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. As a bonus action, you command your animal companion to enter a goading stance. While in this stance, any melee attack roll against your companion gains a bonus equal to your proficiency bonus. When a creature hits your companion with a melee attack, the attacker takes damage equal to twice your proficiency bonus. This stance lasts until your companion is {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or you use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Wild Strikes
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You command your animal companion to attack wildly on its turn. Your animal companion may take the Attack action to make a number of natural weapon attacks equal to your proficiency bonus. All of these attacks are made at disadvantage.

combatMethod.name: Howl of Challenge
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. On your animal companion’s turn, it makes a melee attack against a creature as it roars , howls, or makes some similar threatening noise. All hostile creatures within 30 feet of your animal companion must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, they have disadvantage to attack any creature that is not your animal companion until the start of your animal companion’s next turn.

combatMethod.name: Quick Switch
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. You command your animal companion to switch places with a creature within 5 feet of it. This movement does not provoke opportunity attacks. If the creature you command your animal companion to switch places with is unwilling, it may make a Dexterity saving throw. On a success, it is not moved.

combatMethod.name: Warning Cry
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. When a creature makes a weapon attack against your animal companion, your warning causes your companion to shift position, gaining a bonus to AC equal to your proficiency bonus and possibly turning a hit into a miss. Your animal companion cannot benefit from this method and Vigilant Stance at the same time. Instead it gains the higher bonus of the two against the triggering attack, after which it benefits from Vigilant Stance as normal.

combatMethod.name: Attack in Tandem
entries: {@b Bonus Action (3 Stamina Points)}. You and your animal companion must both take this stance to gain its benefits. When you make a melee attack against a creature, your animal companion gains advantage on attack rolls against that creature until the start of your next turn. In addition, your animal companion has advantage on saving throws and it takes half as much damage as normal.

combatMethod.name: Bring the Mighty Low
entries: {@b Reaction (3 Stamina Points)}. When a creature of up to one size category larger than your animal companion makes a melee attack against it, you command your animal companion to respond by harrying the attacker’s movements. If the creature’s attack hits, it deals an additional damage die and must make a Dexterity saving throw or fall {@condition prone}. On a miss, it must make the saving throw at disadvantage. If the attacking creature is two size categories larger than your animal companion, you may direct your animal companion to get under the creature’s feet and take that creature’s basic melee damage (in addition to any potential damage from the attack) in order to force it to make the Dexterity saving throw as above.

combatMethod.name: Heroic Substitution
entries: {@b Reaction (3 Stamina Points)}. When damage from a successful attack roll would reduce your animal companion’s hit points to 0, you may move up to your base speed to position yourself between your companion and the attack, moving your companion 5 feet if necessary, and take the damage of the attack instead. This method does not cost your animal companion’s reaction.

combatMethod.name: Fortifying Reassurance
entries: {@b Reaction (3 Stamina Points)}. If you are within 20 feet when your animal companion is hit by an attack, you can use your reaction to call out and help it regain its fighting spirit. As long as it is able to hear you, your animal companion regains hit points equal to your level. If your animal companion benefits from this feature and is {@condition charmed}, {@condition poisoned}, or {@condition restrained|tgtt}, it can use its action on its next turn to end those conditions.

combatMethod.name: Strike from Below
entries: {@b Bonus Action (3 Stamina Points)}. If you have Extra Attack, you may use this method in place of one attack. You command your animal companion to leap at a flying creature and target its means of flight. Your animal companion may move up to 20 feet to get into position and gains a vertical jump height equal to half its Speed for the purposes of striking the target creature. The creature is forced to make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, its flight speed is reduced by half until healed and it immediately plummets to the ground (or 60 feet, whichever comes first) and takes falling damage as usual. On a success, it takes damage equal to your proficiency bonus. Creatures more than 1 size category larger than your animal companion make this save with advantage. Regardless of the result, after performing the method, your animal companion lands in an unoccupied square of your choice that is adjacent to the one directly below the target, taking fall damage as normal. If there are no unoccupied adjacent squares, your animal companion falls either into the square directly below the target creature (which may, in turn, fall on your companion) or onto the creature in the adjacent occupied square of your choice. Both the creature that falls into an occupied square and its occupant take double the normal amount of fall damage before the falling creature is shunted into the closest unoccupied space.

combatMethod.name: Covering Fire
entries: {@b Action (1 Stamina Point)}. Making haphazard shots to dissuade enemies, you force opponents onto the defensive by unleashing death from above so an ally can move into position. Choose a number of creatures equal to your proficiency bonus that you can see within your weapon’s normal range. Until the start of your next turn, a chosen creature’s movement does not provoke opportunity attacks from creatures you can see as you pelt the area nearby with missiles. For each opportunity attack a chosen creature would have otherwise provoked, you expend one piece of ammunition or thrown weapon.

combatMethod.name: Doubleshot
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. You palm two blades or nock two arrows, launching both missiles at the same opponent. The next ranged weapon attack you make uses two missiles instead of one. On a hit, you deal an additional weapon damage die.

combatMethod.name: Farshot Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. By arching your back and stretching your arms to their fullest extent, you make your ranged weaponry travel further than usual. When you use a weapon to make a ranged attack, increase its normal range by 20 feet and long range by 50 feet.
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Point Blank Shot
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. With uncanny swiftness you ready a weapon normally only used at range, compensating for making an attack at an opponent within only a few feet and giving your enemies reason to be wary. Until the end of your next turn, being within 5 feet of a hostile creature who can see you and who isn’t {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} does not give you disadvantage when making a ranged attack using a weapon. In addition, when an adjacent hostile creature that you can see moves 5 feet or more away from you, you can use your reaction to make a ranged weapon attack against it.

combatMethod.name: Countershot
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. With awe-inspiring skill you unleash a thrown weapon or arrow, knocking a missile out of the air. When you see a missile from a ranged weapon attack enter into the normal range of a weapon you are wielding, you can use your reaction to deflect it. Use a weapon to make a ranged attack against an AC equal to the attack roll of the missile. On a hit, the missile is deflected and misses its target. At the Narrator’s discretion, certain ranged weapon attacks (like a giant’s thrown rock) may be too heavy to be deflected by Countershot.

combatMethod.name: Quickdraw
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. With incredible swiftness you tear your weapon from its sheath and let fly a reckless attack. As long as you are not surprised, when initiative is rolled you can use your reaction to draw a weapon and make a ranged attack with it.

combatMethod.name: Trickshot
entries: {@b Action (1 Stamina Point)}. As an action, you can make a ranged weapon attack against an unattended object. If you hit, you can use the result of the attack roll in place of a Charisma (Performance) check. When you do so, you gain a bonus to the check equal to your proficiency bonus.

combatMethod.name: Blindshot
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. By closing your eyes and focusing on the sound of the environment, you target an unseen foe with uncanny accuracy. Make a Perception check against the passive Stealth of a target you cannot see that is within 30 feet. On a success, you know the target’s location and do not have disadvantage on your next ranged attack against it before the end of your turn.

combatMethod.name: Missile Volley
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. Seizing upon the moment you make the sky rain death, striking at a multitude of creatures in rapid succession. Make a ranged weapon attack against a number of creatures within 10 feet of a point you can see within your weapon’s range. You may only target a number of creatures equal to or less than your proficiency bonus, you must have ammunition for each target, and you make a separate attack roll for each target.

combatMethod.name: Ricochet
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. With careful judgments of distances, materials, wind, and applied force you let loose a shot that bounces off of one or more surfaces to strike opponents crouching behind walls or otherwise {@condition hidden|tgtt} from sight by cover. On the next ranged weapon attack you make before the start of your turn, you can completely ignore a target’s cover. Only targets that have total cover from being entirely enclosed by an object or effect (such as inside of a chamber with no openings to shoot through or a sphere from wall of force) have cover against this attack, and you do not have disadvantage from attacking a target you cannot see.

combatMethod.name: Dive For Cover
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. Under fire, you dive for cover. When you are targeted by a ranged attack, after it hits or misses you, you can use your reaction to move up to your Speed. If your movement ends adjacent to either a solid barrier as big as you are or a creature the same size as you or larger, until the beginning of your next turn you have three-quarters cover against ranged attacks (+5 bonus to AC and saving throws) as long as you remain adjacent to the barrier or creature.

combatMethod.name: Heartseeker
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You aim carefully at your opponent’s most vulnerable spot. Make a ranged weapon attack with advantage against a creature within your weapon’s normal range. If both attack rolls would hit, it’s a critical hit. If only one of the rolls would be a critical hit but the other roll would still hit, you deal an additional weapon damage die.

combatMethod.name: Mundane Missile Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. By focusing on balance and heft you can throw whatever you have in hand with deadly accuracy. While you are wielding a weapon that does not have the {@itemProperty H|XPHB|heavy} property or {@itemProperty S||special} property, it gains the {@itemProperty T|XPHB|thrown} property.
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Hear the Wind
entries: {@b Bonus Action (3 Stamina Points)}. You can literally hear the sound of incoming missiles and react to them easily. Until the end of your next turn, you gain blindsight to a range of 20 feet. When you are hit by a ranged attack, you make a Perception check against a DC equal to the attack roll. On a success the attack misses you, and on a critical success you redirect it back at the attacker.

combatMethod.name: Horizon Shot
entries: {@b Bonus Action (3 Stamina Points)}. No matter how far away a foe is, if you can see them they’re still in danger. When you activate this method, you take the Attack action and make a ranged weapon attack, as well as any additional attacks granted by Extra Attack. Choose one target you can see. Until the start of your next turn, the target is considered to be within your weapon’s normal range and it gains no benefit from cover less than total cover.

combatMethod.name: Don't Hit Me
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. Even a well-told lie of being harmless only works if you’re really believing it when you tell it—and in the midst of a pitched fight it’s easy to muster some real feeling to briefly fool a foe. When a creature makes an attack against you, you can use your reaction to make a Deception or nonmusical Performance check with disadvantage, opposed by its Insight check. On a success, the creature is briefly perplexed and targets a different creature with the attack instead

combatMethod.name: Jovial Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. As a bonus action, you adopt a lighthearted and unpredictable stance. While in this stance, you gain a bonus to Dexterity (Acrobatics) and Charisma (Performance) checks equal to your proficiency bonus. This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Pie To The Face
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can make an attack with an improvised weapon made of food (like a pie or a flagon of mead). If you hit a creature, it is {@condition blinded} until it uses its reaction to wipe its eyes, or until the start of its next turn, whichever comes first. Until the creature uses a bonus action to clean itself, you gain a bonus to Charisma (Intimidation) checks made against it equal to your proficiency bonus.

combatMethod.name: Quick Spill
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. With daring-do and practiced grace you seamlessly fling an obstacle across the floor with surprising swiftness. With some extra effort, you can distract from the violent intent. You spread ball bearings, caltrops, or oil into a 5-foot square. If a creature is in the area, it makes a Dexterity saving throw with advantage (or normally if you spend an additional 1 stamina). On a failure against ball bearings or oil the creature falls {@condition prone}, or on a failure against caltrops it takes {@dice 1d4} piercing damage that reduces its Speed by 10 feet until it regains at least 1 hit point

combatMethod.name: Pratfall Pull
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. Physical comedy resonates best if performed in tandem, and with the right footwork even the unsuspecting can be made into an impromptu stage partner. When you fall {@condition prone} you can use your reaction to make a Knockdown basic method against an adjacent creature, forcing it to make a Dexterity saving throw or fall {@condition prone} with you.

combatMethod.name: Splash of Humor
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. There’s truth to the old adage that practice makes perfect—and you have endlessly practiced splashing people. You splash an acid vial, alchemist’s fire, or similar liquid onto a target within 5 feet of you, but instead of you making an attack roll, the target makes a Dexterity saving throw. On a successful save, your attack is successful. On a failure, it is a critical hit.

combatMethod.name: This is Yours
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. A good warrior is ready for anything, but even the most experienced opponents don’t expect to receive a gift. Make a Persuasion or nonmusical Performance check opposed by the Insight check of a creature within reach. On a success, as long as the creature has a free hand and is able to understand you, it takes the object you offer to it. On a success by 5 or more, the creature does not need to be able to understand you

combatMethod.name: Just A Giggle
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. Inversion is at the heart of humor, and nothing upends a no-holdsbarred fight faster than a solid joke. Choose a creature able to see you and make a nonmusical Performance check opposed by its Insight check. On a success, your antics force it to laugh uncontrollably, making it {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} until the start of your next turn. After you have used Just a Giggle against a creature, until the end of combat it has advantage on Insight checks to resist this method

combatMethod.name: Old Pal
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. Sometimes putting on a friendly face and sidling up to an enemy thirsting for your blood is just unexpected enough to work. Make a Deception check opposed by the Insight check of a creature able to see you. On a success, until the end of your next turn the creature treats you as an ally (instead of an enemy) for the purposes of traits and other effects. The creature can still target you with attacks, and the effects of this combat method end early if you damage it.

combatMethod.name: Partner Fall
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. The art of the pratfall is to make the dive to the floor spectacular, and nothing impresses like taking somebody down with you. When a creature no more than one size category larger than you uses a basic combat method against you or targets you with a melee attack, you can use your reaction to fall {@condition prone} and force the creature to make a Dexterity saving throw with disadvantage or also fall {@condition prone}.

combatMethod.name: Rolling With The Punches
entries: {@b Bonus Action (3 Stamina Points)}. Suffering in silence is the worst—pain is far easier to bear when you can laugh it off. As long as you are able to speak, did not cast a spell with vocalized components on your last turn, and have an ally who can hear and respond to you, once per round you can reduce the damage you take from an attack or spell by an amount equal to your proficiency bonus. In addition, when you use this stance to reduce the damage you take, you can choose to fall {@condition prone} to reduce the amount of damage you take by an amount equal to twice your proficiency bonus.
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Quick, Drink This
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. No matter how deadly or serious an opponent is, if they can drink, you can splash something down their throat with superlative timing. Make a Persuasion check opposed by the Insight check of a creature within your reach. On a success, the creature drinks whatever item you are offering to it, whether that is a potion, poison, or otherwise. Any alchemical item (such as an acid flask or alchemist’s fire) deals four times as much damage as normal when a creature drinks it in this way, although any ongoing damage is only dealt twice.

combatMethod.name: Really Very Funny
entries: {@b Action (4 Stamina Points)}. A little bit of slipping, an ungainly flip, or a teetering and unlikely turn disorients even the most perceptive of opponents. Until the end of your next turn, creatures able to see your routine have disadvantage on Insight checks and saving throws made against enchantment spells.

combatMethod.name: Instant Allyship
entries: {@b Reaction (3 Stamina Points)}. The art of battle is driven as much by the minds of combatants as it is their muscles, and you have made it your business to get into a foe’s head, even if only for a brief momentWhen a creature makes an attack against you, you can use your reaction to make a Deception check with advantage opposed by its Insight check. On a success, the creature is briefly perplexed and not sure you’re an enemy, targeting a different creature with the attack instead. In addition, until the end of your next turn the creature treats you as an ally (instead of an enemy) for the purposes of traits and other effects, and it cannot target you with attacks. The effects of this combat method end early if you damage it.

combatMethod.name: Make Them Laugh
entries: {@b Action (6 Stamina Points)}. As an action, you perform a display of comedic gymnastics. Make a Charisma (Performance) check. A number of creatures of your choice up to half the check's result become rapt onlookers. Roll a d20 and add 1 for each rapt onlooker. On a result of 20 or higher, you gain inspiration. If you do not roll a natural 20 on either the Performance check or the d20 roll, you gain one level of exhaustion. All rapt onlookers (including enemies) have disadvantage on Wisdom (Perception) and Wisdom (Insight) checks until the start of your next turn.

combatMethod.name: Blackguard’s Blight
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. With a hissing malediction, you place your curse upon a specific foe. Before the start of your next turn, when you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack you can choose to lay a curse on it. It must make a Wisdom saving throw or be unable to gain advantage on attack rolls against you for a number of rounds equal to your Charisma modifier. This curse can only be applied to one creature at a time.

combatMethod.name: Dreadful Edge
entries: {@b Action (1 Stamina Point)}. Foul magic makes your strikes unnerving. Make a melee weapon attack. On a hit, the target makes a Wisdom saving throw or is {@condition dazed|tgtt} for a number of rounds equal to your Charisma modifier.

combatMethod.name: Grasping Shadows
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. At your behest, the shadows themselves grip at your foes, pulling and dragging like quicksand. Choose 4 squares within 20 feet of you that are in dim light or darkness . These squares become areas of magical darkness which are treated as difficult terrain and can’t be illuminated except by magic, such as thelight spell. This effect lasts until the end of your next turn.

combatMethod.name: Shadowy Feint
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. Lashing out at your target gives you a moment to escape amongst concealing shadows. After making a successful melee attack, you may use your reaction to deal basic melee damage instead of normal weapon damage. When you do so, you can move up to 15 feet without provoking opportunity attacks.

combatMethod.name: Green-Flame Strike
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. You draw your hand along your blade, limning it in hellish flame. As a bonus action, you coat your weapon in green flame, causing your next successful melee weapon attack before the end of your next turn to potentially strike two targets within reach. The first target takes damage normally. The second target must make a Dexterity saving throw or take necrotic damage equal to half the damage dealt to the first target.

combatMethod.name: Living Shadow
entries: {@b Bonus Action (3 Stamina Points)}. Severing yourself from your own shadow, you place it at your opponent’s back! Choose a square within 15 feet. Until the start of your next turn, your shadow (AC 10, 1 hp) occupies the chosen space and has a range of 5 feet. It cannot attack, but it can serve as a flanking partner for you and one other creature that you designate when using this method.

combatMethod.name: Necrotic Grip
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. Your grip can wither flesh from bone. When you are grappling an opponent, you can use your reaction to deal necrotic damage equal to {@dice 1d8} + your Charisma modifier in place of basic melee damage.

combatMethod.name: Fell Spines
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. Flesh and bone obey your command and form spines along your forearm. Flinging your arm out, you launch the bony projectiles into your opponents. Choose a number of targets up to your proficiency bonus in a 15 foot cone. Each must make a Dexterity save or take damage equal to your basic melee damage and become {@condition dazed|tgtt} until the start of its next turn. This damage is considered both necrotic and piercing. On a successful save, the target does not take the damage, but is still {@condition dazed|tgtt} until the start of its next turn.

combatMethod.name: Life Drinker
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. With a baleful word and a vicious strike you draw the very lifeforce from your target. Make a melee weapon attack roll. A creature damaged by this attack makes a Constitution saving throw, and on a failure you gain hit points equal to one of its hit dice + your Constitution modifier (two on a critical hit). as if you had taken a short rest. This does not deal additional damage, but does expend the hit die if the creature is capable of spending them.

combatMethod.name: Mistfade Retreat
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. As your foe’s deadly attack falls upon you, your form becomes mist and shadow. When you take damage from a melee attack, you can move 15 feet without provoking opportunity attacks.

combatMethod.name: Deathgrip
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. A phantasmal force manifests around your weapon, waiting to wrap itself around a foe. Make a melee weapon attack. On a hit, the target must make a Strength saving throw or take additional necrotic damage equal to your base melee damage and become {@condition restrained|tgtt} until the beginning of your next turn

combatMethod.name: Hungry Ghosts
entries: {@b Bonus Action (3 Stamina Points)}. You draw up the dead to do your horrible bidding. Choose a number of squares equal to your Charisma modifier within 30 feet. These squares become occupied with shadowy apparitions, which have an AC equal to 10 + your Charisma modifier your Method DC and 10 hit points. Each apparition has its own reaction for the purpose of making opportunity attacks, which it makes using your attack bonus, but cannot help flank. On a hit, the creature takes damage equal to your Charisma modifier and you gain hit points equal to the damage dealt. You can also use your own reaction to direct an apparition to make another opportunity attack if it has already used its own. While maintaining this stance, you can forgo your movement to instead move any apparition the same number of squares. This can be split among multiple apparitions, as long as you have the movement available. The apparitions must stay within 30 feet of you or they vanish. This stance ends early if all apparitions are destroyed.
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Vicious Blade
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You enchant your weapon with clinging shadows. Until the end of your next turn, the next creature you damage with a melee weapon attack makes a Constitution saving throw or takes additional necrotic damage equal to your base melee damage for a number of rounds equal to your Charisma modifier.

combatMethod.name: Darkstalkers
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. You draw forth the unquiet dead and force them to your bidding. Choose a number of squares equal to your Charisma modifier within 30 feet. Each square becomes occupied by a spirit entity with an AC equal to 10 + your Charisma modifier, 20 hit points, and vulnerability to radiant damage. Each entity also has a reach of 5 feet and its own reaction for the purposes of making opportunity attacks, which it makes using your attack bonus and base melee damage. The entities cannot use any movement and cannot flank. The squares they occupy are considered difficult terrain to any creature except you. Additionally, while this stance is active, as part of the Attack action you can forgo any number of weapon attacks available to you to instead direct a chosen entity to attack using your attack modifier and weapon damage. This stance ends early if all entities are destroyed.
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Skull Taker
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. Channeling your dark power into a single brutal attack, you attempt to decapitate your target. Make a melee weapon attack. On a hit, your attack becomes a critical hit, and if your attack would have already been a critical hit the creature makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, it takes an additional {@dice 10d10} damage. On a success, it takes half damage.

combatMethod.name: Challenger's Strike
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. When you activate this method, you take the Attack action and make a weapon attack, as well as any additional attacks grated by Extra Attack, against a single creature. On a hit, make a Persuasion check against the creature’s passive Insight. On a success, the creature has disadvantage on attacks made against creatures other than you until the end of your next turn.

combatMethod.name: Engender Doubt
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. When a creature misses you with a melee weapon attack, you can use your reaction to disparage its performance. The creature makes a Wisdom saving throw or it is {@condition dazed|tgtt} until the end of its next turn.

combatMethod.name: Socialite Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. As a bonus action, you adopt a charming and persuasive demeanor. While in this stance during combat, you gain a bonus to Charisma (Persuasion) checks equal to your proficiency bonus. This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Stylish Tumble
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. As a bonus action, you can move through a hostile creature's space. Make a Charisma (Performance) check contested by the creature's Strength (Athletics) check. If you succeed, you can move through its space, and your movement for the rest of the turn does not provoke opportunity attacks from that creature.

combatMethod.name: Honourable Bout
entries: {@b Bonus Action (3 Stamina Points)}. Choose one creature within 30 feet of you that can see or hear you and has a clear path to you. Make a Persuasion check against the creature’s passive Insight. On a success, you and the creature are fixated on each other until the end of your next turn, and it must spend its next turn safely moving as close to you as possible. While fixated in this way, you have disadvantage on attacks against creatures other than the creature, and the creature has disadvantage on attacks against creatures other than you.

combatMethod.name: Overconfident Gambit
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. When you take the Attack action or use a basic method, you can use your reaction before rolling to make a called shot. You cannot benefit from any bonus to the roll from proficiency or other sources. If your action succeeds, all allies within 30 feet who could see you gain advantage on their next attack roll, ability check, or saving throw made within 1 minute.

combatMethod.name: Wink and Smile
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. A tossed rose, a blown kiss—you acknowledge someone whose eye was caught by your swagger. Choose one creature within 30 feet that can see you. Make a Performance check against the creature’s passive Insight. On a success, the creature is fixated on you until the start of your next turn.

combatMethod.name: Formal Introduction
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. You announce yourself, why you’re here, and what you’re about to do. From your opponent’s reaction, you can take their measure. When you activate this method, choose one creature that has rolled initiative, shares a language with you, and hasn’t had damage dealt to it by you in this combat. You reveal your name, as well as one piece of critical information about you (such as a notable title, your current objective, or a dark secret). Make a Persuasion check against the creature’s passive Deception. On a success, the chosen creature reveals the same information about itself to the best of its knowledge and ability. On a failure, the creature shares misleading information. This method can only reveal new information about a creature once every 24 hours and only once per combat.

combatMethod.name: Sporting Chance
entries: {@b Reaction (3 Stamina Points)}. As a reaction, you can challenge a creature within 5 feet of you. That creature gains a bonus to its attack rolls against you equal to your proficiency bonus until the end of its next turn. During that time, you have advantage on saving throws against being {@condition charmed}, {@condition frightened}, or having disadvantage on attack rolls. If you are currently affected by one of these conditions, you can immediately make a saving throw to end one of them.

combatMethod.name: To My Side
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You beckon someone to your side with infectious fervor, be they stranger or comrade-in-arms. Choose one conscious non-hostile creature within 30 feet of you that can see you. The creature can use its reaction to move up to its Speed in a straight line toward you. This movement does not provoke opportunity attacks. If the creature enters your reach, you can use your reaction to draw one item and administer it to the creature if it is willing. The item must be a potion, medicine, or some other beneficial consumable.

combatMethod.name: Bravado Taunt
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You open your guard in a brazen display of confidence, daring your foes to strike you. Each hostile creature of your choice whose reach you are within must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or use its reaction to make one weapon attack targeting you. You gain 10 temporary hit points for each attack that misses, up to a maximum of misses equal to your Charisma modifier. If any of the creatures are within your range, you can then make one melee weapon attack against one of the creatures as part of this method.

combatMethod.name: Impeccable Presence
entries: {@b Bonus Action (4 Stamina Points)}. The icon of decorum, your presence leaves no doubt of your whereabouts. When you have the attention you seek, you can add extra flourish that makes hearts swoon. Allies within 30 feet of you are always aware of your location, regardless of effects or cover that would obfuscate you. You can target them and they can target you as if you can see or hear each other. Additionally, you are always aware of the location of any creature fixated on you, and you can target them as if you can see or hear them. Finally, if you willingly end this stance on your turn, any creature fixated on you is also {@condition dazed|tgtt} for as long as they are fixated in that way.
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Triumphant Return
entries: {@b Reaction (3 Stamina Points)}. If you are at 0 hit points and regain hit points, you can use your reaction to immediately stand up from {@condition prone} (if you are) without using any movement. Additionally, choose up to three creatures that can see you. Each gains a bonus equal to your proficiency bonus to the next attack roll, ability check, or saving throw it makes within 1 minute.

combatMethod.name: Captivating Speech
entries: {@b Action (5 Stamina Points)}. Through moving words, you stir the hearts of your allies and captivate your enemies so completely that your allies go unseen. When you activate this method, roll a Persuasion check. Then choose up to six creatures within 30 feet of you that can hear you. You can choose which of the following two effects to apply to each target. If your Persuasion check was equal to or greater than the creature’s passive Insight, the creature must make a Wisdom saving throw against your Persuasion check result, or it is fixated on you for 1 minute. This effect requires concentration like a spell and ends early if you lose concentration, drop to 0 hit points, or if the creature can no longer hear you. While fixated in this way, any ally who is not within 10 feet of you is considered to be {@condition hidden|tgtt} from the creature. An ally remains hidden until they reveal their position, such as by touching or attacking the creature or producing a lot of noise. The creature gains temporary hit points equal to your roll. Additionally, choose from {@condition frightened}, fixated, or {@condition charmed}. The creature is immune to the chosen condition for a number of rounds equal to your Charisma modifier. If it is currently under the effects of one of these conditions, the effects are suppressed for the duration, though they resume when the effect ends. Time spent suppressed counts against a condition’s duration. You can only choose one condition each time you use this method.

combatMethod.name: To The Death
entries: {@b Bonus Action (4 Stamina Points)}. As a bonus action, you can issue a formal duel challenge to a creature within 30 feet whose challenge rating is at least half your level. Make a Charisma (Persuasion) check contested by its Wisdom (Insight). On a success, you both become fixated on each other. You both must use your movement to move towards each other and must make at least one melee attack against each other each turn if able. You both gain a +4 bonus to AC and saving throws against effects from other creatures. The duel ends if one of you is reduced to 0 hit points, {@condition incapacitated|tgtt}, or ends a turn out of line of sight of the other. The winner of the duel gains a bonus to attack and damage rolls equal to your proficiency bonus for the rest of the combat.

combatMethod.name: Knockdown Assault
entries: {@b Action (1 Stamina Point)}. Your unpredictable methoding puts your opponent off-balance—when you connect with your weapon they stumble and fall. When you activate this method, you take the Attack action and make a melee weapon attack, as well as any additional attacks granted by Extra Attack. Until the start of your next turn, when you hit with a melee weapon attack against a creature and deal 8 or more damage it makes a Dexterity saving throw or is knocked {@condition prone}.

combatMethod.name: Leading Throw
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. An opponent that strikes at you invites peril as they find the momentum from their attack turned against them. When a creature within your reach misses you with a melee weapon attack, you can use your reaction to try to throw it. The creature makes a Dexterity saving throw or moves 15 feet in a straight line in a direction of your choice.

combatMethod.name: Warning Strike
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. When an opponent opens themselves up to a reflexive strike you can deliver a blow that shakes them to their core and forces them to hesitate. When a hostile creature that you can see moves out of your reach, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack and on a hit its Speed is reduced to 0 until the start of its next turn.

combatMethod.name: Wary Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. As a bonus action, you adopt a keenly observant stance. While in this stance, you gain a bonus to Wisdom (Insight) checks equal to your proficiency bonus, and your passive Wisdom (Insight) score increases by 3. This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Assisted Roll
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. You take the force delivered by an opponent’s failed attack and make it your own, propelling yourself away. When a creature within your reach misses you with a melee weapon attack, you can use your reaction to move 15 feet without provoking opportunity attacks.

combatMethod.name: Off-Balancing Strikes
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. Focusing your attacks on an opponent’s weak points allows you to not just wound, but to confound. Until the start of your next turn, when you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack instead of dealing damage you can choose to use pulls, shoves, and strikes to put your target off-balance. Until the end of your next turn, the target moves at half Speed, and it has disadvantage on ability checks and Dexterity saving throws.

combatMethod.name: Take Weapon
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. You snatch your enemy’s weapon and use it against them. When a creature misses you with a melee weapon attack against you using a weapon, you can use your reaction to flip it out of the creature’s hands. The creature makes a Dexterity saving throw, and on a failure it drops the weapon and you grab it from mid-air. You can spend 2 stamina points to immediately use the weapon to make a melee weapon attack against the creature.

combatMethod.name: Flowing Form
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You turn into a twisting leaf upon the wind of battle, turning every dodged strike into a sublime counterattack. You take the Dodge action. Until the start of your next turn you make one melee weapon attack against each target that misses you with a melee weapon attack, and you have disadvantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks.

combatMethod.name: Heightened Reflexes
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1-3 Stamina Points)}. Bracing yourself with a controlled burst of adrenaline propels you into a state of superior awareness along with the energy to act with unnatural quickness. You gain a number of reactions equal to stamina points spent. You must use these reactions before the start of your next turn.

combatMethod.name: Redirect
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. Your opponent’s blow is not simply turned away but re-oriented entirely, forced into inflicting a wound against a foe unfortunate enough to be nearby. When you are hit with a melee weapon attack, you can use your reaction to force the attacker to make an Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, you take no damage and the attack targets another creature within 5 feet. A Huge‐sized or larger attacker has advantage on the saving throw. Once you have used this method against a creature, until the end of combat it has advantage on saving throws to resist Redirect.

combatMethod.name: Discerning Strike
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. A probing blow allows you to take the measure of your foe. When you activate this method, you take the Attack action and make a weapon attack, as well as any additional attacks granted by Extra Attack. Until the start of your next turn, when you deal damage to a creature you learn its current hit points, hit point maximum, immunities, resistances, and vulnerabilities. If you score a critical hit you can choose one of the creature’s resistances or immunities. The creature loses that resistance or immunity until the end of your next turn. In addition, until the beginning of your next turn you gain advantage on melee weapon attacks against that creature.

combatMethod.name: Retributive Blow
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. You return a blow so quickly that your opponent is shocked. When you are hit with a melee weapon attack, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against the attacker. On a hit, that creature has disadvantage on attack rolls against you until the beginning of your next turn.

combatMethod.name: Strike the Cracks Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You focus on targeting your enemy’s weakest spots. When you make melee weapon attacks, you score critical hits on a roll of 19–20. If you already have a feature that increases the range of your critical hits, your critical hit range increases by 1 (maximum 17–20). You deal one additional weapon damage die when determining the extra damage for a critical hit with a melee weapon attack.
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Blinding Strikes
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. With a vicious strike, you blind your enemy. When you activate this method, you take the Attack action and make a weapon attack, as well as any additional attacks granted by Extra Attack. Until the start of your next turn, when you use a melee weapon to deal damage against a creature, it is {@condition blinded} and can’t benefit from either blindsight or tremorsense. At the beginning of each of its turns, a creature {@condition blinded} by this method can make a Constitution saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success.

combatMethod.name: Reflect Attack
entries: {@b Reaction (3 Stamina Points)}. You redirect your enemy’s attack back upon itself. When you are hit with a melee weapon attack, you can use your reaction to redirect the force back to the attacker. The attack misses you and the creature makes an Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, the attack is redirected and it becomes the target. If you have already hit that creature with a Mirror's Glint method since the beginning of your last turn, the redirected attack is a critical hit.

combatMethod.name: Anticipate Spell
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. You make your move while your foe is busy casting a spell. Until the start of your next turn, when you see a creature within 5 feet of you cast a spell you can use your reaction to make either an opportunity attack or a Sleight of Hand check against it.

combatMethod.name: Deceptive Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. As a bonus action, you adopt a deceptively passive stance. While in this stance during combat, you gain a bonus to Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) and Charisma (Deception) checks equal to your proficiency bonus. This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Painful Pickpocket
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. Sometimes a good diversion is presented to you and though a foe might bloody you, your light fingers find opportunity in the exchange. When a creature hits you with a melee attack, you can use your reaction to make a Sleight of Hand check against it.

combatMethod.name: Perplexing Flurry
entries: {@b Action (1 Stamina Point)}. A rain of short, sharp strikes disorients your enemy. When you activate this method, you take the Attack action and make a weapon attack, as well as any additional attacks granted by Extra Attack. As long as all of your attacks on your turn are melee weapon attacks that target the same creature, that creature has disadvantage on attack rolls against you until the start of your next turn.

combatMethod.name: Agile Feint
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You juke left and right, throw sand, whip out your cloak, or otherwise try to distract your opponent and make them vulnerable to attack. Choose a creature within your reach. You have advantage on your next weapon attack roll against it.

combatMethod.name: Force Hesitation
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. After working your way into an opponent’s guard you convince them that every step will lead to a disastrous end, and that to divert their attention away from you for even a moment invites devastation. A creature within your reach makes an Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, it reacts to a strike from you that never comes. As long as you remain within the target’s reach, until the start of your next turn it cannot attack any creature other than you. After you have used this method against a creature, until the end of combat it has advantage on saving throws against Force Hesitation.

combatMethod.name: Mugging Hit
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. As your enemy howls in pain, you swiftly snatch something away from them. Choose a creature you can see. On a hit with your next melee attack against it before the start of your next turn, you can also make a Sleight of Hand check against the creature.

combatMethod.name: Douse
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. You quickly dash a container of liquid over your opponent at an opportune moment. You splash a held container of liquid, such as a contact poison, holy water, or alchemical substance, at a target within 20 feet. The target makes a Dexterity saving throw or is hit by the liquid. This method can also be used to extinguish a small nonmagical fire in a 5-foot-square area.

combatMethod.name: Feinting Strike
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You make a skillful, deceptive strike which your foe doesn’t see coming. Make a melee weapon attack roll using the result of a Sleight of Hand check instead of your attack bonus.

combatMethod.name: Pickpocket
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. You snatch something from your enemy. When a creature is within your reach, you can use your reaction to make a Sleight of Hand check against it.

combatMethod.name: Blinding Blow Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You pace your attacks in a disruptive flow that makes it easy to follow a strike with methoding that temporarily blinds your foes. After you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can spend 1 stamina to make a Sleight of Hand check, blinding it if the result is higher than its AC. At the beginning of each of its turns, the {@condition blinded} creature can make a Wisdom saving throw to recover from its blindness.
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Spinning Parry
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. A quick step and suddenly you and your foe exchange places. You can use your reaction to make a Sleight of Hand check when a creature makes a melee attack against you, turning it into a miss if your result is higher than the attack roll. In addition, you may also swap places with the attacker (no opportunity attacks provoked), forcing it to make a Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition stunned|tgtt} until the beginning of its next turn.

combatMethod.name: Steal Momentum
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. A quick jab to a pressure point locks your opponent’s joints. When a creature moves into your reach you can use your reaction to make a Sleight of Hand check against its AC. On a success, the creature becomes {@condition restrained|tgtt} until the beginning of its next turn.

combatMethod.name: Armor Lock
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. You swiftly adjust your enemy’s armor so that they cannot move. Make a Sleight of Hand check against the AC of a creature within reach that is wearing armor, paralyzing it on a success. At the beginning of each of its turns, a creature {@condition paralyzed|tgtt} by this method can make a Strength saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success (unlike normal, the {@condition paralyzed|tgtt} creature doesn’t automatically fail this save. )A creature wearing heavy armor has disadvantage on its saving throw.

combatMethod.name: Pilfer Object
entries: {@b Reaction (3 Stamina Points)}. You dart forward and snatch an item from a foe just as they start to use it. When a creature within 15 feet draws an item or takes the Use an Object action, you can use your reaction to move adjacent to it and force it to make a Wisdom saving throw. This movement does not provoke opportunity attacks. On a failure, you can either take the item for yourself or choose the target of the creature’s Use an Object action.

combatMethod.name: Charge
entries: {@b Action (1 Stamina Point)}. Flinging yourself forward you barrel towards an enemy with your weapon held at the ready to unleash an attack before they can react!Move up to 30 feet in a straight line and make a melee weapon attack. You can’t take the Dash action and use this method on the same turn.

combatMethod.name: Eye Slash
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You rapidly lash out in a rising strike targeting your opponent’s brow, leaving a wound that obscures their vision. Make a melee weapon attack against a creature that relies on sight. On a hit, it makes a Constitution saving throw or is {@condition blinded} until the end of your next turn.

combatMethod.name: Speed Over Strength
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. With practiced agility you weave around an opponent wielding a more cumbersome weapon, quickly darting in to strike with a lighter armament. When a creature attacks you with a melee weapon that has the {@itemProperty H|XPHB|heavy} property, or a Large-sized or larger creature attacks you with a natural weapon, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against it using a weapon that has the {@itemProperty L|XPHB|light} or {@itemProperty F|XPHB|finesse} property.

combatMethod.name: Swift Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. You adopt a loose stance that gives you an extra bit of swiftness that can make all the difference in a fight or chase. Your Speed increases by 5 feet.
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Parrying Counter
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. As a reaction at the end of your turn, you can choose one creature you can see within 5 feet. You gain a bonus to your AC equal to your proficiency bonus against attacks from that creature until the start of your next turn. If the creature attacks you and misses during this time, you can make one melee weapon attack against it.

combatMethod.name: Rapid Drink
entries: {@b Free Action (2 Stamina Points)}. As casually as any seasoned alchemist, you pluck the cork from a magical concoction and pour it down your throat or a companion’s. You drink a potion or administer a potion to a creature within reach.

combatMethod.name: Rolling Strike
entries: {@b Action (1 Stamina Point)}. Flowing through battle like the wind itself, you deftly sprint forward through the reach of enemies to deliver an unexpected attack. Move up to 20 feet without provoking opportunity attacks and make a melee weapon attack.

combatMethod.name: Crushing Waterfall
entries: {@b Action (2+ Stamina Points)}. You crash down like a raging torrent, turning gravity itself into a weapon. When you hit a creature with an attack while falling onto it, you can use this maneuver to amplify the impact.
