Increase the damage dice granted by your fall by two steps, meaning each {@dice d4} becomes a {@dice d8}. For every 2 additional stamina points you spend when you use this maneuver, the damage dice increase by two additional steps (for example, {@dice d8} to {@dice d12}, {@dice d12} to {@dice 2d8}, and so on).

combatMethod.name: Disarming Counter
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. With expert timing you apply just the right amount of force to send an opponent’s weapon flying just as they strike. When a creature makes a melee weapon attack against you and misses, you can use your reaction to force it to make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a weapon it is wielding (your choice) flies from its grip to land in a randomly determined space 10 feet away.

combatMethod.name: Rapid Strike
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. With an array of quick, carefully placed attacks against the same opponent you throw them off their guard. When you activate this method, you take the Attack action and make a weapon attack, as well as any additional attacks granted by Extra Attack. After hitting a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can use your bonus action to make an additional attack against the same creature. On a hit with this additional attack, you can use your reaction to spend 2 stamina points and attack the same creature again.

combatMethod.name: Whirlwind Strike
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. Shouldering into a hard spin you lash out at opponents that have surrounded you, striking at each of them in rapid succession. Make a melee weapon attack against any number of creatures within 5 feet of you, making a separate attack roll for each target. You may only target a number of creatures equal to or less than your proficiency bonus.

combatMethod.name: First Blood
entries: {@b Reaction (3 Stamina Points)}. You react and attack quicker than the eye can see. After rolling initiative, you can use your reaction to treat your initiative as if you had rolled a critical success.

combatMethod.name: Flowing Steps Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (3 Stamina Points)}. You focus on moving so swiftly and with such subtlety that nothing can stop you. You allow your movements to adapt to all conditions. You have advantage on saving throws made to resist being {@condition grappled|tgtt}, {@condition paralyzed|tgtt}, {@condition restrained|tgtt}, or {@condition stunned|tgtt}. In addition, you ignore difficult terrain.
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Whirlpool Strike
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. You spin around, gathering momentum as you strike your foes harder and harder. Make a melee weapon attack against any number of creatures within 5 feet of you, making a separate attack roll for each target. For each subsequent hit after the first, you deal an additional {@dice 1d6} damage.

combatMethod.name: Tidal Parry
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. An expert parry turns a hit into a miss. When a creature hits you with a melee attack, you can use your reaction to evade the attack. The creature makes a Dexterity saving throw, and on a failure its attack becomes a miss. A creature that scored a critical hit has advantage on its save.

combatMethod.name: Tsunami Dash
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. You sprint through your enemies, striking at every one you pass. You take the Dash action. During your turn, any time you become adjacent to a creature, you can make a melee attack against it using a weapon that has the {@itemProperty L|XPHB|light} or {@itemProperty F|XPHB|finesse} properties. You cannot attack the same creature twice in a row in this manner or make more attacks than your proficiency bonus.

combatMethod.name: Dangerous Strikes
entries: {@b Action (1 Stamina Point)}. The execution and follow through of your strike is exquisite with not an iota of force wasted. When you activate this method, you take the Attack action and make a weapon attack, as well as any additional attacks granted by Extra Attack. Until the start of your next turn, your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 19–20. If you already have a feature that increases the range of your critical hits, your critical hit range increases by 1 (maximum 17–20).

combatMethod.name: Exploit Footing
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. Deftly surging forward and weaving around your opponent, when their attack fails you quickly move around your foe and force them to stumble. When a creature makes a melee weapon attack against you with advantage and misses, or when it has disadvantage and both attack rolls would miss, you can use your reaction to trip it. The creature makes a Dexterity saving throw or is knocked {@condition prone}.

combatMethod.name: Iron Will
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. Withdrawing into your mind, you focus and concentrate to steel your nerves. When you make a saving throw to resist being {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}, you can use your reaction to gain advantage on the saving throw.

combatMethod.name: Perceptive Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. As a bonus action, you heighten your senses. While in this stance, your passive Wisdom (Perception) score increases by 3, and you gain a bonus to Wisdom (Perception) checks equal to your proficiency bonus. This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Practiced Roll
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. With a series of low steps and somersaults you expertly method around opponents. Move 20 feet. This movement doesn’t provoke opportunity attacks from creatures you can see.

combatMethod.name: Twist the Blade
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. After striking your foe, you twist the blade. When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack on a roll of 18 or 19, you can use your reaction to turn the attack into a critical hit.

combatMethod.name: Use The Pain
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. Seizing on the surge of adrenaline that comes from suffering a severe blow, you lash out with a vengeful strike. When a creature makes a critical hit against you using a melee weapon attack, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against it.

combatMethod.name: Drive Back
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You move inexorably forward with an attack which cannot be stopped by blade or shield, forcing your opponent back. Make a melee weapon attack against a creature. Whether or not you hit, the creature is pushed back 10 feet.

combatMethod.name: Instinctive Counterattack
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. Utilizing reflexes perfected by dedicated training, your body flows into a sublime strike almost without a thought. When your stamina pool is not empty, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack.

combatMethod.name: Mind Over Body
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. Focusing your thoughts inward allows you to rise above the pain of wounds inflicted upon your body. Until the start of your next turn, reduce any damage you take by 2.

combatMethod.name: Dashing Razor
entries: {@b Action (1 Stamina Point)}. You feed off the adrenaline from a solid strike and turn that energy into frenetic movement. Move up to your Speed and make a melee weapon attack against a creature. On a hit, for the next minute your Speed is increased by 10 feet.

combatMethod.name: Perfect Edge Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. By relying on your training and countless hours of repetition, you can easily manipulate the battlefield to position yourself where your strikes are able to inflict the most harm. Your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 18–20. If you already have a feature that increases the range of your critical hits, your critical hit range increases by 1 (maximum 17–20).
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Sharpened Awareness
entries: {@b Reaction (3 Stamina Points)}. You tune your senses, focusing on the fight at hand. You can use your reaction to make a Perception check when a creature makes an attack against you, turning it into a miss if your result is higher than the attack roll. In addition, if the attack is turned into a miss you gain advantage on attacks made against that creature until the end of your next turn. If the attack still hits you, the creature has disadvantage on its next attack made against you before the end of its next turn.

combatMethod.name: Death Blow
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. All your training culminates in this lethal attack. You deliver the perfect killing blow with a single secret technique. Make a melee weapon attack against a creature. On a hit, your attack becomes a critical hit, and if your attack would have already been a critical hit the creature makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the creature is reduced to 0 hit points.

combatMethod.name: Heightened Concentration
entries: {@b Reaction (3 Stamina Points)}. You nearly lose concentration, but you shake your head and stay focused. When you lose concentration, you can use your reaction to prevent yourself from losing concentration.

combatMethod.name: Doubleteam
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. Working in tandem with an ally, you fray the defenses of an opponent so your companion can better land a strike. Choose a creature within your reach. The next ally within 20 feet that makes a melee weapon attack against that creature can use their bonus action to make an Athletics check against the target’s method DC to grapple or push the target 10 feet.

combatMethod.name: Legion Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. You strike your foes in such a way as to leave an opening for your allies to follow-up. When you score a critical hit against a creature, an ally within reach of the same creature can use their reaction to make an opportunity attack against it.
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Shield Wall
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. Falling into step with a fellow warrior and putting both of your shields together makes each of you harder to hit. While you are wielding a shield, you can choose one ally within 5 feet that is also wielding a shield. Until the start of your next turn, as long as that ally remains within 5 feet of you and is able to take actions both you and your ally increase your AC by 2.

combatMethod.name: Shoulder Check
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. Watching a companion under attack infuriates you and you fling yourself at their opponent with a powerful shove. After a creature within your reach makes a melee attack against an ally, you can use your reaction to make an Athletics check against the creature’s method DC. On a success you shove the creature 10 feet away from you and you move 10 feet in that direction. This movement does not provoke opportunity attacks.

combatMethod.name: Back To Back
entries: {@b Action (1 Stamina Point)}. You know how to fight while surrounded by foes and can quickly instruct an ally on how to move in concert to survive against an overwhelming number of foes. Choose an ally within 5 feet. Until the start of your next turn, as long as that ally remains within 5 feet of you and is able to take actions, and both of you are unmounted, you and your ally increase your AC by 2. In addition, each of you gets an extra reaction that can be used before the start of your next turn to either make an opportunity attack or activate a combat method.

combatMethod.name: Double Tackle
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. You and an ally tackle a foe together, your combined strength forcing them to the ground. Choose an ally you can see. Your ally can use their reaction to move up to 20 feet to be adjacent to a creature you designate. As long as you move at least 20 feet and both you and your ally are adjacent to the creature, it makes a Strength saving throw or is knocked {@condition prone}. Alternatively, you and your ally may both choose to go {@condition prone} to force the creature to fail its save. You cannot use this method against creatures more than one size category larger than you or your ally.

combatMethod.name: Dual Grapple
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You join in a grapple instigated by an ally. Make an Athletics check against the method DC of a creature an ally has {@condition grappled|tgtt}. If you succeed, the creature is {@condition restrained|tgtt} until the grapple ends, and either you or your ally (your choice) are also {@condition restrained|tgtt} until the grapple ends.

combatMethod.name: Follow-Up Topple
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. As your ally strikes an opponent, you seize the moment of imbalance to knock them down. When an ally within your reach hits a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against that creature. On a hit, the creature makes a Dexterity saving throw or is knocked {@condition prone}.

combatMethod.name: Hurl Ally
entries: {@b Bonus Action (3 Stamina Points)}. You throw your ally at an opponent, lending them extra power. Choose a willing ally within your reach that is at least one size category smaller than you. You hurl your ally at a creature within 20 feet. Your ally lands in a space adjacent to the creature and can use their reaction to make a melee weapon attack against it. On a hit, the creature makes a Dexterity saving throw or the attack becomes a critical hit (or if the attack is already a critical hit your ally deals maximum damage).

combatMethod.name: Look At Me!
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. Your strikes make foes hesitate, leaving openings for your allies to move free of danger. When you activate this method, you take the Attack action and make a weapon attack, as well as any additional attacks granted by Extra Attack. Until the start of your next turn, when you make a melee weapon attack against a creature you can choose one ally within reach. The chosen ally does not provoke opportunity attacks from that creature until the start of your next turn. In addition, in the next melee attack the chosen ally makes against that creature before the end of their next turn, the ally can add a bonus to the attack roll equal to your proficiency bonus.

combatMethod.name: Bodyguard
entries: {@b Reaction (3 Stamina Points)}. You interpose yourself to take a hit for your ally. When an ally within your reach is hit by an attack, you can use your reaction to swap places with them and become the target of that attack, taking half as much damage as normal.

combatMethod.name: Brotherhood Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (3 Stamina Points)}. By focusing on keeping enemies off-balance, you help allies attack more effectively. Whenever you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can also take the {@action Help|TGTT} action. On a critical hit, the target of your {@action Help|TGTT} action can use their reaction to take the Dodge action.
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Doubletime
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You execute a perfect retreat that distracts enemies and allows your companions to fall back beside you. You take the Disengage action, and up to 3 allies within your reach can use their reactions to take the Disengage action and move their Speed.

combatMethod.name: Rallying Cry
entries: {@b Reaction (3 Stamina Points)}. You offer inspiring encouragement to an ally and they gain their second wind. When an ally you can see takes damage from an attack, you can use your reaction to release a hearty rallying cry. As long as they are able to hear you, your ally regains an amount of hit points equal to twice your proficiency bonus.

combatMethod.name: United We Stand
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. You and your allies combine forces and attack a single foe with devastating results. Make a melee weapon attack against a creature. Any willing ally within your reach can use their reaction to make a melee weapon attack against the same creature. If the creature is hit by two attacks, it takes extra damage equal to your proficiency bonus. If the creature is hit by three attacks, it takes extra damage equal to twice your proficiency bonus and it is knocked {@condition prone}. If the creature is hit by four or more attacks, it takes extra damage equal to twice your proficiency bonus, it is knocked {@condition prone}, and it is {@condition stunned|tgtt} until the end of its next turn.

combatMethod.name: Cavalier Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. You protect your mount as you ride. While you are riding your mount, its Armor Class increases by an amount equal to half your proficiency bonus.
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Lancer Strike
entries: {@b Action (1 Stamina Point)}. You knock your opponent {@condition prone} with a direct hit from your weapon. You must be wielding a weapon with the {@itemProperty R|XPHB|reach} property, mounted, and move 20 or more feet in order to use this method. Make a melee weapon attack against a creature within your weapon’s reach. On a hit, you deal an extra {@dice 1d6} damage and the creature makes a Strength saving throw or is knocked {@condition prone}.

combatMethod.name: Mounted Charge
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You charge your foe using your mount’s strength and swiftness to add power to your attack. When you activate this method, you take the Attack action and make a weapon attack, as well as any additional attacks granted by Extra Attack. You must be mounted to use this method. Move up to your mount’s Speed in a straight line. If at the end of your movement there is a creature within your reach, the first attack you make against it as part of this method has advantage. You can’t take the Dash action and use this method on the same turn.

combatMethod.name: Riding Leap
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. You coax your mount into making a remarkable leap. As long as you are mounted and move at least 30 feet in a straight line first, your mount can jump further and higher than usual. The height of the jump increases by a number of feet equal to your proficiency bonus and the distance of the jump increases by a number of feet equal to twice your proficiency bonus. Your movement this turn does not provoke opportunity attacks.

combatMethod.name: Rearing Menace
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. You rear your mount, intimidating nearby opponents. When you activate this method, you take the Attack action and make a weapon attack, as well as any additional attacks granted by Extra Attack. You must be mounted to use this method. Choose a number of creatures equal to your proficiency bonus that are within 20 feet. Each creature makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, a creature is {@condition frightened} until the end of your next turn. A creature of the same size as your mount or larger has advantage on the saving throw.

combatMethod.name: Spur Mount
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. In tune with your mount, you guide it to greater heights. While you are riding your mount, when it makes an ability check or saving throw you can use your reaction to grant it advantage.

combatMethod.name: Trample
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You trample down enemies in your path. You must be mounted to use this method. Move up to half your Speed without provoking opportunity attacks. Creatures in your path that are at least one size smaller than your mount make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes {@dice 2d6} bludgeoning damage and is knocked {@condition prone}.

combatMethod.name: Launched Strike
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You leap from your mount to attack a nearby opponent. You must be mounted to use this method. You leap 10 feet away from your mount and make a melee weapon attack against a creature within your reach before you land. On a hit, your attack becomes a critical hit.

combatMethod.name: Sacrifice Mount
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. You use your mount as a shield. You must be mounted to use this method. When a creature targets you with an attack you can use your reaction to swing down the side of your mount, making it the target of the attack instead of you.

combatMethod.name: Saddled Blows
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. From atop your mount, you strike down at your foes with powerful blows. When you activate this method, you take the Attack action and make a weapon attack, as well as any additional attacks granted by Extra Attack. You must be mounted to use this method. Until the end of your next turn, when you use a melee weapon attack to hit a creature of a size smaller than your mount, it makes a Strength saving throw. On a failure, it suffers one of the following (your choice): confused until the start of your next turn, knocked {@condition prone}, or {@condiion slowed|tgtt} until the end of your next turn.

combatMethod.name: Prodigious Leap
entries: {@b Reaction (3 Stamina Points)}. Your mount makes an incredible leap, soaring through the air. When your mount makes an Athletics check to jump, you can use your reaction to grant it a fly speed equal to half its Speed until the end of your turn. Your mount can only fly in a straight line.

combatMethod.name: Spirited Whistle
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. When you are separated from your mount but can see it, you can use an action to whistle for it. As long as your mount can hear you, it uses its reaction to move adjacent to you. If this movement is more than twice its speed, it gains one level of exhaustion.

combatMethod.name: Steely Steed Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (3 Stamina Points)}. You focus on a deep and unspoken connection with your mount, lending it strength and working together as one. When you make a melee attack against a creature, your mount gains advantage on attack rolls against that creature until the start of your next turn. In addition, your mount has advantage on saving throws and it takes half as much damage as normal.

combatMethod.name: Reassuring Pat
entries: {@b Reaction (3 Stamina Points)}. Your mount takes comfort from your presence and gains its second wind. When your mount is hit by an attack, you can use your reaction to pat it reassuringly. Your mount regains hit points equal to your level. If your mount is {@condition charmed}, {@condition poisoned}, or {@condition restrained|tgtt}, it can use its reaction to end those conditions.

combatMethod.name: Wheeling Charge
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. You repeatedly charge your mount at your opponent, attacking again and again. Your mount takes the Dodge action. If it moves at least 10 feet in a straight line and there’s a creature within your reach, make a melee attack against that creature. If you can continue your movement for at least 10 more feet, you can turn around and attack the same creature again. You must end your movement at least 10 feet away from the creature.

combatMethod.name: Imposing Glare
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. For a moment you unleash your fury forcing your enemy to hesitate. Choose one hostile creature you can see within 30 feet. If it can see you, it makes a Charisma saving throw or is {@condition frightened} of you until the end of your next turn. After you have used this method against a creature, until the end of combat it has advantage on saving throws to resist Imposing Glare.

combatMethod.name: Purge Magic
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. As soon as your senses seize upon the uttering of an incantation or a hand working the gestures of magic your body reflexively reacts with violence. When a creature you can see within your reach casts a spell, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against it.

combatMethod.name: Striding Swings
entries: {@b Action (1 Stamina Point)}. With supreme confidence you march forward with your attack, stepping heavily into every strike and driving each blow home. When you activate this technique, you take the Attack action and make a weapon attack, as well as any additional attacks granted by Extra Attack. Move up to 15 feet. This movement can be through the space of hostile creatures that are up to one size category larger than you, and the spaces of other creatures do not count as difficult terrain.

combatMethod.name: Zealous Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. As a bonus action, you enter a fervent stance. When you make your first attack on your turn, you can decide to gain a bonus to your melee weapon attack rolls equal to your proficiency bonus until the start of your next turn. If you do, attack rolls against you also gain a bonus equal to your proficiency bonus until the start of your next turn. This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Faith Within
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. As soon as you spot the workings of magic nearby you can reflexively draw upon your willpower to fortify your mind, body, and soul. When you fail a saving throw against a spell or other magical effect that makes you {@condition charmed}, {@condition frightened}, {@condition poisoned}, or {@condition stunned|tgtt}, at the start of your turn you can use your reaction to repeat the saving throw.

combatMethod.name: Gaze Of Conviction
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You lock eyes with an opponent with a fury so potent that it cannot be ignored. You stare down a creature you can see within 30 feet, and if it can see you it makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the creature is compelled to attack you. On its turn the creature moves towards you and makes as many attacks against you as it can. The effects of this method end when you attack a different creature or the creature is unable to attack you.

combatMethod.name: Stunning Assault
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. You strike your opponent with enough force to stun them. When you activate this technique, you take the Attack action and make a weapon attack, as well as any additional attacks granted by Extra Attack. Until the start of your next turn, when you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack it makes a Constitution saving throw or is {@condition stunned|tgtt} until the end of your next turn.

combatMethod.name: Break Spell
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. You interrupt a spell to stop it being cast. When a creature you can see within your reach casts a spell, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against it. On a hit, the creature makes a Constitution saving throw (against your method DC or as normal, whichever is higher) to keep its concentration while casting or the spell is interrupted (expending the spell slot) and has no effect.

combatMethod.name: Defy Magic
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. The potency of magic is dependent upon whoever wields it, and whether arcane or divine most spells and spellcasters pale in comparison to your obsessive conviction. When you see a creature within 30 feet make a spell attack against you, you can use your reaction to defy it. The creature makes a spellcasting ability check against your method DC or automatically misses with its spell attack. If the spell is of 7th-level or higher, this combat method has no effect.

combatMethod.name: Dispelling Assault
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You attack with such fury that your enemy’s magical defenses wither and fail. When you activate this technique, you take the Attack action and make a weapon attack, as well as any additional attacks granted by Extra Attack. Until the start of your next turn, your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 19–20, and when you score a critical hit against a creature any spell of 3rd-level or lower on the creature ends. If you already have a feature that increases the range of your critical hits, your critical hit range increases by 1 (maximum 17–20).

combatMethod.name: Devoted Assault
entries: {@b Bonus Action (3 Stamina Points)}. The battle around you narrows as you focus your ire on a single opponent, ignoring other threats or enemies. Choose one creature you can see within 30 feet. Until the start of your next turn, you cannot attack any other targets and you have advantage on weapon attacks made against that creature. On a critical hit, you can spend stamina to use a Tempered Iron method that you know against the creature so long as it can be activated with an action or bonus action.

combatMethod.name: Disrupting Charge
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. You charge a spellcaster in order to interrupt their spell. When a creature you can see casts a spell, you can use your reaction to move up to your Speed. If you end your movement adjacent to that creature, you can make a melee weapon attack against it. On a critical hit, the spell is interrupted (expending the spell slot) and has no effect.

combatMethod.name: Spell Shattering Strike
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. You strike a spellcaster and disrupt their control over magic, fracturing the power behind a spell and transforming the energy so it roots them to the spot. When a creature you can see within your reach casts a spell, you can use your reaction to make {@action Disruptive Strike|TGTT} against it. On a hit, the creature makes a Wisdom saving throw or the spell is interrupted (expending the spell slot) and it rolls {@dice 1d4} to determine how the fraying magic affects it:

combatMethod.name: Branding Steel
entries: {@b Bonus Action (3 Stamina Points)}. You permanently mark your foe with your sword. Before the start of your next turn, when you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack you can choose to brand it. A branded creature has disadvantage on concentration checks and on attacks made against creatures other than you. In addition, a branded creature able to see you cannot move closer to you without making a Wisdom saving throw. The brand on a creature is removed when it regains 30 hit points or more from a healing spell or similar magic.

combatMethod.name: Burning Embers of Faith
entries: {@b Reaction (3 Stamina Points)}. Digging within, you stave off the call of death and surge renewed towards your foes. When you are reduced to 0 hit points, you can use your reaction to heal a number of hit points equal to your proficiency bonus. If you were reduced to 0 hit points by a spell, you instead heal a number of hit points equal to your level. In addition, for the next minute you gain advantage on attack rolls made against the creature that reduced you to 0 hit points, and your weapon attacks against it deal extra damage equal to your proficiency bonus.

combatMethod.name: Bounding Strike
entries: {@b Action (1 Stamina Point)}. You leap and bound toward your enemy with animal fury. Move 15 feet in a straight line and make an Acrobatics or Athletics check to jump as you do so. If a creature is within your reach when you land, you can make a melee weapon attack against it using the result of your check instead of an attack roll.

combatMethod.name: Raking Strikes
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You lash out with one strike that sets up another, faster and wilder attack. Make a melee weapon attack. On a hit, you can make an additional attack against the same target. You have disadvantage on this additional attack.

combatMethod.name: Ride Enemy
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. You leap atop your enemy just as it attacks. When a creature at least one size category larger than you attacks you with a natural weapon, you can use your reaction to make a Grab On basic method, making your Strength or Dexterity saving throw with advantage.

combatMethod.name: Springing Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. As a bonus action, you enter an agile stance. While in this stance, your jump distance is increased by a number of feet equal to your proficiency bonus, and you gain a bonus to Dexterity (Acrobatics) checks equal to your proficiency bonus. This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Expert Tumble
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You throw your body across the battlefield, careening around opponents in a route and manner that makes it difficult to anticipate where you’ll move next. As long as you move your full Speed on your turn (and your Speed is greater than 0), your AC increases by an amount equal to your proficiency bonus until the start of your next turn.

combatMethod.name: Leaping Strike
entries: {@b Action (1 Stamina Point)}. Like a stalking predator you run down your prey and pounce with a lethal attack that throws your opponent to the ground or knocks them back. When you activate this technique, you take the Attack action and make a weapon attack, as well as any additional attacks granted by Extra Attack. Move up to 40 feet in a straight line. If you hit with a melee weapon attack at the end of your movement, the target makes a Strength saving throw, and on a failure it is either knocked {@condition prone} or pushed back 10 feet (your choice).

combatMethod.name: Primal Intercept
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. The instant you sense an opponent has moved within your guard you immediately attack in response. When a creature makes a melee weapon attack or attempts to grapple you, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against it.

combatMethod.name: Gut Strike
entries: {@b Reaction (3 Stamina Points)}. When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can use your reaction to inflict debilitating pain. The target gains one level of exhaustion for 1 minute. If the attack was a critical hit, it gains two levels of exhaustion instead. A creature cannot have more than two levels of exhaustion from this method.

combatMethod.name: Rake
entries: {@b Action (1 Stamina Point)}. With two deft attacks you rend at your opponent, striking at them first to diminish their defenses then delivering a ferocious second blow. When you activate this technique, you take the Attack action and make a weapon attack, as well as any additional attacks granted by Extra Attack. Until the start of your next turn, when you hit a creature with two or more melee weapon attacks, each hit after the first deals {@dice 1d8} extra damage.

combatMethod.name: Wild Capering
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. You move through the environment like a cat, running along walls and easily leaping across gaps. Move 30 feet. Until the start of your next turn, you gain a climb speed equal to your Speed and your minimum jump distance increases to 10 feet vertically and 15 feet horizontally.

combatMethod.name: Blind Instinct
entries: {@b Bonus Action (3 Stamina Points)}. You use all your senses to focus your attacks on a single enemy. You gain blindsight out to a range of 30 feet. If a creature leaves the range of your blindsight, you can use your reaction to mark it as prey and move up to your Speed. The next melee weapon attack you make against that creature before the end of your next turn deals an additional weapon damage die.

combatMethod.name: Bloody Roar
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. A victorious battle cry strikes fear in the heart of your foes. Make a melee weapon attack against a creature. On a hit, you release a mighty roar that shakes the very earth and firmament in a 30-foot cube centered on you. Each hostile creature in the area makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes psychic damage equal to twice your proficiency bonus and it becomes {@condition frightened} until the end of its next turn, or on a success it takes half damage and does not become {@condition frightened}.

combatMethod.name: Mercurial Striking Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. By tapping into your basic survival instincts you not only unleash your potential—you make certain that every strike counts. The minimum result on a damage die rolled with a natural weapon, unarmed strike, or weapon with the {@itemProperty L|XPHB|dual-wielding} property is a 3.
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Furious Barrage
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. You unleash blow after blow, striking your opponent again and again, oblivious to your surroundings. Make a melee weapon attack against a creature. On a hit, make another melee weapon attack. You can keep attacking until you miss. You cannot make more attacks than your proficiency bonus in this manner. Attack rolls against you have advantage until the beginning of your next turn.

combatMethod.name: Wounded Animal Gambit
entries: {@b Reaction (3 Stamina Points)}. Playing dead, you surprise your foe with a sudden attack. When you lose hit points, you can use your reaction to fall {@condition prone} and pretend to be dead. All hostile creatures able to see you consider you to be dead or dying. If a creature moves within your reach before the beginning of your next turn, you can immediately stand up and spend stamina to use a Tooth and Claw method you know against it. No action is required to activate the method and any melee attacks made as part of the method have advantage. After you have used Wounded Animal Gambit against a creature, if you activate it again before the end of combat it receives a Wisdom saving throw. On a success, it realizes you are playing dead.

combatMethod.name: Dangerous Signature
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. You have mastered a strike that is unmistakably your own. Choose a simple symbol (such as a letter) to be your signature. When you have advantage on a melee weapon attack roll, on a hit you can leave your signature on the creature or object you attacked. Your signature remains on a creature until it is fully healed and has taken a long rest. While a creature bears your signature, you can use a bonus action to try to intimidate it. The creature makes a Wisdom saving throw against your method DC or becomes {@condition frightened} of you until the start of your next turn.

combatMethod.name: Throwing Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. You wield your weapon with a practiced familiarity that makes it deadly even when a foe is beyond your reach, able to hurl it as accurately as a thrown dagger. Choose a weapon when you learn this combat method. While you are wielding the chosen weapon it has the rebounding property and {@itemProperty T|XPHB|thrown} property (range 30/60 feet).
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Victory Pose
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. Your victory pose spurs your allies. Choose a weapon when you learn this combat method. When you score a critical hit with this weapon, you can use your reaction to pose dramatically as you finish the strike. As you dramatically pose, choose a number of allies equal to your proficiency bonus that you can see within 30 feet. The chosen allies have advantage on the next death saving throw each makes before the end of combat.

combatMethod.name: Wounding Strike
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. Swinging or thrusting with your weapon like you have thousands of times before, you know precisely where its tip grazes and your swing hits at the smallest flaw in an opponent’s armor to deliver a painful wound. Choose a weapon when you learn this combat method. If you hit with your next attack roll using the chosen weapon against a living creature, you deliver a wound that deals {@dice 1d4} ongoing damage. At the start of each of the wounded creature's turns, it makes a Constitution saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success. Alternatively, the wounded creature, or a creature within 5 feet of it, can use an action to make a Medicine check against your method DC, ending the ongoing damage on a success. Healing magically or from a trait (such as Regeneration) also ends the effect.

combatMethod.name: Deflect Strike
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. With a bit of effort as an opponent strikes at you, you can knock the blow away and divert the attack. When a creature hits you with a weapon attack, you can use your reaction to reduce the attack’s damage by a number of d6 equal to your proficiency bonus. If this method reduces the damage to 0, the attack becomes a miss.

combatMethod.name: Instant Strike
entries: {@b Bonus Action (3 Stamina Points)}. You quickly draw and strike with a weapon in the blink of an eye. Choose a creature within your reach. You draw a melee weapon and use it to make an attack against that creature. You cannot use this combat method against the same creature more than once per combat.

combatMethod.name: Preternatural Strikes
entries: {@b Action (1 Stamina Point)}. By executing your attacks with perfect precision your weapon can slip beneath even supernatural defenses. When you activate this technique, you take the Attack action and make a weapon attack, as well as any additional attacks granted by Extra Attack. Choose a weapon when you learn this combat method. Until the start of your next turn, your weapon attacks using that weapon ignore resistance to nonmagical weapon damage.

combatMethod.name: Disarming Assault
entries: {@b Action (1 Stamina Point)}. Instead of waiting for an opponent to strike so you can slap their weapon away, you lay into them with attacks that knock it from their hands. When you activate this technique, you take the Attack action and make a weapon attack, as well as any additional attacks granted by Extra Attack. Until the start of your next turn, your strikes can deprive foes of their armaments. In addition to dealing damage, when you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack it makes a Strength saving throw. On a failure, an item of your choice that the creature is holding (including a shield) flies from its grip to land in a randomly determined space 10 feet away.

combatMethod.name: Expert Sidestep
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. You respond to an attacker’s weapon with a peerless defensive step that displays your prowess as a warrior. When a creature makes a weapon attack against you, you can use your reaction to gain a bonus to AC equal to your proficiency bonus, possibly turning a hit into a miss.

combatMethod.name: Unsettling Injury
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. Your attack unnerves your foe, making it hard for them to concentrate. Choose a weapon when you learn this combat method. Make a melee weapon attack using the chosen weapon, and on a hit the target makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failure the target loses concentration on any spell it has cast, any combat stance it is using ends, and it cannot use combat methods or cast spells until the end of your next turn.

combatMethod.name: Any Weapon Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. What you wield isn’t what makes you a deadly combatant—you are the weapon. Attaining mastery of some weaponry has taught you how to focus and bring all that practice to bear even with lethal implements you’ve never seen before. You gain proficiency with all weapons (including rare weapons).
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Mistaken Opportunity
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. Mastery is making sure even a mistake becomes an opportunity. You follow through on a miss to attack a different enemy. When you miss with a melee weapon attack against a creature, you can use your reaction to take your momentum and target a different creature within your reach with a melee attack using the same weapon. This attack is made with advantage and deals extra damage equal to your proficiency bonus. If both attack rolls would hit the creature, it is {@condition stunned|tgtt} until the end of its next turn.

combatMethod.name: Wind Strike
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. You use a melee weapon to strike a foe from a distance. Choose a weapon when you learn this combat method. You can use the chosen weapon to strike a distant target with air pressure, giving your attack a normal range of 20 feet and long range of 60 feet. This functions as if your weapon had the {@itemProperty T|XPHB|thrown} property except you don’t actually throw it. You have advantage on attack rolls made using this method. If both attack rolls hit, you deal an additional weapon damage die. After using this method to hit a creature, it can use an action to make a Perception check against your method DC. On a success, for the next minute any uses of Wind Strike do not grant advantage on attack rolls made against the creature.

combatMethod.name: Heart of the Sword
entries: {@b Bonus Action (3 Stamina Points)}. You focus on the bond between you and your weapon. Choose a weapon when you learn this combat method. For the next minute, attacks made with the chosen weapon score a critical hit on a roll of 19–20 and ignore any damage resistances a target might have. If you already have a feature that increases the range of your critical hits, your critical hit range increases by 1 (maximum 17–20).

combatMethod.name: Perfect Assault
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. As an action, you can make twice the number of attacks you could normally make with the Attack action this turn. At the end of your turn, your stamina pool is reduced to 0 and you gain one level of exhaustion.

combatMethod.name: Hamstring
entries: {@b Action (1 Stamina Point)}. You slow your quarry down and keep them from escaping by hobbling their legs or wings. Make a single ranged weapon attack against a creature. On a hit, the target takes an additional {@dice 1d6} damage and its Speed is reduced by 10 feet until the start of your next turn. If the creature has multiple movement speeds, pick one.

combatMethod.name: Resteady
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. You catch yourself about to blunder, steady yourself, and adjust your aim. When you make an attack roll but before you know the result of your attack, you can use your reaction to reroll that attack, taking the new result.

combatMethod.name: Stoic Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You ignore distractions and focus on the task at hand. You make all saving throws to resist the {@condition frightened} and {@condition dazed|tgtt} conditions.
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Thread the Needle
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You see through all distractions and obstacles to strike true. When you activate this method, you take the Attack action and make a ranged weapon attack, as well as any additional attacks granted by Extra Attack. Until the start of your next turn, your ranged attack rolls ignore half cover and treat three-quarters cover as half cover instead.

combatMethod.name: Harrying Shots
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. Your precise attacks lead an opponent when you want them to go. Until the start of your next turn, when you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack, you can forgo damage to instead drive it into an adjacent unoccupied square of your choice. This movement does not provoke opportunity attacks. If this movement would drive a creature off a cliff or into a similar hazard, it makes a Dexterity saving throw with advantage. On a success, it isn’t forced to move.

combatMethod.name: On Task
entries: {@b Reaction (2 Stamina Points)}. Despite any number of intrusions into your mind or distractions surrounding you, your mind is unimpeded and on task. When an effect causes you to be {@condition charmed} or confused, you can use your reaction to prevent it.

combatMethod.name: Run Down
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. With your quarry in sight, you make a final sprint to close the distance and strike like a predator pouncing on its prey. Move up to your Speed in a straight line. If at the end of your movement there is a creature within your reach, you can take the Attack action with a melee weapon, as well as any additional attacks granted by Extra Attack. The first attack you make as part of this method has advantage.

combatMethod.name: Overwatch
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. Attentive and watchful, you snap off a shot at whatever dared to try and catch you by surprise. When you are surprised, you may immediately draw a ranged or thrown weapon and make a single ranged weapon attack at the start of initiative.

combatMethod.name: Singular Focus
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. No obstacle can impede you, and no noise can distract you—there is only you, your quarry, and your determination to bring it down. When you activate this method, choose a creature you can see. Until the start of your next turn, weapon attacks you make against the chosen creature ignore any condition, effect, or circumstance that would impose disadvantage.

combatMethod.name: Stalking Crouch
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. Amidst the chaos of combat, you set your attention on your quarry while keeping yourself {@condition hidden|tgtt} from them. While in combat, choose a creature you can see. You take the {@action Hide|tgtt} action, rolling your Stealth check with advantage. The chosen creature’s passive Perception is considered to be reduced by 5 for the purposes of detecting you, while other creatures consider theirs to be reduced by 3. As long as you remain {@condition hidden|tgtt} and do not move more than half your Speed in a round, any creatures trying to actively detect you make their Perception checks with disadvantage.

combatMethod.name: Hunting Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (3 Stamina Points)}. As a bonus action, you enter a stance and choose a creature you can see as your quarry. While in this stance, you gain a bonus to attack rolls against your quarry equal to your proficiency bonus, but have disadvantage on attack rolls against other creatures. Your speed increases by 10 feet when moving toward your quarry. As a bonus action on subsequent turns, you can choose a new quarry. This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Merciless Ambush
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. You’ve waited until just the right moment to spring your ambush and bring down your quarry. When you activate this method, choose a creature you are currently {@condition hidden|tgtt} from and take the Attack action and make a weapon attack, as well as any additional attacks granted by Extra Attack. Any attacks you make in this way are made with advantage (instead of only the first as normal for surprised creatures) and each deals one additional weapon damage die.

combatMethod.name: Predict Movements
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. Even the swiftest and most erratic quarries have predictable patterns in their movements for the hawk-eyed hunter. Until the beginning of your next turn, any creature that you target with a ranged weapon attack does not add its Dexterity modifier to its Armor Class for the purposes of these attacks.

combatMethod.name: Blood Tracking
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. Every shifted blade of grass and drop of blood tells a story that betrays the prey to the hunter. You quickly scan your surroundings and pinpoint your quarry. You immediately know the precise location of every living creature within 60 feet that is currently below its maximum hit points and do not treat such creatures as {@condition hidden|tgtt}.

combatMethod.name: Split the Arrow
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. When you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack, you can use your reaction to zero in on it. Until the start of your next turn, you gain a bonus to subsequent attack rolls against that creature equal to your proficiency bonus. If a subsequent attack hits, it becomes a critical hit. If it was already a critical hit, the target takes additional damage equal to twice your proficiency bonus and is knocked {@condition prone}.

combatMethod.name: Knockdown
entries: {@b Action}. You can use the Knockdown method to trip or push a creature down. If you are able to make multiple attacks with the Attack action, this method replaces one of them. The target must be within your reach and be no more than one size category larger than you. The target makes a Strength saving throw against your method DC. On a failure, you deal basic melee damage and knock the target {@condition prone}.

combatMethod.name: Overrun
entries: {@b Action}. You can use an action or bonus action to make an Overrun method to move through a hostile creature’s space by forcing your way past. Make a Strength saving throw against the target’s method DC. On a success, you deal basic melee damage and can move through the hostile creature’s space once this turn. The creature can choose to just let you pass instead of rolling. You have advantage if you are larger than the target, or disadvantage if you are smaller. If you are two or more sizes smaller than the target, you can move through the hostile creature’s space without making a check. You still provoke opportunity attacks if you move beyond a creature’s reach.

combatMethod.name: Disarm
entries: {@b Action}. You can use the Disarm method to attempt to knock an item or weapon from a creature’s hand. If you are able to make multiple attacks with the Attack action, this method replaces one of them. The target must be within your reach and be holding an item (either carried or wielded). The target makes a Strength or Dexterity saving throw against your method DC. On a failure, you deal basic melee damage and the target drops the item in its space. The target has advantage if it is larger than you or holding the item with two or more hands, and a target smaller than you has disadvantage.

combatMethod.name: Grab On
entries: {@b Action}. While you cannot grapple a creature two or more size categories larger than you, you can grab onto it. By jumping onto its back or climbing up a limb, you can make a Strength or Dexterity saving throw against the creature’s method DC to cling to or balance upon its body. On a success, you move into the creature’s space and can move upon its body as if it is difficult terrain. While grabbing onto a larger creature, you gain advantage on attack rolls against it. The larger creature may be able to attack you as normal, depending on its limbs or attacks. It can also use an action or replace one of its attacks on its turn to try to dislodge you, such as shaking you loose or bashing you against a wall, forcing you to make a Strength or Dexterity saving throw against its method DC or fall {@condition prone} into the nearest space adjacent to the creature, taking its basic melee damage in the process. If the creature chooses to roll over in order to dislodge you, it becomes {@condition prone}, but you have disadvantage on the saving throw to hang on.

combatMethod.name: Grapple
entries: {@b Action}. You can use Grapple to grab or wrestle a creature. If you are able to make multiple attacks with the Attack action, this method replaces one of them. You must have at least one free hand to attempt a grapple, your target must be within your reach, and the target must be no more than one size category larger than you. The target makes a Strength or Dexterity saving throw against your method DC. On a failure, you deal basic melee damage and the target becomes {@condition grappled|tgtt} (see Appendix 1: Conditions). In addition to things that end the condition, you can release the target at any time (no action required). Escaping a Grapple. While {@condition grappled|tgtt}, a creature can use its action to attempt to escape. It must succeed on a Strength or Dexterity saving throw against your method DC. Moving a {@condition grappled|tgtt} Creature. When you move, you can drag or carry the {@condition grappled|tgtt} creature with you. When you do so, your Speed is halved, unless the creature’s weight is within your carrying capacity and you are not encumbered by bulky items. Freeing a {@condition grappled|tgtt} Creature. You can use Grapple to try to free another {@condition grappled|tgtt} or {@condition restrained|tgtt} creature. The creature grappling or restraining the target makes a Strength saving throw against your method DC. If it fails the saving throw, the {@condition grappled|tgtt} or {@condition restrained|tgtt} creature is freed. Additionally, you may move the freed creature 5 feet in any direction.

combatMethod.name: Shove
entries: {@b Action}. You can use the Shove method to push a creature away. If you are able to make multiple attacks with the Attack action, this method replaces one of them. The target must be within your reach and be no more than one size category larger than you. The target makes a Strength saving throw against your method DC. On a failure, you deal basic melee damage and push the target a number of feet away from you equal to 5 + 5 for every 5 points it failed its saving throw by. You can move the creature in any direction away from you (to the side, forward, or diagonally away). A creature that is shoved off of a precipice is propelled off of whatever it is standing on—it does not receive any saving throw or ability check to grab onto something to avoid falling down below.

combatMethod.name: Taunt
entries: {@b Reaction}. Abilities:Charisma You taunt your opponent into attacking out of rage instead of skill. When you are the targeted by a weapon attack, you can use your reaction to taunt your opponent. The target makes a Wisdom saving throw against your method DC. On a failure, it makes the attack at disadvantage, but deals an additional damage die if the attack is successful.

combatMethod.name: Gain the High Ground
entries: {@b Reaction}. Abilities: Dexterity, Wisdom, Intelligence You move higher on a slope, leap onto a table, or otherwise gain height on your opponent. If you are targeted by a melee attack and it misses you, you can move up after it hits or misses you, you can use your reaction to move up to 15 feet, without provoking opportunity attacks, onto a higher surface in range that allows you the benefits of Attacking from Above and the hazards of Pulling from Below. Depending on the arena, the Narrator may rule you can’t use this dueling method.

combatMethod.name: Goad
entries: {@b Reaction}. When a creature targets you with a melee attack, you can use your reaction to goad it. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it gains a bonus to its attack roll equal to your proficiency bonus, but after the attack, you can move the creature to an unoccupied space of your choice within 10 feet of its current position.

combatMethod.name: Low Blow
entries: {@b Action}. Abilities: Intelligence, WisdomYou attempt to catch your opponent off guard by stomping on its toes or or striking at some other vulnerable part of its anatomy. The target makes a Constitution saving throw against your method DC. On a failure, the creature takes your basic melee damage damage and is {@condition slowed|tgtt} until the end of its next turn. At the Narrator’s discretion, this dueling method may not be effective against certain creatures, or you may have to succeed at a relevant knowledge check to be aware of a creature’s weak points.

combatMethod.name: Knee Strike
entries: {@b Bonus Action}. Abilities:Dexterity, Intelligence, WisdomYou precisely strike at your opponent’s knees or other load-bearing point. The target makes a Constitution saving throw against your method DC. On a failure, it takes your basic melee damage and its base walking speed is halved until the end of its next turn.

combatMethod.name: Blinding Diversion
entries: {@b Bonus Action}. Abilities: Dexterity, Intelligence, or WisdomYou hamper your enemy’s vision by throwing dirt in their face, poking at their eyes, or utilizing some similar tactic. The target makes a Dexterity saving throw against your method DC. On a failure, they are {@condition blinded} until the beginning of their next turn. You must have one free hand to attempt this dueling method.

combatMethod.name: Block
entries: {@b Reaction}. Abilities:Strength, ConstitutionYou use your weapon, armor, or shield to block an incoming attack. When a creature makes a weapon attack against you but before you know the result of its roll, you can use your reaction to make a saving throw against a DC equal to 8 + the target’s attack modifier. On a success, you gain a +2 bonus to your AC against the triggering attack, potentially turning a hit into a miss. On a failure, the attacker rolls an additional damage die for this attack.

combatMethod.name: Reposition
entries: {@b Action}. Abilities: Strength, DexterityYou drive your enemy where you want it to go. When you activate this dueling method, you take the Attack action and make a weapon attack, as well as any additional attacks granted by Extra Attack. Until the start of your next turn, when you hit the target with a weapon attack, you can move it to an adjacent square of your choice (maximum 3 times). This movement does not provoke an opportunity attack.

combatMethod.name: Center
entries: {@b Reaction}. When you or your opponent takes an action or bonus action, you can take a moment to consider an opening to exploit. The next time you use a basic method or dueling method, you can use your choice of Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma to calculate your Method DC for that method, as well as to qualify for any ability score prerequisites it may have.

combatMethod.name: Deflect
entries: {@b Reaction}. Abilities: Dexterity, WisdomBy intuiting your enemy’s movements or through sheer speed, you maneuver an attack away from you. When a creature makes a weapon attack against you but before you know the result of its roll, you can use your reaction to make a saving throw against a DC equal to 8 + the target’s attack modifier. On a success, you gain a +3 bonus to your AC against the triggering attack, potentially turning a hit into a miss. On a failure, the attacker rolls an additional damage die on a hit.

combatMethod.name: Trusting Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. Relaxing your mind, you trust your instincts, allowing your psionic energy to guide your actions. When you roll a 9 or less on an attack roll with a starglaive, you may treat that roll as though it were a 10.
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: I Have The High Ground
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. When you have the high ground, you have the advantage. If you are 5 feet or more higher than your foe and they make a melee attack against you while within reach of your starglaive, you can use your reaction to interrupt their attack with an attack of your own. Make a melee attack. This attack takes place before your attacker’s attack. Quickdraw (1 point)

combatMethod.name: Reactive Re-arm
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. When your opponent disarms you, you recall your weapon instantly. If you are disarmed, you can use your reaction to instantly teleport your starglaive back to your hand.

combatMethod.name: Guided Throw
entries: {@b Action (1 Stamina Point)}. You throw your starglaive at your target, and it returns instantly to your hand. Make a melee weapon attack with a starglaive against a creature within 30 feet. If you have additional attacks granted by Extra Attack, this method replaces only one of them, and you may use the remaining attacks as normal

combatMethod.name: Pass-Through
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You deactivate your blade momentarily just before it would be blocked by your enemy’s, and reactivate it to finish the strike. When you make an attack against an enemy with a starglaive or an energy-imbued weapon, you make the attack at advantage using a method to neatly bypass their defence.

combatMethod.name: Imbued Strike
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You imbue your strike with additional psychic energy. When you next hit a target with a starglaive before the start of your next turn, you deal an additional {@dice 2d6} psychic damage.

combatMethod.name: Blinding Light
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. Focusing your mind on your weapon, the illumination from your starglaive intensifies, blinding your foes. Use an action to intensify your starglaive briefly. All creatures within 10 feet of you who can see the light of your starglaive must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they are {@condition blinded} until the end of their next turn.

combatMethod.name: Shielded Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. You stand firm, deflecting incoming missile attacks. While you are wielding a starglaive, you can use your reaction to slice through or block ammunition when you are hit by a ranged weapon attack. When you do so, your AC against ranged attacks is increased by double your Wisdom modifier (including against the triggering attack) until the end of your next turn.

combatMethod.name: Earthstrike
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You strike the ground with a starglaive maul, causing a psychic shockwave to knock nearby creatures {@condition prone}. Use an action to strike the ground with a starglaive. All creatures within 10 feet of you make a Strength saving throw against your psionics save DC. On a failed save the target is pushed 5 feet and knocked {@condition prone}.

combatMethod.name: Psychic Sunder
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. Focusing your psychic energy, you strike an object with concentrated force. You make a single attack against an unattended object of size Large or smaller, such as a door, with a starglaive maul. On a hit, the object gains the broken condition. If the object is reinforced, you must instead beat its AC by 4 points or more in order to sunder it.

combatMethod.name: Deflect Missile
entries: {@b Other (1 Stamina Point)}. You block a missile and direct it back at your attackers. If your Shielded Stance causes a missile to miss you, you can spend 1 stamina to make a thrown attack with it. You make this attack with proficiency, regardless of your weapon proficiencies, and it has a normal range of 20 feet and a long range of 60 feet. If you hit, the missile does the same damage it would normally have done to you if it had hit you. You do not need to spend any action type to deflect a missile as long as you are able to expend stamina points, but the maximum number of missiles you can deflect each round is equal to your proficiency modifier.

combatMethod.name: Reeling Strike
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. With a gesture, you telekinetically pull a distant foe towards you and strike them with your starglaive. One target you can see or sense within 30 feet makes a Strength saving throw or is pulled towards you until it hits a stationary barrier (e. g. a wall or force field) or is within reach of your melee attacks. If the target ends up within reach of your melee attacks, you immediately take the Attack action and make a weapon attack, as well as any additional attacks granted by Extra Attack.

combatMethod.name: Sever Limb
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. As an action, you make one melee attack with a starglaive blade or polearm with disadvantage. Your attack ignores the target's damage resistances (but not immunities). On a hit, you sever one of the target's limbs. The target takes an additional {@dice 1d10} psychic damage and gains one level of exhaustion. A limb is not severed if the target is immune to the weapon's damage type or is two or more sizes larger than you.

combatMethod.name: Total Dismemberment
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. As an action, you make one melee attack with a starglaive blade or polearm against a creature within reach. This attack is made with disadvantage. Your attack ignores the target's damage resistances (but not immunities). On a hit, you sever {@dice 1d4} of the target's limbs. The target takes an additional {@dice 1d10} psychic damage and gains one level of exhaustion for each limb severed. A limb is not severed if the target is immune to the weapon's damage type or is two or more sizes larger than you.

combatMethod.name: Unstoppable Attack
entries: {@b Bonus Action (3 Stamina Points)}. Your attacks are ferocious enough that nothing can stop them. Attacks you make with a starglaive bypass immunity to its damage until the start of your next turn.

combatMethod.name: Anticipated Intercept
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. You intuit your enemy’s movements and hamper them. When you see a creature start to use its movement, you can use your reaction to move up to your Speed to a square 5 feet from where it started its movement. The creature makes a Wisdom saving throw or its Speed is reduced to 0 for the remainder of its turn.

combatMethod.name: Blind Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. Even when you can’t see, you are able to sense your environment. While in this stance you have blindsight to a range of 30 feet, and you are immune to the {@condition blinded} condition.
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Gunner’s Focus
entries: {@b Action (1 Stamina Point)}. Bringing a heightened focus to the gunner’s seat, you can make a difficult shot seem easy. You make an attack using a vehicle’s direct fire or indirect fire weapon with advantage.

combatMethod.name: Always Prepared
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You are always looking for the right opportunity. You take the Ready action as a bonus action.

combatMethod.name: Thoughtful Assistance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. You have a level of awareness and the presence of mind to support an ally in need. You take the {@action Help|TGTT} action.

combatMethod.name: Elusive Maneuvering
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1-3 Stamina Points)}. You are ever-present in the moment, allowing you to evade foes with ease. You take the Dash or Disengage action (1 point) or the Dodge action (2 points) as a bonus action.

combatMethod.name: Martial Alacrity
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. With a little quick thinking on your feet, you can adjust your attack strategy to find additional ways to hit an opponent. When you activate this method, you take the Attack action and make a weapon attack, as well as any additional attacks granted by Extra Attack. Immediately after, you can make two unarmed strikes against the target of your last attack.

combatMethod.name: Calculated Trajectory
entries: {@b Reaction (1 Stamina Point)}. When you make a ranged weapon attack, you can use your reaction to gain a bonus to the attack roll equal to your proficiency bonus. Alternatively, if using a firearm, you can forgo this bonus to ignore half and three-quarters cover.

combatMethod.name: Psychosomatic Encouragement
entries: {@b Bonus Action (2 Stamina Points)}. You can uplift an ally’s spirits and boost their endurance. Select a friendly target that can see, hear, feel, or otherwise sense your presence. You impart the target with temporary hit points equal to {@dice 1d6} + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier (minimum 0).

combatMethod.name: Assess Defenses
entries: {@b Bonus Action (3 Stamina Points)}. A quick scan reveals weaknesses you can exploit. You can use a bonus action to gain advantage on all attack rolls made during an Attack action you take on the same turn.

combatMethod.name: Pilot’s Trance Stance
entries: {@b Bonus Action (1 Stamina Point)}. You put your game face on as you strap into the pilot’s seat. While piloting a vehicle:
This stance lasts until you are {@condition incapacitated|tgtt} or use a bonus action to end it.

combatMethod.name: Kinesthetic Geometry
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. When you take the Attack action, you can make your attacks with thrown weapons. Until the start of your next turn, any weapon you use with the {@itemProperty T|XPHB|thrown} property has its range doubled, you gain a bonus to its attack and damage rolls equal to your proficiency bonus, and if it hits a target, it rebounds back to your hand.

combatMethod.name: Tactical Retreat
entries: {@b Reaction (3 Stamina Points)}. When you realize an expedient exit is needed (perhaps due to the arrival of enemy reinforcements), you can quickly identify the best route and create the space for you and your allies to make a hasty escape. You use your reaction to make a Wisdom (Deception) check opposed by an enemy’s passive Perception. On a success, you and allies within 30 feet (or within the same region during a space battle) can take the Disengage action and move up to twice your Speed (or engage your FTL engines) without invoking an opportunity attack.

combatMethod.name: Sympathetic Heart Stop
entries: {@b Action (2 Stamina Points)}. By first aligning with then clashing violently against the exact rhythm of your opponent’s movements, breath, and even heartbeat, your physical touch interrupts their life force. A living creature you touch makes a Dexterity saving throw or takes psychic damage equal to {@dice 6d8} + your Wisdom modifier and is {@condition stunned|tgtt} until the end of your next turn. On a success the creature takes half the damage and is {@condition slowed|tgtt} until the end of your next turn instead of {@condition stunned|tgtt}. A creature immune to being {@condition stunned|tgtt} automatically succeeds on its saving throw. The effort of activating this method drains you and you are {@condition dazed|tgtt} until the end of your next turn.

combatMethod.name: Starfield Vision
entries: {@b Action (3 Stamina Points)}. While aboard a capital ship, you can use your action to immediately take one of the ship's system actions. Any attack rolls made as part of this action are made with advantage, and any saving throws forced by it are made with disadvantage. Additionally, until the start of your next turn, you and any allies you communicate with can gain a bonus equal to your proficiency bonus to one saving throw, ability check, or attack roll of their choice.
